sample_reviews.text: I generally love food trucks when the food is of high quality. This food truck had excellent food when I visited Indianapolis. Not only do they not use any MSG or other preservatives but they had all the best Americanized Chinese Food dishes available (including fortune cookies for dessert), and as I was visiting the USA it was a great opportunity to have some Americanized Chinese food dishes...

Oh gosh, I adore this little place, especially for lunch. Their lunch menu is a steal of a deal with every option being about $13 and which includes lemongrass soup, spring rolls, one entree and rice. My go-to option here is the green curry with bamboo shoots (I've tried both beef and chicken) but all of the options are great!

What can I say, this is certainly my favourite stop for Vietnamese subs in downtown Edmonton. I've stopped by twice since they reopened in this location and I've thoroughly enjoyed the chicken sate subs that I've eaten. This location is pretty unassuming, but I'm happy that they have their expansive menu.

Quite honestly I've experienced far better than this place. I've been here a couple of times now, once for dinner before a show at the Citadel (an experience I'm unlikely to repeat) and once for drinks with friends. My pizza at dinner was overcooked and not very good when I came for dinner.

While I didn't actually eat food, the rest of the group that I was with did. I was incredibly disappointed in how long it took for this place to bring me my drink order. I was very nearly tempted to leave without it as it was taking far too long.
They have parking stalls for electric cars!
Reopened this May!

sample_reviews.text:
This is another one of the many nice additions to Seminole Heights. The cityscape in this neighborhood is now changing with a newly constructed multi-story building next door to the front porch restaurant right across the street now. I almost barely recognize the area.

The food selections are pretty decent and so are the drinks. About 50% of the seating is outside while the rest is inside.

I went to this location for the first time during the grand opening. The lines were wrapped around to the back of the building.  They were more folks than I expected who wanted to be a part of their Grand opening giveaway promotion.

Had good times here! For years, I would pass by here many times and always forget to come in here. That was not until recently. When you first walk in, you enter their wine store before you head out to either of the bar counter room on the left or straight ahead into a smaller room with a few dining tables to the right of the store's checkout counter. 

The food was amazing!

It was only my first time here. It was an okay experience. The scallops I had were a bit more salty than I expected though. Otherwise, the deals are great. I happen to have come here on a Friday where they had $2-$3 specials on drinks. This used to be Mangroves, but now under this new ownership, the scene is a lot more casual and beachy than before. I liked it for its laid-back environment.

A very good place to bring a family with children and/or family members who survived the 1950s. This place is filled with a bunch of memorable and a large train train track with functioning train. The pizzas are phenomenal. I have had the pleasure of indulging in both New York and Chicago style pizzas.
Call ahead to place orders and/or to hear updates on their voice messages.
They actually open at 11 AM on weekdays.

sample_reviews.text: With the same high quality standards for delicious food and the grace with which they manage front of house during normal times, Chef John and Julie Cuevas made the pivot to serving customers in these unusual health crisis times through "Family Meal" take-away beginning at 4pm  until 7pm.  Originally available Tue - Sat, as of April 7 they now offer this Thu - Sat (closed Sun - Wed).

Update 'heads up' for a new "Thursday Family Meal" that Certified offered for the first time this week (8/20/20) as delivery - including for a nominal fee to my distant from Boise location of Eagle.  Most of the quality ingredient Boise eateries we enjoy don't deliver to our neck of the woods and that Certified is on our quality list and delivered for only $10 gets a "woohoo" from us!

"Thankful Thursday" (11-19-20) and Yelp suggests we share about a business for which we are thankful.  My choice (and my hubby's) is Madre and for Chef John Chavez and his wife and co-owner Julie who have done such a good job of providing great food and responsible stewardship in these tough times of covid19 with ordered eatery closures and reopening.

This is owned by the same person as is Barrelhouse.  My husband and I would not have gone in had we known because among our all-time worst dining experiences for hospitality and food was a dinner at Barrelhouse.
CLOSED.  Although with a 2.5 star average on Yelp and owner who seemed to let this place slide from marginal at opening to worse as time went on, I imagine it isn't going to surprise or upset many.
Now open in the former Starbucks location in Eagle Plaza.

sample_reviews.text:
I stopped in for lunch, and the ordering process couldn't have been easier. You order your food at the window, pay and make your way to the side patio and await your meal. I ordered the Duo with Chips ($8), which comes with a sizable serving of salsa roja and queso.

The salsa had a nice zing to it.

Man, it's been ages since I ate here. I think it was back in my pre-Yelp days, even.

I stopped in here with a hankering for a burrito (pretty commonplace for me). I first started with some chips and queso and the restaurant's own bean dip and salsa. It was all perfect.

For my entree, I got the Burrito Gigante with shredded chicken and cheese sauce.

We have another case of a mysterious disappearing review on our hands.

I have been coming to Pho Cam Ly for years and it is always consistent and good. I get the pho with brisket and it's perfect. The broth isn't overwhelming and everything is balanced nicely.

My boyfriend and I went here a while ago and it was a disaster.

First of all, one of the fries for one of the meals weren't ready, so of course we had to pull up and wait for the other fries to be cooked.  But, of course, while we wait, our food (and the other order of fries) is cooling rapidly.

Dear Mr. McKinley Eastman,
  I suppose you saw it appropriate to confront me yesterday about my previous yelp review and call it "racist".  I sense that you thought that I didn't note the sarcasm in your voice when you presented your business card (after calling my review racist) and asked me if there was anything you could do to change the rating from the two stars I previously gave your...
They no longer serve alcohol.
Only poke on Sunday

sample_reviews.text:
My Dad has talked about Cafe Bosna for awhile, and I finally had a chance to try it out. 

The personal service is the best, if you're okay with that. The owner will treat you like part of the family. My dad says that she never forgets a face, so I'm already looking forward to going again. 

The secret to ordering is to look at the menu, then ask Sevala what else she has for the day.

There's a few Las Palmas locations in and around Nashville, but this particular location has set the bar for me with fajitas. I can't go anywhere else now and get fajitas that are as consistently delicious like I get at this Las Palmas. There's something about the marinade or seasoning that they use on the meat and veggies that sets it apart from all the others. I think it might be made of crack.

Since I was a kid, I have been fascinated with the Spaghetti Factory. I'm sure it started with the love of the trolley in the middle of the dining area. as a kid, can it get any better than that? Because of this, the Spaghetti Factory is my favorite restaurant in Nashville.

As an adult, this love and fascination has carried over.

I wasn't sure what to expect out of the Local Taco, except maybe tacos. What I found was a quaint place with very helpful and friendly staff and a dining area full of neighborhood people socializing. 

I also found a disappointing experience with the food. I had one of the popular dishes, the Shrimp Enchiladas.

Maybe it was just a bad day for Cheddar's because I've been to this location once before and other locations a few times and never had a bad experience. This past visit left me feeling melancholic. 

The place was packed, though we didn't have a wait to be seated.
The Bacon Cheeseburger Pizza is pretty darn awesome.
Gumbolicious!

sample_reviews.text: It's gotten to the point that when I know I'm coming here, I start skipping.  I can't wait for their deliciousness to get in my belly.  We came here the other night with visiting folks from Maine.  It did not disappoint anyone in our party.  We were seated right away and our drink order taken.

A great lunch at Brio Tuscan Grille before going to the movies.  Brio's is in Plaza Frontenac.  A nice size restaurant that we have eaten at several times but only for lunch.  We sat outside on a beautiful September afternoon.  My last review is dated from January but we have been here recently and I did not review it because of the service.  Sometimes a place can be a little bit off.

How can you not love Ted Drewes?  It's one of my favorite places to get a tasty treat. This last time I went I got a heath bar. Vanilla custard with crushed Heath bar mixed in.  Wonderful. My favorite is the brownie custard. It has large chunks of brownie in it. There is a place to get a cup of water by the windows to go with your treat.  Don't look at the crowd.

What happened to this IMO's pizza. We had the worst pizza ever last night (10/2).  We decided to pick up a pizza on the way home from a ballgame so we called to order it while still downtown. It only takes fifteen minutes to get to Imo's so we knew it would be ready when we got there. 

We wanted a bacon and sausage pizza.

Sometimes you have a bad experience from a restaurant with the food or the service or both and after a while you want to give them a second chance.  After all, can it really be that bad?  Seven months after my last visit, yes it can.  DO NOT ORDER FROM HERE.  GO TO DOMINOE'S OR PAPA JOHN'S OR PAPA MURPHY'S OR PI PIZZARIA BUT DO NOT ORDER FROM HERE.
Scape is permanently closed.
Only service dogs are allowed to be on the patio.

sample_reviews.text:
The chicken is like POW!! 
We stopped in after church to see Lil Dizzy. The buffet is $17 and well worth it. 

Fried chicken, ribs, mac & cheese, grits, beans, gumbo. Am I making you hungry yet? 

We enjoyed our meal outside. It is small inside and there is a wait during peak hours. Just be prepared for it. Food is well worth it.

We ordered curbside and the food came really fast. The large portions are perfect for those days that you just don't want to cook. 

Carrabba's Italian Grill has carryout, delivery, curbside and dining options. 

Carrabba's is also offering a variety of Family Bundles that serves up to 5 people starting at $39.99.

BEST MARGARITAS in CITY of NOLA hands down!!

The bartenders are great and the new chef is consistent. The pollo tacos and Elote' (corn) is muy bien. Loving the patio area and heaters out there. This is a perfect location on Tchoupitoulas and great for social distancing.

Was in the mood for a burger to smash with my sisters. We were in the area already so decided to give this place some play. We had no idea of what to do. Stand in line or plant at a table. Walked to the counter greeted by very busy cashiers and servers. Decisions, decisions decisions! Isn't this the beauty of America? In summary, I chose a baja spicy chicken burger. It was average.

I have been dying to try this place. Heard alot of great things from people who visited in other places on the East Coast. It shocked us that on a Sunday that they had happy hour. We were not expecting that but price was regular hour if you ask me. My french martini was $11 during hh. We got the crab cakes on happy hour menu for half the price. They were so GOOD.
They are open
Brunch is the best ticket in town! Saturday and Sunday from 9:30 am - 2:30 pm.

sample_reviews.text: Uh... Please just order the Morning Cuban... Just wow... and this is coming from a guy who only order the Chroizo Benedict because I thought that was unreal... 2 amazing items for breakfast on one menu? Seriously? If this place wasnt on the run down strip mall with the closed Cue N Cushion this place would be at the top of peoples list every year fr breaskfast!

I drove all the way out here for the Cochon Boudreaux Pizza. This is another cute establishment that is a converted home Into a restaurant. Parking wasn't an issue for me but I can see it being a problem if this place is crowded. I got there right when they opened at 11 and was created by Katie and seated at the bar. The bartenders were extremely friendly and Personable!

Decided to try out a breakfast spot near my brother's home and this was an unexpected surprise.

Atmosphere - Cute kitchen themed breakfast-brunch spot. They have all the fresh pastries lined up on racks for display between the restaurant and the kitchen. The bar area is laid out in a way to allow for lots of patrons to sit and eat. I found the whole decor to be very welcoming.
Decent pizza. A little pricey. its in an airport so what do you expect?
Best part of Brew Brothers is the fact I get free beers and wings from the Eldorado. I wouldn't go here if it wasn't free. Asian Wings are the only thing I order when getting a beer.
Baked eggs!
The Blake with Bacon. Life changing. Just do it.

sample_reviews.text:
Amazing Cuban Sandwich and Roasted Chicken!
Simply delicious cuban sandwich, it's hot and crunchy, meaty, perfect size and great price, I highly recommend this! 
I also got the Roasted Chicken, this was also very good, incredibly tender and juicy meat, this comes with 2 sides. 
I called in the order and everything was ready within 15minutes for lunchtime.

Great pizza and service.  Came in for a late lunch and service was quick and professional.  They do have indoor and outdoor seating, employees wear masks, tables and seats are clean. 
As for the food, we got 3 different medium pizzas.  The Holy Shiitake Pie was the favorite of everyone at the table, the mushrooms add a vaguely sweet flavor without being overpowering.

This has become my new favorite place for pho because of the Beef Back Ribs, the meat, the broth, the noodles, just everything about this dish is fantastic!  Also this type of meat opton is not commonly found at other restaurants.  My 3rd time coming here and obviously they are doing things right, the place is packed each time.

Came on a Thursday night, place was about half full.   We were seated within 1 minute, table for 2, but for unknown reason we were then completely ignored for at least 15 minutes.  No server came by the table to even say "hi, be right with you" let alone get our drink or food order.  Other tables around us were being attended to.

Terrible Enchiladas, decent Quesidilla, but understaffed and slow service made for a bad dining experience. 

SERVICE: 
We came on a Monday night, it was not busy at all.  Maybe the place has half full.  There was no hostess to seat us, the front of the restaurant was getting a bit crowded as 3 more parties came in behind us.

sample_reviews.text: I've had Poke a few different places around the country but none have them have been in coastal areas.  That said, I don't know that the sashimi-grade fish used in Poke is going to vary a lot as I believe most of it is brought in frozen.  Salmon and tuna are pretty run-of-the-mill these days and the rest of the stuff offered is pre-cooked.

Five stars for four bones!

I remember the nearly life-changing experience that was my first bite of the ribs at Sugarfire.  I can't really say that I've experience a similar paradigm shift with pig since then...but that changed at Grace Meat + Three yesterday.  Those four bones were quite possibly the best ribs I've had in my life!

Wow!  That was one impressive benedict!  

I was here for brunch with a group of Yelp friends, the wife and Kid 2.  Pretty much everyone at the table ordered a benedict of one sort or another and I just couldn't pass up the idea of the fried chicken benedict.  Kid 1 considers herself a purist when it comes to benedicts and will only eat a traditional eggs benedict.

Sorry to say, this was my first and most likely last visit to this place. 

The wife wanted a little dessert after the ISC last night and I drove past a half-dozen possibilities to come here. We walked in and the woman behind the counter yelled out that they were closed. I pointed out that the door was open, the signs in the window were blinking and the lights were on.

Have you ever heard the old story about the moron who tries to stick up a tavern filled with off-duty cops?  Pretty sure I witnessed the Yelp equivalent of that colossal screw-up last night...except we were "under cover" because a good Yelper never throws around the Yelp name in an attempt to influence an establishment.
M-F 12-3 is half off entrees
Thanks for the military discount!

sample_reviews.text: Two times I've eaten here, two times with a large group, two times with amazing service. Oddly enough we [my entire lot of family 15 strong with 2 babies] sat in the exact spot the first time we ate here - upstairs overlooking the restaurant, against the rail. Such a great view. It's a little cramped though upstairs since we were close to the stairs the service staff take up to the 1923 Lounge.

I'm so happy the Indianapolis restaurant scene is getting national recognition. When I came home for a quick visit, I was quite determined to check this place out (among others in Ftn Square) since being put on a Best New Restaurants list. One of my best friends and I decided to meet up for brunch there. I assumed since it's a small setup & really good reviews = a wait.

Livery blew me away when I was visiting Indy a few weeks ago. Every once in awhile I hear about an amazing hip, new restaurant that's bound to be uber successful and I put it on my to-do list for my next TBD adventures in the 317. Livery was just that, and an unfortunate circumstance led me and my family there.

Walked here from my abode a few minutes away on a whim for Saturday breakfast with a friend. A little pricey but very tasty! I got an egg sandwich on a bagel & couldn't finish it to save my life- it was just too big! My friend got a breakfast burrito & inhaled it, homemade sauce on the side included. His latte was spot-on too.

Dang. I've been on an Indian kick recently, and I was really looking forward to Amber once I saw the many many positive Yelp! reviews. I dined with a few people over the lunch buffet, 2 which know a thing or two about Indian food (since they are Indian) and they were very nice to go and explain what everything was.
A lot of local beer on tap- don't go for a bud light/dos equis/etc
They accept limited reservations. Only for parties of 6 or more. VERY limited number on the weekends.

sample_reviews.text: My fiancé and I came came here for lunch today... He got the Randy's Wrap (special of the day) and I got the rib sampler with 2 sides and both were tasty! We enjoyed the 4 different bottles of BBQ sauce at the table as well... Gave each bite a kick. The menu gives you lots of options and prices seemed about right. I would come back come back for a quick bite :)

Came here for dinner tonight with my now fiance... we again got the drunken noodles and a different curry dish (the Red Curry) and it hit the spot. Both dishes were just as delicious as they were the first time we came. The restaurant was busier this time around but the waitress was still friendly despite having to run around to serve everyone. This place is a diamond in the rough.

Came here on a Monday night when it was practically empty... 
Wish I knew about this place sooner! My bf and I had curry and drunken noodles and they were both delish. We will be returning again to satisfy our Thai cravings. Try their fried bananas for dessert as well.

I've been to both the Jenkintown and the Dresher location for dinner and found the food to be overpriced for what it was. Just recently I had the turkey burger which was an overcooked turkey burger on a cold bun with tomato and lettuce. Their breakfast specials sound great though so maybe worth another try for brunch. Otherwise... not really worth it.
I enjoyed the view of the city at sunset more than the food... This was a while back, however. Wiling to try again after seeing these other reviews.

sample_reviews.text:
Blue Fin has been open for a week and I've already been there twice, so I think that sums it up. But I'll elaborate.

This is a brightly lit, squeaky clean, tastefully decorated Poke spot that serves not only solid Poke, but Poke with added flair.  The owners have definitely gone the extra mile and included some mix ins that I haven't seen at other places.

Love me some Blaze!!

I enjoy being able to see all of the available ingredients to customize my pizza while I'm making those ever important decisions.  Fresh mozzarella, ricotta, parm? Pepperoni, mushrooms, olives, Brussels sprouts? Yep, Brussels sprouts!! And they were delicious!

The concept isn't brand new when you're talking sandwiches, but it's new to me when it comes to pizza.

This Ruby Slipper is wonderful! Don't get me wrong - every Ruby Slipper I've visited has been wonderful.  This , meaning the Ruby Slipper franchise, is by far my favorite breakfast spot.  From the local flavor, to the originality of the dishes, to the quality ingredients, I just can't get enough.

And the service at this location is excellent as well. 

My go to dish is the Eggs Cochin.

I had lunch at Wasabi a couple of times when they were new and had good experiences.  The food was OK and the service was above average. I had dinner there recently and ordered snow crab rolls. They had a sour taste which led me to believe that the snow crab had spoiled.  The service had gone downhill also.  I doubt that I'll be back.

I had lunch here Monday and am on the fence.  We arrived at 11:30, were greeted and seated right away. Our drink orders were taken a couple of minutes later and our waitress brought our menus.

We ordered pork and shrimp spring rolls, I ordered a grilled pork Bahn mi and my husband ordered lemongrass chicken vermicelli.
Their pizza is still delicious and the takeout service is stellar!
The young lady who gave me my order at the drive thru window wasn't wearing a mask or gloves. I asked her to put on her mask and she looked at me like she had no idea what I was talking about.

sample_reviews.text:
I just moved out here from out West, where you'll find lots of divey little taco shops - $1 tacos are a thing!  I haven't found anything close to the what I used to have in my neck of the woods, and certainly not at the prices I used to pay.

I finally took a wander over here for lunch as a Friday treat and was so happy I did. Got the staple of 3 carnitos tacos for under $9.

Highly impressed with this restaurant experience, because that's what it is - you're not dining, you're having an experience. We were lucky to grab Sunday brunch reservations for 11:30 AM.  Any later, and even with a dedicated parking lot in the back, I can imagine time wasted looking for space in this compact Fishtown location.

Staff here are ON POINT.

So very pleased to welcome this bright, modern and authentic Filipino restaurant. By accident, we happened to meet one of the owners, and we had a great chat and learned a lot about their vision for this restaurant. Thanks, Mark!

If You Have Never Had Filipino Food Before:  Don't be skeeeered. Ask questions and don't hesitate to ask for a little taste if you're not sure you're going to like it.

It's nearly 2:00 am, and you're stumbling back from Bourbon Street towards your hotel next door to this Arby's.  But wait!  Late night munchies!  The neon lights beckon.....

And so here I am at 2:00 am, eating my $3 roast beef sandwich which took 18 minutes to get because the restaurant staff moved slower than molasses, entertaining myself by counting how many drunk people can find the bathroom...

It was late, it was cold, I was kinda craving something hot that was on my way to the train. This was right next to the PATCO stop.

I haven't been in a Burger King in a while, so I opted for a Whopper Junior and asked for no onions and no ketchup, mustard, any of that.

Kalamatas had a line out the door when I visited for lunch. It didn't take long to see why.

I went to Sam's before I even moved to Nashville, and got hooked. It is now a spot I gravitate to. I end up there twice a week at least. 

I once overheard Sam say "I am a food guy that opened up a sports bar not a sports guy that tries to serve food" and it shows. 

Several items on the menu are the best in town, or the best I have ever had.

I wanted to hate this place so badly.

Seriously, eleven dollar burgers?  The staff wear v-neck t shirts, the deep ones, and suspenders.

They all have the most amazingly bad haircuts that cost 140 dollars.

Local Taco should be ringing in at five stars.  They should be competing for best tacos on the planet...they are so freaking close, there is only one tweak that needs to happen, more on that later.

The parking sucks, but the location is neat, cool neighborhood.

This place is a real find.

It is nestled between a bar and a pizza joint and is very unpretentious.

It is quite small but I have never had to wait more than a few minutes for a table.

The staff are always...

sample_reviews.text:
Nestled in the middle of the Village at West Clay. It's a great place to enjoy a drink and a meal outside. Kids are welcome. I saw some dogs outside too. Parking is easy. 

The burgers are good. The Italian sandwich is delicious. I like the food we tried. This is our first visit and I'll be back to try more later!

Honey Baked Ham is a part of our family tradition.  We've lived in the midwest and the east coast.  All our homes have had a Honey Baked Ham brought in for Christmas Eve dinner.  This year, 2020, was no different.  We called, pre-ordered the ham.  I drove up to curbside pickup and my ham was brought to me - easiest thing ever.  It was new for 2020, but I hope they keep that process going.

My wife and I ordered carryout off the 2021 Valentine's day menu.  We brought home: Arancini (fried risotto) for an appetizer, Chicken Involtini for my wife, and I had a seafood plate. 

I thought the food was excellent. Mouth watering, filling, tastes from someone who is an artist in the kitchen. 

We plated our dishes at home. I say we did a pretty good job.

The Cake Bake Shop a picturesque cake bakery.  You can get desserts (cakes) by the slice or a whole cake.  The store is tucked away in a neighborhood in Broad Ripple - off a side street, but still close to the action.  It has parking.

Once you go inside, it is pretty magical.  It is like you are being transported to another time in far away France.

I'm not sure why I pay to shop. I'm not sure why I stand In a long line to pay. I'm not sure why I walk through a very busy, giant store full of tired, annoyed people. I am not sure why I buy way more canola oil than I need just because "it's cheaper."  If I figure these things out, I may increase the stars on this review.
Outdoor seating!
They have self service coffee. Avoid the line (cash required)

sample_reviews.text: I've had Pharoah's donuts before at various events and from different businesses that sell them. I am sad to say I never made it down to their original location! I was so happy to read they were opening a location in the CWE right on Maryland. I came this morning around 8:30 and they had a steady flow of customers. I got the cinnamon roll, Apple fritter, pink sprinkle, and a cinnamon cheesecake.

How have I never reviewed La Patisserie Choquette? This place is next level when it comes to pastries. During the pandemic online ordering has become a little like the Hunger Games. Once 9am hits (or 8am on Saturday) may the odds be ever in your favor.

This was the first time I had visited this location since I was in the area. I ordered ahead but chose to go through the drive thru since I had my baby with me. There was a guy taking orders on a tablet when you drove up and I gave him my name for pickup. The line was long but moved through quick and efficiently. My order was ready when I got to the window and was prepared correctly.

I usually go to the Schnucks on Hampton which is my absolute favorite local grocery store but this Brentwood location is also convenient to my house so I occasionally stop in. The store isn't organized as logically at this location and I feel like people are constantly trying to run me over with their carts trying to get through these narrow aisles.

I used to really love Strange Donuts and I want to still love them because they're in my neighborhood but they are just average to me. They have really creative flavors which is the draw but for the price you would expect them to also taste excellent.
This is a new location for them and I am able to order delivery through Postmates hoorah!
They always have great Groupon deals so you can try without breaking the bank

I frequently shop at Nordstrom and decided to try the Nordstrom Grill for the first time today.  It's a great place for lunch, a little on the pricey side but the food was very good.  We were seated immediately (which is very rare at noon in Green Hills) and the service was great.  

I had the Chicken Paillard salad and my friend had the Crispy Chicken sandwich.
This restaurant is in a weird spot but worth the search.  Fantastic Thai, descent prices, great atmosphere!  We will definitely be back.

Chick-fil-A is by far my favorite fast food restaurant ever! The location and Charlotte West is really nice. Just like the other locations they have ample seating in the dining room. When I visited this location over the weekend,as usual there was a very long line both inside and in the drive-through. I opted to go inside and they were able to get everyone through the line very quickly.
Burger is great but way overpriced.  We stopped in for an early dinner/happy hour.  We each had a beer and ordered the burger and a steak sandwich.  Our bill was almost $50.  Not worth it.

I know people love the Pancake Pantry but I am not a fan.  It's very touristy, expensive for breakfast food and the lines are always ridiculous.  

The food is good but there are much better breakfast options in Nashville to wait that long for a table in my opinion. I prefer Noshville's griddle cakes or even something from Fido (both in the same neighborhood) if I'm going to splurge on breakfast.
Sweet potato skins!

sample_reviews.text: This is perhaps less to do with the general decor as with the premise of the place itself (pun intended). It looks a little bit rundown, but the staff are absolutely lovely, the people seem happy there, and you can get lost for hours (for a flat rate! yes! it's real!) in hard to find, unique board games. This place just makes me really really happy. Come here if you want to be happy.

I mean, call me basic, but I love Tim Hortons. I shamelessly love their sugary drinks and loaded bagels. This Tim Hortons is a standout for their quick service, happy staff, and general cleanliness. There is also quite a bit of natural light!

I came here with a massive group of friends and we were accommodated easily for brunch. The bistro was actually quite empty. 

I am a self-professed French Toast connoisseur. I loved their French Toast. My oh my, it was delicious. I definitely recommend it if you come by for brunch, although my friends enjoyed their Eggs Benedicts as well.

I'm a vegetarian who very occasionally allows herself fish, so this place is not the most conducive to my diet. However, I saw a Groupon for this and snapped it up because Urban Shabu's golden buns are very delicious. I still had a fulfilling meal despite my dietary restrictions. 

Although service is usually quick, they usually shortchange your order like many AYCE places.

My first stop on Downtown Dining Week! I love tres carnales so I had to visit. I was disappointed that vegetarian options were not offered on the menu and the hostess was so sassy that I didn't want to inquire as to substitutions. However, knowing that it was a meat-focused restaurant I had resolved myself to break my "meat fast."

And it was worth it.
Extra condensed milk for shaved ice costs extra, so be judicious with your first saucer!

sample_reviews.text: Met my girlfriend here  for drinks and a snack. Had it not been pouring we would have sat outside. The food was fantastic! We sat at the bar and ordered the octopus, the fried artichokes, the meatballs and a burrata  pizza. Terrific time. Delicious food. Can't wait to go back.
Delicious! Fresh tasting sushi & sashimi.  Definitely try the eggplant lady and jays special roll.  Prices are reasonable.  Clean. Cheap. Delicious.

Great place! Another spot I tried during restaurant week.  Guacamole made to order once you choose ingredients. Love that!  I tried the flatbread and shrimp entree. I filled up on the guacamole & chips and flatbread so, I couldn't enjoy the shrimp.  I will definitely be back. Oh, if you like tequila this is the spot to try a flight.

Armed with my $20 living social deal I tried La Creperie Cafe today. I walked in and to my surprise the place was pretty empty. The host/waiter brought me to my wobbly table for 1 and left me with the menu. If crepes are your thing, there are many to chose from. I went with Feta et Lardon and Suzette, both poor choices. The bacon was dried , almost like beef jerky, not crispy or flavorful.

Recently my parents and I had lunch here. The menu offers many options. We tried the Cuban sandwich, pulled pork sandwich, soup of the day which was a pumpkin and beet salad with chicken.  The soup was to sweet. Portions for lunch were huge. The fries were tasty and for me the best part. It was ok. Not sure I'd go back for a meal.
Great cocktail and tasty food!!
Perfect spot for cocktail and snack. Try the chip with smoked onion dip.

sample_reviews.text: It has been a while since I have been here but the food is still just as good as I remember. We stopped in for a quick lunch before going to a dog bar and luckily this place is dog friendly and have plenty of outside seating. My husband got the fish and chips, we split the deviled eggs and I had the duck wings.

I will say that it was disappointing that they were out of tea the time that I went there but otherwise I would give this place 5 stars. My friend suggested the sushi place before we went into the crumble cookie place next door for a light lunch.

Overall I give this place 5 stars because the food was delicious with great flavor as were the cocktails. I will say the food is a bit pricey for the portions but delicious. My best friend comes here often with her husband and I haven't been in almost 10 years. It has been remodeled and looks totally different. It's beautiful and much larger.

This place was quaint and had a nice atmosphere. The service was friendly. I give this a three which might not be helpful due to the fact that I just didn't take to Ethiopian food. I love cultural food and I was excited to try Ethiopian food as my friend married an Ethiopian and raved about this place.

I would say 3.5. Why? This is a good option for a to go morning breakfast while at work or if you want to get a quick bite to eat before going about your day. I will focus on the good about Proino's. I have done to go orders there regularly because they are near my job. I like the sizing of the portions of food and I do feel that the prices are very reasonable.
It can get busy on the weekend a bit.
Make reservations when you can. It's packed but worth it!

sample_reviews.text: I've enjoyed concerts at the Schermerrhorn in past years but am excited that they have really stepped out of the box and have been offering so many diverse options for enjoying their venue. They're truly trying to draw and entertain all ages and tastes.

We've been to this location a number of times. It's a great half way point to meet the family for casual celebrations. We're able to get a good variety to meet the various likes of everyone at a reasonable price. We're never had anything but great service and satisfying food. The Newk's Q is a favorite. The salads, soups and pizzas are an overall satisfactory choice.

What a jewel! Can't wait to return. I didn't expect what was inside. I figured based on the outside we would be waiting a while for a table. Not the case. There was a huge dining area with plenty of seating. 
We started off with the cheese dip. It was delicious and we had plenty of it too. We each had two tacos with sides; El pastor, shrimp, fish and chirrizo.

We've been eating here for years but I've never written a review. I like to try a place for a while to give a true objective opinion. Not true! But if that were the case then I'd have to say that Top Hog is consistently good. Don't go in thinking that this is a
pretty, trendy burger cafe. It's not! It's a place where construction workers, bankers and senior adults eat good food.

Packed house but great as usual! All around good food, clean and extremely friendly staff. Although I'm not vegetarian I do like the occasional veggie option. I can get a veggie fajita that's seasoned as
If there were meat in it. This is one of many in Gallatin but it is a favorite of many.
What a jewel! Can't wait to return.
Mexican Twinkie Popsicle

sample_reviews.text: If you haven't been here yet, you need to go! Worth the drive if you are not close by too! I stopped by here tonight while wanting a healthy takeout option and looking to support our local businesses. I was able to order online and customize my own bowl for only $11. The bowls are generous!!! Three scoops of your poke choices and nearly unlimited toppings of just about anything else they offer.

Support your local businesses! I absolutely love Alpine for my party needs. This liquor store has replaced the chains for me. They have amazing prices, a darn good selection and the staff is freaking awesome. If anyone in prior reviews said the staff was rude or unhelpful, it's probably because they were nasty to them first.

I can't believe I hadn't been here before!!!! I've passed by a million times but never took the time to stop by until this past week for Happy Hour. Such an awesome place in the neighborhood!!!! Happy hour prices are great and the food was really tasty. My friend and I split the charcuterie board, wings and charred avocado.

Yes and No. This Walmart is much better than others in regards to parking and crowds. They have self checkout which can sometimes help. During busy times (weekends/holiday season) you can expect to stand in line forever and a day to check out.

Great place to hang out in Tampa if you are looking for excellent people watching! If you're a gambler, you'll be even more satisfied than I am with this place. By no means is it Vegas worthy, but you can get your fair share of table games and slots. The restaurants are decent (although i have not tried the steakhouses) and the entire facility is very clean.
All day happy hour on Wednesdays
$3 mimosas on Sunday

you're never at vetri cucina unless it's a damn special day in your life... unless you're one to casually drop $170 for a casual dinner then good for you.

came here for my birthday last night and it was quite an experience that every philadelphian needs to experience by mr. vetri.

we started with some fancy aperol-like spritzes which i enjoyed.

Came for a date night on a Sunday and had an amazing meal!

our server was so friendly and not pretentious at all compared to some of the servers i get around the city. he helped me order and made sure i got to pack all my leftovers home.

we started with the carrot hummus which was DELICIOUS!

had such a wonderful night at this quaint cozy BYOB!

it's perfect distance from Bok Bar as well so we went there first for some pre-dinner drinks and came here for a date night dinner.

we started with the escargot, gnocchi and fried oysters. i'm not a huge fan of escargot but my boyfriend said it was scrumptious!

If I could give 0 stars I would. This place is TRASH. UBER EATS REVIEW:

i had such a craving for mapo tofu on a cold night i decided to place a delivery here since i had a good dine in review! i was so disappointed...

the mapo tofu was like an orange color, nowhere near the vibrant red and spice that i wanted. the tofu wasnt't even the soft kind that's usually used, it was hard and not what i wanted at all. no flavor, didn't taste good.

sample_reviews.text:
I have been to a lot of beer festivals and this is one of the best in the state.   Although there are about 5000 attendees, there are so many breweries that you never have to wait in line for more than a couple minutes.

There are plenty of food trucks available and porta-potties galore.

This is a very different kind of brewery (hence the name).   It falls into the nanobrewery category due to the limited production.   In fact, I think the only place you can get their beer is from this location.

Even though it is a small production brewery, Deviate always has 8 beers on tap.   You can buy them by the pint or sample 4 or all 8 at a time.

This is an amazing place.   I can't believe how big it is.   It's as big as a Vegas casino.   We were passing through Nashville and had to spend the night in town so we decided to check out the Christmas decorations.

I cannot understate how amazing the place looked inside and out.   I know now why several people have told me I have to check out the Gaylord during Christmas.

I ducked in here after walking the strip on a Sunday afternoon.   It was a hot day and I just wanted something cold and sweet to satisfy me.

It was just OK.   Better than your usual chain ice cream shop but not any better than your typical locally-owned soda shoppe.

Their freezers may have been on the fritz as my ice cream started melting before I even left the store.

This is a placeholder review as I went for lunch and I know this is known for being a breakfast joint.

I usually avoid chain restaurants like the plague, but this is their first location in Indiana and I had never tried it in my travels.

Service was a little scatterbrained but that could be because it was so hectic at lunchtime.  The food itself was just OK.  Kind of greasy spoon in my opinion.
1/2 price boneless wings on Wedneadays
FYI.    The "Join Waitlist" function on their Yelp page does not work.   The business is working to have it removed.

YUM! I'm glad that Charleen S. suggested this place as a brunch date with our respective Ben S's today. It was a great experience! 

I was a bit worried about parking considering it is in Clayton. We parked on the street across from the Ritz and walked a block easily. Free meters on the weekend! There is a garage too but I'm sure it's a pay lot. 

It's a cute and inviting space.

I love Dewey's. Out of ALL the pizza possibilities in this town. Dewey's is my #1! My preferred location is the Webster Groves location due to its proximity to my house, but I recently found my way to this location due to it being close to where I was at the moment. 

All Dewey's locations are completely consistent in terms of quality and service, and this was no different.

I've been wanting to try this place since it opened and finally got a chance on my 5th wedding anniversary! While I take my baby son out to many restaurants, this isn't the kind of place I wanted to spend a bunch of money at and have to worry about him. So we came child-free. We made a reservation, which was a good idea. It was packed!

My colleague and I were craving coffeine this morning, so I decided to run to Northwest, which is only a couple minutes from work.

All I can say is BLECH. Ordered a chai latte. Definitely the worst one I've ever had. It was watery and utterly flavorless. I wouldn't have thought it even had any "latte" in it other than the foam on the top. It tasted like a tea bag on the third brewed cup. Nasty.

The International Supper Club headed to Momo's last week and I was really looking forward to it!

Too bad that it didn't deliver. At all.

We had a large group - probably about 14 people. First mistake. NEVER, EVER go here with a group, unless you like having to use a calculator and split the check at the end. They won't split checks "because it's tapas" - uh, ok.

They offer carry out but you have to show up, order and wait- no calling in. Best to come right at opening (already a line at 10:55!) or later in the afternoon but there is also the chance of selling...

Made online order; they said 30
Minutes. Got delayed and got there 45 minutes later and it wasn't even started. Not fun with two hangray kids and a hangry hubby.

Another great brewery in Indy!!

- Wonderful Beer!! They have a great selection of their core beers that are always on draft and then the seasonal/rotating beers that they create are great to try when they last..

- Great staff, never​ had any issues with the staff, they are all super helpful and super chill

- Location is super easy to get to right off the interstate or if you live downtown just...

I have eaten or ordered take out here at least 50 times over the past couple years since i went vegan and i will say this is hands down the best vegan spot in Indy, one of the best in the mid-west. 

I highly recommend the Sheltonator, The Satanderloin, The Rich Girl, Seitan High Fives and the Buffalo Mac.

I know not everyone likes Whole Foods. But let me tell you about why I do and especially this Whole Foods. 

I will start off first by telling you that I generally come here during the week in the afternoons. That is the absolute best time to come, no wait outside (for COVID rules) and even before the pandemic there was never many people in here.

The food is really good and i love their vegetarian and vegan options. 
Their beer selection is great, lots of locals as well as their own beers.
Great seating options, small bar to sit at, but large bar dining room, then a family dining area and a huge patio to sit at.

Well when your related to St. Elmo's and Dunaway's you've got to prove your a part of the family and Harry and Izzy's does. You have a great selection of decently priced lunch options, some moderately priced dinner options, amazing (but pricey) steak and seafood options and yes the infamous shrimp cocktails!!
They have a full vegan and gluten free menu as well!

Try the  burgers! They are great!
Also don't park at the bottle shop, you'll get towed

My friend who went to Haverford told me that when I move to Philly, I have to try the best burrito he's ever had in his life.  What a disappointment.  I've lived in southern California for 10 years and this is really not what I expect when I think of authentic and delicious Mexican food.

These guys are pretty new, but already I am seriously loving this place.  It is a real gem of a coffee shop, and has serious charm, not to mention dynamite beverages.

They have an entrance through their building on Arlington, but the main cafe is actually located on Roff Way, which is almost more of a back alley, to be honest.

Yes, I know I just recently reviewed this place.  But dang it, I cannot stay away, or say enough good stuff about Reno Coffee Company.  Too often the fancy espresso drinks get all the glory.  But you will find none of those here.

Record Street Brewing has been talked about around town for a while, and especially during these crazy times it is good to see a new business opening up and taking a stab at continuing to build and develop the Brewery District here in Reno.

As the name would suggest, they are located on Record street, and the intersection with Fourth, inside the extensively renovated old Alpine Glass building.

When talking about the brewery scene in Reno, this place seems to get swept under the rug a bit, which is a shame, since it is one of the old school breweries here in town.  And I would call this one of those Reno institutions you just HAVE to try at some point.  So when the rating level says "A-OK", that is exactly what I think it means.

My first Canfest!  I'd been trying to attend for the last couple years now, but something always come up preventing me from attending.  Needless to say, I was excited to finally experience this event for myself.

Hosted this year at the Grand Sierra Resort, it is a celebration of beer from breweries all across the region and the country.

Yum yum. This is a cute little place located in the heart of broad ripple. We came about an hour before closing. We got 2 regular Cuban sandwich's and the rice, bean, pork mixture. The staff was friendly. The restaurant is clean and I love the decor. 

The food was really good. The sandwich was so filling I had to take the other half home. I could have went w the mini. We will be back for sure.

Yes they are cash only, yes they have early hours but let's talk donuts.  My husband works in Plainfield and brought some of these home.  They are so good.  Maybe dare I say it the best I have had in the Indy area.  Many small local donuts shops are cash only and that is just how it is.  So grab a couple bucks, roll out of bed and pick up some of these delicious local hand made goods.

Came here for some beers and Karaokee. Cute little neighborhood bar. They do not have Sun King so that is sad but drinks are cheap.  I didn't eat. Place is clean, servers are kind. It is smoking but it isn't one of those nasty smokes bars, I feel their filtration system works well.

I used to complain about the $3 entertainment fee that this place slips in on the bill and it still annoys me a bit.  I just wish they would have someone collect the cover charge at the door...  Anyways...

My daughter loves Fazoli's so I brought her here. She is 15 and she happened to pick kids eat free night. (Do not go on Tuesdays if you don't have younger kids). I could tell by the sad faces when we walked in people had been waiting a long time. We ordered and got our drinks. I felt $19 for 2 pieces of pizza and a little pasta meal was a bit prices but my daughters happiness is priceless.
They now accept cards. Also the Grog coffee is amazing.
$1.99 margaritas Wednesday and Sunday.  Try the jalapeño one!

sample_reviews.text:
Sometimes smaller is better!  Not too many guys say that but we'll stay on the subject of hoagies!

If you sell something for over 35 years and survive you've gotta be doing something right.

4 1/2 stars influenced a coworker and I to visit this postage stamp sized place for lunch this week.  It's off the beaten track about two minutes from Lancaster Ave.

This was an excellent BEEFivore experience and that was exactly what I was looking for.  I blew in here for lunch after an aborted jury duty experience.  

My first impression was IMMACULATE.  This place was spotless!  In the men's bathroom I swear you could eat off the floor.  I didn't check the ladies room as I didn't want to deal with the yelling and screaming that would ensue.

The word "Fermentaria" connotes strange things in my mind.  Does it mean that this is where very old beer aficionados go to die and get turned into ashes?  In this case it might mean die of happiness as this is a very good beer emporium with excellent food to boot.

I blew in here on a Thursday around 2:30 for a late lunch.  Even at this odd time the place was jumping.

What a beautiful building!  Walk inside and look up.  It really is beautiful architecture.  Now that I've said something positive I'll move on.

You go here if you are a tourist.  It has every Philly souvenir store imaginable.  It's definitely a one stop shop for Philly stuff and for that it's convenient.

It has a large food court with all the usual chain suspects.

This place is in the back of Manoa Shopping Center.  There is no front entrance.  The good news is parking is a breeze.  That's about the only good news.

Szechuan Lei Lei has been around for many, many years.  Probably too many.  The décor is dated and the place looks a little beat up and the lighting is dim.  

The food is at best nothing special.
The old Zac's is morphing into a Toni Ron's!
Although the Yelp hours say they are open for lunch, that's not true quite yet. I tried today and was told it would be a few days. Dinner only for now as they work on getting their act together.

sample_reviews.text:
Long live the Bookstores.  I love Books and Bookstores.  There is nothing like being in a place with others who appreciate the written word.  This location is huge, bright, and cheery.

Barnes and Noble offers more than just books.  They have Toys, Gifts, and Collectables.  There is a Starbucks inside this Barnes and Noble.  Seating options all over this store make you want to sit down and read.

Sauce on the Side is one of the best things that has happened to St. Louis.  I love these warm, cheesy, melty, gooey folded golden calzones.  The portions are right on, the flavors are right on, the left overs are almost better than the first bite!  

The salads at Sauce on the Side are strong enough to stand on their own.  If Calzones are not your thing, stop in for a salad.

Schuncks has blown me away with their safety protocols since the pandemic began.  They are taking every precaution to keep their customers and employees safe.  They were one of the first stores around to place Plexiglas shields at the registers, mark 6 ft spots near the registers, offer hand sanitizer, host a senior hour and time for those with compromised immune systems, and promote mask...

Certainly not the best, but they "deliver".  Delivery takes at least one full hour or more- beware.  Foods need a little help with red sauce, soy sauce, or red pepper flakes for flavor.  Fried foods very greasy.  Co-workers like more than I do.

Fast Pizza Spot.  Walk in service and Delivery.   This location has indoor and outdoor seating.  Kid friendly.  Excellent place for teams to go after games. 

Lunch Special of 2 slices and a soda is $5.43 with tax.  Very filling.  

The best part of this pizza is where the cheese gets a little toasted on the edges- yummy.  They use mozzarella cheese.  Thick crust pizza.
Whole Store is Nut Free and Gluten Free
Closed for Vacation from July 31-August 13, 2018

sample_reviews.text:
A trip to The Fountain on Locust is like I got into my DeLorean, fired up with flux capacitor, and set it for the Roaring 20's. Hellloooo McFlyyyyy...this place is awesome! 

Every trip to the general vicinity around the Fountain becomes an excuse for us to stop in. I've only gone for the ice cream, but they do have a menu of soups, salads and sandwiches.

Welcome to the neighborhood Pan d'Olive because you are awesome! From service to dinner fare, my first visit was perfection.

Let's start with atmosphere. This place is contemporary and beautiful. My favorite part is that we were seating in an enclosed booth that created a very intimate dining experience.

I admit, I don't often give a 5-star review on my first visit, but I was blown away by Three Flags Tavern's service, food and atmosphere. Don't judge this place by its outside appearance. We had a lovely dinner here and I will definitely keep it in mind for special occasions.

Get the brisket burger. Just get it! This burger is top notch - literally dripping with awesome meat flavor.

Visiting Crown Candy around once a year is a must for me. We're so lucky to still have this little reminder of the St. Louis' rich history.

Yep, it is in somewhat of a shady area - BUT, I was surprised to see on this visit that there has been quite a bit of rehabbing done. They are definitely working on it.

The service is clearly a result of the line you have to wait in to get a seat.

Upon entering into Mi Ranchito,  you will ask yourself one or more of the following questions:

1. Did Mexico throw up in here?
2. Am I here for a secret meeting with a member of the Mexican mafia?
3. Will the neon green hot sauce on the table actually kill me if I try it?
4.
They have the best homemade blue cheese dressing.
Join their vip program on their website. Not only do they send you coupons weekly, their emails are hilarious.

There's pizza, there's good pizza, and then there's everyone stops talking because they're too busy stuffing their face pizza.

Dewey's pizza renders me instantly speechless.  I love love love it.  It's the best pizza in St Louis, hands down (I realize that this statement is likely heresy in the Arch City, but I stand by it).

This was the best meal I've had in St Louis in long time.  I had heard good things on yelp, but the meal we had exceeded my expectations.  The standouts were the bacon wrapped meatloaf with the most buttery mashed potatoes I've ever enjoyed, as well as the breakfast for dinner.  That was easily one of the best breakfasts I've ever had...and it was for dinner!

I didn't think it was possible to have a better meal than one I had last time I was at Farmhaus, but I was wrong.  This meal exceeded what I thought was a near perfect meal.

Standouts this time were the porchetta sticky buns, which were like a punch in the mouth full of deliciousness, and the grouper with herb gnocchi and spring vegetables.
Interesting flavors (I especially like the chocolate with hot pepper in it), but the consistency of their gelato tends to be grainy. Not the best gelato to be had in St Louis.

Meh.

I was here a few nights ago with some friends.  Our original plan was to get some tapas, but after reviewing the menu I settled on the butternut squash soup and the roasted beet and apple salad with goat cheese.  

Why go to a tapas restaurant and not order tapas?  Well, they looked too complicated. I'm an adventurous eater, so it was not an issue of me not wanting to try new things.

sample_reviews.text: Absolutely amazing!  So I figured that Sunday after Thanksgiving would be a good time to check out FD because lots of people would still be eating leftovers, and I think I was right.  I got there a little after 11:45 and was informed that they were just doing wings either 6 or 12.  I love bonchon chicken in NYC so the wings were a perfect option for me.  I got number 32 and ordered 6.

I'm going to add on to the 5 star reviews because this place is awesome.  I had zero experience with chaat, but when I was in the area and did a yelp search for restaurants, I had to try it.  The owner is super friendly and will guide you through the process of making your meal.

I've managed to grab a platter from the elusive falafel truck a few times.  It's a huge plate of really delicious food.  Most recently I had chicken with couscous? hummus, and a kind of sweet orange sauce, plus a piece of bread and a pita.  It was amazing.  It's way too much food, and I try to make it last two days which is hard because it tastes so good.

Currently, Makiman makes me angry, but I would be willing to give them another chance.  It was a Friday night, and Katrina C. and I wanted to grab a quick dinner before a party.  We were in a hurry, so we figured if we ordered take out it would be faster.  I asked the hostess if that was true and she said yes.

I really want to like Crazy Sushi, especially because so many people are giving them good reviews, but I went back last weekend to use a groupon and I still wasn't impressed.  We ordered a couple specialty rolls and a basic roll.  It just seemed like the rolls were all filler and very little fish.  My friend commented on this as well.
Iced coffee is awesome!
Free lunch!

sample_reviews.text: If you are pregnant, you really need to waddle your way over here.  While they will work on any patients, the doctors specialize in prenatal chiropractic.  I have never been to a chiropractor before this, but I have been so miserable with my pregnancy, I was desperate.  I was afraid they would crack and beat on me, but they were so gentle!

I bright pink front door, hand pies the shape of Indiana, and the sweetest shop owner?  Yes, please!  As an extra bonus, they happen to sell delicious pies whole or by the slice.  There's indoor seating and also outdoor tables for the summer (sadly writing this review in snow-covered January). The sugar cream and blueberry peach are delicious.  Can't wait to try the key lime!

Samples, samples, samples, samples!!!!

Everything here is great and I love the free samples.  A few of my favorite items:
- underwear; the Felina brand is great!
- sun-dried tomatoes; great price and so tasty
- individual servings of guacamole
- cheapest portobello mushrooms around
- cheese selection!
- thin crust pizza kits with sauce packets included

Messing up Mexican food is like messing up pizza -- it's got to be REALLY bad.  The red "enchilada sauce" tasted like tomato soup with less spice.  The chimi-cheesecake was wonderful -- but quite obviously store-bought.  Same with the queso dip.  

Another gripe -- we were the ONLY ONES in the restuarant and still struggled to flag down our waiter for our order and more water.

According to management, the fire marshal will not allow you to park your stroller by your table (despite having ample room to get through).  They could see we had a sleeping child in the stroller, but made no effort to come up with any sort of accommodation.  There were many tables at the front of the restaurant where we could have kept the stroller right next to us.
Bathrooms are just as gross as you'd expect.
Drew is a friendly bartender.

sample_reviews.text: Everything that passed my lips was delicious during my two visits. You get a good amount of amazing food for a reasonable prix fixe. I can't rave enough about the Foie Gras Soup...a great way to start a delicious meal. It looks like prices have gone up since I last visited, but I still plan to head back.

I made reservations for a Tuesday evening at 7pm, but it was unnecessary as the restaurant was fairly empty. Our waiter forgot our green tea, and when we reminded him, he kindly made it on the house for the inconvenience. The food was delicious and fresh - my friend loved her Uni (which she had been craving) and Rock Shrimp, and I was delighted by the quantity and quality of fish in my Chirashi.

Suraya was always amazing when we could enjoy dinner or brunch at the restaurant, and COVID-19 takeout keeps up that high bar. The online ordering and pickup process for the reasonably-priced Tasting Menu for 2 was easy. We especially loved the variety of mezzas (the Labneh is so good!) and the well-prepared Halabi Kebab and Samke Harra entree options.

I ordered the Smoked Salmon Eggs Benedict and was pretty disappointed - the English muffins were overtoasted and hard, the salmon was skimpy, and I'm not even sure Hollandaise sauce was even present.  Other things people ordered looked more appealing, so I could be persuaded to return, but it won't be for Eggs Benedict.

I started with the Mixed Green Salad - fine, but nothing exciting.  My entree was the seared tuna, but was pretty disappointed - there was too much sesame crust that overwhelmed the fish and the tempura-style rolls were oddly spongy and tasteless.  The best part of the dish was the grilled sesame-flavored vegetables.

sample_reviews.text: This is why you need me eating cheesesteaks!  You want me eating cheesesteaks!  A day of drinking and cheesesteak hit a high note.  We got the Leonardo and it moved us like a masterpiece should.  Flavorful seeded roll made in house.  Broccoli Rabe, Sharp provolone and long hots.  This was a sinus opener!  I don't think the roll should have been scooped.  Watch my video for more.

Different Cheesesteak #898.  Guest Eater Jon O'Connell.  This is a good bar cheesesteak.  It comes with cooper sharp cheese.  Very Cheesey Cheesesteak.  Meat was easy to eat and covered in cheese.  Guest Eater Jon pointed out that cooper sharper is a bit salty.  This was better than, 'I'm at the bar and need something to soak up the alcohol' cheesesteak.  Bonus cheesesteak spring roll video.

Different Cheesesteak #912.  Guest Eater Dan Richwine.  I'd like to thank everyone that made Sam's Brick Oven Pizza possible.  Sam's is a craft beer & wine bar.  They have a bar area, dining room, patio and sold a lot of take out while we were there.  This was a restaurant quality cheesesteak.  I wish it was bigger but it was only $9.  $9 and it came with good Italian Style French Fries.

I had the cheesesteak.  Yes, I went to Spot Gourmet BURGER and got the cheesesteak.  It was tasty and I can see where they are trying to make a gourment Cheesesteak.  That area is going to be good, they do a lot of promoting of each other's business.  Very neighborhoody vibe.  I do not recommend the cheesesteak.

I got the Cheesesteak.  I was pretty much the only person eating a cheesesteak.  I quickly found out why.  150 beers, the pizza smelled amazing and the cheesesteak was average at best.  If I go back it will be for a pizza and beer special.

sample_reviews.text: We were lucky enough to find this amazing mobile pizza 'cart' after a festival downtown.  They were set up in the front lot of Maynards and since everything around was so busy we figured we'd order a pie and try something new.  Everything was great from the very start.  We walked up and while deciding what to get Scott offered us some bread to taste and helped us place our order.

Wow!  Very good ramen and while maybe just under Obon it isn't by much.  The location helps too :)  I have visited a few times and yes there is always a wait, but if you plan to go during the off times it isn't nearly as long.  Both times the most we waited was 15 minutes.  Yes service is rough and rushed as I think they're trying to feed as many Tucsonans as possible but it didn't ruin my meal.

I visited this location for the first time last week after spending a very hot morning at the zoo.  I had heard a few good things but overall wasn't really sure what to expect and I loved it! Our bartender was very friendly and helpful, telling us the happy hour specials after we ordered beer so we could get in on the deal.

Sadly I have experienced better Chinese food than this and that's not saying a whole lot because I live in Tucson which isn't known for Chinese.  

So we ordered sweet & sour chicken, General Tso's chicken, house lo mein, eggrolls and soup.  Out of all that the noodles and sweet and sour chicken were the best.

I had visited Mother Hubbard's before it was revamped and now after.  Before: typical greasy spoon diner, After: spruced up menu and decor.  Can't say I was blown away by the food.  I agree that portions are on the small side and while I was excited they had so many varieties of turkey sausage what I received was small and very dry, not appealing.

sample_reviews.text:
Perfect combination of sweet and savory. The chicken is delicious and the donuts are mouthwatering, or the other way around.  I especially love the chicken sandwich. 

This place is slowly becoming a Philly staple and I'm loving that it's expanding and popping up in places like the Citizens Park and Spruce Street Harbor.

It's so great to have such a great food option in Philly. The decor is spot on, modern but very authentic. The small bakery and gift shop in the front has so many adorable trinkets. The service was beyond polite and attentive. 

The food was absolutely delicious. The platters are the way to go but we honestly did not have one bad bite.

What an amazing experience! The food was perfectly though-out, deliciously prepared, and oh-so tasty! As the dishes came out, we got more and more excited for the ones that were still coming.  Although it's hard to pick a favorite, the stand outs were the croquettes, garlic yogurt, baked cheese, tagine, shrimp, and chicken.  I would easily say that this is one of the best spots in the city.

You walk into a very industrial space. You get the feeling of an abandoned construction site, or a manufacturing space that has been adorned with modern and sometimes elaborate art work and interesting light fixtures. The whole thing works and although it's not a unique concept, this is one of the few local restaurants to embrace the idea and really make it work well.

You walk into a very small space. In fact, its so cozy that there are no secrets in this place.  The pastas are made and cooked well, however, the sauces could use some creative inspiration. The servers were nice enough and the food came out quickly.  I thought Porcini was very cute and certainly very affordable, nonetheless, it would not be my first choice for pasta.
Great gelato and bruschetta!

sample_reviews.text: I am a fan for sure of this market. There was plenty of variety packed into this little place. Everything was very fresh and looked awesome. The prices are insanely awesome. I was able to get a ton of vegetables and fruit for about $7. I will go back for sure.

Really good Hibachi! We came here for lunch with a group from work and we were all really impressed. The food was great, portions were awesome and everything was cooked great! The servers were very quick and it was overall awesome dining experience. Highly recommend this Hibachi one of the best in the area that I've found!
So yummy!! Veggie omelette was delicious! Have to try the grilled corn bread muffins probably the greatest thing I've ever eaten!!!!

Not a fan what so ever. The only positive I could find was that they have poutine.
The set up when you walk in is weird coffee culture and union burger in the same space, we were greeted by a girl when we walked up to thr Counter who stood on the coffee culture side (even though it's just a long counter shared by both) and she was the only one to greet us so when we were ready to order we went to...

sample_reviews.text: On the 4th attempt, I finally got to eat here. All it took was coming in during an Eagles game. The space is warm and intimate. On a quiet Sunday afternoon, it felt like a great place to sit back and drink and eat one of the best burgers in Philly. And so it was, until the place got packed and it got loud and crowded.

One thing that people in Philly fail to realize is how many great restaurants are hidden within strip malls in South Jersey. Kuzina by Sofia is one such place. Just off of Route 70, it's nestled in a strip mall with ample parking.

    The inner space is nicely decorated, yet not overly kitschy or corny. Our waitress promptly took our orders.

I'd heard the hype. Seen the NY Times article. Nevertheless I was skeptical. Mainly because most people have at best, two reference points for donuts, Dunkin Donuts or Krispy Kreme. Neither of which really makes truly great donuts.

Ok beside not allowing 1/2 stars, my other pet peeve about Yelp is not being able to give zero stars, as I would give this place and that abomination of a "music venue" known as Starlight Ballroom. 

If I"m standing by the sign that says "Please wait to be seated" that's a pretty good indicator that I actually want to be seated.

So I decided to write this review so you won't think that the last reviewer was a lone wingnut in a sea of contented patrons. I've been here three times. The first time the service and the food was ok. The second time the service was very slow, though the food was ok, so I chalked it up to a busy night. The third time, I came on a Sunday night, starving.

sample_reviews.text:
What a beautiful restaurant with great food!

My partner and I came in on a random Tuesday evening for dinner. I made a reservation via OpenTable. The space itself is beautiful and sleek - with a lot of nice accents (a lot of wood and natural textures.)

The food was delicious, too.

Not vegan, but always down to eat delicious food! 

A friend and I stopped by Wiz Kid for a quick to-go dinner recently and I loved every second of my experience.

I was initially going to order the classic Wiz Kid Philly cheesesteak, but saw the KFT sandwich calling my name... I also was very interested by the Okonomiyaki Fries (korean bbq sauce, kewpie, nori). WOW.

I've heard great things about The Twisted Tail for years and finally swung by for a casual happy hour / light dinner at the upstairs bar.

The space itself is big - the upstairs bar is dark and worn and feels like you're stepping out of Philadelphia and into some other city.

I've heard (and read) extremely mixed reviews of Wishbone. We ended up having an ok experience, that I am rounding up to 'good' because I think at the core of this place, there are good things happening.

Despite an obnoxiously loud event happening at the bar (a night of "Brosé" and 80's yacht rock...) we had a really delicious dinner here last Friday.

We started with the deviled eggs and crispy burrata. Both were good - if not an elevated standard. 

The hot, steaming Whiskey & Sage bread arrived with a giant slab of butter.

Wellspent may just be my new favorite brewing company in the area; I already have plans to go back next week; seriously! 

The place is open, clean, inviting, bright, modern, etc. The bartenders are friendly, funny, smart, cool and knowledgeable! Like, for real. I had such a great time at Wellspent the other night after work.

When I woke up this morning I knew it was going to be a good day! While browsing instagram, I saw that 4 Hands had a new release Contact High (Blueberry Lemonade) and at that moment, I knew I had to try it! I love all of the Contact High flavors and since I ran out of the Radler earlier this week, life just hasn't been the same.

After being at the park for the day, we were hungry and just wanted to pick up something quick and easy. Since we were near the bluffs, we decided to place an order online for pickup. 

They have the option for pick-up curbside or pick-up inside. I opted for the inside pick-up just because I have not had much luck with curbside lately.

I haven't ever reviewed a super market and so I am willing to give this a try. Do I like Schnucks? No. Do I shop there? Yes. Why? Because STL doesn't have a Publix! I wish they had a Publix! 

This Schnucks is the most convenient to where I live and so I go here. It is always busy and it is always loud. I go in to get what I need and I leave. I have never interacted with the employees.

I finally made it back for a "regular" visit. The last time I was here was for the Elite dinner and I gave it a 5 star review. Today I was not as satisfied with the service or the food. 

I arrived around 2pm to meet a friend for some lunch. I believe the hostess saw us, but we actually had to ask her for a table. We were not greeted at all.
$2 Tuesday's!
Bottomless Mimosas all day everyday!

sample_reviews.text: Ok so one of my friend's birthday came around and I was not quite sure what to get them. They never really said or would tell anyone what she wanted to get for her birthday because she is one of those people who feel as though people should not be unconvinced on her birthday for buying a present.

With warmer weather that gives me enough of an excuse to go out and enjoy some ice cream. Now because I am in St. Louis I am always ready to try a new place for ice cream instead of that one that is the most popular attraction around when it comes to the frozen treat.

I went here the other night with a group of friends for a joint birthday dinner.

Like before the place was pretty neat inside, it has that art deco vibe going on, which I have always enjoyed. It makes me feel like I'm Dick Tracy or something.

We got there and the place was packed full, thankfully we already had a table and we were away from a noisy group so we didn't have any problem listening...

The first time I went to Rue 13 was for their 80's night where they have old music video projected onto the walls. Well, that's basically the only time that I go there. But when I do go, I have a blast. All my friends do as well.

The Tin Can, where everything comes from a can. The Tin Can bar is a neat little bar with that neat little theme of serving all their beers, no matter which type in a tin can. Now they do not put the beer in a tin can and then serve it to you, they just serve it in its original tin can that it comes in, so it pretty neat.

The Tin Can is a fun bar, a small bar, but still fun.

sample_reviews.text: It would be easy to walk by Rowhouse Grocery and not even realize it .... but especially if it is Tuesday afternoon you do not want to do that.  You see on Tuesdays, Rowhouse bakes Pulla - the wonderful bread pastry from Finland.

In a gritty looking neighborhood - but one not far from increasingly cool gentrifying ones - is this storefront Laotian restaurant.  It's a gem.  Though not officially related, this restaurant is run by the daughter of the owners of the well-known Vientiane Café in University City.  The interior of the restaurant is stylish with Laotian art on the walls.  And the food is very good.

Wayne Junction is usually thought of as a small neighborhood at the beginning of Northwest Philadelphia that one passes through on the train or highway.  But guess what?  There is a popular eating establishment worth getting off of the train or highway to patronize. It's called Sister Muhammad's Kitchen.

I don't get this place as regards donuts...I don't get it...I don't get it!  I made my third (and last) donut purchase from Federal Donuts recently at this location on the edge of Northern Liberties.  btw - There is ample parking here which is a plus for people wishing to patronize this establishment.

I found nothing exceptional here except the tasty Three Devil's Hot Sauce.  The fried macaroni and cheese balls were on the bland reheated side and you got only two for $4.50.  The king cake was on the hard and stale end of the spectrum.  Admittedly it was a few days past Mardi Gras when I ordered the king cake .... but still - it should have either been reduced in price or tossed out.
Austrian Chorherren cheese is in stock here!  Buy it while you can!! Also Philly's St. Lucifer hot spice #13 is sold here...
