sample_reviews.text:
I don't think there's anything to say that hasn't already been covered by the other reviews outside of my own recent (and first) experience at Crown Candy Kitchen.

This place is for the carnivores! If there's a heaven, my first meal there would be one of CCK's BLTs, which satisfied my addiction to bacon for a good 12 hours (that's a really good thing) and went well along my chocolate malt.

I work on South Grand so I might be a little biased... But here's my take on Mojo Tapas Restaurant & Bar:

It's nice to have a tapas restaurant that not over-the-top pricey and has accessible food both in price and flavor.

It's been a while since I've visited for anything other than a beer (or ten). I tried the fried chicken and mash.

AMAZING.

Keep up the good work Tin Can! One of these days, I'll finally go to the beer can pyramid contest.

The original location of Cairdeas Coffee will be sorely missed. Back then,  I could order a tasty beverage and something to eat on my way to Forest Park.

There was plenty of sitting room, and a well-stocked table chock-full of current newspapers and magazines.

They offered doggie biscuits for my pup, and had a really cool water bowl shaped like a coffee cup.

Cairdeas had a quaint little patio...

My first visit was months ago, and I ordered the fried chicken and got wings and drummies. Yes, it was technically chicken. Yes, it was fried. And yes, it tasted okie doke. 

But come on, man. That's like ordering a steak (even a crappy one!) and getting a burger instead.

Soul food is supposed to be good... and soulful.
Legit. No bs. No unnecessary hype. Just good ramen.

sample_reviews.text: I've been to Khyber many times for beer events. The draft list here is in my top 20 of Philly. They always have a wide variety of rare craft beers, from barrel aged stouts, to sours. So the beer list alone keeps me coming back.  I also love the atmosphere here, its very chill, dark, relaxed pub. Never too crowded, always just right.

Spice Finch is one of my favorite new restaurants in Philly! I really love this place!! The food is amazing. 

Some of my favorite dishes are: 
1. Date Truffles - wow! Dates with pomegranate & cashew, these little balls are small but packs a delicious mouthful of flavor! Love these. 
2. Stuffed Grape Leaves - these are not your typical grape leaves, like every other Greek restaurant.

During the covid 19 lock-down, I needed to try a new restaurant for takeout that I haven't yet tried. Jasmine rice was offering a $10 off grub hub special, so that was an easy choice. My husband and I both LOOOOVED our delivery from jasmine rice. 

I had the Massaman curry dinner which was a thick coconut curry sauce, chicken, peanuts, carrots, onions. Holy hell it was delicious.

I come here for breakfast alot - the egg & scrapple melted with cheese on a croissant is sooo delicious, and only $3.75. 

However, for as many times as I've been in here, I've now been triggered to write a review.  I actually bought a sandwhich for lunch (usually I have salad and its "ok"), but my chicken sandwhich on croissant was quite dissappointing.

I'm updating my previous review from 2 months ago.  I decided to go back to Brodo after a dissappointing oven roasted chicken croissant sandwich 2 months ago.

Today, I ordered half salad, half sandwhich.  The salad was good, although they were out of cranberries, I subsituted grapes & apples which was totally fine with me.  The dissappointment was the half sandwich.
Grand opening Jan 24
Happy hour 4-6pm

sample_reviews.text: (Late post) In March I came in after a pedicure and didn't expect much, but much to my surprise the owner and staff were so friendly. I sat with my pizza and really enjoyed myself without feel the rush to leave or eyes glued to what I was doing. I believe I had a margarita style that was fresh tasting and filled that hunger I had for something delicious.

Outside dining during dinner was really nice in the court area. Parking is available in a nearby lot and about a block (1-2 min walk). Aside from our table not being ready when we had a reservation, it was a good service. We weren't rushed to leave and our server was handling the tables pretty well considering that staff had to run dishes inside and out during the outdoor only phase 2 of 2020.

I love it here. Finished up my first visit and can tell this will be my new hot spot: wine, mimosas, ciders, cocktails with a fine looking menu & cheese plates. I'm not the only one who is a fan so reserved seats suggested but they do have a small bar area.

I ordered a ($15) mimosa flight. for brunch I went with quiche that comes with salad & I added potatoes.

One word: NO. Just say it when you're dining friends suggests coming here. Re-start the search. Game over. This is the best advice I can give you for this one.

The service was HORRIBLE. We waited 25 minutes for the salad that comes with our entree and it was lettuce with dressing and two small roma tomatoes. They ran out of soda. Seriously.

Tonight I had tickets for live music, wine, food and to walk the trails and pick a crop. This week was sunflowers so one would think the fee covered the sunflower you pick or at least that was the way I read it. No, the $15 fee was for you to just hang out. It didn't cover anything else at all, not even a ride to the sunflowers. Food, wine, sunflowers/crops were all extra.
Go for the dessert! Wow!
They announced that they will only be selling pizza this week. No other items like paninis, french fried or cheesesteaks will be available once the inventory is gone.

sample_reviews.text:
So many markets in Edmonton every summer, this year I went to the 124 Street market for the first time and I was so impressed. It's on the smaller side but that is not a negative point at all. Quite the opposite, I loved that it was quality over quantity with the vendors there. 
Food vendors were great with enough variety of cuisine.

FINALLY!!! I got to Yelo'd with Bailee M last week and I'm so glad I did. After reading so many reviews of Yelo'd I couldn't wait to sample some of that soft serve for myself.
I chose the Ube and Masaflora combo which comes with coconut flakes on the side and the combo was just perfect. Not overly sweet at all. I'm not really a sweet person but ice cream is my jam!

I went here twice in one day because I was so blown away by the service at lunch. DOSC stands for Drunken Ox, Sober Cat as they have an incredible cocktail situation in there but also a very elegant coffee shop. 
The decor really blew me away, so much attention to detail, I especially loved the artwork above the entrance to the open kitchen with the mountains and constellations.

This place is relatively new on the scene. I went to see a few DJ's I knew do a set but it just was not that busy. Such a shame! 

It has a great location right underneath Denizen Hall, which on the same night was jumping. I thought that people would have a few drinks there, then head down for dancing but it doesn't seem to be the case.

Dropped in here to here a tea. Looks like the drive thru is super busy all the time so I just walked in. Then about six members of staff said hello to me before I got to the counter to order. It was a bit too much! Imagine I hadn't had a coffee yet!! 
I ordered a tea and asked him to leave room for milk.
They sell pizza crunch!
Kids need to be 7 years old to go into the cat lounge

sample_reviews.text: Came here for our wedding anniversary a few years back. My husband and I were really impressed. The food was delicious. The service was excellent all around. I'm a wine and craft beer kind of drinker, and I'll never forget the strawberry basil martini I had here! Amazing! Also, at the time, there were great views of downtown from our table upstairs.

I've been eager to check out Thai Esane since several of my friends said it was their favorite Thai restaurant in the area. I had read about this place in some local blogs as well, so I was excited to see if it lived up to my expectations when a girl friend and I both ate here for the first time last night.

I was overall very impressed.

I had not heard about this local establishment until I discovered it on Yelp doing a search near where my little sister from Big Brothers Big Sisters lives as I was picking her up and taking her out for brunch yesterday. I had decided to check the place out based on the good reviews, and it did not disappoint.

I love Italian food, and I already have my favorite places around Nashville we frequently visit (Mama Mia's and Molinari's). However, after a couple I work with stated that this is their favorite Italian place, my husband and I decided to check it out. I thought this place was a chain (since there is one near my work in Nashville and the one in Cool Springs near our house).

A group of us were staying in the Bywater neighborhood on a recent trip to New Orleans, and we decided to eat here one night because we had read decent reviews and it was literally less than a block from the house we were staying.

The inside is a bit of a dive, and basically empty at the time we were there. Our waitress was very nice and attentive to our group.
You can order sans frozen yogurt for a fruitier treat. Or sub bananas if you want to keep the creamy texture!

sample_reviews.text: Great spot for beer with a group. We stoped in to start our own bar crawl. Music playing was too 40 hits, local beer on draft. They always have a great selection on tap to please everyone's palate. They have seating inside and there is a patio outside as well. Didn't venture out there though. Very friendly bartender.

Where else does the hostess walk you to your table with a basket of fresh hot rolls & cinnamon butter? I have no clue, but these guys do! Bucket of peanuts on the table along with an empty bucket for the shells (reminds me of Topeka's). 

Their menu shows the calories for each meal item too. Our server was polite and detailed when reading our orders back. Answered all our questions too.

I always had issues with milkshakes. don't get me wrong, I love them, but sometimes the milkshakes would come out way too thick couldn't even drink it through a straw and other places it was just too. 

Since I was already in the area and it's a hot day out, I decided I wanted a strawberry milkshake.

My experience kinda sucked. I have to cross off the pad Thai from my favs here. Tonight it was dry and had too many bites with egg shells. My previous time I got the pad Thai, again it was dry. Not sure what happened. Change of cooks? And I was given the wrong order too. Got to my car and checked the box and it was something different.

First timer here, never had a coal fired pizza. When I think coal fired, my thoughts go to "blackened or charred". We ordered a Paul & Young Ron, and let's just say, they don't skimp on the toppings! So much sausage, it literally covered the every inch of the pizza. Dollops of ricotta and we got half with sweet peppers (for me) and the other half hot
Peppers.
DO NOT ORDER MOBILE ORDERS! My husband ordered a salad and a spicy chicken meal and he gets home to find it they only put fries in the bag and my salad!

They don't devein shrimp...my shrimp
Scampi was watered down flavor

sample_reviews.text: One of the best coffee shops in the city. Quaint, quirky, fun, awesome proprietor, and great selection of teas and seating choices. Love it.

LOVED it. So thrilled I live so close by and as a preservationist, delighted with the space! The artwork on the side, the whole 9. Tried the cauliflower + shishito - pretty light/ simple. The Patatas bravas - a perennial favorite of mine, anywhere. Here, they use small fingerling potatoes here. The sauces are dreamy, and could go on anything.

So glad we finally got one of these here. And by "here," I mean downtown Indy. Went to/ enjoyed the Louisville Bardstown location a few years ago. The Eggs Benedict actually is pretty light. Also like the small doughnuts w/ cinnamon and sugar. Love the ginger beer they stock. Looking forward to trying the dinner menu- I saw they have bacon wrapped dates--my favorite thing!

I'm not sure how everyone else has got this joint on par with The French Laundry (a la cubana) buuuuut....my experience was baaaad. My mom ordered some beef dish (don't remember, it's been awhile) and a family friend who isn't anywhere near as picky as mom ordered the same thing. They agreed that an old tennis shoe would've been tastier.

First, the service sucked. The waiter quickly seated me, as he did everything else: quick, quick. He acted like serving me was an inconvenience he didn't have time for. I asked which tacos he thought was best between the carnitas, chicken or seasoned ground beef, and he had a nondescript answer there--"just whatever you like." 
He asked if I wanted hard or soft shells for my tacos-- over his...
Evidently a new menu started around May 2019! Looking forward to trying it!
The drop off at the drive thru is permanently down. Lame that they don't fix it. This pharmacy isn't even that old!

sample_reviews.text: Still in protracted soft opening, presumably waiting for rear parking lot construction can accommodate more.  But already crowded and bustling....and extremely loud due to all the wood, metal, brick and stone surfaces....almost had to write out orders.  Great selection of apps, entrees and desserts and superlative service, with manager dropping by twice for introductions and reactions.

Been watching and waiting for opening and now we know.   An excellent sandwich shop, esp. to go.   Menu is more extensive than food truck but includes all those favorites, plus dishes like the excellent beef marsala sandwich and more veggies and specials.  Yesterday Italian special meal at low prices....spaghetti and lasagna with veggies and garlic bread.

Top of the line BBQ.  Had a fine lunch with family today.  Have been to the 4 Rivers on N. Dale and now needed to try again and found equally high quality beef.  Fairly simple but complete menu....nice they offer option of lean or juicy brisket, also enjoyed BBQ beans and collards, both liberally fortified with burnt ends.

I like German food, so finding a rare German restaurant that is bad is a special let-down.   The food here is not terrible, but not particularly good either.    Even worse is the service and uncomfortable atmosphere.

Have eaten there a couple of times, once with a family group.  First time service was by a woman who was slow, and sorta limp.

Meh.....One of the inevitable burger chains in W. Tampa offering standard BK menu & service.  I generally avoid chains except when rushed, traveling and hunger/rush trumps quality.    

Probably a Whopper is as good as most other standard burgers offered by the new wave of boutique burgers, but nearby you can get a really good and distinctive Cubana Frita burger with tangy chorizo and crunchy...
Great lunch specials.fish n chips today

sample_reviews.text: Eclipse was the final stop of a Jersey tour of breweries, and it was also my favorite. You can't beat the prices, the flavors, the people, or the homey feel of their quaint set-up. I had the pleasure of chatting with Beth, who alongside her husband, established Eclipse Brewing a little over two years ago.

The Cubby Hole is the perfect name for this spot: it's cozy, comfortable, homey. It's the kind of small town establishment that surely inspires the creators of those holiday Hallmark movies, as it sits perfect situated among other local businesses on the picturesque Main Street of Moorestown.

If you're looking for fancy or formal or great variety - look elsewhere. If you're looking for rustic and memorable grub and a warm staff, you're in for a treat at Ted Peter's. The menu is simple, the beers are cold, and prices can't be beat. There's lots of parking and plenty of space for both indoor and (covered) outdoor seating.

First of all, I have to praise our server, Shawn. He was such a genuinely kind, attentive, accommodating server and made my experience at Grand Lux very memorable. I honestly think he may have been the best service I've ever experienced at a restaurant.

A solid happy hour. As a Texan, I wouldn't come here for nor rave about the food. It's definitely a Tex-Mex menu, with Americanized versions of your Mexican favorites. The only thing I've ever had beyond chips and guac here was a BBQ brisket quesadilla.

sample_reviews.text: The Cunningham Restaurant Group has taken the Indy food scene by storm, and they never fail to please. Due to the diversity of offerings, comparing one of their restaurants to another is a welcomed impossibility.  Livery, the newest addition to the company's line of exquisite eateries, is certainly their most unique.

Shopping at Trader Joe's is a special shopping experience.  On the surface, it is just a grocery store that carries their own brand-name products.  The core of the Trader Joe's experience is much different: the chain carries high-quality products at a very reasonable price.

If there's one thing I love, it's Indian food.  Patience is a virtue that I am still acquiring.  Fortunately, for all of us inpatient, Indian food-loving souls, there's a new kid on the block.  Tandoor & Tikka is a locally owned, fast-casual, modern Indian restaurant.  With food served in a similar fashion to Moe's, Qdoba, or Chipotle, one doesn't have to wait long for a sumptuous meal.

As a big fan of Vietnamese and Chinese food, in addition to the restaurant coming highly recommended, I had expected my experience at Sizzling Wok to be extraordinary.  It simply was not.  As I walked through the door, the first thing I heard was rap music.  When I am dining out, I expect there to be a certain relaxing ambiance.  The rap music destroyed that.

Ten dollars is pocket change these days; however, if a $10 bill fell out of my pocket, I'd still be disappointed.  I recently spent this amount on a cover charge for an evening at the ICON Lounge.  While I wouldn't take the evening back, I'm looking forward to telling you why this "establishment" isn't worth the dirt on my shoe.

The ICON Lounge is a shady place, and I recognized this before I...
India Sizzling II will do curbside if you phone in your order and request it.  They can take your payment over the phone. The package the food very well, so it will be hot when you get home!
Gregory's is now serving a European themed lunch.  An entree and a drink is only $9!

sample_reviews.text: Very good falafel, fries,and baba ghanoush.  Not very good shawarma though, it was light on meat and a little dry.  Quick and efficient service.

Was a big fan of the station bistro, this place, at least for brkfst, was just as good if not better.  More reasonably priced, and good service as well.  Great brkfst potatoes...pretty sure that's smoked paprika!  Only tiny snag was the music was too loud, not pleasant at 9am in a space where sound really carries.

This place is great...friendly people and well made, medium rare sandwiches of real filet.  I really hope that at this price point, they can last. Not that they are overpriced at all, but serving fillet sandwiches is always going to be expensive compared to a roast beef hoagie at Our Deli.
Everything was so incredibly over salted, they should install a saltlick at every table and save some money.  I've literally drank a half gallon of water today. I'd pass. PS-parking was ample.

Got a double cheeseburger, and was so overdone and dry I couldn't finish it.  It was literally hard.  Shame, because it looked like it started out as quality meat.  Fries were pretty good, as was the service (except for the floor near the soda machine that they allowed to stay wet the entire time I was there...they walked through it repeatedly while bringing people their orders).
Good beer and good food.  Service was OK.  Nice open air surroundings, would return again.

Honestly wasn't expecting much when I arrived at Panasian Buffet this afternoon. It was my third choice within 20 feet. Decided against Pho because I didn't feel like it and already had Cafe Saigon banhmi twice this week already. 

Browsing Panasian Buffet's yelp page showed much props for the sushi section and for $7.99 lunch special, its honestly worth the risk. 

Boy were they right.

Was in the neighborhood the other day and decided to chomp on some SE asian food. Saw Sky Cafe on yelp and thought I ended up in the wrong place because I was expecting more people considering the amount of praise it gets on Yelp. But it was only us so whatever we already here.

Whatever else we ordered wasn't that memorable but holy crap the Wontons are good.

I'm not much of a beer drinker but if I was, I guess this place would be the equivalent of a kid in a candy store. 

There lies against the wall a large selection of beers from all over the world and Frank was kind enough to go over his selection of craft beers and suggest me something that I might like even though I am not much of a beer drinker.

I passed by this Japanese restaurant yesterday after getting my windows tinted at Shades of Gray and decided to grab some lunch special.

I got the Oyako donburi and a specialty roll. While the Oyako lunch special was priced relatively well at 7.95, the chicken was dry and the sauce that came with my salad seemed like a sricacha blend instead of the usual ginger sauce.

After hearing about this place from some of my relatives I decided to check out this alleged new restaurant that serves dim sum in the North East. 

I honestly had low expectations going in because come on seriously, it's in North East Philly. As with any dim sum place, you judge the quality of the place by the "Phoenix Claws" aka chicken feet.
Closed damnit

sample_reviews.text: Sabrina's West Street Kitchen is a small, cozy spot, with a "south of the border" inspired menu that enticed me and my crew to sample as much as we could in one sitting. Beverages were served in Mason jars---presumably to match the lighting fixtures--and the housemade ginger ale was pleasant, with half a squeezed lime at the bottom of the glass ($3).

Tucked next to a busy freeway interchange, there is nothing fancy about recently opened Chavelita's Cocina Mexicana. It's tiny, the decor is minimal, and the counter staff aren't completely familiar with the menu.

El Tumi--for several years Reno's lone Peruvian restaurant--has recently transformed into Sabor Latin Cuisine, mixing flavors of the Andes with Argentinian and Mexican favorites, and a couple burgers for good measure. After reviewing the new menu, me and a couple friends knew we had to check it out.

We started with beverages native to Peru.

My wife, son and I went to this place a while ago and have never been back.  The service was terrible, the menu overpriced, the noise level extra loud, and after waiting for nearly an hour our meals were served to us cold and bland.

Sometimes I'll give one bad experience a pass and try again to verify before forming an opinion.

I stand by my original tip on this joint: Horrible, rude service. Marginal food at best. AVOID.

Seriously, I can't figure out how they stay in business. The food itself is on the very low end of cheap, strip-mall fast food Chinese fare available in town, and the only reasons you might choose this joint are: (a) you're drunk, (b) you're broke, (c) you have no taste-buds or self-respect.

You're...
Try not to sit directly next to a speaker, unless you like smooth jazz. Ugh
Left with a belly full of balls!

sample_reviews.text: Finally had a chance to check out Maynard's w/a friend & fellow Yelper! I wish I had stopped inside earlier, but now I know. :) Their happy hour is really reasonable--quality beers on tap for $4, wine at $5, and craft cocktails at $7. Lots to choose from on the HH food menu, too. My friend and I ordered the potato pizza, and it was delicious!

Ted Drewes is indeed the best. There are an indefinite number of combinations one can have here, and all the hype in my opinion is definitely worth it. I've had the custard w/fudge sundae, and a concrete mixed w/fudge, and both were delicious. I also appreciate that they're open even in the colder days--you can always stop by and pick up one of these amazing treats!

This may be a Yelp first for me--writing a review for a supermarket. But--this review is really all about the gooey butter cake--a STL phenomenon that was unbeknownst to me until recently.

A friend told me that Schnucks sells this cake, so I went to go check it out. Dang, it is good.

It's been a minute since I've been in college (!) but the Trident Grill delivers on that sports bar near campus type of feeling. I had the buffalo chicken sliders and was there during happy hour so the price point was really good (another plus in the great cost of living column for living in Tucson). Each beer/app was only $4.

Light, fluffy donuts--I got the cinnamon roll (which was really more like a cinnamon roll donut) and a Boston creme filled type of a donut, and they were both quite good. Not too dense and hit the spot.

Riverside, NJ, is no longer the thriving Delaware River side town it was in the middle of the last century. Scott Avenue is still a wide expanse well designed for Independence and Veterans Day parades, but the storefronts on either side are now either boarded up or operating as low budget businesses working hard to survive in this dusty backwater now that its industrial base is gone.

"Music gives Soul to the Universe,

What an amazingly awesome coffee shop located in downtown Ybor City at the corner of 7th Avenue and 19th Street. Calling Blind Tiger a 'coffee shop' really doesn't do this place justice. Sushi-Haru is a tiny little sushi bar and Japanese restaurant located along Northdale Boulevard in northern Carrollwood, directly across the street from Whole Foods. I was planning a trip to Whole Foods one evening, and wanted to find a place for dinner.

I hesitate to even review this place, because I do my best not to eat in crappy restaurants. But today, my winning streak came to a crashing halt when I stopped for lunch at The Earl of Sandwich in the Bay Street outdoor food court at International Plaza in Tampa.

This eatery is located across the way from Too Jay's Deli, and also Brio's, and next door to the wonderful British Pub.

You'll find Village Inn located right...

So Iron Hill is a solid place for lunch and dinner.  The beer is good, the food is good (if not a little over-priced) and the ambiance is a cross between a chain restaurant and brew pub.

The fish tacos are good (fried fish) but a little bland so needed to add some heat.

The onion soup is good with the stout beer.

The pulled pork sandwich was tasty and I liked the BBQ sauce.

Other than that it...

What a hidden gem on Station ave. The place is small and primarily a takeout joint. The portions are big, the service is pleasant and the facilities clean.  

The food is actually quite a few steps above you average Chinese takeaway.  Also one thing I particularly liked was the fact that when it said spicy it was actually spicy.
Excellent craft roastery.  Did a preview tasting of the different roasts and was exceptional especially the El Salvador.  Highly reccommend for retail.

Went for lunch on a Tuesday.   We were the only people in the place a d it scared me.  But with the Yelp reviews we decided to give it a shot.

Well I am glad we did.  The food was very good.  I had a Korean dish and the sushi to go.

Not a destination place but if your in the area I f recommend giving it a sbot.

Finally popped into Baewon (Stacey's BBQ) for dinner with friends.

The place is large with hoods over every table to handle the smoke from the in-table bbq grills. 

We sampled a large portion of the menu including the scallion pancake app, steamed dumplings, kimchi...etc.
I have added some photos my my earlier visit,
Get the oysters.  Seriously.  Get the otsters!

Oh my goodness a little gem in the middle of Berlin, NJ. They have the best lump crab cakes around. For $15 you get two very large crab cakes, 2 delicious sides, soup, salad & ice cream, pudding, or fruit cup. The server was great and it's a BYOB. Just be careful when walking in its a very steep incline.

My first visit here and it was great. It's a small place designed for take out but if you do decide to dine in it's a great experience. The service is great and the food was yummy. I hope they stay around. A lot of good places in that area never get the support they need from the community. I don't live close by but I will visit again with the hubster.
It's a taco bell, it's fast food, it's  convenient & it doesn't make me sick. The kids dig it & that's cool with me. The sangria blast, was a pleasant surprise.

sample_reviews.text:
After talking about visiting Prohibition Taproom for the past 2 months, the boyfriend and I finally made it there last night, and we will be back again soon!

When you step inside, you feel like you've just gone through a time machine to be back in the 1920s.  They have a lot of neat, little touches that give the place a certain je ne sais quoi.

Wow, Zorba's Taverna  has the best Greek food that I have ever had!  This place is a gem, and you could walk right by and not even realize what you are missing.

Zorba's is on the small side, so I would recommend calling ahead for a reservation if possible.

I joined a friend at La Fusion Cafe in Willow Grove for lunch last week, and we had a great meal.

We started off with soup, which of course I now forget the name of - nevertheless, it was chicken and relatively spicy, but quite delicious.  I got the sushi combination for my meal- 3 rolls for around $15, which is a great deal.  I tried the spicy tuna, california roll, and philly roll.

Do not waste your money.

This location was such a disappointment.  After seeing commercial after commercial with their stupid jingle, my boyfriend and I finally decided to give in and try their pizza.  The restaurant itself was dirty - we had both been running around and wanted to wash our hands in the restrooms.

We went to LaScala's for the first time last night for dinner.  They have a very nice ambiance and the hostess was pleasant - she seated us 20 minutes early for our reservation with no problem.  Unfortunately, we were seated right underneath a speaker where the stereotypical Italian restaurant music was blasting.

sample_reviews.text: Looking for a casual but delicious place to get your lunch on? August Rhodes should be on your list for these reasons: Fresh bread baked in house, plenty of sandwich options for all palates (veggie, vegan included), salads, sides and soups. Order at the counter, take seat and enjoy your meal in one of the cutest spots in town!

Missing Charro steak big time. Our last order during Covid was a little complicated due to the time on their system not matching up with the website to place our online order. But that's nothing a quick phone call didn't fix! The charro burger and half bone in chicken were cooked to perfection. I love a simple burger and charro has all the right ingredients for a classic. Thank you Team Charro!

I've eaten at the original Scottsdale location several years ago and enjoyed it very much. I finally  made it to the Tucson location, although this time for takeout, due to COVID. The dining room is open, but I have stuck with takeout during the pandemic. We place our order over the phone, although it looks like you can order from Yelp as well!

Location is everything and this one in particular doesn't always feel the safest.  It's not in my neighborhood, so that could factor into why I don't feel too secure at this one. I don't really use this one unless I have to.  Although the staff are all
Super friendly and always very helpful.

We chose the Ritz Carlton for our most recent staycation over Memorial Day weekend. Due to the high volume of guests at the resort, they opened Cayton's up for dinner on a Sunday night. We had a pretty large group, so we played it safe and went with the restaurant that could accommodate us all, and they certainly did! Upon arrival we were directed to a private dining room.
They have special iced tea drinks on the menu!
Parking is tight! Make sure to use the back parking spot

sample_reviews.text: Dessert heaven! They did a friends wedding and did an awesome display. The cake was so good and fluffy. All the desserts here are top notch!
Great spot for a Thai lunch! Their lunch specials run around $10. The yellow curry with chicken was delicious. Wish there was more chicken had a bunch of rice at the end. Taste was awesome.
Really enjoyed the food from here! Their toasted raviolis are a good deal and tasty. Their salad with Italian dressing is great too!

Wanted to have a better experience here it just didn't happen. The burger was extremely salty and presentation was just bad. The onion ring and bacon looked old af. Shake was nothing special either and they didn't fill it all the way.

Nice little donut spot on the corner. It's pretty small inside so might get cramped when you're ordering. The donuts were delicious tho and so were the pies. Could use some updating minus a star for the aesthetic. I guess if it ain't broke don't fix it. Coffee was great!
Yelp check in dine in only they should mention that.

sample_reviews.text:
Fortunately for me, my friends moved to the 'burbs and tricked me into heading to Franklin for Mexican food!  I admit I was unenthusiastic at first, but this little Mexican joint, tucked quietly into paved paradise, is definitely a top notch find!

I ordered the fish tacos, which came out flaky and combined with delicious flavors and other tasty goodness.

Okay, I admit it.  I'm a fake coffee lover.  No, no, not decaf.  By fake coffee, I mean add creamer, some syrups and some honey, a dash of cinnamon, and maybe a sprinkle of cocoa powder.  Ahh, now that is a coffee!  Or so I thought.

Upon arriving to the newly opened Coffee, Lunch., I went for a non-fat latte.  Much to my fear, I took a sip without any additions.

Um, how have I not written a review about this place yet??  I have dreams about their food!  Sweet, savory, delicious dreams...

The place is basically a gutted, old house, turned classic, tasteful restaurant.  It still has the feel of a house, like the comfort of walking into an old friend's house.  And the staff there is wonderful.

Ninki has all the intentions of trying to serve Japanese food.  However, it falls short of that, amongst other things.

While its location is what brought me here, it is not AT ALL what you expect to see when you walk into a Japanese restaurant of any sort.  It looks like a late 80's, early 90's bachelor pad.  Kind of like black laquer threw up on Japan.

Okay. Awkward...

The set up in this place is supposedly "upscale" or "swanky" I guess...  It looked kind of fancy and I was curious about the wine, so we gave it a shot.  If you can't grab a table, be prepared to face the restaurant by way of awkwardly seated couch.  Who places a random couch facing towards the bar and right next to the bathrooms?
Trying out the challah!
happy birthday mike clifford!

sample_reviews.text: We stopped in on a whim on a busy Saturday night for a light bite of dinner.   Usually Italian is not the place for a light bite, but here they make it work very well.  The scene is kind of upscale/casual if there is such a thing.   The appearance is classy, but the place is loud and really has a kind of fun, neighborhood vibe.  If your neighborhood is Ladue, that is.

Yelpers do not lie, at least in this case.  Reeds is fully deserving of its great reviews.  

We came on a random Sunday evening, unaware of the tremendous happy hour deals they have ALL DAY Sunday (okay, after they open at 5).  $5 glass of wine, and good wine at that.  We'll be back for the $10 burger, which I've heard is great, but this time we tried the meatloaf and the catfish.

This is an easy 5 star review. Our food and service experience were amazing.  This is a pretty small restaurant, so reservations are a must on weekends I would think.

There were so many appealing and interesing items on the menu it was hard to narrow down our choices.  We ended up with the octopus appetizer and I had the King Kup filet (I think that was the name).

We tried several items on the menu and found three things that are very good:  Egg Foo Yung, Chicken with Chinese Vegetables and Crab Rangoon.  We don't like their fried rice, Hot and Sour soup or Kung Pao Chicken, which is pretty disappointing for a place like this.

I predict the Market Grill won't be around very long.  It's the definition of meh.  You walk in, look around and it just feels kind of incomplete, like it was designed by committee and no one could agree oh how to decorate, so they just didn't.  The owner should be sentenced to five nights at the Venice Cafe.

sample_reviews.text: Best place to get take-out beer in the city.  They've got way more variety than your usual place, so if you like German, Belgian, local microbrews, etc., this is the place for you.  They also let you do a mix-n-match 6 pack, which is awesome.  I usually want to buy way more than 6 bottles whenever I go, but I keep myself in check.

Ryan Christopher's (formerly Caviston's) is simply the best restaurant in Narberth.  They've got great food, whether it's brunch, lunch, or dinner.  Everything on the menu is terrific, the prices are reasonable, and the atmosphere is welcoming.

Finally, a burger joint for those of us who care where our food comes from!  Elevation Burger opened a few months ago, and they sell only 100% organic food, from the meat to the potatoes they use for their fries.  Now people can enjoy a burger without worrying about hormones in their food or about the inhumane treatment of the cows or the negative impact on the environment of that burger.

I ordered from this place one day because 1) I'm always happy to try a new pizza place b/c I love pizza and 2) they're one of the few places I can order from using delivery.com in my neighborhood.  So I ordered a pizza and something to drink.  Then I waited.  An hour later, I called to check on my order (since I only live 7-8 minutes away, I figured I should have gotten my pizza by then).

So, I'm a little disappointed in this place.  

First, it has a weird setup.  The storefront used to be the natural foods/vitamin store/vegan restaurant on Narberth's main strip, but now it's a thai restaurant/vitamin store.  Apparently, one person owns and operates the vitamin shoppe by day, while another person owns and operates the thai restaurant in the evenings, all within the same space.

sample_reviews.text:
I took my grandmother here for lunch for her birthday and she LOVED it, as did I. My grandmother is allergic to tree nuts and told the waitress and immediately the owner came out and assured my g-mom that she would hand make my g-mom's lunch. AMAZING!

I also go fairly often for the ice cream in the cute side parlor. It's nice to stroll around on E. Passyunk.

As soon as we were finished our main meal, my husband said, I'm definitely coming back here again!" The service was amazing, so pleasant and attentive. The food was delicious! For people who are hesitant to try new foods, I would highly recommend this place b/c the cuisine isn't too overpowering, but it has many subtle flavors, at least in the meals we ordered.

This tucked-away secret-garden kinda place was fantastic! I never would have found this gem if our friend who lives in town didn't invite us here. A large group of us (about 15) slowly trickled in through a fine April Sunday evening. We enjoyed live jazz and music hall-y stuff in the backyard.

I know I'm the billionth person to write a review for this place, but I want to say how polite the staff is here, which I don't know why, but I was surprised by, especially the host up front. He kept calling people's names when they wouldn't respond in case they had left and come back because the wait there is SO unpredictable.

Just celebrated a friend's bday lunch here. There were nine of us in the group and the service was great. We made a reservation without a problem and the server remembered all of our orders, despite all the chaos we carried on with changing drinks, etc. I like the laid-back atmosphere and the huge tables to sit at.

Ka-Pow

Lips and I wanted to eat well tonight. It is Restaurant Week and unbeknownst to us there were a cadre of paparazzi wanting to catch us. We were able to disappear before we arrived at Zeppoli. We really hoped to have another 5 star food experience.

Ka-Pow!

What makes a good restaurant into an Excellent restaurant? Consistently fantastic eating adventure. Zeppoli has accomplished this level of consistency. 

Lips decided to go casual. New Brown Patent Leather Gucci shoes, New Hermes Bracelet along with the Emerald Cut Diamond Band.

Ka-POW! No I don't work for Zeppoli, however I am offering you my opinion of a wonderful restaurant. Yes, its small and yes it gets a bit cramped, maybe also a bit noisy, but the food overwhelms all the preceding challenges.

We were celebrating and a small group of us decided to try something adventurous.

Typical Philly..anointing an eatery which is far from anything special.
The Crab Fries? ummm fries with Crab seasonings. ooo, wow BIG DEAL! Who cares? Nothing special.. 

Its an over priced bar with lousy service. 

Fun place to watch football but then again so is many other places.

Gosh this place sux now.

Burnt food

Employees having inappropriate discussions within ear shot of their customers.

Holy cow this place has gone so far down hill.

no mas no mas!

Fatty says SKIP THIS PLACE!

sample_reviews.text:
The cheesesteak pierogies are absolutely delicious with the caramelized onion on top. The staff is super friendly and welcoming. The apple sauce is great too.

Stuffed cabbage has creamy tomato sauce and well seasoned.

This supermarket is a great addition to the city. It's a literally giant modern supermarket. Upon entering the store you can find a nice Starbucks cafe. There's an escalator for human and escalator for the shopping cart. On the second floor there's a door to go to a nice size patio to enjoy beautiful weather and city view.

Small bakery offering very affordable Asian desserts including zhongzi and steamed pork bun. Super friendly owner and staff. 

Zhongzi has a lot of fillings, very authentic and delicious. The steamed pork bun has good amount of fillings and tasty too.

My favorite is the pineapple bun.

The review is for waffle, shiumay and takoyaki.

Waffle isn't freshly made. They just warmed it up. My waffle was ready in a secon. As a result, it's soggy and lacks of crispiness on the edges.

Shiumai: very small and there's more filler than the meat. You can pass.

Takoyaki is decent. I expect more octopus, the sauce is pretty good.

The pork mofongo is really good. Highly recommend. The chicken pastelitos is Ok, not too much flavor. The seafood stuffed filer is very salty, lack of flavor. Also I saw a couple of fruit flies flying around
Friendly staff and amazing fresh donuts. Get the fried chicken too
Very nice and organized beer store. Staff is helpful.

sample_reviews.text:
BENTO BOXES.

Kampai is officially getting the FSB (Five Star Bump.)

It's now my favorite lunch spot in the West End specifically because of the chicken teriyaki bento box. You get the customary soup, followed by a tray of chicken teriyaki, rice, salad, a 4 piece California roll, and potatoes. And it's under ten bucks. How do you beat that?

Dewey's is such a solid place to get pizza. It's always great and I don't have a single negative thing to say!

Their wine list is great, as is the entire menu. I love their salads (not as a pizza replacement, of course. Are you crazy?) I love to switch up the pizzas I order, but sometimes end up going with a classic pepperoni.

I took our eight-year-olds to the Tower Grove Farmers market last weekend, and we got their early (around 8:30am) in search of breakfast. I was thinking we'd grab a crepe, maybe a breakfast sandwich, but nope... the boys had their eyes on the French fry truck. We hadn't been out in a while and their eyes were so lit up with excitement that I couldn't say no.

I definitely don't enjoy writing two-star reviews, but my experience here was not a good one (and clearly out of character for them, considering all of the five-star reviews.) Maybe it was an off day, but so many things happened that were inexcusable. 

My mom, sister and I needed a good brunch before driving five hours back to St. Louis, and I used the Yelp app to find a good spot.

The Good Pie was just okay for me. 

I love the inside of the building! Super cozy and vintage, with lots of old bikes hanging up on the walls. Totally the vibe I typically enjoy. I love how you can also see the staff working away making pizzas.
For lunch I have to go with the chicken salad & herb popover. Comes with a great salad on the side, too!
Get the goat cheese, steak with bleu cheese, and a glass of Tempranillo. Enjoy!

I was all set to move when I found out a Target was being built in my "backyard." I am glad I didn't. With 3 children and another on the way Target has been a time/life saver. 
My personal favorite is their dollar section.
Wow. I never thought that I could taste Italy again. This is the best Gelato I have had since Florence. Nothing in America I have had compares definitely in Tucson.

Stopped by there after an afternoon at Mt. Lemmon. Very tasty Mediterranean fare. Nothing was over seasoned. 
The Zayna fries, "fresh-cut half moon shaped fried potato, seasoned with Aleppo pepper and finished with lemon garlic dressing" were great.
The feta on a pita was "seasoned with oil and Za'tar herb mix" was light and yummy.

I have to agree with Adam. Caruso's is terrible. Ketchup and 40 minute pasta would taste better. Being from NY, I was ushered there the first year we moved here after complaining about the local Italian. Everyone said you'll love this. They were wrong.
My brother in-law swore by this place and it did not deliver. The place is a dump. The service was poor at best. The wings are not meaty at all and the flavors did  little to impress me.

sample_reviews.text: WOW I had no idea this place was in RTM or that it even existed! I walked up, and the first thing I thought was that I was back in NOLA!!! The smells, the menu, etc. I was thinking that since we are in Philly there is NO WAY this place would be just like being in NOLA, and boy was I dead wrong!!! I got the gumbo and the red beans and rice! The gumbo was super flavorful and spicy!

All I can say is WOW! By no means am I a vegetarian, I LOVE steaks, and chicken, etc. Very small place. We got take out and the food was awesome! I can only imagine how good it is if you eat in. The flavor of this food is amazing! The portions are HUGE, and the food is delicious! My only problem is that it is VERY expensive.

Only had breakfast here 2 times. The ham egg and chz Sam is the best I have ever had in my life! If I'm in a diff terminal I will walk all the way to this terminal to get one that's how good it is! I can only imagine how good their lunch sams are!
Place caught on fire the other day! Pretty sure it was gutted! Not sure if they are going to reopen or not though!

Hands down the WORST indian food I ever had! Ordered dal yellow spicy number 2 (they asked from 1-10 how spicy she told me 1 being mild 10 being hottest), the spicy number 2 was like spicy number 5 or 6. It was SO damn hot. It was also very very watery almost soup like. The samosa was very salty and greasy and again very spicy, the sauce was spicy too!
Don't waste your time or money. You'll get lettuce that's old and brown and not fresh. Completely disgusting.
Amazing place! The food is tasty and fresh, the place is cleaned the owner is an awesome guy and the service was so good! I'd definitely come back! Was very impressed!

sample_reviews.text: this cart turns out some pretty tasty eats for cheap. 5 usd will get you a  no-frills lunch (including a drink.) since i'm sans-gluten, i get the lamb salad with the white sauce and a decent pour of their hot sauce. the vendor has been nothing but nice each time i've gone. he's been very polite and provides efficient service. so don't be discouraged by the (often) long line...it moves quickly.

i decided to try this place after a "soup nazi" type of lunch experience i had there in the fall. (aside from the diva-ness, i wasn't incredibly wow'ed by my meal.)  i went for dinner with a friend since she had never been to this restaurant. as always, the decor is awesome; i like the clean semi-cramped feeling.

such a fun place for a group of people! before you read further, make sure you make reservations because this place gets busy, and based on the length of the dining experience, you might be waiting for quite a while to get a seat on a popular night. 

as others have said, the food is plentiful so come prepared to eat.

yes, the iced coffee was good...except one thing was missing: SOYMILK! (or any non-dairy beverage). seriously, in a city that has a fair number of vegans/vegetarians, why no dairy alternative?

p.s. the bathroom is pretty nasty.

i've only come for ice cream so i can't attest to this establishment's other charms per the 4 and 5 star reviews. 

after dinner, a friend of mine suggested ice cream, and this was the place closest to the restaurant.

sample_reviews.text: Book early as this restaurant fills up quickly and after visiting I see why.  Excellent visit from the food to the service - stellar in every way.  The atmosphere is definitely high energy as one would expect with a completely full restaurant.  The seating is tight but is to be expected.

Still as good as previously noted.  This trip we sampled the Seattle Grunge Dog and the Big Kahuna Dog, both of which were excellent.  I was expecting the spicy orange habanero aioli to be more spicy as I didn't really taste any spice in the Kahuna dog but it was tasty nonetheless.  The tamarind coconut chutney sauce for the fries was amazing.

Finally got the chance to stop by Cheu this past weekend.  Definitely one of those situations where you're super excited to find a new favorite spot, but know its so close to home that you're going to want to go 2-3 times a week.  We snagged a seat at a table but now regret that as the counter seems the way to go if you are lucky enough to get a spot.  Next time.

80s themed food with a slant towards gin cocktails? Yes, please, sign me up! Unfortunately, my visit to Juniper Commons fell short. I've enjoyed all of Kevin Sbragas restaurants in the past and frequent them often but this seems like a misstep. 

The space is large and open with rust and gold hues dominating the interior. The service was good, friendly and attentive.

Stopped by last Friday night for a quick bite.  It's a little awkward when you walk up, you're either escorted to a seat in the West Side Gravy side or the sister Blackbird side, but nevertheless we got a seat in the fairly empty restaurant and settled in.
The ham and cheese with pimento cheese and Bentons ham is amazing.
Great draft list!

sample_reviews.text: I had the opportunity to try Bourbon and Branch for brunch this past weekend. I wanted a restaurant that took reservations since the brunch wait can be sort of ridiculous in NoLibs. When I called to make a reservation I was told that they only take them for parties of 6 or more, but that I shouldn't have a long wait for brunch.

I was in the area, put "coffee shops" into my GPS, and located a Starbucks. When I arrived at my destination I realized that Grindhouse was right across the street. In my desire to support local businesses I decided to try it out. My husband got an iced coffee and I got a caramel frap. Both were delicious and served by a nice young lady. It's cute inside with plenty of seating.

I think this is probably a 4-star restaurant, but considering the location, I'm giving 5-stars. This is the best Mexican food I've found in DelCo so far.

I got to try the carnitas burrito, rice and beans, and chorizo potato quesadilla. Everything was delicious. Beware the green sauce - it's not guacamole and is very spicy. I actually appreciated all the salsas and spicy touches here.

This was such a disappointing experience. 

-Almost 8 dollars for a gallon of apple cider that's not even FROM the farm? We bought a half gallon and it was super bland. Definitely not worth buying.

-The apple spice jam (6 dollars) was also only OK...the consistency was too gelatinous for me. 

-The chocolate chip cookies (6 dollars) were not good.

Imagine you're a Pennsylvanian who's had a long, hard day of travelling around New Jersey - you're trying to understand why you can't make a left, and learning the hard way how to navigate traffic circles.

If you're like me and go here on a weekend to get a ton of stuff ... save everyone time and avoid a headache by submitting your order in advance to stoltzfusfamilyfoods1@gmail.com so it's ready for...
On Sundays (and maybe Saturdays, too) you will find fresh baked, hot, bagels in big paper bags. They're excellent!

sample_reviews.text: Came here for brunch and wow they did a great job. Their brunch is fantastic for the price. Just note that it gets busy but it's worth it.

Came here for a bday dinner and I've seen this place for years and always wanted to try it. This is one of the top Italian places in the city for sure and I can see why it's so popular. Good wine selection, good food selection, & good service.

I've been here numerous times over the past few months or so and never had a bad experience. While the food may be a bit overpriced, it's good & the portions are generally pretty big. I've had the red fish (it was delicious), the crawfish etouffée (my favorite dish here thus far), and their gold burger (also delicious).

OK I cant give this lower stars BC it meets all the criteria for a dive bar: kinda-rude & no f**KS given bartender (think Moe from Simpsons), heavy smoking, old-timer's who are regulars, cash-only, & cheap-ass beer. I can't complain BC I knew what I was getting into when I came in here.

It's not the best food but it's definitely far from the worst. I haven't had everything on the menu but all the stuff I have had had been good. This time it was the roast beef with provel & it was pretty good. I think I prefer imos roast beef and cheese more but this isn't bad.
$1 beers (specific ones) Fri nights from 9pm to close...best deal in town
Penne Rosa (hold the shrooms) is amazing with grilled chicken breast & parm cheese.

sample_reviews.text:
Great place to stop by for a quick great tasting smoothie!

I always get a strawberry banana smoothie mmmmm!
Less than $5 and taste so fresh with real fruit! 

You can get a non dairy or yogurt option smoothie.

Also offers soft serve frozen yogurt self serve buffet. Lots of toppings in the buffet bar too.

They do have the trendy Bubble teas and now Thai Rolled Froyo bowls.

Hands down the best Mexican establishment in the area. Always worth the 30min drive out of Indy. Great prices and delicious entrees. Salsa is prepared better here than most as well. Always a great authentic taste =] "hidden gem" would definitely recommend to all friends and family.

Had a lot of great options for breakfast and lunch  . Huge portions!!! All the sandwiches goin out looked like a dinner for two. The pound cake French toast is super fluffy and filling definitely worth trying out! The grits are good too. Cook eggs very well to order.

The absolute worst location you can goto. It's a tacobell/kfc and they are always under staffed or have no idea what is going on. The workers are always so rude. One day I asked the manager if they keep anything stocked and he said "probably not" seriously?! And tonight was going to go after work and they got rude with me in the drive thru and said they just closed. Hours say they close at 11pm.

The worst ever always out of food options and don't even bother trying to order Pizza Hut express options. This place should be shut down. The service is horrific and the workers are rude. The food when they have had it in the past is made poorly and takes them a very very very long time to get orders out in the drive thru. It's like they have 1 worker and no management ever.
Ask about all additional charges bc they have a lot for most changes here.
Go here for a diner atmosphere and great prices high quality diner food and shakes!

sample_reviews.text: As far as Americanized Chinese food goes, it doesn't get better than New China. Their lo mein and crab rangoons are top notch, and served in massive portions. Service was very friendly, as well. I'll drive the extra mile to go here when I need that MSG heavy, super satisfying and gluttonous meal.

Absolutely perfect. BD is a great middle point between over the top donut creations and your neighborhood donut shop. They've got donuts topped w/ candy bars, cereal, etc., but also excelled at the classics. Employees were super friendly.

This place is an underrated gem. As far as STL Italian goes, it's up there with Anthonino's and much better than the old standards-not gonna name any names. I've eaten here half a dozen times before covid, and 2x since. Their family meals are easily the best value/quality/taste I've ordered since the pandemic.

Poor service rarely sways a review for me, because as long as the food is good, I'm a happy man. We came in today for two specific items listed on the Sunday menu, but they had removed them and not updated the online menu. We were ignored and not handed menus, initially. Had he offered other suggestions, we might've stayed, we were already sat, but I guess they didn't want our money?

One of my top 5 favorite bars in St. Louis. 

It's kitchsy, and you can smoke there. Bring your grandma.
Half price lunch all week
Park your bike right in the bar

Yelping No. 2500. 
This one go to local restaurant Harvest Bowl. I believe they have very good potential. The place is great to to visit since they converted fro paint store to restaurant with huge space. This place includes eatery and food market. I like the concept. The only down side is not much of seating area. Very limited due to space fill with groceries items.

Authentic cuisine number 59. Philippine cuisine. 

This restaurant is close to low side of 4.5 stars. I see many potential potential for this restaurant. They have many good things going on this food truck. I round them to 5 stars. 

If you come here, you got to try their halo halo. Their halo halo is different than most Philippino cuisine. The bean and jelly are home made.

They actually do not have the buffet. We were there thinking they have the buffet. But they have the tali plate. Which is better to my opinion. 

We got the meat tali. It has a lot of chamber to fill the plate. I like it because the appetizers also meat. My two entrees is the mutton curry and the fish curry. These two curry were very good. Especially the mutton curry Kat fresh out the kitchen.

Many can go wrong with technology that sum the restaurant problem. 

Like - I like restaurant has opening look. Good wall art. Nice party look of the bar area. Seem like everyone have a good time. 

Bad - Service less restaurant. The order is made at the counter. An electronic buzzer is issued to let you know your food is ready. Then you pick up the food your self.

This restaurant is evaluating for their food not their local brew beer. The two star was given due to after food experience. After dine their food I have to go to restroom several time. It take me a day to clear my stomach pain. Some of the food is not up to the quality. 

The restaurant do have a lot of customers. Seem like a good joint. Especially the private local brew beer.
Grand opening is 6/24. Coupon with $10 of $50 discount.
Grand opening 25% off 2/12 to 2/14

sample_reviews.text: Two of my daughters and I had brunch at this Nudy's today.  They had both already been to this location a few times, while I had only gone to the ones in Devon and Ardmore.  They both got the California Benedict and the non-vegetarian also had some turkey bacon (which basically tastes and has a mouth feel like ham).

My daughter had been wanting to try this place for a while, so I treated her for her birthday recently. My two grandsons (one toddler and an infant) were with us as well.  We went for dinner on a weeknight and sat in the dining room.

Our last few visits to the diner have been really good.  We went for dinner tonight and had a great meal.  I had the Texas Meatloaf Special - two nice pieces of good meatloaf topped with grilled onions, cheddar cheese, bacon and BBQ sauce.  So good it should be on the regular menu!

Tom Jones isn't my favorite but we go there once in a while... It's obviously a favorite of lots of other people because every time we go there, the place is jam packed and you have to wait for quite a while to be seated, especially for breakfast/brunch on the weekends.  The food is pretty good though and the prices are reasonable.

Well I really wanted to love this place since it's right down the street from us and we know one of the hostesses (she's a doll!!!) butttt unfortunately my husband said his Buffalo Chicken Ranch sandwich wasn't good at all - it was dry and had no flavor... I had the Italian Omelette and home fries and didn't have any complaints about that...
I love Wolff's but damn, their flowers are expensive.  Stopped in yesterday on a whim to pick up a flat of impatiens.  I thought maybe it would be around 15 bucks or so.
The giardiniera relish and au jus put the Chi Town Beef over the top!!!!  SO GOOD!!!

sample_reviews.text: We have tried many of the bakeries in Chinatown and beleive that this is the best one. The quality of their pastries is superior and their deserts are also more attractive than the other bakeries. This is my go to for a chinatown bakery.

The owner here is so friendly and is always present in the store which is so nice to see. He does a lot for the community too. The pizza is tasty and we have also enojyed some other menu items here like chicken fingers and a tuna sub.

The banana split that I had here was one of my all time favorite highlights during covid. I am grateful that this location is not closer to me or I would be tempted to go too often. Service was very friendly and efficient. Bravo.

This would have been a one star review had the manger not had the foresight to comp us a round of drinks.  We went here for center city sips last night and the place was busy but by no means filled to capacity so it was baffling that it took more than 30 minutes to get our first round of drinks.  Food came out a bit quicker but not much.

I wanted to like Izumi but didn't. The host/waiter certainly was accommodating so service was not the issue. For the price I found the portion size to be very small.  When paying $13 for a roll I expect it to be much more hearty in size than an appetizer type roll that is more like a side dish than an entree.
Wonderful! Run to this Francisville gem on the corner of Poplar and Ginnodo!
Walked in and walked out. No air conditioning and flies all over.

sample_reviews.text:
Surprisingly approachable and delightfully crunchy Vietnamese hoagies.

Nhu Y turns out savory goodness in a perfectly toasted baguette. Filled with tasty pate, tender slices of meat (unless you go for a vegetarian one, which I haven't tried), and condiments galore, this is a spot I send tourists and locals alike.

Bagelicious delivers quality bagels, and you can order online[1] too. By far, they're the best place for bagels in the King of Prussia area.

Try their raisin bagel with jalapeno cream cheese - it's deliciously flavorful, and they put on a heavy spread. Get it lightly toasted, and enjoy a wonderful crispy bagel bread. Need something more substantial? Their pizza cinnamon raisin bagel is sweet.

Go. Get their Boston creme doughnut. And get me one while you're at it. Please?

When I first saw their magnificent Boston creme doughnut, right away I said "I'll split this with you."

And I meant it; I don't work out as much as I used to[1]. But after that first damned bite, I couldn't stop eating. It was like there was cocaine baked into its delicious frosting.

Foul. Avoid this place unless paying money for butter cooked in butter is your thing. Everything I ordered (from the side of mushrooms to the undercooked slab of meat) was soaked in butter. Everything lacked any spice or pizzazz. (Prices are expensive, but that's a given for Morton's.)

On the positive side, our server (Chinyeri) was great.

Anjou serves pan-Asian cuisine, particularly Japanese and Korean fare.

I made reservations for two 6 pm Thursday. Upon our arrival we were seated immediately. Only a couple other tables were occupied which wasn't surprisingly, considering the day.

My friend thought the decor was out of place and didn't fit any conceivable theme.
Killer beer selection.
Credit cards accepted here!

What an amazing food experience!! This gem is located off Park in the Lafayette Square neighborhood not far from Soulard.  Upon entry to building, it seems a little obscure, but you are in the right place. Head to the elevator, past the lovely couch, and take a ride to the 3rd floor.
Exiting the elevator, you'll meet the host table and see the amazing ceiling jewelry that makes up the decor.

Last review was 5yrs ago, but the 5 star still holds true. Today's visit was for Brunch. The one thing that North County doesn't readily have available and Hendel's got it right. A Buffet at a $12 price point with $5 Mimosas is amazing! The buffet, at 70% breakfast, 30% other in options had a strong layout of food.

In general, I'm a fan! 1st time at this location but had visited Clayton several times. This location is much bigger than Clayton and a little newer. 3 of us and we shared appetizers, Nachos with Extra Cheese, Potatoe Skins and Wings. I opted for a house salad with Ranch as well. True to form, everything else great.

1st visit and for me, 1st impressions are the most important.  Best thing about this visit was the gentleman who was working the front.  Very personable, very friendly and open to conversation.  Be advised, this place doesn't serve pork...no pork bacon, no pork sausage.  You will have your choice of Turkey or Beef.  This is fine for some, but for breakfast, I need my pork bacon.

I tried the eggplant parmesean and while the meal was ok, I would have really preferred for the skin of the eggplant to have been removed vs. included.  The skin didn't make for an enjoyable experience.  Friend of mine at the table ordered the chicken parm and I tasted a bite of that and it was very good.  Next time, I'll go for the chicken parm.
Bottomless Salad, Fries or Broccoli
Happy Hour until 7p. 1/2 Off All Drinks!

sample_reviews.text: Mead mead mead. A chill little nook shop with delicious mead. Mead is like a sweet wine made with honey---in my explanation of it. Located into fountain square this is a very chill place and perfect for a girls afternoon or just chilling with friends. I get a flight and just hang out.

Came here for trivia and a friends birthday. The staff was very nice and even filled up a birthday goblet I had gotten for my friend for her birthday. They do trivia weekly and the host was fun and it was loud enough you could hear everything. They were a little understaffed but told us up front so it wasn't a big deal that drinks took a little bit. Food was okay, fried pickles were great!

Really fun experience and food! We went for lunch and got in right away, our waitress was super fast and attentive! We all got hibachi dinners, which is the grill they make food on in front of you. They chef went table by table. So we shared our table with 3 other people but it was fine. We all got soup and salad that comes with the meal.

Meh, cool happy hour place but very expensive . The waitress got mad at my friend and I after asking For the well vodka for our drinks.  I felt bad bad for being a middle class working citizen and not wanting to pay $10 for a drink when I could order it for half the price sp I asked. And their food wasn't that great for what I paid.

Fun beer hall, great for a happy hour or to hang out with friends. Choose one of their beers or a guest beer to enjoy with your food. The prices are decent as the portions are large. I ended up with steak loaded fries special.
Really good! Came here for lunch and had excellent food & service!
They seem to have really good specials throughout the week! Perfect for happy hour !

I've been to this location twice now and just like the downtown location, they are accommodating and quick!  It doesn't quite have as much space for seating, so this one is mainly all take out, which is what I did tonight.

I have a thing for their kid's chicken bowl...I haven't gotten away from it yet, but I'm hoping soon I will try something else.

I like this place, it's very quick and the...

Several visits since the reopening.  I've dined in twice and did a take out order...

My typical go to is the chopped salad.  I always order the small because it's huge!

My to go order was of 4 small chopped salads for a home dinner gathering and Monique was super sweet and accommodating....

So, I had never heard of this place but my friend suggested it and I am not a big "smoked Mullet" fan, so I opted for the burger.

Let me tell you.....I tasted the mullet and it was actually pretty darn good.  I couldn't eat an entire one - it was huge!  And what's super surprising is I had a burger and it was big and REALLY good!

This place is good.  No doubt.  I like the creativity in the tex-mex cuisine.
My sister in law loves this place.

We had ceviche, it was ok, the texture wasn't great.  My husband really didn't care for it at all.

Then we had our entrees, which were pretty standard, very decent sized portions and the taste was good.

Not a huge fan of chains, we would go back if someone really wanted to go there,...

Outback is a backup steak shop.
They have pretty decent food, if you order the right thing.
Their steaks (filet) is pretty good, yet pricey.  Not always cooked to order.
The green beans are always great, but they get so cold so fast.
Their salads are really good, always fresh, crisp lettuce.
I like the big outdoor area with lots of picnic tables on the pavers. I think they are building an outdoor lawn fun and games stuff :)
Love the new self checkouts!!!

F and I stopped in today famished and on our way to NewPodCity. A big bowl of rare rib steak noodle soup for me and a big bowl of beef noodle soup for F. So fresh and tasty! Filled our bellies with warming goodness.
Le, the owner, is an absolute sweetie and very proud of her establishment.

I can't believe it's been so long and we are trying Hummingbird for the first time.
F and I stopped in recently because we were competing in the Passport To Trenton restaurant contest. Our first stop was Jamaica since we were already downtown.
This corner cafe has been serving the community for a number of years now and still going strong.

F and I are lucky enough to live so close to this amazing bakery. Italian People's Bakery is going on 80 years in business and 3 generations. 
They are world famous for so many of their baked goods. For example torpedo rolls, sesame twist bread, cream filled donuts, cannolis, cheese cake, pinole cookies...and the list goes on.

Meh.
Only went here for a bridal shower. The restaurant is in a strip mall. The decor is kind of pizzeria meets Italian restaurant. The brussetta was more like a Sicilian pizza. Salad was tiny. Riggitoni ala vodka was the best part of the meal. It was served family style.
Chicken marsala was ok.

We were looking for a nice cocktail for our anniversary, when we came across Village Whiskey. Sitting at the bar we were well served by Alejandro. He is a man passionate about whiskey and creating a well crafted drink. That man deserves a raise. He literally turned my husband into a whiskey fan. Kudos!
The fact that this place is a short walk from my house... awesome!
Bar pie...yum!

sample_reviews.text: Folks, this is a sexy looking place.  As others have mentioned, it's owned by the same folks as next door Laurel.  When you walk in, it's dark and moody and beautiful.  The bartenders are nice, the cocktail list is interesting, personally, I enjoyed the wine list while we were here.  Appetizers are thoughtfully selected, we enjoyed the trout rilette.
Agree with others, the honey turkey and mozzarella is where it's at. Busy at lunch, but the line moves fairly quickly.

For starters, we drove by the dedicated lot for Suraya twice even though it's clearly labeled as we were not paying attention to what seemed like just another hipster mural in the neighborhood, oops! We were 3 plus a stroller but thankfully had no trouble finding seating, and with my mommy brain, I had to tweak my order after placing it, and the person working the counter could not have been...

We went here because of a Groupon we had purchased, and I'm glad we did because it's a bit pricier than what we're used to spending on sushi. The presentation was stunning, with some sort of lights and glasses and all kinds of decor, I felt like I was eating my sushi out of an art piece of some sort. The rolls themselves were all just okay, I wasn't blown away by the flavor of any of them.

Scallion pancakes were quite good. Pork chop soup had great flavor and crunch. We also opted for one of the Indonesian curries, which had a nice mix of flavors. Courteous staff. When we asked for our leftovers to be packed to go, we didn't specify that the rice needed to be packed as well, and ended up with curry sans rice as leftovers.
New drink specials board!
Iced chai on tap is delicious- sweet and spicy!

sample_reviews.text: We had a great experience here! Good service, good custard/ice cream. It wasn't very warm out so we look forward to coming back in the summer. The strawberry shortcake sundae is the best! Love that they have nice size pup cups too!

This place is so nice! It's been a year since we last visited for dinner but the name continues to come up. Tonight we started with a salad-delish!, then my husband always has pasta-very tasty and full of flavor, and me pizza-quite large and fresh. Nothing we've had has been disappointing. Oh, and the bread and dipping oil is unique and good!

Loved this place! I've been for drinks but not food and I have to say we were impressed with everything we ordered. I really liked the peach cider local from Oliver Winery. The tempura cauliflower to the jam burger to the naShville chicken was all excellent. Our server Sasha was also the best and very helpful with suggestions. Will definitely be going back!

Sushi wasn't bad but the service was terrible. Very inattentive, we had to ask for everything when we could get the server to come to our table. We asked for silverware and was given only one fork for the one person who asked, no one else at the table. Will not be going back.

I really hate leaving two stars for such a cool looking restaurant we were excited to try but the experience was really lacking and disappointing. We were seated by a hostess who also ended up being our server. She was nice and friendly but seemed very aloof and disappeared for much of our time there. Sounds just like it could be the same staff member mentioned in other reviews here.
Cute little place. Coffee drinks and hot chocolate were nice. Food was okay.
Pancakes are delicious!!

sample_reviews.text: Vegan visitors to Philly, fear not about missing out on the cheese steak! Wiz kid has a completely vegan philly cheese steak! No, it isn't an exact replica of the real thing, but it does a damn good job! So feeling, with amazingggggggg bread. Completely filling. Got fries on the side too with a vegan cheese topping. Can not wait to come here again!

I've been on a Yelp hiatus but I'm back. Just ate here literally 30 minutes ago and ran to write this review. First of all, the atmosphere is so homey. You feel welcomed and taken care of from the minute you walk in. Side note -- expect a wait (but it's well worth it). 

You'll be seated around beautiful paintings, everything perfectly curated. Service is kick and kind.

I love this place. Vibes = immaculate. Pretty spacious, in a really fun part of town. Now I've only had the drinks here, but the bartender snaps every time. I actually order a wine, and the waitress mistakenly brought me a cocktail with a similar name (she misheard me). I decided to try the cocktail and oh my gosh.. it was incredible. Best mistake ever.

For the price, you're not getting what you should. My burger was no where near exceptional (as I had expected from the reviews), the service was pretty slow, and like I said, the price is just not compatible with what you're getting overall. There are far better burger joints in Nashville. The food wasn't horrible, but like I said, I've had better, and I probably won't spend my money here again.

Y'all no :( the price :( come on now. The cookie dough was good, but 1. Y'all really don't have that many flavors 2. Or options for vegan friends 3. And this tastes no different than the run of the mill pillsbury joint

I'm sorry I had to do it to y'all but I don't think I will come back. One scoop of cookie dough is OVER 5 DOLLARS. Outrageous. You can get two packs at the Kroger for that.

sample_reviews.text:
This place easily gets top rating from me.  It's a slice of americana.  It's cheap, unique, and oh so good.  

Whenever I have guests in town, I take them here after dinner.  I've never heard anyone compare Drewe's to an establishment they have in their city.  And they're always blown away by how good it is. 

I guarantee that the $15 I spent here impressed as much as the $150 I spent at dinner.

This is one of STL's gems.  If you're in the mood for a sandwich or dessert give them a try.  My friend Camille ordered a chocolate malt.  I have no clue how someone can try and drink 5 of these (Man v. Food episode).  I doubt I could even finish one.  I had a Fire Chief sundae.

One of my bucket list items this year was to learn how to shoot a gun.  I didn't grow up around guns, and knew little about them before attending the First Shot class earlier today.  I went with my wife.  

The class was surprisingly small.  There were only 5 students... 3 female, and one other male.  The instructor was an ex-FBI agent.  He took us through a couple hours of classroom training.

If you want to know what a Schnucks looked like 30 years ago, go here.  It really is run-down especially when you compare it to the Schnucks or Dierbergs in Warson Woods or even the Shop n Save in Maplewood.  I do frequent this Shnucks the most though.  It's right by my house and conveniently at a corner that I drive by every day (Brentwood and Manchester).

The atmosphere was quaint, and the waitstaff was very friendly as we entered.  However, the sushi was too spicy.  I usually like spicy foods, but they went overboard.  All 3 rolls (Vegas, Red Dragon, Fancy) that I ordered were so hot that I could barely make out the other flavors.  The fried ice-cream afterwards hit the spot to cool my palette.  

I do have to hand it to them.
They could call it Cock... I'd still eat here.
Wait your turn in line and don't screw up your order. Or else, NO BULGOKI FOR YOU!
