sample_reviews.text:
I have some good friends that live in Safety Harbor and frequently talk about how awesome Crooked Thumb is.  After a day at Safety Harbor Resort and Spa, my husband and I decided to check out Crooked Thumb before our dinner reservation.  I couldn't agree with my friends more, it was great!

I wanted to do a flight.  They noted that they don't do flights but they do "small beers".

I am extremely happy that C House opened.

OK, so I live 2 blocks from the C House.  I was being a curmudgeon about trying it out as I'm currently pregnant and can't take advantage of the champagne, but I begrudgingly went along with my friends one evening.

The atmosphere is different than I expected.  The outside patio is a casual area with TVs and a full bar.

All I can say is YESSSSSSSSS!!!!!!!!

So, I live two blocks from Harvest Bowl and have been anticipating their opening for a while.  I try not to get my hopes up too much when a new restaurant comes to the neighborhood because a lot of them don't last.  The good news is, I get the feeling Harvest Bowl is here to stay!

First of all, it's not just a restaurant.  It is also a market.

I have tried and tried to like this place, and in the end, I just don't.  My fiance' LOVES Three Coins and always suggests it for breakfast.  I've just really never had anything that I have liked a whole lot.  And I've had hair in my food, more than once.  I don't usually consider myself a picky eater...but I'm just not into this place.

It is somewhat hard to review this place.

The pool:  Really nice!  I loved the bar and the large pool area.  We ordered lunch and it was super delicious, however, one of my friends ordered fruit which took a ridiculous amount of time for the food to come out.  They did compensate us accordingly with a watermelon flavored liquor drink.
Get the flight... $9.50 for 6 huge samples of local craft beers.
Blue mountain vineyards is a great place to relax and have a glass of wine away from the hustle and bustle of the market.

sample_reviews.text:
Authentic Japanese spot from the Chef/Owner of Nippon Tea who was a James Beard semifinalist for Rising Star Chef of the Year. 

The restaurant is very small, and seems like a hole-in-the-wall from the outside. The space has 40 seats and an eight person chef's counter that comes with a pre-set tasting menu. 

They serve both lunch and dinner, but lunch is counter service and much more laid back.

Classic American restaurant with a full bar & a patio, inside of a a building they was constructed in 1873 as a general store. Brunch Buffet on Sundays 

I came for lunch and got a turkey&brie sandwich. It was delicious

Hole in the wall bakery that is very cheap and has delicious pastries. Not really any seating inside, and only a few homemade drink options however. I got a blueberry scone and an iced chai and both were delicious.

Low-key bar (with no sign out front) with a couple of arcade games that is known for their extremely cheesy Chicago-style pizza (also have non-Chicago style as well). Pizza has cracker thin crust and is very buttery. 

I went on a Friday night with my friend around 6:30 and it took 1.5 hrs to get our pizza!
Subway in Mallinkrodt building at WashU. Gets incredibly busy around lunch time every 30 min. Can order ahead of time and pick up your food through snap finger app.

sample_reviews.text:
Me and the wife went for valentines day, and I came away very impressed. I didn't issue a 5th star because I'm reviewing that night, and the food was very good but not 5 stars.  I fully expect that had I gone any other night I would have been more impressed.

Everything about this place is excellent. I wish the drink menu was little better, but the rest was just about perfect.
Been back a few times since.  The food it outstanding and one of the 5 best restaurants in the city.  I love going to Farmhaus for lunch, the $10 blue plate special is hard to beat.

Upon subsequent trips to The Good Pie this restaurant has become one of my favorites in St. Louis.  The pizza's are always delicious.  The Chopped Salad is simple and sublime.  And their beer list continues to impress despite its size.  Their use of simple and fresh ingredients and warm friendly service makes me and the wife come back time and again.

I find it hard to believe there are so many people with such low standards when it comes to the Daily Bread.

It is a ripoff of St. Louis Bread Co/Panera Bread, which in of itself I don't mind at all.  I have eaten multiple times at the Daily Bread and have yet to have an enjoyable experience.  The food is so-so at best.  Their sandwiches are basic and lack any real flavor.

Very good spot.  The beer selection is small but well represented by different tastes and quite delicious.   Overall the ambience is nice with simple brick walls and simple decor.

We had a delicious salad that was appropriately seasoned and the Good Pie pizza which some have said was soggy but I chalk that up to more with the ingredients.

I will return to the Good Pie again.
Solid Mexican place for an easy meal. Nothing special, just good cheap Mexican food. The carnitas were delicious.
Wheee!

sample_reviews.text:
I do not want to give this place 5 stars because I do not want it to get any more packed!

Great, attentive, non-judgemental (if you are not a beer afficienado) staff.  The beer selection is always great.  And while there are a lot of "beer bars" in the area, this is the tops for food.  I have been there a dozen times and had something different every time.

We pretty much had one of everything on the menu.  Yes, we were fat.  Really fat.

We each had a signature drink. I cannot remember the name of mine but it had raspberry in it and it was amazing (sorry, not very helpful).

Everything was good.  Really.  There was no dog on the pile but that would only give it 3-stars for me.  So, why 5? Oyster Sliders, HOT Chicken and Banana pudding.

So thrilled to find this place.  I will be honest, I am not sure this is a 5-star place BUT it is 5-star for being in the area.

The Grandma Thing is perfect. Really it is a great pizza.  The garlic knots are perfect.

Their plain pizza is good but I would not say great but a tie with Palermo's.

The cheesesteak was good, as were the fries, etc.

They also deliver!

Liked Blue Bell Pizza the first time. Went there tonight because I read the wings were good and that's what we wanted. My husband had to wait 30 minutes longer than quoted. He got home and they forgot part of our order. I called and the girl was helpful and nice and said they would deliver it right away. 45 minutes-nothing.

I was really excited about the potential of this place-good reviews, big menu. I did take out and I only got the chicken Pad Thai. It had absolutely no flavor. None. It wasn't a bad flavor, it was complete lack of flavor.
I gave it two stars instead of one because my food was ready on time, it was hot, and the place was clean.
Broad menu. Great service. Everything was really delicious. And there is a bar!
Maybe it's growing pains but the service is terrible. They are also out of a ton of beer and food:(

sample_reviews.text: This can't be my first review for Batch. We've been coming for years because my wife loves the paleo cupcakes, so they make the perfect occasional surprise or birthday treat for her. Heck. I love em too! And ever since my now three year old son learned what a cupcake was, we've had three people that love them in the house!

Oops I did it again. Failed plans to visit another local pizzeria for some patio dining due to kiddos landed us back here for some of the best pizza in town! 

Ya know what? I ain't mad about it either! This pizza is that good, possibly the best in town. Yes. I said it...again!

The three day ferment on the dough does magic in the flavor department.

It's been a minute since I stopped by Old World. Oddly enough, this far south. Midtown has fallen outside of my normal routine of the week in these post pandemic times, so it's hasn't been top of mind, but I fortunately found myself nearby this week on Two For Tuesday! 

Having run out of coffee, I went first thing after dropping off the kiddo.

I hate to see a business that is pennywise and pound foolish, endeavoring to save pennies, when there are many dollars to be made from customer loyalty in the long run.

This is the case with Asian Pho, and after giving them multiple attempts to see that I want nothing more than to be a great customer, their inability to understand that is the only reason this review is being lowered from 3 to 2...

With a unique menu, and a location only a mile from my house, I was excited to give Asian Pho a try. If restaurants were only judged by their food, this place would be 4-5 easy, but there's always that element of service that operators want to overlook. A little bit goes a long way, and I'd be singing high praises if they showed they had any regard for me as a customer.
Order the smoked corned beef sandwich or on St Patty's Day, get the plate!
They've added some Japanese whisky to the menu!

sample_reviews.text: Came in on a Thursday evening for family dinner. Lovely atmosphere and noise level was low to moderate. Cute date spot ! Definitely will recommend and be back for fresh sushi. We enjoy all the rolls and kids love it also.
Come here for breakfast every weekday morning! Always great customer service and fast ! Why doesn't love some Chick-fil-A?

Fast - Accurate & Delicious. Conveniently located and always courteous. Overall highly recommend for ordering pizza. Normally get their 3 topping large pizza deal. I have been to this location for carry out for 5 years now with NO issues.

This location has a small parking lot that on a normal workday morning the drive thru is completely jam up! I prefer to recommend you the Starbucks on Anderson Rd.  - by AMC 24. Due to very narrow direction of car flow it sometime can be HARD to access this location.  Neither less to say it's always busy. Drive thru is quick so I do suggest it unless your lucky enough to find a parking space.

Seems to be a freshly remodeled Wendy's!  FREE WiFi / charging station too.  I dine in today & got a combo Baconantor. The fries were hot & crunchy. The food & service is 4 stars quality. Fairly busy for a typical Friday this us my second time here & to let you know - "I will be back"! Dining inside is modern /sleek with LARGE flat screen TV that shows the local news.
CLOSE on Monday's !!!   4/26/2021
More sitting in the back OUTSIDE...

sample_reviews.text:
I've been wanting to try Canes for some while and with friends in from out of town, this was our chance. 

We're still in the world of Covid, so we socially distanced when we placed our orders: A raspberry tea, a frappe and an italian soda, 2 bacon, cheese and steak pies and a cuban sandwich.  We took a seat outside and waited for the order to come.

This review is for the gift shop. I've not yet had the pleasure for the actual tea room, but that will come soon. 

My love and I had decided our "One tank trip" Would take us to Down Town NPR and all that it had to offer, which is surprisingly a lot compared to when I was a kid. The down town area has shifted and changed and now offers Up Scale and fun things to do.

I drive past this place frequently and finally decided to stop in.

They have La Segunda Bread. That's some damn fine Cuban bread! 

Feed the cows! For a small price, they will give you a helping of veggies they've cut up for the bovines out back and you and your littles can go feed them. They will thank you, your kids will thank you and you, might just enjoy the break from being on the computer.

Stopped in to get myself my usual Carmel swirl latte... I was told that the Caramel was on recall. Funny, the next one down the road didn't have that issue. I dislike being lied to. REALLY dislike it. Consider that I won't stop at this particular location.
As of this writing, the new location on Main St in Tarpon has closed. It is sad to see this happen.
Today they had garlic veggies instead of all pasta on the pasta bar! Great option for those of us doing low carb eating
They JUST expanded their hours on 11/1! No longer are they just s bruncherie! They're now open until 9pm every day of the week!

sample_reviews.text:
Service is very friendly.

My party of 7 came here on a Friday after work and were able to get seated right away.
The stew chicken was recommended and that's what I went with.  Loved it.  Good sauce and chicken very tender.  I also got the mango smoothie.  Very fresh.  Had leftovers and even more delicious the next day

we also shared the tres leches cake and flan.  both really realy good.

I came here for dinner tonight and I had to write another review to give FIVE stars.  My friend and I shared the combo appetizer and I got the octopus entree again. Can't say enough how good the food was!!!  Service was great too.

This place was SOOOO good.  My friend and I ordered A Taste of Suraya and got to try a little bit of everything.  LOVED EVERYTHING!

I can't wait to go back!

Comparing to Cheesecake Factory, I would rate this place subpar.  However, I think we ordered the wrong dishes.  My friend and I shared Tapas/Appetizers - many of the dishes being fried.  I did enjoy the short rib tacos with kimchee and sauce very similar to gochujang.  I would go again and try some other dishes

We ordered the Lombarda pizza and I could see how this could be a successful pizza; however, mine came with a burnt crust. The sausage was good but even while eating nonburnt areas, it had a charred taste.  We also ordered the charcuterie  the artichoke mostarda was delicious but I was expecting some bread/crackers to come with it so a little disappointing.

sample_reviews.text: New outpost of the Bistro joins the best noodle soup block in Philly. I wanted to try something of the Laos side of the menu. My eyes immediately went to the khaopoon, a coconut curry noodle soup. Not only is it served beautiful, but this soup is so flavorful: lime, mint, some spicy, and the ground pork that was recommended as the protein.

Had a great Wednesday night dinner at Olly. Loved my tequila rhubarb cocktail. The cheese ball is a great snack to start. We ordered the Wednesday night special - lasagna, a big stack of homemade noodles, beef, and plenty of cheese. Speaking of cheese, the broccoli salad is 40% cheese which means very very delicious. Ricotta gnudi was probably the favorite of the night.

Offering easy/COVID safe pick up from their side door. Lamb burgers and hanger steak were fantastic. The burgers barely needed seasoning, just a pinch of s&p. Looking forward to trying the chicken sausages, and I will be back soon for more proteins to enhance my Corona cooking!

Beer and ambiance, fine. Food, no bueno.  Thinking the tuna guacamole would be like Jose/Sancho Pistola's, I was horrified to find out that Black Sheep's version was basically canned tuna, mayo, and a little bit of avocado. Filet Mignon sliders were hard to chew. The fries that came with it were strangely dense. The wings had a decent flavor but were not crispy enough. I learned my lesson.

Worn down signs intrigue me.  I had biked passed 16th Street Seafood a ton of times, always wondering what was going on inside.  Now I know the answer, and the answer is- not much. At least in terms of decor. There is a gumball machine, 2 empty candy machines, plus a counter to order at and that's pretty much it.

sample_reviews.text: So I am half Ukrainian. I grew up eating and making perogies, and cabbage rolls.  I actually helped make 1000 for a wedding one weekend as a teenager. I would say that I know the perogy business. This place is the real deal. Delicious affordable Ukrainian food that is just as good as Baba's.

This is my favourite restaurant in Edmonton. I have been coming here since it first opened. Everything is delicious. Things you must try: Any of the dips with the bread, sea bass, and the Adana kabab.  I was just here for Edmonton Dining week so they had a special menu which was delicious.

So I have to say that if it wasn't for the free perks I wouldn't have driven there. I live pretty much on the opposite end of town.  However, after being there I would totally go back. It is in the southgate mall, so I wasn't expecting much. However, overall it was the nicest atmosphere that you can have in the mall. I really like how they support other local businesses with hand made items.

So we love seafood and decided to give this a go. Really disappointed. Food overpriced. Drinks WAY overpriced. My draft beer was a ridiculous 14 dollars. Some of the food was okay, but other's were not. And so we ate here twice because we were meeting friends, and after we ate we all felt unwell.  Don't bother unless you are the type that likes getting soaked.

As a disclaimer I usually don't go to chains. However, chains have their place I think. When you have a big group of people with a wide age range and food taste...And sometimes when you kid is starving and you need somewhere kid friendly that is fast.  My auntie decided to treat us to Ihop because she thought my little miss would like it. I inwardly sighed but decided to go ahead anyway.
Local booze and food

sample_reviews.text: We were in town for work, and headed to the Armature Works, with the idea that everyone could get what they wanted, but we could still sit together, and we all ended up getting food from Hemingway's!   The menu looked amazing and it was hard to decide what to get, but I finally decided to go with a bowl with ropa vieja, and chose black beans, plantains, and yucca fries as my sides.

How have I not reviewed Ritter's before?!   The frozen custard is creamy, fresh, and delicious.   They have flavors of the day that rotate, and all of those that I've had have been excellent.  I often circle back to my standard order though of a small Butterfinger Glacier.   They put in just enough of the Butterfinger pieces to make the perfect ice cream to candy ratio.

I actually had a long enough lunch break today to go out for lunch.  Yay!  This is actually only about a 5 minute walk from my office, and they were super-efficient, so I need to remember this would be do-able even with a shorter break than I had today.     The food was delicious!  I love falafel, and the falafel pita was great.

We tried to get in at Delicia, but the wait was longer than we could manage (45 minutes) since we had tickets for a show later that evening and so the hostess suggested we try their sister restaurant next door.  

The food was good, but the menu available at dinnertime is very limited.

I had an early weeknight dinner here with friends before heading to an evening work meeting; we were looking for something that would be quick, but were also trying to avoid fast food places.  The menu is basic consisting mostly of steaks, basic seafood, sandwiches and salads.   There is nothing too imaginative on the menu, but plenty of solid options that fit with what we were looking for.
Drive through only now due to being short staffed
Closed at 6PM on a Friday

sample_reviews.text: I hate fast food, but this is the only fast food I eat. I usually come for lunch, sometimes its super crowded, sometimes its not but on the days that it is crowded, they make the line go really quick. I never had an issue with any of the employees, one time I wanted the grilled nuggets but I said the number of the fried nuggets but said I want them grilled instead (politely mind you), I got a...

simply the best! everything from here is good! I come from a very Italian family and whenever there is a family event, there is always Termini brothers dessert trays! Their cannolis are amazing! Highly recommend.

If you are looking for something different to eat in NE philly, this is your place! The blue duck is a delicious and cute little restaurant. I have been here multiple times and had a few burgers and a few of the mac and cheese entrees and I was never disappointed. I have yet to try it for lunch. The service is top notch too. I hope to get back here soon!

all the bars in the Mayfair area, this one my friends is the worse to me. Don't try coming here when its packed and expect to walk around freely and get a drink, I came here around st patty's day and walked right out, came here once on a random weekend and hated every second of being here. I could also count the amount of creeps who tried hitting on me as well.

I live right around here and I used to take my brother here when I babysat him all summer for an afternoon for something different. I almost had parents arguing with me and kids running wild asking me for tokens or trying to take our tickets!

sample_reviews.text:
When I saw this location in the yard, I got so excited! It took some convincing of my friends but thankfully they decided to come with me to test out some wine.
The location is their third in the Indianapolis area, there is one in Carmel one in mass ave, and now one in the yard.
This location is just as cute as their other locations, they have a tasting bar and you can select up to 5 different...

Still one of my favorite places, I pop in once a month to use my wine club credits. I use the fishers the location, it's close to my house and I love the set up and just relaxing inside.
Right now, they have stopped tastings, but it is still enjoyable to go in a glass or a flight.
For those who don't know, this is an Indiana family that makes wine from California, and it's tasty stuff.

This place has been on my list for awhile, finally got the chance to visit last night and I'm glad I did! When you arrive you can put your name in and they will text you once your seat is ready.
There is a great selection of cocktails, I had the emerald Buddha and the gin and tonic.

This was a new place for me but I really enjoyed it a lot. Very friendly wait staff and they were all very attentive to the drinks and food orders. I enjoyed the atmosphere with all the eclectic decorations and it had all the meaning sports games on as well. Awesome beer specials with a ton of variety, curious to see what the other days of the week have as well!

Bars and dive bars, 2 separate categories and the dugout is certainly a dive. But a clean drive! Located in the hipster foodie paradise that is fletcher place you can find this gem. 
when you walk in there's a longish bar, and on the other side is a bunch of tables with dart boards, TVs and a juke box.
Outside of that, it's just your typical bar.

sample_reviews.text:
CONSISTENCY!!!
My biggest complaint regarding all the new bourgie restaurants is that they are not consistent from day to day. Maybe I'm old fashioned, but some people do read my reviews for recommendations, and I don't want to send them somewhere unless I know that they have consistently exemplary service and a quality product at a fair price.

BITTERSWEET IS ALWAYS 99% PERFECT
...and at...

THE BEST BACON I'VE EVER HAD
and I hate bacon. When I was a kid my mother would start frying bacon about 10 minutes before waking us up for school. So, I've always associated that smell with the unpleasantness of a shrieking alarm clock. But today the non greasy, nitrate free, locally sourced, baked bacon at Refuel was a revelation.

I'D LIKE TO USE MY TIM GUNN SAVE
After 3 years back uptown, Superior Grill has proven to be the restaurant where I feel most welcome. It's my first choice for celebrations, birthdays and big events. It's where the managers and employees not only know me by name, they know my order and greet me with a handshake.

TASTES LIKE A USED ERASER
I had just thrown away about $40 of inedible slop from LA Thai on Prytania but was still craving Asian. My only nearby option was August Moon because everybody uptown knows that Jung's is the worst Chinese in the city and Green Tea is the worst in the known universe.
Don't ask the hostess to see a menu of the specials, if they deign to speak to you they will tell you no.

WHEN DID THEY GET SO OUTRAGEOUSLY EXPENSIVE!!!???
Most items are around $20. They don't have lunch or weekday specials. No locals' discounts.

sample_reviews.text:
Get here before the hoards of students take it over! For now, apparently, the secret is not out, and this place seems to be having a relatively quiet opening week. But once news spreads that you can have your cake (fast food) and eat it too (cuz it's plant-based), I'm sure the lines will wrap around the block much like the Rittenhouse location.

I ordered the Udon Noodle salad.

The new Doc Bakers location has finally opened and it is a lovely, spacious restaurant.  There are tall chairs at the bar, a large wooden table at the front, and a few two and three person tables all around the perimeter of the restaurant. This spot is more like a cafe in that when you arrive here, you order first, then sit down, or take it your order go.

So, so many vegan-friendly options! I will definitely return. Service was excellent, food served quickly, and the ambience was very cool with spoon-covered light fixtures and comfy high-backed booths.

My husband, a friend and I started out with a large order of sweet fries with buffalo sauce and smoky sauce, and the pickles and cole slaw starter.

People are pretty harsh on this establishment, here, but I think it does what it does well. I've gone in there when the line is to the door, but I'm always through in 5-10 minutes. The signature salad is always good, full of fresh greens, grapes, pistachios, blue cheese and dried cranberries.

This place was one of my favorite joints for a fairly inexpensive meal in Philly when I first started hanging out in the city. I would tend to order a grilled cheese with Havarti cheese and apple slices, which was just heavenly. On multiple occasions, I ordered the turkey burger which came with cranberry sauce and hummus on top, a killer combination.
Juice here is 100% organic!
Tofu Verde burrito and veggie tacos are two of my favorite meals here.

sample_reviews.text: A tour guide perfectly described the difference between Bourbon Street and Frenchman Street. Bourbon Street is for drunks, drag queens, and prostitutes. Frenchman Street is where the music is. I highly recommend Three Muses, which is a gastropub on the street. The food is incredible. The mac and cheese is amazing. The cheese it uses is great and the dish comes out sizzling hot.

This café is one of my favorite breakfast/ brunch spots anywhere. The Banana Foster's French Toast is unbelievable. I loved everything about it. The toast is very soft and sweet. The banana cream inside and the Banana Foster sauce on top are incredible. They are sure to satisfy any sweet tooth. I devoured the entire dish quickly.

I saw the Creole Creamery on the Food Network's Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives and knew I had to try it whenever I visited New Orleans. It did not disappoint. It is as good as any ice cream I have tasted. It features some unique flavors. There are a couple of samplers, 4 mini scoops for $4.50 or 6 mini scoops for $6, so that you can try a few flavors.

Luke is a John Besh restaurant. It is known for their 75 cents oysters at Happy Hour, from 3PM-6PM every day. If you like oysters, this place is definitely for you. However, I tried Luke for breakfast. I ordered the chicken and biscuits. It was awesome. The challenge with fried chicken is that it is easy to dry out the meat.

The best Pho I have had was in Ottawa, the capitol of Canada. The only Pho spot I love in New York burned down and I am waiting for it to re-open. As such, I was happy to hear there is a large Vietnamese population in New Orleans. Lilly's Café is one of the top places I saw on a list. I ordered the Pho Rare Flank and Brisket (Tai & Nam).

I must admit.
I've been sceptical.
If indeed they don't keep regular hours...phone ahead.
Just induged in a Super Donair after reading all the positive reviews.
I've eaten here a few times in the last few years.
I think because on my first visit I watched the chef pull the meat from a tray...and not off the spigot...I was turned off.

Surprised by all the negative reviews. What do people realistiy expect from a franchise such as this these days. It's not Tres Carnalles...it's Mucho Burrito! 
My personal favorite is the Chorizo sausage.
I look at thus change as a nice change from Subway, and the pita places. Hot. Spicy. Filling....and not an overpriced franchised hamburger.

Best in Sherwood Park. And I have tried them all!

It's high time I update my review.
This is no longer the Famous Donair of old.
I went once years ago. Much like the older reviews state...I wasn't impressed at that time.
That however...was years ago, and I have recently learned that was under the original owner. He was an "old school", "my place...my way...my hours" kinda guy.

Decided to give Dimaggio's a shot tonight for dinner. Been driving by since they opened.
Not too shabby.
As they label themselves a steak and pizza place, I settled on the House Steak calzone. A nice mix of both. Indulged in a poutine on the side.

Friendly server. Never did get a refill on my Iced tea...
Good food.

sample_reviews.text: Finally was able to get to Kermit's. We tried 3 different pizza's - Pesto, Shitake Mushroom, and Sausage/Pepper. Each one was exceptionally good. Lot's of unique flavors, herbs/spices on each pie. Not sure why some have said it tasted like cardboard - we enjoyed every bite. And I'm a bit of a food snob/pizza snob - if the pizza was bad, I'd be the first to say so. But we were very impressed.

Been back twice since 1st visit. Very impressed with entire operation. The staff is nice and knowledgable. The pizza is really good. So are the pockets filled w/ meats, etc. The taste and flavor's are unique and creative. The management/chef clearly are talented and think outside the box.

The baked goods are way too good.

Fantastic, great tasting dishes! The stewed goat and roast pork were better than delicious. The chicken soup was exceptional, except it was served luke warm - once they made it hot it was great. Rice and beans, plantains great too. Look forward to going back. Nice and pleasant ownership and servers too.

First off I need to state that I am a big fan of Primo Hoagies. I like their rolls from Liscio's, they carry above average meats and I've never been unhappy with any of their many franchised locations - until now.

I've been going here since 1986 when they were located on Pine Street. Recently ordered delivery for the first time - very disappointing. 

If you want:
1- cold, completely unseasoned shoe string fries (they would have been good if they were hot and someone cared enough to sprinkle a little bit of salt on them).
2- only two tiny plum tomatoes on a BLT that should be renamed a "BL no T".
3- cold...

sample_reviews.text:
Just when I thought brunch at HWK couldn't get any better, it goes and amazes me again. 

I came here on a Sunday morning with Joselyn H. and Sarah S. We had rezzies for 10AM, which was an ideal time, because it started filling up shortly afterwards. Definitely make reservations! 

I started with the pear mimosa, which was one of the best mimosas I've had in my life!

I'm really sad I waited so long to try The Block! But...better late than never, right? 

I met a few friends here on Sunday afternoon for an early dinner. We arrived shortly after they opened, at 4pm. 

The place is beautiful. Open space, and an atmosphere that reminds me of a farmhouse. I loved it, even though it got a bit noisy as more customers arrived.

It's official. Plush is my new favorite small music venue in St. Louis. 

I came here last night for a show with a friend (which was also pretty rockin, by the way). We were hungry, so after the first act left the stage, we headed over to the restaurant side of Plush. For those late to the game, Plush has two sides: a concert side and a restaurant side. There! Now you're all caught up.

Eau Cafe? Haha...how about 'No' Cafe? Okay, that wasn't that funny. I never said I was a comedian...

Anyway, I went to Cafe Eau a few weeks ago for dinner and dessert. I flew solo, because it was a last-minute thing, and sometimes I prefer eating alone; the fewer people to judge my dietary choices, the better. And let's be honest, I'm kind of a pig. Oink! 

Moving on.

What in the WORLD is going on, Bottleworks? 

This used to be one of my favorite places to eat. But the last few times I've been there have been VERY underwhelming. 

Last night was sort of the last straw. 

I ordered their turkey burger (comes with chili and guacamole).
Obsessed with this place!
It's national ice cream day...why not? :)

Oh Yazmin...Yazmin...Yazmin...How do I love thee? Let me count the ways..  

I loved Thee from the moment my eyes locked upon your enticing menu, offering me more choices, than I needed: but, somehow, I wanted them all!

My moist lips, were anticipating your arrival and as the sweet aroma filled the room, I knew then, it would only be moments away...

Not being able to resist your temptations any...

My Dear Yazmin, we met again last night.  You were calling to me and I gave in to those desires by wrapping my lips around those wonderful, moist Duck Dumplings, with the most perfect sauce. Then and only then, did I indulge myself with your General Tao Shrimp and I lathered my taste buds with the flavors that exploded in my mouth.

Philadelphia's Chinatown has been graced by this amazing Thai gem, everything about it was perfection, from the very attentive service from both Yui and Chu, to the soft music, low lighting and candles flickering everywhere.

I really should not be giving my opinion on this restaurant, since you all had better experiences than the six of us did. 

We tried this restaurant, right after they opened and we all got very sick.  We were not impressed with the food , the service ( most of the staff was outside smoking ) and no one to greet us or wait on us for awhile.

And Another One Bites the Dust!

I was never happy with Red Lobster, except for those amazing Biscuits.  I always said, "I know they microwave their food!"  Well, on a layover and change of planes in Dallas, TX.  I happened to be standing next to Executives from Red Lobster.

This coffee shop is just beautiful. I enjoy their coffee and it is super chill inside. Lots of open windows and nice tables as well as plenty of plugs. 

Bandit Coffee isn't my favorite coffee shop in town (that would be The Lab), but when I'm in St. Pete it is definitely a spot I like to stop by at.

Pacific Counter is a fairly new poke and sushi burrito shop in Downtown St. Pete. Prices are fair and will run you about $12 for a bowl or burrito. Portions are more than sufficient.

I had a Golden Hour sushi burrito which is mainly made with salmon and krab. I really enjoyed the taste of the nori (seaweed) wrapper and the vibe of the place is very Polynesian with a touch of surf.

Al's has long been one of my favorite BBQ spots in Tampa Bay. They keep it simple and delicious. I stopped in Saturday for a lunch plate with their pulled pork and mac + cheese, served with a drink for just $8. 

You really can't beat the price when some bbq places in town are charging upwards of $15 for a plate.

Noticeable is the way the meat is so tender and flavorful.
Service and food are on standards here at this McDonald's but there is nothing special about this one. The last time I was hear they messed up everything. Don't go here.

Sake House was on the ABC Action News Dirty Dining List for having over 300 roaches last year (both dead and alive) in the restaurant. It wasn't their first offense either and the state closed the restaurant for a day or two because of the critical violations. Like most places, after the incident they fired someone and then claimed they had cleaned up the mess.
$15 credit card minimum
Kay is an excellent waitress.

sample_reviews.text: Finally made it to Gurus and wow was it worth the wait. We knew we were in for a treat when we walked in to find the place packed with people. They have a full bar and wine and beer. Complimentary papadam and chutneys were brought to the table as we looked over the extensive menu of traditional dishes. We ordered the samosas chaat, chicken tikka masala, allo gobi, and malai kofta.

Wow! Awesome pierogies either hot or cold for heating at home. Very generous portions with perfect dough and filling. We ordered cold pierogies with the potato filling. I sauté onions and garlic in butter and add the pierogies.  Just amazing! Highly recommend

Really good hibachi served at hibachi tables. They also have standard seating as well. We ordered the chicken and filet mignon hibachi. It's impossible to leave hungry as the hibachi comes with noodles (lo mein style), fried rice, fried veggies, and soup and salad. The items were cooked and seasoned perfectly. Good service.

I've been here twice now and find this place to be good, not great. I like the quiet side of the dining room and enjoy being left alone to catch up with friends. The food is a little on the Olive Garden side of Italian - ordinary.

If you're looking for something different in donuts, this is a good place. The donuts themselves remind me of Hostess donuts (dense and chewy as opposed to light and airy). The toppings are what make this place unique. The one we like is the breakfast special topped with bacon and maple frosting.
Creative homemade ice cream with interesting flavors and flavor combinations.
26 years old of making and serving the best water ice in the area.

sample_reviews.text: I can't believe I don't have a review for Chatham Tap on Mass Ave until now.  I remember going here for lunch and enjoying the English Pub atmosphere even when there wasn't a soccer game on television and I wasn't having a beer.

Let me fully disclose that we met some long time friends for dinner and we had a great time visiting as well as enjoying the friendly service (great job Andrew B) and tasty food.  We had such a fun time that I am sure it puts a positive light on my rating and review.

There are a few minor things to be aware of that would make for an even better experience.

Although I don't eat here regularly, I have been dining  with this Harry and Izzy's for ten years.  I used to come over for a nice lunch when I worked on the circle and really appreciated that they have a special lunch selection that makes it easy to get in and be fed without being late back to the office.

For lunch on Saturday, my wife and I headed to Irvington to try BitterSweet.  Having lived on the east side for eight years, we were really hoping that this restaurant would be another link in the restoration of the Washington Street area.  Sadly, we left a bit disappointed.

Arriving just after noon, we found parking behind the restaurant.  We followed directions and seated ourselves.

Based upon my lunch time visit, I was expecting a fabulous dining experience with my wife and our friend.  Sadly, all our entrees were improperly flavor balanced.  Maybe it was the chef's night off.

We arrived on Thursday evening and were seated promptly.
The menu is quite detailed and it would make for a more pleasant visit if you had an idea what you want to order before getting in line.
If you order one of the large covered apples,  just ask and the staff will slice it to make it easier to eat.

While the servers tend to be at full "yat" and a bit on the rude side the seafood here is as good as it gets.  I have yet to have a bad meal here, the oysters are always amazing and the boiled seafood is the best there is!

Plan to wait unless you go at off peak times

Pillowy sugary goodness and the best cafe au lait awaits you here.  All you will find here is coffee and beignets so don't come expecting a full breakfast.  There is a reason this has been a New Orleans tradition for years.  They do it right and they are the original, nuff said!  The quality never fades and the prices are downright cheap!

They now are open for breakfast and serve up the best breakfast tacos you will ever have.. 

***pro tip that green sauce is NOT guacamole it is a VERY hot chile sauce.

Food is ok, not great or even good salsa needs help!  I remember in the past thinking it was SOO good, but then again after a few of their margies who wouldn't think it was good.  On a more "sober" lol trip with my child I realized this place is lack luster at best.

Unfortunately in a city filled with amazing Italian cuisine, this place underwhelms!  Not only its is expensive, but it's simply not as good as several of the places.  With such stiff Italian competition I dont see this place lasting

Today was my second visit here and it did not disappoint. As others have mentioned, yes, there is a wait. And yes, the service isn't as great as other places (although I'd never consider it bad service). But, the food is so amazing that I can't help but give it 5 stars.

Let me start my saying that I am super picky about coffee in a breakfast joint.

This is one of our favorite breweries in Pinellas County! They have a variety of beers available for all palates, including lagers, pilsners, pale ales, IPAs, berliners, speciality beers, etc. The ambiance of the brewery is always inviting, with wide open spaces and both indoor and outdoor seating.

My husband and I visited on Easter Sunday and absolutely loved our visit. We'd heard about the place through the grapevine, but haven't had the time to come until now. It is a great spot for social distancing, as they have a huge outdoor area that's also covered by sun sails. It is located in an unassuming part of South St. Pete.

Worst Dunkin' in the history of the world. Didn't have the bagels we asked for (only had plain). When we ordered breakfast sandwiches, they didn't  even toast the bagel itself. Asked for hash browns and didn't get those, either (luckily, they also didn't charge us for them). Only had 2 staff working as well. Might as well skip this and go to McDonald's down the street.

Not big on fast food but I was starving and on the way home. Ordered the BK veggie burger and value fries. I love BK fries and they were actually good. Staff were friendly. However, I asked for no mayo on the veggie burger and they put it on anyway. Bread was kind of limp and soggy, too. I guess you get what you pay for here.
$5 minimum on credit/debit purchases
Great place to unwind with a cigar, a beer, wine, and/or coffee! Staff are friendly as well.

This place is really great. I'd seen it a few times in passing and always wondered what the food was like, and just the place as a whole in general.

Just to give you an idea of the mood prior to going to Cork last night: It was pouring down rain, it had been a long and stressful week at work, and two people I'd known had just passed away, one of which was a tragic and unexpected accident that...

Awww yeeaa this place is so beyond fuckin' dope. It's killer amazing. Been back a few times with Seanie boy and ate at their little restaurant thingy. Alcoholics rejoice! You can choose from any of their numerous bottles of wine to imbibe without paying a corkage fee plus you don't even have to tip! My friend and I tried to once and they said they couldn't take it.

SHRIMP N GRITS. Ask for the BBQ sauce on the side if you're like me and have problems digesting barbecue sauce... 

Also as I mentioned previously, the Uncle Earl is my new favorite drink there. It may smell like rubbing alcohol, but I assure you, it tastes like sweet, sweet amazing lemon tea. Unffff.

Also the employees are great! Jason is an awesome server. :)

Came here only for the Victorian Fetish Ball. And it was kind of a joke. Firstly, they didn't even have HALF of the stuff they'd promised. And my date and I dropped a ton on VIP tickets for a measly little tiny-ass VIP area with barely enough seating for all of the so called "VIP" ticket-holders.

"Meh."

I came here a few days ago with some friends for some pre-trivia pizza. First of all, there are no servers or anything, you just have to order at the bar and they call your name when you're ready.

Everyone has to be 21+ so if you want to come here just to eat, and you're not 21 (or forgot your ID at home like one unfortunate fellow in our group), then I guess you're SOL or you gotta...
I would eat here if it was non-smoking. :'(
Join their wine club and get drunk on the good stuff.

sample_reviews.text: Living in Port Richmond you have a choice of two Pizza Huts and this one has friendlier service! This food is always hot and quick and the staff here do a great job keeping the place looking nice.

This Sonoco is above average.
They have many food options to offer including cheesesteaks, burgers, hoagies, and my personal fav breakfast sandwiches!
They have the breakfast sandwiches on sale for 2 for $3 quite often and it's the best deal around! They also have other sales like a $1 big gulp, or 2 hotdogs for $1. This place is great! I go here over the wawa next door anytine.

My husbands family hosted our Rehearsal Dinner here in June.
The food was incredible, the service was quick, and the catering room was perfect for our event!
The menu consisted of salad, chicken marsala, veal parm, pasta, and veggies. They were so accommodating!
I accidentally left behind an item I needed for day of the wedding and they set it aside for us and were so grateful for our business.

I've only been here a handful of times (usually starving after a long night of drinking) and there's a reason to go here while wasted. I admit that I'm a cheesesteak snob but their steaks are soggy, meat is barely there, you get twice the amount of wiz than you do meat.

Hate to be a dick and low rate a popular spot BUTTTT my first and last experience just wasn't great. I only went here to begin with bc it was my gfs birthday and she got to pick the dinner spot.
We had a reservation since it was dinner for her birthday but when we arrived they didn't have a table reserved for our four top. So they placed us at the long picnic like table with another couple..
Decent food, crazy wine prices. Stick with the $4 Kenzinger.
Half priced games Wed. Also, half priced mixed drinks 8-11pm. Awesome deal.

sample_reviews.text: Always a good choice. Never had a bad meal or service. This one of our regular stops. Bring your dog and enjoy the patio. Every Friday and Saturday they are open for dinner. Tonight we are having the mussels in white wine.

So, it has been over 5 years since I have been here. Use to be a hang out before I retired. Any way, food was very good. I don't think that the prices Havel changed very much in the last 5 years. I ordered the chicken teriyaki with sashimi and the wife ordered shrimp tempura with California roll. Both were boxes. Ice tea all around.

First time here. I believe that they have only been open for a few months. This is the same as Opa's bets on Broadway. Great Greek food. So we were seated immediately and I had my doubts. The place was empty and it was 12:30 when we arrived. The menu is the same as the one on Broadway. We both ordered iced tea and the usual plate of hummus, pita bread and veggies was served.

So thought I would give Lonchos/Tio Wills another try. Bad decision. Ordered two shredded beef tacos. Go home with them and they are 90% potato's and 10% beef. Far cry from a shredded beef taco. Don't know which I am more upset about, the terrible quality of food or the fact that they lie about being Tio Wills just a new name to keep people confused. This is a COVID rich establishment.

You would think I would learn my lesson. But, I always hope they can change and fix things. Guess not. Went to this Wendy's for lunch and as usual, they got it wrong. Ordered a single with cheese, small fry, small chili with cheese and onions on the side a large root beer and extra hot sauce for the chili. Well, no fries, no cheese and onions and no hot sauce for the chili.
One of my go to places. Always good food and service. Stop by and say hi.

sample_reviews.text: Pretty cool NOLA themed bar that's deceptively large (there are two sections). We sat at the bar, watching an old movie on a vintage TV while we sipped on our beers (good selection), and ate our fried chicken po boy (not the greatest, but the debris is a must). The place is pretty dark, but the music is great and the crowds are chill.

Nice little piano bar/restaurant. They were unable to seat us for dinner, so we just posted up at the bar (where you can order food anyway) and eventually got enough seats for our group.

Their menu selection is a step up from what I would've expected. A lot of Asian-inspired dishes, and some nicer stuff. I got the duck confit crostini, and the grilled lamb. It was all pretty good.

Every time I go to New Orleans, I come back with a new favorite restaurant. Compere Lapin was just perfect in every way. I was able to grab a seat at the bar during Sunday brunch with no issues. Had a much-needed CL spritz to start off my day right.

The first thing I had was the smoked hamachi everything donut. The description was just too much to not order.

Mike's is a place you can walk by twice a day for a week and always pass over. It's easy to ignore, but they actually have delicious ice cream. The flavors aren't too crazy for the most part, but what they make, they do it well. Tried all sorts of things. My favorite was the Almond Joy, which basically tasted like the candy.

It's a surf bar, through and through. From the color schemes, to the shark and surfboard paraphernalia everywhere, to the summery drinks, to the domestic buckets, to the bartenders who would fit in at a more tropical locale.

We stopped by as we had some time to kill before lunch. Bartender was awesome and fun to talk to.

I had their frozen lemonade, which wasn't that great.
WiFi password: StRoch123
There's a Banksy across the street! Umbrella Girl

sample_reviews.text: I recently hosted a Ramadan dinner and had the catering done by Sansom.  It was SPECTACULAR.  Everyone raved about the chicken kabob and ground beef kabob.  In addition to kabob, we had lentil soup (amazing) and lamb and chicken qorma.  The owner was kind enough to throw in an eggplant dip (I forget the name) which I could have eaten by the spoon.

This is the BEST frozen yogurt in the Willow Grove area.  Their flavors are cool and creamy and don't have that "tart yogurt" taste that most other FroYos have.  

Their selection of toppings is another plus.  They have everything you could want.

I tried a heavenly mixture of Peanut Butter, Banana, and Chocolate with a little hot fudge sauce on top and I will be going back for more.

I am never disappointed when I come here.  The food is always consistent and the staff is always on point.

Hands down, the best brunch in Jenkintown.

Kuzina was pretty good Greek food, however, not the best I have ever had. 

We began with a plate of sampler dips which included Tatziki Sauce-(Great), Hummus-(gross), Olives, Grape leaves (very good), and an eggplant dip (outstanding). 

We also ordered a hot appetizer which was phylo dough and cheese (like spankopita sans spinach).

I had been looking forward to trying this place for the longest time, and I have to say, I am a little disappointed. Although, everything is fresh and organic, and the service is excellent, the food lacks flavor.

I had a sip of my son's groothie, which was okay, but a little too sweet.

sample_reviews.text: This offers a great space to gather with friends for a lowkey outing or for someone such as myself who needs to study. They have a great atmosphere with great music, customer service, and products being served.

For $20, you get homemade pasta by two "From ITALY" chefs who know the art of pasta. I love this place! The $20 pasta is usually their pasta of the day and it is da bomb! 

The atmosphere screams DATE NIGHT.  Whether if this is a first date or simply a night out with your honey of 2,000 years, PennePazze does not disappoint.

What have I been missing! I have never been to a Slim & Huskey's in the 2.5 years that I have lived here. Now, that I have had my fix, I am more than thrilled that I get to write the first review at the 5th & Broadway. Might I mention this is also the first black owned establishment on lower Broadway. It has made history along with great pizza!

Slim and Huskey's reminds me of &Pizza in DC.

Disgusting. Nasty. Gross. No good. Messes up your stomach and causes your to have chills, diarrhea, vomiting, and fatigue along with a fever. I was out of it from Friday to Wednesday. This place needs to close all locations and be done with it. 

I had the boneless buffalo chicken wings with ranch. Whatever it was that caused this was certainly not cooked or something was spoiled.

I'm on a quest to find the best Chinese take out in this city, and well...this ain't it.

It had a few people in the place for a Tuesday afternoon at 2:30pm. One cook, one cashier taking orders. To kill time while everyone else ordered in front of me, I went to the bathroom to wash my hands. Pretty sure my hands were cleaner before entering that bathroom. When I walked back down the hall...
They have weird hours. Call ahead. 1 woman runs the show.

sample_reviews.text: The food here is not what the description "Native American comfort food" lead me to expect.  I was thinking frybread and mutton stew, but that's not what you'll find.  Instead, the menu is mostly a gourmet take on traditional American diner fare with a lot of flavors borrowed from pre-Colombian Mexico.

One of the best places in Tucson.  It's often crowded, always with locals who obviously know where to find the best food in the neighborhood.  This is the type of place where women line up on Sunday mornings with empty pots to get menudo for the family.

Piping hot frybread is a guilty pleasure of mine that has always been reserved for the rare trips through one of the local reservations.  Until now, that is.  Now, the Barrio has its very own indoor frybread restaurant, and oxymorons aside, it's a welcome addition to the neighborhood.
Generally good food but inconsistent quality.  Some of the dishes were excellent but two people in our group got carne seca chimis and they were both inedibly dry and greasy.

After going back I have to say the quality is still very mixed--some dishes are okay, some poor.  Given that the place is relatively pricey for Mexican food and that this is Tucson (home of some of the best Mexican food on earth), you're better off going somewhere else.  (Try Mi Nidito if you haven't already).

sample_reviews.text:
What a lovely addition to the Tchoupitoulas Brewery row! It is located right off Tchoup. They have free off street parking and a huge outdoor sitting area with games like corn hole. 

Where you order your beer is covered and filled with friendly bar tenders eager to serve. The menu has only a handful of beers but I was not disappointed.

We got dat! They certainly do! Especially when it comes to booze. I come here for my booze runs because they are always the ones that have the hard to find stuff. They were the first to have bumbu rum, which is the best rum in the world, it even tastes like bananas! And they have reasonable pricing and sales on their liquor.

Food and service were great! I need to come back here and drink; seems like a fun place for dinner. I really wanted to order the all you can eat boiled seafood, but at the last minute decided to get fried shrimp instead. I was very pleased with all the seafood that was served. Our waitress was so sweet and -- side note -- she had the most beautiful eyes.

As you know, I'm a boudin snob ad I did not think these balls were any good. They had barely any batter on them and they were small. My ideal and perfect ball would be from Billys in Scott La. They are huge with an inch thick of batter. The taste with these balls wasn't there. Tasted like flavorless meat. Maybe I should have gone with a burger.

I went here recently for COOLinary at lunch. We were the first ones there and ate in the event space I did not know they had. The first time I came here was for dinner back in March about a month after a food critic gave this place raving reviews and we were turned away because they had too many people even though there was a two seater table available and open..... but back to lunch...

Boyfriend and I had a great meal here yesterday! My boyfriend told me he's sick of the "hipster crap" I force him to eat, so he picked this joint. The menu appealed to him since it is very straight forward and predictable, but still interesting. 
The vibe was cooler than I expected. It seemed more like a cool bar than a steak house, which I appreciated.

Boiling House is my new hot spot! I love seafood and this place kicks ass. I also love create your own anything and the CYO seafood boil here was awesome. 
I got snow crabs and shrimp in the Boiling House sauce with medium heat. Oh, and the garlic noodles which are a must by the way. Everything was fresh and fabulous.

Polka Deli is just one of many reasons I love living in Port Richmond. The Polish influence is real on the mean streets of the Port and it's because of fabulous places like Polka Deli. While most of their clientele seems to be Polish natives, they are very kind and hospitable to us English speaking third generationers.

I really wish I could write a review about how awesome the food is here, but unfortunately I can't! I went the other night on my break from work to check out the honey bar. The honey bar, where they mix fruit, honey, and fun toppings like chocolate chips or coconut shavings, sounds like an awesome healthy snack to enjoy while working.

So sick of this place. I've given them so many chances but something is screwed up literally every time I order. I am currently the owner of a dry ass pile of noodles, since honeygrow forgot my sauce! Wtf does one do with a pile of sticky dry noodles? They are inedible. I'm so pissed!!

In my opinion the novelty is going to wear off pretty soon and the long wait times/ridiculous credit card fee/sometimes answering the phone sometimes not is going to get old and people aren't going...
They have pineapples and apples for juices just not for açaí bowls. Not sure I comprehend.

sample_reviews.text: I'm not usually one to update my reviews very often because I try my best to get it "write" the first time. 2021 Reno Food & Drink Week last business for this years event....  Empienada had a chosen special.  But we didn't get it, because my wife just wanted what she wanted!  Instead of the Mediterranean pot pie special we loaded up for a few meals.

sample_reviews.text: We showed up to this little mom and pop restaurant and the place was empty.  However, people quickly flooded in after us, taking up almost every other table. This is the beginning of my experience with Cambodian cuisine, but it won't be my last.  We ordered three dishes and shared between the three of us.  Everything is served family style, plates come out as they are ready.

This place is awesome.  Low key, casual, and exactly the right vibe that I was looking for.  Live music while you are eating good food and drinking good wine is perfect.  This is what I want my back yard to be.  We ended up with more bottles of wine than we should have and really enjoyed ourselves. 

The mussels were very tasty and well seasoned.

I can't believe this place has been open for two years and I didn't know about it. They are making terrific, interesting beer here. Several strong beers, and most of them don't taste as strong as they are, something which seems to be difficult for most breweries to accomplish. 
There seems to be a lot of experimentation here. The coconut beer was one of my favorite.

Very overrated BYOB.  I am pretty shocked about how high the reviews are for this place.  The food was just simply not good. I tried almost ever one or their tacos (which others have noted, are small), and all of them were dry, even the chorizo was dry which I didn't think was possible.

I was never that big of a fan of Harper's, but it seems to have gone even further downhill after the change of ownership/management/whatever.  It is a pretty cheap bar, and there are pretty much always specials going on for beer.  It's a bit of a dive, and not in a good way.
Black lentil soup with pork is awesome!
Stop having a DJ. It's almost like they hire the DJ because the wait staff wants to go home. The DJ drives everyone out and people can go home early.

What a fantastic brunch. I have nothing but positives to say about my experience at Suraya. The environment is open and bright. 
The service was fantastic. 
My favorite thing I ordered was the Lebanese Chai tea. Smelled like a bath bomb!! Tasted amazing.
The baba ganoush was perfect. The pomegranate added the perfect crunch and sweetness. I enjoyed the traditional hummus as well.

I will never eat Northeast Philly sushi ever again. It doesn't even compare to Tuna Bar. I sat at the bar and tried the Rittenhouse Roll. Spicy tuna, cucumber, avocado, kombu soy wrap, lobster tempura, and a truffle masago sauce. It was absolutely delectable. This is the holy grail of sushi. My mom ordered a happy hour saki which is graciously overpoured. Her spider roll was also to die for.

I've been on the hunt for good sushi without having to schlep in the city. Let me tell you, this is not good sushi. It is great sushi.
It is fresh, and the takeout menu is pretty vast and eclectic with something for everybody. 
I ordered a salmon skin roll, spicy tuna, James Bond roll, and a garden salad with ginger dressing. Everything was fresh and spectacular.

Good pasta. Poor management. 
We sat at the bar. The bar staff was attentive. 
I ordered the black spaghetti which was delicious. A little overpriced, but really good nonetheless.  
Here is my gripe. 
I saw shaved truffle on the menu. It has been a dream of mine to try truffle. I've had truffle oil. But never the real thing.

Just an average bagel place. My breakfast sandwich hit the spot (bacon egg and cheese on a blueberry bagel), but my boyfriend's hash brown was mushy. Service was average and the line was confusing for someone who has never been here before. The line starts to the right of the counter. You order, you then make your way around to the left to pay.... if the staff ever takes your payment.
They do have cold pressed juices, despite what one of the reviews says. They are located in the fridge to the left of where you order.

sample_reviews.text: Ordered to go for lunch and got a potato skins. Potato skins tasted fine, everyone else's food was good as well. Everything was cooked fine. It was nothing spectacular but it did the job.

The food here is excellent. We had this as a to go order on Friday and we will definitely be coming here again. The portions are very large and they tasted good. The price was reasonable as well. We had the Hill Special (a sandwich with salami, ham, turkey, cheese, lettuce, tomato, and onion on french bread) and it was 7.99 but I had half the sandwich left and a handful of chips.

Great customer service, they were very friendly from the moment I walked in. Quick to go order. I had the Alfredo which was really good with extra mushrooms and the Parmesan chicken. Had plenty of meat and veggies and the small was enough to fill me up before work. My only disappointment is with the chocolate chunk cookie, I didn't realize it was oatmeal.

This place is always a zoo, the parking lot is shared with a walmart and not enough spots. Never enough cashiers. The bakery always loses cake orders or messes up the cakes. And if they don't do one of those things its never done by the requested pick up time.
I had the potato skins to go and they were awful. They must have been old or over cooked. They were hard and dry and a lot more cheese than potato and some hard crusty bacon.
The prosciutto is very delicious. Some of the best I've had.

This is no ordinary sandwich shop. Freshly baked soft but firm bread. Fun bomb sauces and garlicky dressing. Good amount of ingredients within sandwich.

I got my sub to-go and I could feel the hefty weight of the amount of ingredients.

Extra plus, it maintains its shape even when kept in fridge to be eaten later.

Lots of choices of toppings/ingredients.

4.5 stars for this chain Italian restaurant. Sign up to be on their email list and they have many generous take-out and dine-in offers.

Large, clean restaurant making Covid standards easy to adhere to. The servers, though, need to walk far for their drinks and plate pick ups.

Large square rosemary bread given with EVOO. 

Got the chicken parmesan and it was thick and huge.

Quite possibly the most cleanest lounge/bar during this Covid era. Bar tops meticulously disinfected, staff wear masks with gloves and patrons mask wearing is enforced. A+!

I have challenged bartenders Vanessa, Tia and Kagen to make me certain types of drinks. True craft conjurers.

Came in for the Sunday Mexican buffet complete with a couple of mariachi singers.

It has all the usual suspects, salad bar, pizza, pastas, 10 mexican dishes, Asian area and desserts. The MEAT selection was overwhelming in a good way. Ribs, chix, sausages, big meats and more big meats that I couldn't even attempt to try. The brisket and ribs are beautifully blackened with a flavorful crust.

Beautifully redone with a capital B ! Feel like I am in a lodge. So clean. Easy parking and entering from the streets. Enthusiastic and professional staff.

The rotisserie chicken was plump and juicy with an exceptional smokey crispy skin.
Yummy and hefty sub sandwiches custom made in the deli by a very friendly lady!
$10 for 4 taster sizes of happiness!

sample_reviews.text: I've tried multiple places near Indy in search of good, fresh korean food and I've finally found it! They just have a few staples as they are primarily salad driven but if I can get kimbap and bulgogi when the mood strikes, I am one happy girl.  Employees are friendly, place is clean, ingredients fresh, and lots of options for those who come for the non-korean part of the menu.

Delicious street tacos from a small shack with no seating? Yes, please! The tacos are fresh, cheap, and quick. There can be a line depending on what time you get there but it's worth it. They also have some interesting choices of meat if you're feeling adventurous.
Solid food in a calm, generic atmosphere.  It's a place I would come with my parents or if interviewing someone.

Nice Decor, reasonably priced, convenient location, nice interior. Needs bigger TV. Girl at bar was very friendly and greeted us when we came in. Our waitress, however, was pushy and abrupt. Needs to be more patient with first time customers who have questions about the menu.

Easy place to meet up.  Ample street parking. Nice atmosphere. Open, modern warehouse-y feel. Lots of choices for drinks. Decent sized patio for those who prefer to dine outdoors. We typically go for $2 taco tuesdays--used to be a variety of meats (their chicken was good) but now it's just beef.

sample_reviews.text:
Craft & Curd is a locally-owned wine store that can be found in a strip plaza along Gandy Boulevard. It is truly one of the hidden gems in South Tampa that continually shines. 

There is a wide variety of wine and sparkling sold here. There is also a limited selection of beer, cheese, and charcuterie that is also for sale.

I always save my 100th reviews for my most favorite places and #400 is LONG OVERDUE, especially since we have dined at Pacific Counter SIX TIMES since they opened their doors in early September. PC is hands down the BEST POKE PLACE in the Tampa Bay Area. Their bowls and burritos are reasonably priced, and for ONLY $12 the portions are an excellent value.

Congrats to Grain & Berry for opening up their 13th location in DTSP! I couldn't be happier because I will pass this location several times during the week/weekend!

We stopped by during their Grand Opening Weekend and were greeted right away by one of the owners (who actually has been a friend of mine for over 25 years LOL).

After several u-turns, I finally managed to find this place. Parking is limited, so I was glad to get there early to meet my coworker for lunch last week.

The restaurant is very small, clean and fills up quickly. The staff was very friendly and the service was very good. The portions of food are enormous. Great value for the price. 

With that said, I found the food to be bland.

This is your typical McDonald's, so there really isn't too much that can be said.  This one is open 24 hours and is good for a late night snack.

I was coming home from an event held in St. Pete and needed a quick soda. The wait for the drive-thru took longer than usual. But, this is to be expected late on a weekend night. 

Not a huge fan of this drive-thru. The setup is strange.
Get there early! Baked goods normally sell out in the morning, especially on the weekends!
Will only seat you if your party is together.

sample_reviews.text: Just had the fake meatball pizza for the first time...it's amazing....vegetarians and non-veggies alike love this thing....get it, love it, share it, take the extras home...trust me, you will have extras...

Came here for a girls night out, and I have to say I find this wine bar absolutely perfect.  The ambiance is great, the service is stellar, the selection is vast and the quality of the food is excellent.  Mix that with the totally fair prices, and you have a hell of a little wine bar nestled into an often forgotten section of South City.

I really like this place. Popped in after a long day of shopping on steamy main street. They had awesome salads (I suggest the Greek salad) and they even had fresh cut watermelon instead of chips if you were so inclined. Staff was friendly good amount of choices on the menu and totally reasonable prices. Would definitely recommend.

Yea...it gets a big...well....meh from me.  It's not that the sandwich is inedible...it's not...you can totally eat the thing.  But....it's just....not that good...at all...I mean, I could make a better sandwich with wonder bread at home.  Altogether unimpressed.

If you are looking for a great place to get cafeteria style...well...cafeteria food, look no further.  That is exactly what this place is: rows upon rows of jello, green beans and corn in small bowls, fried chicken and ham hanging out on plates under heating lamps and of course, the rows of pie hanging out at the end.

sample_reviews.text: We were here for happy hour. Service was very quick. From 3pm to 6pm, drinks are half off and oysters are $.50. The oysters were nice, plump and yummy. Luke's spicy cheesy grits were my favorite in NOLA. Drinks are half off but would otherwise be a bit pricy. We also tried Blind Pelican who has $.25 oysters but I wish I stayed here because Blind Pelican was so slow.

This is one of my favorite restaurants in the city. As for Mediterranean I am torn between Dimitri's and Kanella.  

I've been to both Dimitri locations and really like both a lot, maybe too much. I've enjoyed everything I've ever had there which is probably more than half the menu at this point.

This is a solid family restaurant. I hadn't been to an Old Country in over a decade but my 4 year old son kept raving about it after going with his dad and grandparents so the two of us had a mommy son date on a Thursday afternoon. There were only a few tables of guests and they had us spaced out so the customers were spread out.

I am losing faith in this store. I have been going to Rite Aid for years for my prescriptions. On the evening of December 12, 2013, I dropped off an underwater disposable camera for processing. My boyfriend and I purchased an overpriced underwater disposable camera while on vacation in Belize and used it to take photos of the coral reefs while we snorkeled and spear fished.

I was here on Saturday night with a large group. They put us in the back room next to the kitchen which is a good room for 10 to 20 people.

Our waitress quickly brought us a pitcher of tequila mix. I wasn't planning on drinking the tequila mix and asked if she can bring water for everyone.
Best hot chocolate I've ever had. Worth the price. What a fun place!
Best tacos!!!

We ordered two staples we usually order at Vietnamese restaurants. The Pho with beef brisket and vermicelli noodles with spring rolls.

Suraya was one of the most anticipated restaurants to open in Philadelphia in 2017. I had read about it, and I love Cafe La Maude and was really excited to get there. I really don't know why I waited so long but after a few months of not getting to Suraya, we finally ceased an opportunity on a Sunday afternoon in May for our first visit.

Suraya is fairly large space and it is well designed and...

This week we finally were able to get into the Hearthside BYOB. Reservations are extremely hard to get (usually a month or more in advance) and we had our table of four lined up for quite some time.

There seems to be a few restaurants outside of Philadelphia and in South Jersey that are building great reputations.

We finally made it to the Village Whiskey and it was on my holy grail quest for the best hamburger ever. We went on Memorial Day evening thinking the city would be empty and there would be a short wait. No such luck and at 8:00 the wait was 45 minutes. We decided to wait it out as it was a really nice night and we walked around.

This is another time I wish Yelp had 1/2 stars. If Yelp had this feature, my review would be 3.5 stars.

We went to Han Dynasty based on Yelp reviews and also the suggestion of my buddy Superfly T. I had long heard the glowing reviews of Han Dynasty in Old City and Manayunk. Did Han Dynasty Chery Hill live up to the hype?

sample_reviews.text: I've been to a number of Target stores but I was impressed with this one. On a busy Black Friday, there were Target employees walking around the store asking customers if they needed help finding anything. Not just that though. Some of the best sale items of the weekend were available in sufficient quantity and displayed at the front of the store as I entered.

Yeah, I know what you're thinking. One Wawa store is pretty much the same as another, so why even bother writing a review? But I think we have a winner here.

When this Wawa store opened for business, gas prices at gas stations all along Concord Pike fell by 30 cents per gallon just to compete with them.

Shanghai Express is in the Meeting House Square shopping center in Marlton. Parking was abundant on a Saturday evening, which may not be the busiest time here. I tried the General Tso's Chicken combination and I thought it was done pretty well. Certainly above average for South Jersey.

Where do I begin? The very first thing I noticed as I walked in the door was an obviously drunk man hanging out by the order counter. He reeked of alcohol and kept harassing the staff, poking fun at customers, and butting into conversations. I was absolutely amazed that McDonald's didn't see fit to throw him out.

I revisited Buffalo Wild Wings yesterday evening. Last time, I noted that I wasn't sure if parking was always adequate. This time, I knew it was definitely inadequate. The parking lot was full. Good thing there is an underground garage with a few more parking space accessible from the side of the building. The dining area is even noisier than it was the last time.

sample_reviews.text:
While on vacation, I decided to check out a few new spots this week. Today I headed to Alma Llanera Venezuelan Food in Pinellas Park. I was told this place was a stand out and it definitely was today.

I studied the menu before I arrived so I knew exactly what I wanted.

Yesterday, we decided to check out Cru Cellars in south Tampa for brunch. I had heard the brunch menu was impressive and it did not disappoint. 

We made reservations at 11 and the restaurant filled up fast after they opened. They offered indoor & outdoor seating. We grabbed a table outside on the back patio. We started with a mix of coffee, mimosas, and wine.

On Saturday morning, we decided to check out Bandit Coffee on Central in St Pete for breakfast. Their rotating menu always looks good, and we decided we could not pass up the chicken biscuit. We ordered online and our food was ready for us about 15 minutes later. The place it in a bag, at a table, with your name on it outside for pickup.

We decided to check out Yeoman's Cask & Lion (St. Petersburg) last night for the first time with a group of 8. Sadly, it didn't leave a good first impression. I did not take any pictures from our visit as I was frustrated and we had a large party.

We walked in and we were seated right away. The restaurant was at about 1/4 of the capacity.

I wish their were half stars, as I would rate this place a 3.5 stars. 

The first thing to note about this place is the decor outside. It looks like a Mexican shack, but once you walk in, it is wonderfully decorated. Just a note, if the front parking lot is full, they have a lot in back. Once inside, we were seated immediately. They seated us at the communal high top tables in the bar area.
Great beer garden!
Great place to take home a roti. Only one table in the place.

The chaat here is full of crispy bits, as well as bits of fresh vegetables (radish, onion), nuts, and sauces or...

I wouldn't really choose to come here again, because I prefer flavourful food that tastes fresh but I also wouldn't put up too much of a fight if I was nearby and...

sample_reviews.text:
As a lover of Always Sunny, I was clearly obligated to come here. I often hit this place first whenever I go to Old City. It's a cool bar that always has a good crowd. They have a great craft beer list and the prices are fair. 

My two complaints are that the bar is pretty small so it gets very crowded very quickly, and the bartenders can sometimes have an attitude.

I am obsessed with this place. The guys who work there almost know me by name, which is kind of embarrassing. I always thought I hated indian food so I went here with a co-worker to give it another try. It's probably super americanized, but I don't care. This stuff is amazing. For $5/lb you can mix and match whatever you want from 5 daily rotating items.

Amazing new vegan option on the Philly scene! Their menu was full of comfort food favorites and I could not decide what to order. I went with the cheesesteak with Calabrian chili whiz (what!?) with mushrooms and onions and fries with beet ketchup/chipotle mayo for dipping. The seitan is some of the best I've tried texture-wise, and the whiz was super flavorful with some heat.

Just ok. Went for 2/3 roll special and ended up getting the 3 rolls for $10.25. I figured I could save some for later but they turned out to be pretty small. I also requested brown rice for which they told me they had to charge me .50 extra per roll. I hate it when places do this, rice is rice, and the extra charge is just more annoying to the customers than it's worth in my opinion.

I was very disappointed in Ants Pants. From reading their menu they look really good, and they are relatively close to where I live so I was excited to try them. We ended up waiting 30 minutes to be seated on a sunday afternoon, but I guess that it to be expected. Especially since the inside of this place is TINY. Probably about 5 tables and some counter space.
3 rolls for $11!
Get the tasting menu! Worth it!
