sample_reviews.text: Let's just say I had very high expectations when I read that this place was named Ben's BBQ & Beer and was not disappointed. Living in Bensalem there are a ton of chain restaurants in the area, so it is hard to find a good mom & pop place, but even harder to find a good BBQ place.

I have come to Mango's twice after going to the Paws farm next door. Basically Paws is for my kid and Mango's is for me! The Peruvian flavor is great and something I really miss from living in North Jersey. Besides great authentic Peruvian dishes, they also make other Latin dishes with great flavor. 

Our favorite plates to order are the roasted chicken, the roasted pork, and lomo salteado.

I am a HUGE fan of Metro Diner and was so sad that it opened up in Bensalem JUST as we decided to move north. We would travel to Bensalem to have the delicious chicken and waffles every so often, but with a newborn it was difficult to get out.

Yes, I get Chinese food is not meant to be mind blowing, but this is below mediocre. I came here for lunch and ordered General Tso Chicken that swam in a puddle of oil in my styrofoam plate. If I am eating artery clogging food, I don't want to see the oil ooze out of it.

Having been to many mojito bars in the South America, Miami & NYC metro area - this was a watered down version of the decent ones.

Here's the good: the decor is really cute, you can bring your dog in, they have a band playing and the Mojitos don't have too much mint, that way your not picking it out of your teeth the rest of the day.

The not so good: we ordered food and it was served on a tiny...
Surprisingly Great Chinese food.
Cash only, bring at least $20.

Edley's was on my list of places to try whenever we ventured back down to Nashville and on this most recent trip we finally stopped in to see what the locals were hyped up about.

This location is in the 12 South area, which is a fun little neighborhood with shopping, eating, outdoor areas, etc.  How we've never stopped here before I do not know!

When hungry in Clayton...stop at Sauce on the Side!

Sauce on the Side are calzones fit for a king.  They have so many great choices and if you don't like one that they've designed, you can design your own.  The sauce comes on the side to dip your calzone in.

Company's Christmas dinner was held at Firebirds and it was a great time.

We had called ahead for a table of 10 which they were accommodating for.  Our waitress was amazing, shared all the specials and was ready to suggest whatever we might be asking of.  She kept all the drinks flowing and made sure our refills were never empty.

The menu is plentiful.

Quality and food selection = 5 stars
Customer service = 0 stars

A quick stop here after a Blues game for a munchie turned into one of the rudest customer services I've ever experienced.

I stopped in their bathroom first and was disappointed to see that there was not a hook to hang my purse.

My first visit to Friendly's wasn't so friendly.

Came on a Friday night, which happened to be a Blues game night.  No biggie, found parking and figured we'd have to wait a bit for a table.  Found the dining room and someone told us a table was about to clear up, they'd clean it and it be ours.

Nutshell:  Delicious pizzas, amazing prices.  This is not fast food, but definitely worth the wait.

It was a Thursday evening and I was in the mood for pizza, but something different.  A well time news article about the best pizza joints in STL alerted me to this place's existence and I was all in.  When I got there, there was a group of folks in the back area, the employee, and me.

As good as downtown without the downtown parking fiasco.  Love Sauce.  I always get the Duke, because BBQ sauce/chicken/bacon/mozzarella is never a bad combo.  Its not the cheapest, but you walk out needing a wheelbarrow (in a good way, of course!). I've brought bosses, friends, dates here...not at the same time...that'd be awkward.

Nutshell:  its ALLLL good.  All of it.

Stopped in on a Monday around 7pm and the place was pretty full.  The bartender was friendly and attentive.  The bar seemed well stocked, not a drinker so take that as you will.  Now the food...THE FOOD was fantastic!  I ordered the Pear Gorgonzola Melt.  How they fit all that amazingness on one plate is impressive.

Nutshell: fairly priced sandwich shop, flavor is lacking though.  Wouldn't really recommend it unless you're in a rush.

My friend raved about the Earl of Sandwich shop in Orlando, so when I found one in the airport I was like "sweet!". Not sweet...like most airport restaurants the food here is boring with thawed out ingredients.  I went for the Jerk Chicken on wheat.

Nutshell:  its all about the sauce, straight forward menu, good price, food is a bit lackluster.

Came in for dinner after work, about 5:30ish.  Apart from the employees, I was the only soul in the place for a good 10 minutes.  Looks like a lot of folks do pick up orders, which is good to know.  I wanted to dabble, so I got 3 strips and fries.
This is no longer Pita Pit.  Its called Pita & More.

sample_reviews.text: Honestly, one of the best meals I've had in a long time. I ordered the Lamb Burger and I didn't like the way it was cooked. I brought it back up and they were just so nice about it. Most places give you attitude and sass you if you send food back. Not only was my sandwich perfect once I got it back, the cook gave me new fries, and an extra container of Tzatziki Sauce.

My friend and I stopped in before going to Mini Donut Factory. We absolutely loved this place! My friend ordered a special flavor, for the life of me, I can't remember the name. I ordered the Hawaiian Salted Caramel Latte and it has got to be one of the most amazing drinks I've had ever. Goodbye Starbucks! Parking was a breeze and the coffee shop is all around cool shops and restaurants.

Such an awesome little place with amazing food and coffee. The Caprese Sandwich was heavenly and the baklava was ON POINT. I am going to be craving that sandwich all of the time and I've talked my husband into going back this weekend to get another one. I also got a Maple Cinnamon Latte. Yum, yum, yum! The parking is tight, but the parking garage is a 5 minute walk away.

This place is super cute. I ordered a Lavender Flat White and a piece of the Gruyere and Spinach Quiche, which was amazing. I also ordered the Maple Banana Walnut Donut which was even better than the quiche. Overall, this coffee shop is super cozy and very unique. It is well worth a visit if you are crazy about coffee.

I drove out here special, two days in a row, just to check this place out. The building is beautiful and is a short walk from an accessible parking lot. The shop is super relaxing and has a completely different style than any place I've ever been. I ordered the Maple Spice Cappuccino and a Candy Apple Macaron. If I get the chance to come back out again, I'd have to give the coffee flight a try.
Closed
Times are not accurate. Call before you make the drive.

sample_reviews.text: I still feel this is Philadelphia's finest bean and that the Baristas are well-trained. This location started selling sandwiches a couple months ago. A Veggie sandwich - , Ham and cheese and Chicken Salad on Ciabatta roll for $7.00. I also think this is my favorite La Colombe location. it is the original location and I have been coming here for as long as I have been in Philly (13 years).

I was hungry for Asian noodles or Sushi and forgot about this restaurant. It is a hidden gem, next to subway sandwich shop, below. There is a small bar in front and the restaurant has around 8 booths. They do have a good selection for Vegetarians and will accommodate dietary needs or level of spiciness. They have a lunch special of dim sum and soup selection and noodles/rice dishes.

I have walked by several times and today I decided to try this place. Mac n Cheese was one of my favorite dishes as a child. They have 5 Vegetarian options and 12 with meats. Plus a "think outside the bowl". I eat  vegetarian and tried The Rittenhouse. Classic mac topped with cheese, garlic sauteed spinach and artichoke dip topped with potato chip panko crunch.
fyi- this place is closed!!! my friend and i tried to go here today but it's all boared up :(

My friend and I needed a place to eat before we attended the Bryn Mawr Film Institutes Sing-a-long Wizard of Oz, so, we saw the menu posted outside and decided to give it a whirl. 

Now, it was crowded with local folks having dinner and happy hour, but, we were served very quickly. the menu seems reasonable, with Wraps,Hot Sandwiches,Panini's, Irish traditional fare and Pastas.
Still delicious. Good portions. Plenty of vegetarian options. Pretty quick during lunch crowd too.
This is Middle Eastern food they have tahini, not tzatziki sauce.

Sorry, Tsaocaa, although you are not my favorite milk tea place (too watery for my taste), you do win when it comes to fruit tea in the city.

bill: $70 for 4 unsatisfied people.

sample_reviews.text: Sometimes you just get blown away. This is one of those occasions. Stoped in Boyertown on the way home. They were having a community festival. We were hungry. Live music. Train rides, food, crafts. But as I was choosing between many options there was this smell. Hotdogs, burgers, onions. There wasn't even a real sign. I actually walked past. But I went back and boy was I glad .

I think it's just wonderful. Small very limited menu. So we had a chance to try almost everything. The breakfast  sandwich is a fratatta on a brioche bun. I had mine with bacon. The eggs were amazing! Coffee was super solid and they have the best scones ever. ( Lemon / blueberry)
And to top it all off the serve Romano's Real Stromboli's to go. A happy Super Bowl coincidence!!

It's literally the last great donut shop. Cake , yeast , fritters. All crazy good. You can have your "Duck Donuts" or Founding Farmers., and DD they all pale in comparison. I've been going for over 39 years. I'm not sure how long they've been around. They open super early. Like 5:30. They run out early ., especially on Sundays. Forget it if you are not there by 11:00. Old school classic.

The food is not that good but what kills me are the servers standing in the alley smoking. Not just once in a while but all the time. Does the management really think this is ok. Buffet at lunch is tired food sitting out maybe you have to arrive early. Had two very bad dinners. Soggy over sauced pizza and poorly made salads. I really wanted to like it but.....

Wow, all these great reviews and now mine. I feel bad writing this . The gumbo sucked. Really. I threw it away after two bites. Lots of shrimp and andouille . Bitter rue. No peppers no celery no okra no onion . I was so disappointed that I made a batch myself today . It wasn't that tough to make right. On top of it all $14 for a quart . Doesn't anyone who eats here know what gumbo is?
Amazing Vietnamese food
Super empty.   Times are changing

sample_reviews.text: Came here for trivia Wednesday night, nice mix of categories and not too busy. The guest brewery doppelbock was a smooth, easy drinking beer and a good complement to the delicious tamales. The stout was strong, not too bitter at the end. Trivia ended around 930, not too late, it's also nice that they are pretty serious about you not having your phone out.

Everything a five star restaurant should be! From the second we arrived, we were greeted and welcomed. They are using what used to be the bar area of Element as the primary restaurant. Our server, Bobby, was personable, had great recommendations, and very detailed descriptions of the dishes. Not a single question he couldn't answer.
Great service and even a low caffeine option. I tried the cinnamon swirl, made with oat milk, cinnamon syrup and a splash of espresso.  Delicious, light, delicately sweet and perfect for winter.

Don't know what was up today. Went in, walked up to the counter and was stared down by the young girl at the counter and not greeted at all. I told her, I think I want a brownie a la mode, but I wanted it to go. I picked out a brownie and was about to buy it.  I asked her a couple of questions, and she seemed pained to answer them. 
I asked her to warm it up and put the ice cream on it.

I love this place, but I am SO tired of all this wishy-washing... I called at 3 to ask what there hours were. I had previously stopped by a Sunday morning, super excited to eat their delicious brunch only to discover they were apparently closed.  So, remembering that I figured I'd better check if this place even existed anymore.
They are moving to the closed down shop n save at Gravois Plaza March 13, so this location will be closed from March 12 going forward.
Aldi's has sushi and frozen cauli rice, they are really stepping it up.

sample_reviews.text: Tight menu. Happy hour had around $1-3 knocked off of most entrees and small bites. The portions were kind of tiny, but they were extremely flavorful. Had the brussels sprouts, blue bay mussels, and pizza. Each of these are solid, and honestly we left pretty full and satisfied. Every night has a special that you can order for an entire table.

Another great Tria iteration. Awesome draft beer and wine list, and chill spot to hang out outside. The turkey sandwich that is written about as one of the most popular items is as delicious as it's lived up to be. Great bread, crispy bacon, and awesome for the summer.

Awesome spot, the service is very friendly and prompt. We came for brunch and overall it was a really great experience. Everything was cooked perfectly and super flavorful. They had outside seating next to heaters and it was pretty comfortable. The time that we went, they had a steak and eggs special and it was super delicious.

Sat at the bar for 10 minutes and couldn't get the attention of any staff, gave it a chance because it was a busy night, but I've never encountered hospitality like this at any restaurant in Philly. We ended up taking our business elsewhere with no regrets.

$12 for mac and cheese is pretty steep, especially considering that the serving size is maybe less than a pound. I had the "In the Buff," which is apparently the number one customer favorite here. The serving temperature was good, macaroni was a solid al dente texture, but the toppings were underwhelming. Additionally, for a 7 cheese mac, the flavor just is not there.
Great food, the mussels were solid and the broth was delicious. Excellent burger.
Crunch burger medium rare... got food poisoning.

Came here for breakfast, we enjoyed the experience.  The food and service was great, relaxing atmosphere.  Allen, our server, was very nice and provided great service.

We came here for dinner on a Saturday night around 5:30, the wait for a party of 2 was less than 10 minutes (they do not do reservations); they do practice social distancing and staff have on mask.  
The dishes on their menu sounded delicious, what we ate exceeded what we thought...everything was yummy.   They truly take the traditional dishes and take them to the next level.

The service here was horrible, this rates in my top 5 of horrible customer service experience.  If I had come solo I would have left.  I came here for lunch on March 22nd with some colleagues. From the start the all of 2 staff that I saw treated us like we were bothering them.

sample_reviews.text:
An STL staple. 

If you're visiting, GO! 

If you're a native but you've never been, GO!

Ted Drewes has been apart of my hometown glory since I was a little girl walking from Spring & Itaska with nothing more than 4 quarters. I was excited to have more than enough to cover my $.80 concrete. Although the days of an $.80 treat are long gone; you can certainly get a custard fix for a nominal $2.

Lovely fresh market grocer with prices that won't knock you into bankruptcy. I love the vast variety of fresh fruits & vegetables in particular. They always have exactly what I'm  looking for; and if they don't a friendly employee is usually within ear-shot to guide the way. 

They have the common departments such as Bakery, Hot Food, Deli, Seafood, Frozen items, Health/Hygiene, etc.

Michelle was an absolute doll. I had questions before arriving and she was super helpful. Fast forward travel time...Michelle continued being great.

I was craving a gluten free treat and New Day was perfect in a pinch. They offer an array of sweet treats from cupcakes to cookies sans gluten; even vegan options.

Ladies room was horrendous; as if an employee has arrived & got ready for work in the restroom. The hanger still in the Accessible stall. Paper towels all over the place & stall out of tissue. I'm perplexed because it wasn't the end of a super busy night it was 3pm. 

Service was horrific. Cody was so engaged with another table he totally failed to connect with ours.

Terrible customer service. I could feel the thickness of bad attitude immediately. It was cold out & I needed a warm place to wait for my Lyft sooooooo.....I waited in the non-existent line for one of the 3 non-busy employees to acknowledge me. I ordered a Pike because I didn't twist then to craft my usual espresso with care. The young man placed my coffee on the counter & walked away.
$40 takeout (I'm insane)
Know before you go

sample_reviews.text:
Get ready to inhale your fluffy and warm plate of beignets when you stop by Cafe Beignet on Bourbon Street. The open-air dining scene is perfect on those comfortably mild mornings in NOLA.

It's easy to order. Just request your beignets, coffee (or, in my case, mocha!) and whatever else you'd like from their well-crafted menu, and have a seat.

Talk about a great first impression.

While I never made it to the food truck, I knew I had to stop by when SpOt opened a location 1) conveniently on my way home from work and 2) a fairly short walk away.

Eclipse is actually adorable. The brewer Chris and his associate Beth couldn't be more friendly if they tried. The brewery itself, which opened last October, is a teeny tiny operation in a repurposed home/municipal building right near the center of Merchantville. And the beers? Awesome.

They have room for up to 18 taps right now but many of the beers had kicked by the time we arrived yesterday.

Oh, Rode's, I wanted to like you. Really. The lower Glouco/Salem County area is a royal pain when it comes to dining out. I mean, you've got your slew of pizza places and a couple chains. Coming across a nice place to eat outside of those distinctions? Good luck.

I've been corralled here several times, and I always just groan to myself when it's mentioned as the next destination. Smith's is in competition for my all-time least favorite bar in the city.

One time I asked someone to repeat whatever he was saying so many times that I whipped out the Notes app on my phone and wrote: "I have no idea what you're saying.
Check their Facebook page for giveaways and specials.
Covid: They are not distancing tables, they're operating above 25% capacity, no hand sanitizer, and the owner was walking around shaking the hands of his regulars.

sample_reviews.text: This market is a Mecca of Italian goodies & groceries! In the 6 or so months since it opened it has become my absolute favourite place to shop. The store itself is gorgeous; wall to wall white marble that gleams in the light that shines in from the large, floor to ceiling windows.

Good Greek! This new venture by famed Etown restaurateur and Chef, Yanni, will have you screaming "Get Me To The Greek!" With only 9 tables that sit under a canopy of vines and twinkling white lights - as well as under shards of broken plates (OPA!!), you literally feel like you are a private guest in Yanni's backyard... What can I say, the name really fits!

Love it!!! Not only is this not the closest cafe for me to get to from home, I just L-O-V-E it! It is so cute, warm and inviting inside, with the most adorable décor. So many new coffee shops are stark and cold, this place makes me feel like I am in my living room. 

Every time I've been in all of the employees have been entirely women, who are all so warm and friendly, and I love that too!

I have been coming here for years, through 3, oh, now 4 reincarnations of a "sports" bar, all with different management and most with different owners. For over 10 years now, every Monday for Monday Night Football this has been my haunt. So it is hard for me to dissect all of the different points that I have come to know, very well, about this location.

Lit-erally one of the cutest wine bars in the city and a perfect 104th Street hot spot. This Italian style wine bar is always packed and buzzing. Although, it is hard not to be busy in a place that is so, in a word, Lit-tle. With seating for around 30, not the most comfortable in the world, mind you, it does well to have a reservation if you are going to be a guest.
Rrrrrrr.... why are there no Self Check-outs at this location?!
Wing Wednesday - half price wings 6pm to close!

sample_reviews.text: I had dinner at J. Gilbert's last night, a Friday, and once again everything from making the reservation, the food, cocktails, service & atmosphere was on-point as usual. My streak was cooked perfectly medium rare and I think that the au gratin potatoes are one of the best features on the menu.

We had dinner on New Year's Eve at J. Gilberts for the second year and as always, loved our experience. It is by far my favorite here in St. Louis as most everyone knows. Consistency--I think that and the au gratin potatoes are my favorite things--LOL! Steak, crab cakes, salads, chicken, bread, Mojitos--all done really well here.

Took my husband today for a birthday lunch--he loved it. Wings and spinach salad were great and all of the sports themed wall hangings and of course the tv screens--my husband and I had a great lunch with great service--thank you Alec P.

As an avid reader, watching a great store like B&N die a slow death over the past 20 years has been hard. The store's shelves are now somewhat bare, the floor-space is empty, and it is a place that has seen its best days long ago. 

The customer service that I received at this location today explains much of what went wrong.

Met my friend for lunch there and although very busy, we had no problem being seated. The service was very fast and friendly. I ordered the house wings and they were very good--tangy, juicy and not overly saucy. Also ordered the corn fritters and shared them with my friend--not quite what I was expecting--they were small fried bites with corn and breading.
My favorite--rarely disappoints!

sample_reviews.text: After frequently going to the N. Dale Mabry location as well as the Platt st location (both have poor service-lucky to ever get one drink refill), we have become regulars at the Westchase location. The staff is super attentive on every visit so it is obviously a standard set forth by management. Also, there is a manager (Monica) who I swear must work 7 days a week and is always friendly.
Grand China is our go to take out place for consistently good food. Ready in ten minutes every time. Ps- get the crab Rangoons - you'll be glad you did.

What a great find with the option to use open table and book your spot while walking around the French Quarter.
I love oysters and my husband loves seafood so we were excited to give this place a try while in town for the college football championship.

Like so many others have already posted, we too had a terrible experience. We went tonight to give it a try.


We were seated quickly about 9pm on a Friday which was a good start. After a long wait for a server, a waitress came over and her first question was did we speak Spanish. We said no and that was the end of our service- literally!

Three stars only because the sushi was good. The service was beyond terrible and we only went to the sushi side of the restaurant. There were only two other tables seated so there was no excuse for the terribly inattentive server. We may go back only to try the hibachi side but not excited about the slow service.

Tip: for party of 6 or more, they take reservations - call in advance as they are very busy (because it's so good) so don't expect to call on a Wednesday and get a prime time weekend reservation spot...

The main bank of windows to the dining room are arched shaped windows which face east so if you are eating here in the morning, you may wish to sit on the opposite side of the restaurant as it will...

sample_reviews.text:
Location opened mid-May 2014, just a 1 minute drive east from I-65 exit 130.

Everything is nice and new, bright and clean. No real surprises in store layout or product offerings - it's all as you expect. Pharmacy. Lots of parking. Self checkout isles near BOTH main entry doors.

Hippie factor: Baby organic dry goods section is fantastic with many options.

Grilled pork, grilled pork. Order anything here that comes with grilled pork. The crisp, the char, the juiciness, the seasoning ... it's what all pigs should taste like - if it's not made into bacon, of course.

Saigon's grilled pork banh mi is the BEST SANDWICH IN INDIANAPOLIS. Loved it for years now.

Smokehouse salad was just about as good as a salad gets, and I don't rave about many (any?) salads. I left full and impressed. The size of the bowl is legit, the quality of the ingredients and amount of protein also legit.

Average pub food in an extremely loud room. The scene is probably pretty cool if you're into noise/beer/soccer but those are not personally my three favorite things. I will say that if you love beer, the options here were extensive and the waiter was both friendly and quite beer-knowledgeable.

I got the St George's Philly with the garlic cheese fries.

3.5 stars (in the Asian-style food court category of eats). Surprisingly good if you are a fan of this genre! It's not fancy, but that doesn't mean it can't be dang tasty.

Bourbon chicken, tender chunks in a sweet-but-not-too-sweet sauce ... plus no vegetable filler to get in the way! Spicy Cajun chicken, cooked well again with a a bit of noticeable Cajun heat and not too many filler vegetables.
Ask to substitute chicken on the noodle bowl if you're not feeling like tofu.
Located inside the Salesforce Tower, near Yolk and next to Fast Burrito.

sample_reviews.text:
I have no idea how I have never noticed this stand sooner. It's a bit hidden in the mall, located by the ship. 

They sell all types of cinnamon treats. Mini rolls, CinnaDippers, Twists, Cinnamon Shakerz and the list goes on. They have probably the best Cinnamon buns I have ever had. They are great to snack on while shopping but be warned you will become addicted.

Fulton Market Burger can be compared to Fat Burger... but Fulton is way better. It has the same type of set up as fat  burger too. You order your food, grab a seat and then they bring it out to you. The place itself is really nice for a burger joint. It has brick walls, windows all around and spread out seating.

Gateway lanes provides a nice alternative to your usual activities. If you are feeling bored I suggest you gather up some friends and go bowling.

It's a decent price and it's a lot of fun. The place is really busy on the weekends, especially at night. So if you really want to bowl you have to get their early. I am not sure if they reserve lanes or not.

In one word this place was Meh!

 The pizza was burnt and the way payment works here is odd. Odd doesn't necessarily mean bad. You order everything at the front and pay right away (kind of like at McDonald's) but  the problem with that is you pretty much have to decide on everything including desert and drinks up front.

Mary Brown's tries to be KFC but lets face it.. no chicken place can compare toe greatness of the chicken, gravy, fries or burgers at KFC.

I found their chicken to be dry and a bit tasteless. The gravy wasn't great either.

sample_reviews.text: Got dinner from Avalon.  Despite it being in walking distance, I had never been there before.  We got the feed up to 5 people meal that includes meatballs, penne pasta with vodka sauce, and a Caesar salad.  It also came with a side with roasted peppers, cauliflower, and bordetella.  Our favorite part was the cheesecake (not pictured).  Kids gave the meal a thumbs up!  So much food.

I tried to order dinner from a place when they opened today but they were already sold out (apparently other people plan earlier than I do for dinner).  

Anyways, as a result, I ordered Taco Tuesday special from Gypsy Saloon (since everyone only says nice things about it here) along with Calamari and guacamole and chips.  Everything was spectacular and everyone had a favorite item.

Amazing chicken wings.  Tea tastes much fresher than other places on the same street.  A notch above Kung Fu (which is near my house).  My kids love the egg waffles and so, we always end up buying several and air frying them the following am for breakfast.  I was not a big fan of their chicken sandwich but hubby liked it so likely just a matter of preference.

Bubble tea was good.  The sandwiches not so much - too much mayo, daikon was under seasoned, meat was lacking in both flavor and volume, etc.  Service from cashier was good.  I spent over $40 on food and owner was less than friendly despite my being the only customer.
I ordered the cheese pizza and chicken salad for my staff at Wilmington.  Delivery was prompt.  Service was great.  Chicken salad was awesome but pizza was greasy and disgusting.
Good pizza, great Spanish fries and cheesesteak.  Great service!
Great pesto that comes with complimentary bread.  Profiteroles and creme brûlée are awesome.  Canard and cassoulet were good but not great.

sample_reviews.text: We dropped in to City Market this week to grab some quick carryout lunch and give some love to the businesses currently open in the market. I decided to mix up my usual routine to support one of the CM newcomers, PROX Salads. I had heard great things and thought a salad would pair nicely with my Sweet Corn Chowder from Circle City Soups.

I loved Livery before COVID hit but I'm loving it even more now for a variety of reasons. We enjoyed a dinner on their outdoor patio during chilly November and didn't mind weathering the elements for their buttery tender short ribs and delicious cocktails. Flash forward to January and cannot stop raving about their Devour Downtown menu.

Hallelujah! We finally made it to TeeJay's after driving by way too many times in a hurry and knowing how busy they are. Thanks to their current drive-thru setup, the whole ordering and flow process was a cinch. We were craving an afternoon treat after visiting Holliday Park for a hike and I quickly remembered this spot that I've had bookmarked for years.

Real quicklike: 

Maybe my three stars is based on the silly fact that I walked into a horse racing venue expecting casino slots - this is t-totally my fault. 

The food was mediocre - I tried the beef/veggie kabobs and chicken "tenders" - the skewers were very good. I appreciated that they used bok choy - never would have thought to add this in. Also enjoyed the gourmet mustard - seed intact.

It is only fair to preface my review by telling you that I was at Pearl Street for a cocktail hour/mixer, so my consumption was limited to cheesy breadsticks, margherita pizza and nachos topped off with a P.S. Collins. Okay, so when I list it like that I suppose I did eat enough food to make my review credible after all...

I would compare the food to a plain pair of blue jeans.
Drive thru ordering options available during COVID-19
Decent flower prices- tulips are 2 bundles for $10

sample_reviews.text: So lucky to have this neighborhood cafe nearby! I've come here on a few occasions with a friend who initially recommended it and have been pleased every time--with the food, the service, and the ease of getting a table immediately upon arrival. Yes, it does get packed especially on weekends, but for what you pay for the portions and service you get, it is all worth it!

Why I ever gave Axis only three stars is beyond me.  Since I first ate there, I have come to discover how expansive their menu is! Not only do they have the salad bar and delicious and innovative pizza varieties, they also offer plenty of breakfast options (served all day), pasta dishes, sandwiches, and the list goes on!

I used to go to High Point in Mt. Airy every single day when I worked in the area (more specifically the one at Allen Lane train station) but now that I don't get to Mt. Airy as often as I used to, I am so happy that there is a High Point Cafe in Fairmount.

I've been to both the King of Prussia and Ardmore Suburban Square locations and nothing really distinguished either one for me, though I have to say I really liked the half-moon ravioli, even though it was drowned in tomato sauce.  Careful though, or you might splash yourself!

This is not your average 7-11 or Wawa but it does the trick if you need to grab a quick bite or drink and don't want to shell out the cash to eat at a nearby restaurant.  I'll give them credit for being well-organized and having a half-decent selection, but it's not like I would go here to do my grocery shopping.
Only $0.49 per ounce!
Birthday breakfast: peanut butter kandy kake pancakes!

Had lunch here about 2 weeks ago this place is supercute! I loved all the art and furniture and dinnerware. The owners are supernice and the food is good and satisfying and priced very fair for the area. Their vietnamese iced coffee is great! 
I was looking forward to trying the strawberry and avocado topped spring rolls everyone had been raving about.

I wanna give them another half star! I've heard from many peeps that this is a favorite of all the Nola classic Restaurants and now I know why!
They never had a disappointing meal there and that's where they call to celebrate an event in their life everything from birthdays to graduations.

They are the best food truck in the city!  These guys know how to cook! One of them used to work at LeForet and the other worked at Brennan's. 
I've had 3 of their items from their ever changing menu and all were great!
Garlic Parmesan Fries, Creamy Mac and Cheese but my absolute favorite are there Empanadas!

i have to say i have never dined here but one night i was lucky enough to find a parking spot to go inside and order some donuts. i was surprised at how expansive the space was. one half of the space is just for donuts and the other half for sit down diner food.

its true what Harden says you can find cops eating there all the time at night.

ate here for my birthday  with my family which included my 3 year old niece and 5 year old nephew. we wanted some place affordable but nice. the place was affordable yes nice not really. for a place that has an expensive frutti di mare on the menu.
Don't order the hot and sour soup cuz it's not your typical with mushrooms and tofu and egg? They made it with carrots and daikon :(
Go on tues-Thursday

sample_reviews.text: Drunk Ox Sober Cat... I've driven by here a couple times and the name intrigued me. I came for dinner and it was easy to make reservations with opentable as well as make changes to the date... I was aiming for a non-event night as parking can be outrageous in the area if there was a concert or hockey game happening at Roger's Place.

If you plan to eat here on a weekend, definitely make reservations in advance or call ahead to check the wait times. I came around lunch hour on Sunday with friends without reservations and we had the option of waiting an hour for seats...hard pass. However, if you opt to come on a weekday, you can pretty much be guaranteed quick seating without reservations.

I've eaten from here twice, once as dine-in and once as take out. Although not a fan of having to
sign up on their website to order from the menu, the options are fantastic if you are looking for something different/unique in terms of patties and for specific burger add-ons. I'm not overly adventurous, so I've tried the boar & the beef burger, basic fries and onion rings.

I came here for a quick bite and was immediately attracted to this place because it wasn't busy at 6pm on a Saturday ...but I kind of see why. It's nicely decorated & sets a nice ethnic vibe inside, plus the front windows open to Whyte ave for a cafè-like feel as you watch pedestrians pass by. There's also a TV and Middle Eastern music playing overhead to add to the ambience.

I've been here about 3 times, there's a pretty authentic feel to this little Korean place. The parking lot is small, so if it's a busy evening, you might be parking on the side street. I do like the kpop music videos that constantly play on the TV there.
Hot drinks on menu that have a "*" can be served as an iced drink!
If you only want to buy a freezer pint,  you can skip the lineup.

Yep actual 5 star review. The girly and her family, while on walk-about today, were on the Saint Charles street car with the thought of walking to Blue Frog for some Chocolates for her little brother's teacher.

Damn you Crabby Jacks for being closed today! Damn you to hell!

sample_reviews.text: My mother had an appointment at St Francis hospital just 8 minutes south of here and we picked here because of the 4 star rating and the independent nature of the place.  Having a good soup was paramount to my mother enjoying her time here and they have a great beef stew!!! I am not sure if they have it everyday, but they had it on Monday.

Cannot believe the first 2 times here I did not know about the cool patio out back.  The front room is colorful and vibrant.  The drinks here are very good and the staff really works at keeping the customers happy.  I like the nacho platter here with a Margarita.  I usually just come here to drink, but I sometimes also get the nacho platter.

Love this place!  The lunch special is so special that soup and salad is included as well!  You can easily do lunch here for under $10 plus the tip.  On this particular day it was cold and raining, so I ordered the hot sake.  Absolutely a must if it is cold outside, but they have other drinks and sake as well.

Awesome view!  What a great idea to have a patio on the roof.  They have some plants around to provide evening shade and a few umbrellas.  We were quite comfy in our plastic lawn chair with a nice breeze.  Our waiter took our drink order and brought us our drinks right away.  We must have arrived right before the 7:30 rush because he never came back over.
No complaints, they got My order right. I was amazed by how much my friend that took me there actually ate.  The vanilla shake was good.
They are closed
Come early to get the best selection!

It's a cool shop right on a busy street.  Plenty of offstreet parking.

[440]

This newly built Whole Foods Market is huge compared to old one down the street.  

This store was well thought out as all the aisles are HUGE which allows for more then one cart to walk past without worrying about squeezing by.  The main aisles are not cluttered with stand alone displays.

The produce section is large along with the bulk food sections.

[1089]

2/2020

I stopped in to this Williams Sonoma location on my lunch hour since I LOVE the quality of their aprons.  

There is street and meter parking but no lot due to this location being in the middle of Center City.

The store is nicely laid out with tons of options.  

The staff was friendly and helped me find everything I needed.

I saw on Philly.com that a new Mac and Cheese place was opening near my office so I had to try it out.   The place is clean and cute but did not have much of a seating area so we had to  take it to go.  

The layout is backwards.  The line was out the door instead of streaming it thru the restaurant so I held the door for a  few people (no choice) and I was the 4th person in line.

I usually visit this location early morning. It gets really crowded at night and weekends.

The shelves are full and there is plenty to buy at 7am.  I like that I am able to pick up salmon even thought the meat dept is closed (prepackaged in the case).  

The ladies at customer service are really nice and helpful

Today I went in and they got rid of hand baskets.
Gas pumps have Apple Pay
Now open

sample_reviews.text: I really liked Indo, an Asian fusion restaurant in the old Good Fortune restaurant location in McCree Town/ Botanical Heights. The Shikoku 75 cocktail (a play on a French 75)was delicious, and the yellow belly sake sashimi was to die for.  The cooked crab nigiri was  pricey for two small pieces at $20 and it was just ok.  That was my only disappointment.

Great new Honduran restaurant  in S City.  Very authentic. No website, but they do have a Facebook page the ladies that work here are very sweet. I don't normally like to be called honey, but when they say it, it's adorable.  They serve plantain's, beans, rice, whole fried fish, and a variety of meats. Come check it out! Credit cards accepted. This was a Mexican bakery at one time.

Every time I take friends to Nadine's for weekend breakfast, they love it.  Nice variety, big portions, great prices and fantastic mimosa ideal! Also, they have a big patio.  I'm not a biscuits and gravy person, but everyone seems to love those.
Picked up some sushi to go for an outbound flight and it was terrible!  The fish did not taste fresh.

Located on Delmar in the Loop, Thai Country Cafe's food is heavy and greasy.  The decor is dated and scruffy.  They're lucky that they have location going for them and that they make Thai food.  No matter how much you screw it up, it still manages to taste good.  But, there's much better Thai to be had elsewhere.
Try calling ahead to place your order. They are shortstaffed.
Great New Indo Nepalese restaurant on S Hampton!

sample_reviews.text:
Cafe Roma is my favorite pizza place of all time. The pizza is BOMB. My sister from out of town always wants to come here.

Any pizza you get will be awesome, but you can't go wrong with cheese. It's amazing.

I wish I could comment on the other food, but I haven't actually tried anything else. It's really nice to sit outside when the weather is good.

My favorite steakhouse in the city. It's an old New Orleans classic - the owners family came over from Croatia and opened this place up and it's been there ever since. It's not a trendy, hip steakhouse. It's even better. Real New Orleans style.  

So start off with the mushrooms - they're delicious. Stuffed with crab (shrimp maybe) they're giant and fried and yummy.

This place.... is great. Wow. 

First off, it is SUPER small. There must be about 10/15 seats in the restaurant. It's super tiny. There's a great bar to eat at! 

The guacamole is super creamy and indulgent. It's not watered down thank god and you can tell it's real avocados. I'm craving it right now. Also, the portion is HUGE!

Okay, I don't know why I was expecting this to be good. I had been here a couple times before, but not since around 2013/2014. So I figured it was time to come here again. The prices are still high, but I was okay with that if it was going to be a good salad.

First off, the girls working in the shop were two of the rudest service industry workers I've ever experienced in my entire life.

Smash burger is pretty good. 

They have TONS of different options - from pre-determined combos to build your own. 

I got mine with guac last night - YUM! It was great. They also over avocado slices, and I like that you can get either. 

The girl taking my order was SO NICE and awesome. She was great. 

The smash fries taste delicious - garlic and rosemary.

sample_reviews.text: How has it taken me so long to visit Indiana City?  I feel like I've been to every brewery in the city but never this one.  I love the industrial and open feel to this space.  I like the intimacy in the bar area, but if you are with a large group you can spread out in their large, open space.  Their beers are average priced and the beers I had were fantastic.  We will definitely return!

If you are into craft beer you cannot help but love this place. The playlist is amazing. The beer flowing is delicious. There is always a good selection on styles of beer. I always love the berliners here. Sometimes the stouts are a bit much (gasp!).  Be sure and come hydrated. If you forget they have some water for you. 

If they're releasing a beer or having a special event there may be a line.

It's such an easy choice every time when OG BBQ is an option.  Every single thing I've had from them has been amazing.  The Fritos wrap I had this latest time is one of the best things I've gotten. 

I love the space. There is plenty of parking at Metazoa.  It's so easy to pick up yummy take out. OR you can bring your dog and enjoy some beers!  The dog can enjoy themselves at the dog park!

This location is *goals*.  I love the brick.  It gives a cozy but modern vibe to things.  I love that it shares a space with Ash & Elm.

As far as actual product, I was let down.  I got a cold brew and it was very acidic, I didn't even drink the whole thing.  The one thing I expect with cold brew is smoothness.  My husband got a drip coffee and it was decent.

Both times I have been here I have experienced good food. This time, however, I tried a beer (the wheat IPA). It was all great! I love the atmosphere in here and the service was great. 

I will say that the growlers are quite expensive but they are awesome looking!
6/27/20 COVID operations are Wednesday - Sunday 11a-7p. CASH ONLY.
Go for the ginger latte. That's where it's at. I prefer it iced but I'm sure you can't lose either way!

sample_reviews.text:
Found via a Yelp search while enjoying a Bloody Mary at Soulard Farmers Market, we decided to give the unknown spot a try.  Located at Lafayette and Broadway, this is a former library turned bar with a food vendor inside.  
According to their events list, they have live music on Saturday nights and karaoke on Sunday nights.  It's a huge space as you'd imagine.

Always a great choice for sushi.  Good service, great options.  We started with a salad and miso soup.  For our meal we shared Unagi nigiri (eel), a tuna avocado roll, the Kamikaze roll and the Itchiban roll.  My favorite was the Itchiban, great fresh flavors.

We've enjoyed every visit to Novella.   The owner is a warm, inviting woman who is very knowledgeable about her wine.  The food is delicious as well.  The space is comfortable to enjoy with friends.  She and her husband are working on developing the patio space which is dog friendly.

We redeemed our Groupon for Mizu last night and had a terrible experience. I made a 7pm reservation and mentioned the Groupon per the instructions. We got there on time and were seated immediately by very harried staff who complained about how busy they'd been. This should've been our first warning of what was to come.

Three stars for the service and presentation.  Unfortunately I can't give them more because the flavors of the sushi just weren't there.  The rolls we had (Seadragon, Grove, Unagi Eel and Crunchy Philly Roll) were presented beautifully and there was nothing off about the tastes but there was nothing particularly memorable about the tastes either.
They recently started delivery
Expect to take 1-1/2 to 2 hours for a weekday lunch.  Manager's reason was that their sushi chefs get backed up during lunch and there's only 2 of them.

I was hesitant to come here because I was convinced that I'd never find anywhere to park, but lo and behold there is a huge nearby lot that has ONE HOUR FREE PARKING!

I had been to the Burger Republic in Lenox Village but that is very far away from me, so when this one opened and I learned of the free one hour parking a visit was inevitable!

The better half and I stopped in a lunchtime.

Finally, it opens!

The shell of this Starbucks had mocked the better half and I for at least a year. Driving past it twice going to visit her folks "Opening soon" it said for ever and ever.  Then, suddenly it was open and operating!

The staff seems more than excited about the new location.
If you don't mind a long wait for breakfast, its really some of the very best you can get. When my parents visit from out of town we almost always try and go.

I stopped into this Starbucks near closing time, and was told that they were "out of coffee beverages". I ordered a Frappuccino so I can only assume that they meant THAT and not that they were actually OUT OF COFFEE. It was close to closing time, and its possible that the staff just didn't want to bother, but I had to drive to Mt Juliet and a Frappuccino would have really helped.

I wanted to like The Local Taco because of how close to me it is. But unfortunately that did not come to pass. 

The food was average to downright bad. I am no expert on Mexican, so I can't speak to that, but I found the meal to be bland. The rice was dry and the portions were not very large for the cost. About the best thing going for it is that they sell "Mexican Coke".
The lever on the Diet Coke sticks so you may have to pull back on it yourself or you'll have Soda all over yourself.
Live Music AND recorded music at the same time. What dubious entertainment

sample_reviews.text: Love tropical smoothie! Best smoothies, fresh and big. Can get made with or without Splenda instead of sugar. Particularly love the island green and the sunrise sunset smoothies! Sooo good. This location is kinda small but the employees were nice.

Nice little drive thru. Really good coffee. Yummy turkey and cheese breakfast croissant sandwich. Not a fan of their eggs, in the eggs and cheese croissant. They are square and weird. It's like they scramble them and pour them on a baking sheet and bake them then it's like a sheet of eggs and they cut it in squares! It's a weird solid squishy square lol.

Love mazzaros!! Oh man I come here just to eat lunch! The deli and bakery are both phenomenal. I always get a sub and a dessert. The chocolate rum cake ball is so boozy and rich and moist! They have tables set outside under a covered porch. 

The market and wine shop are great as well as a gift shop. I got some adorable oversized polka dot mugs from here that I use as soup bowls.

I was going to give this place 5 stars until I saw a bug in the salad on the buffet. I had already eaten before I saw it (and the food was really good and flavorful) but after seeing that I was done. Sorry, I don't eat bugs.

I'm changing my review of this store because I just realized they sell expired items on their shelf! I was there August 6th and I just opened up a granola bar wondering why it tasted stale. Expired July 17. Didn't think at a store like this I would need to check expiration dates so carefully. 

Original review:

Very expensive. Not my normal shopping store.
Awesome burgers! And you can substitute fries for fried pickles!!
Great happy hour everyday 4-6!

Stopped in to get pierogis and Keilbasa for Easter. I ordered in advance to make sure I got what I needed. The owner made some special accommodations for me, I needed to add an extra dozen to my order(3days) before Easter. Stand up job by them! I appreciate it.


I will continue to support this small business, great food and great customer service. Solid 5 stars

Dagwoods is an all around good spot to stop for food or cold beers. We haven't been there in a while so we decided to stop Saturday night. Upon arrival the place was packed but we were sat immediately.

My wife ordered the clams and pasta. The clams were huge! And everything was seasoned well. She really liked it. I ordered the French onion filet special wow! The filet was delicious!

Uncle Dave's is fantastic! It's located in the market area of shady brook farm. They have a large variety of homemade flavors. Last night we went with the coconut, cappuccino and the cookies and cream. All were very good and you could tell they were homemade. The coconut was by far our favorite. Solid 5 stars for quality and price. We will continue to go back.

I was a huge fan of this Panera, not so much anymore. I would eat here often, 3 days a week. Last week I orderd French onion soup. Upon opening the soup and stirring, there was hair about 8 inches long. I approached an employee about my issue, he implied I must have put it there. Just an FYI I'm A bald guy, not sure how or why I would be able to put a hair in my own soup.

Okay so we were on the go tonight and had to grab a bite to eat. The rolls at this place are to die for! Tonight we arrived and were sat immediately. My wife ordered the sirloin with sweet potato. Her steak was cooked to perfection! I went with the porterhouse with mushrooms and onions, baked potato and a salad. Their salads are always spot on, I really enjoy the egg. My steak was a disaster.
Closed!
We stopped by Saturday with friends for brunch. Come to find out they only have brunch on Sundays. That's a bit of a bummer! Most places have brunch both Saturday and Sunday..

sample_reviews.text: I wanna throw a little more love to India's Kitchen after an excellent Sunday lunch buffet experience.  It is really fantastic, with 6-7 dishes each of meat & vegetarian dishes.  Good stuff too, like goat curry and palak paneer.  There's also a good salad bar AND dessert, which featured gulab jamuns -- . fried spongy milky balls soaked in rose scented syrup.

Some say it's rare in life to experience true perfection, but if you have 4 bucks in your pocket, perfection can be found at this custard stand, in the form of something called an Hawaiian Concrete.

It's a blend of custard (vanilla/chocolate/twist, I choose the twist) with pecans, coconut, banana, and pineapple, and it is just amazing.

Strange Donuts is not a mere donut shop.  It's a full-on movement, and a huge asset to St Louis.

Let's start with the donuts.  Honestly, I don't care much for the standard yeast donuts.  I've tried a few and been underwhelmed.  If it looks like regular donut with some stuff on top, I stay away.

Where this place really shines are the cake-style (and specialty) donuts.

Breakfast and pizza.  2 of my most favoritest things.  But what happens when you combine the two? WHAT THEN!?  This is what I came to investigate.  After Cairdeas Coffee in Dogtown stopped serving their awesome breakfast pizzas (why God why?!), I needed to find a new fix, and quick.

A mediocre lunch experience.

The menu is pretty boring.  I went for a turkey club ($6.50). It featured avocado, pepperjack cheese, and bean sprouts, which made it sound a little interesting.  Unfortunately there was very little avocado & sprouts.  It tasted OK.  The bread was generic.  There was no visual appeal.  It was like something you'd put together in your kitchen if you were in a rush.
An ice cream cookie sandwich is a good way to beat the summer heat. The gingerbread/butter-pecan was amazing
House infused watermelon ginger tequila. Whoa.

sample_reviews.text:
I had been hesitant to try this restaurant as I was judging a book by its cover and assumed it was a greasy hole in the wall, but once I heard they had good authentic Mexican food, I had to try it out. 

This restaurant specializes in burgers, philly cheese steaks and tacos, tamales, etc. it's counter service style with plenty of indoor seating.

I think I found heaven in the form of a spacious, multi building italian market extravaganza overflowing with delicious goodies in St. Pete.

The seafood is fresh and plentiful, the olives were some of the best I've had, the authentic italian meats and cheeses are out of this world, the baked goods are made fresh daily, the selection of fresh pasta is endless and desserts are beyond tasty.

HOLY MOLY.

If I could start every day with their delicious coffee, I would never complain about getting out of bed again. My cousin and I were roaming the Saturday Morning Market and she insisted this was the best coffee around. At first, I resisted (as I already had 2 cups of coffee at home).

I've only been here once and there is a reason why. I like all kinds of international food, especially Indian and Vietnamese, so I was excited to give this place a try, but disappointed shortly after arriving. Perhaps I just don't care for Ethiopian cuisine, but it definitely left something to be desired.

Let me start this review out by saying that my boyfriend and I had stopped here in hopes of getting a slice of pizza after the lightning game last weekend. When we arrived, it appeared they were currently out of pizza as there was none in the front display case.
This place has had way too many negative visits from the health department over the years. No thanks. I'll stick with Mrs Ts pierogis from Publix, cooked in my own kitchen.
Tried to come at 9 pm on a Thursday night. No seats or standing room for even 1 person. Busy place

sample_reviews.text:
Yeah this is my favorite lunch place in the area.

You get an obscene variety of food in a bento box that you don't get elsewhere:

Miso soup
Salad
Sunomono
Some california rolls
A single takoyaki
2 gyoza.
Rice
annnnnd your entre.

Grilled mackerel, which is awesome in and of itself, with all that is less than 10 bucks before tax.

Their kalbi, more expensive, is quite delicious as well.

Of all the lunch places that I frequented during my time working downtown, Blues City deli is the place that I miss the most.

The Italian Beef with sweet and hot peppers is by far my favorite sandwich here, but the overkill of green olive tapanade on the muffeletta is a close 2nd.

The fact that this place is the seed to the future development that is Crown Village makes this establishment amazing in and of itself.  
http://www.crownvillagestl.com/

Experiencing the ridiculously huge BLT, awesome candy, and solid no-too-sweet shakes and malts should make anyone realize just how this gem of an operation has persevered through the times.

This is a place that St.

I've been to Cafe Brasil only two times and I wasn't very impressed with my first visit.  I haven't had the buffet, which is what they're known for, so some guys convinced me to go there again.  

I got there a little earlier than the rest of the party so I sat in the bar area.

My burger was amazing.  The problem is everything else doesn't make me want to come here unless someone really really wanted to come and there were only 4 of us.

First problem was that they obviously don't know how to under-promise things when giving a time frame for when a party of 9 can be seated.  Initially it was 5 minutes that ballooned to 20+.
Digging the soundtrack.
Get a deviled egg while you wait.

sample_reviews.text: The Doylestown Farmers Market runs from late spring through mid-autumn in the parking lot at Oakland & Hamilton Streets.  There is a good selection of produce, meat, dairy, egg & beauty product vendors.

Honestly, this place's sorbettos and gelatos rival Capogiro in Philly and any of the joints in NYC.  The Biscotti, Vanilla, Irish Whiskey (tastes like Bailey's) and Banana varieties rocked my world.

I had a first-class affogato with a delicious espresso pulled just for me.

EVERYthing is home-made - cakes, novelties - even Pizzelle.

Can't wait to return and so glad a local chick turned me on to...

While no longer owned by the Luberto family, the food is still magnificent here.  Our favorite Italian spot in the D-town area.

Try Chicken Marsala, Veal or Chicken parm, Crab Ravioli (a special - if they have it, get it) or anything in pink vodka sauce.

Delicious! And big portions to take some home.

Casual.

Just had an amazingly AWFUL experience at Mixx.
It was about 7 PM on a Friday night - but the place was slow (about 3 tables occupied).

I ordered the sushi/sashimi combo.
When it arrived (After the iceberg lettuce salad) the "white tuna" (likely escolar - certainly not albacore) sashimi tasted fishy.

The server had it replaced with yellowtail - which was good.

Then I tried the tuna, snapper...

I received a flaming email from someone at this restaurant immediately after I posted my review.

My review stands and I stand by what I experienced.  I had a horrible time at Mixx and now, after being harassed online there is no way i will ever return here.
Smells great on a Sunday at lunch time.  Getting a large for someone in the hospital.
At iron hill

I really like this place and was surprised by it.  I have wanted to visit it for awhile and took advantage of an instant deal as I was downtown for work and hungry.  It was a good choice.

I ended up ordering the Frank blinchiki and the Yummy Pelmeni.  The Frank was a hot dog (2?) wrapped in a thin pancake with tomato, lettuce and onion, "Chicago" style.  Excellent.

I loved this place.  I didn't go until around 10pm as my plan was to just go in for awhile and check it out.  I had just left a place down the street where I started my night.  I saw some of the reviews which are mixed so wasn't sure what to expect but I knew they served their full menu until close.

Easily one of my top favorites ... (along with Pie Oh My!) for baked items.  They have a nice space, friendly staff and always a good selection (and they supply many other business with their stuff). They are FAMOUS (to me) for their homemade pop tart ... I will make a trip just for that.  The flavor is always changing and on this visit it was PBJ, so good.

One of the worst experiences I've had in a long time and the only reason they get two stars is because I know they tried.  Service was pretty friendly and I've been told by a good friend the pizza is excellent but it appeared someone didn't come in that night.

Well, this was not a good experience and that all rested soley on the person who waited on me. Why 3 stars? Because I am a fair person and I could tell by the quality of what I did eat and the other wait staff that this is probably a good place. Why review at all then? Because, that's the fair thing to do. No one is perfect so I'll count this as a blip and go back again.
Didn't see Sal's tip until I got in here.   :(  trying a breakfast sandwich anyway.
Lemon quinoa is the best and super healthy.  They have a cherry version now too.

sample_reviews.text:
Simple facts:

-New Day is one of the coolest places in Fountain Square
-The mead that New Day makes is awesome. You can drink it, but get a little extra and use it as a marinade for chicken on the grill. It's delicious!
-Tia and Brett are two of the coolest, most awesome and sincere people you'll ever meet.

If I was designing the perfect community that had everything I ever wanted, Flying Cupcake would be there. The smell of their Red Velvet cupcakes filling the air and the display cases would be packed with delicious treats like the Birthday Cake and the Wedding Day cupcakes.

I love stopping in and seeing what flavors they have for the day, but usually end up with one (or two) of my regular go to...

Stopped in on a Saturday morning for breakfast with a friend and was really impressed with this cute little place.

Coffee was delicious and quick to come out. We also each ordered a snack size savory crepe and couldn't finish them.. they were huge! Mine had ham in it and his had brie and blackberries and we split them down the middle. Seriously delicious!

Really enjoyed this place.

I feel like I really let my friends down with this one. It was my birthday, and this is the place I picked for dinner.  What better place to dine al fresco than atop the Fountain Square Theater Building  with the downtown Indianapolis skyline as the backdrop?

I've experienced better, and I've experienced worse. I'm afraid to say it, but the atmosphere at Henry's has changed over the last couple of years. Gone is the local coffee shop vibe where you could hang out with a book or surf the net and pass the day away. Now it's too commercial.. the food quality isn't quite the same, and they close at 7..
The cranberry sorbet is good mixed with vodka. It makes a nice little slushie. Grab a pint and try it.. you won't be disappointed.
Inside bottleworks Garage

This was another of my "Stumbled Upons".
 
Riding about looking for a new breakfast spot we drove by what my diner detecting eyes saw as one I had never tried.  As we strode up the ramp to the front door we saw that it was indeed a diner. My eyes nearly popped  out of my head when I saw it was called Izenberg's. I had discovered a Jewish diner/deli in Conshy of all places!!!!!

Fabulous "old-school " pizza 
made with love by the owner who was born in Italy. He also makes a nice variety of trendy pizzas.
It's not a fancy place. There is a small parking spot out back
 where you can pull up to get your pizza but that's the extent of the fuss at this place. 

What you get is really decent, authentic pizza.

Wanted to add as a tip but not tip area on this listing. I Still love this place but wanted to add that you might considering going inside to order if you see 4 or more cars in line. Food is made to order so it's at least 5 minutes per car. Last time I did the drive in I was car 6 or 7 and It was 30 minutes for my order.

They have no shades on the western facing  windows. NOR are there any remedies for it as stated by the manager.  They just aren't going to be purchasing window shades. Waitstaff  admit people always complain about it. 

We brought in a party of 30. 
So 15 of our party of 30 had to sit with the sun burning our retinas for an hour.

This place never changes as far as being inefficient. 7 Minutes for the clerk to find my item on his computer. Then he had to call the manager to give me change because he isn't permitted to. In the meantime she sends him to clear tables and hands my change to him to give me.
Check your bag b4 pulling away. 10 cold fries and requested sauce for my nuggets were not in bag. Happens to much at this location.
Soups are excellent.

sample_reviews.text: We really enjoyed our happy hour here at Luke. We were from oot and came for oyester happy hour and honestly don't have a lot of money. The place was very nice. I love These donuts! We get donut holes and take them to parties. We really enjoy. Except this time they were almost out of holes. Have you not tried donut drive in??? well get over there! Is it a drive in?? No they are not on roller skates. It is a counter place. So go in and get some donuts.

We loved gringo tacos! Great happy hour specials. Good salsa and chips and I liked the steak tacos. I'm partial to flour tortillas and I really liked the steak taco. I thought they were kinda filling with the chips and we also got the gringo fries. The wait staff is sooooo nice. We love this place! I think we went three times in one week.

I so love Jamaican food, yet when we moved here in Yardley 4 years ago the only nearby options were in Trenton.  Well no more, Paradise Jamaican Cuisine delivered big time for me.  

From the second I stepped foot in till walking out with my night's dinner was a perfect experience.  I was immediately greeted and helped.

WHOO-HOO, finally found a proper cajun New Orleans restaurant that delivers that awesome cajun flavor.  No surprise finding it in a major city but in the smack middle of crazy busy Reading Terminal in Philly; too funny and way too crowded.

Had a hankering for really good seafood, and not fried, simply boiled bucket seafood like in Mass, Cape Cod etc.  Sadly I can't find anything remotely like The Boiling House near home (Yardley PA).  I remember my days living in Hoboken NJ and going to Just BeCaws in Jersey City.

We never made it past our appetizer the tulip dumplings which was supposed to be a mix of shrimp and chicken it comes out very hard packed it's really no flavor the skins are very moist wet if you're not delicate the way shoe my type dumplings are flies are annoying the heck out of us and we decided to cancel our main water basin are feeling for the appetizer dish which I'll see in a photograph...

Yeah a local place that serves up a really good tasty brunch.  A no fuss place where they don't say no.

Fresh ingredients including the fruit.  Mexican flare to traditional breakfast items.

We loved the French toast with sliced bananas and strawberries.  Mickey cooked, not under not over cooked.
Don't look at the menu, have Preya and Chef Guru surprise you.  Awesome, excellent, fantastic service and food each and every time

Wow!!! This is one of the few restaurants in the Tampa/St. Pete area that actually looks like a classy restaurant that you'd see in big cities like New York City and Sidney, Australia. Almost an izakaya...just a little too easy to find. :D If you've been to Izakaya Den in Melbourne, Australia or Sake Bar Hagi 46 in New York City, you know what I mean.

For the most part, the food is awesome.

Ok, anyone who can make homemade hash browns as good/delicious/yummy/scrumpdeliouso/fantastico/perfecto as Maggie Mae's deserves 10 stars!!!!!

We go to many restaurants that claim to make good hash browns. They normally look and taste like canned hash browns. But, Maggie Mae's hash browns are worth additional visits.

The staff is nice and friendly. They always kept our coffee cups filled.

For Florida, Mazzaro's is a great Italian Market. The service is friendly and helpful.

It has:

1. a great variety of pasta, both homemade and ready-made
2. decent variety of cheese, tomato sauces, pastries, deli items
3. good variety of things like anchovies, balsamic vinegar, and coffee beans
4.

This restaurant should have a 5-star rating. The decor is elegant, chic, but still comfortable. The service is attentive, polite, and professional.

Unfortunately, the food was not good:

The Greatfruit: This cocktail is light, refreshing, and delicious. The grapefruit peel added a nice hint of citrus to the drink.

Tamarind Orange Chipotle Glaze Wings: This appetizer sounds promising.

Awesome and tasteful decor. Clean. Service is friendly and quick. Great music. Good coffee.

The only reason why I can't give this location a 5-star rating is because they don't have the yummy sandwiches found at the Kennedy location. *very sad face*
Must have all 4 tofu!!!!

hands down the best pizza in the Philadelphia area - at least by my reckoning. Pizza is like religion - no one is ever going to convince you that their pick is the best. But like a cheesesteak, or pork sandwich , its all about the bread. 

The pizza dough here is phenomenal - crispy , airy, chewy and charred. puurfection on a plate.

So I have to say I've been coming here for a long time and under the current management this place is absolutely phenomenal. 

Their Craft cocktails are solid, well executed with outstanding ideas and continued experimentation.  they impress -- some of the best in the area outside of downtown Philadelphia.

Honestly I can't review everything about this place -- but I can tell you this:

 the Montesano special hoagie is the best damn Italian hoagie I've had in a long long time. That's one hell of a nine dollar sandwich and I will be back to try more of their food

Say it ain't so! How does a place that was so so good, become so so bad? I am sorry to say this,  as I was one of the first to find the gem that was  Han Dynasty, I must report that it is not even a shell of its former self.

I can't remember the last time I went to a Chinese restaurant where the food wasn't even edible. Unfortunately, this will be my last visit.

Look, if you like the standard sports bar and are ordering beer and wings, I guess its ok. Little ambienance and none of the local "tappy" charm, but passable in a pinch. Personally, my preferences run to the less mundane. If there weren't better choices, I might return. Guess that's why there is chocolate and vanilla...

sample_reviews.text:
Recently moved here from California. Definitely my first Southern style breakfast! Bronuts are heavenly! 
Although breakfast Biscuits and gravy were fantastic, skipping breakfast and going straight for Bronuts and mimosas! I have never tasted orange juice this amazing! Coming back for sure regardless if line is around the corner. Surprisingly it moves very quickly!

This bakery is a hidden gem! Granted they are horrible at answering phone calls to place an order and I had to drive 10+ miles out of my way but all worth it for the tres leches cake they make. The half sheet almost broke my back...very heavy! It definitely was a hit with the entire army I fed with it! A sliver is truly enough to indulge in the richness!

Valentino's is definitely a hidden gem! Quality, food, service, freshness, and selection are spectacular! I cannot say enough about their wine list, their bread, the seafood pasta and veal dishes we had. It really was one of the better experiences I've had in. Nashville since moving here from Cali five months ago! It is definitely on my fav's list now!

I gave Ozeki another try today for lunch.  Where should I start? The service - Jessie, the waitress, although only a few weeks in, does not have a future in the food service industry! Manners & common sense are not part of her. Maybe coaching will help but common sense cannot next taught! Looks can only get you so far! 
The egg rolls were served to two of us.

Inchin came highly recommended. 
The only reason I am giving this establishment more than one star is because their drink menu is very unique and looks fantastic. However, the appetizer which was simple like a spring roll had something which resembled little chicken bone in them. 
Needless to say, we left after one drink and a couple of bites of appetizers.

Man do they know good mexican. I would highly recommend the Short rib tamale. It was insanely flavorful and I wish I hadn't filled up on cerviche and guac before it came out, but those were excellent too so no harm no foul.

Love how they do the checklist for the guac. Getting to try guac with chipotle and oaxaca cheese was a whole new experience of guac and I loved every second of it.

Oh my gosh was this delicious. Byo too fellas ;-) I got the green curry chicken which was really wonderful and my boyfriend's beef chow fun (i think it was) was the best noodle dish weve ever had. So much flavor! Everyone that got a noodle dish here struggled past the full point to eat it all because it was too good to leave.

This place was so so incredible. We went for brunch and enjoyed the heck out of everything. I got the duck benedict and it was so beautifully plated and delicious. They gave a generous amount of duck meat as well. They had some unique wing flavors and we tried some blue moon ones to share and the blue duck fries. Sooo worth any stomach heaviness after. Everything was perfect.

this was definitely an interesting one. We went for brunch and truthfully it wasn't too great. I got  smoked salmon eggs benedict. Could've gotten the same quality at a diner, but the service and atmosphere was lovely and it's certainly unique. I would definitely give it another shot with lunch or dinner.

Very typical Philly bar, but I always have a great time here. It's pretty packed...always, but worth it occasionally. You have to fight for a table for a bit, but once you get settled in, the noisy atmosphere gets the rest of your friends going and it's a great drunken time. Don't come in here with a headache!

4.5

It may have taken Nashville a while to go all in on the acai bowl craze/fad/trend (is it still a thing?)* but they're definitely a mainstay of the breakfast/replacement meal set. Rush Bowls does what it does very well and is in the absolute perfect location for it to continue to do its thing.

Today is Lynne's last day in business and the health(ier) food segment of the Nashville food scene is poorer for it. I don't know the details behind the closure but I know that this was the Chad's decision and not something that was forced. Whatever the reason, I will miss coming here.

In addition to the delicious food/juice options, I'll miss the good conversations and vibes at the store.

4.5

The folks in Donelson have been trying to tell us for a while: spend some time here. Things have changed. While natives and transplants alike were focused on this and that 'upcoming area,' Donelson slowly and surely raised its profile, added the businesses and waited for people to catch up.

It worked.

A day or errands caught me in Donelson.

Maddening. This should be a 4.5/5 star review. Nashville is at a tipping point when it comes to bars/restaurants. I have heard time and again how difficult it is to find/keep good FOH/kitchen talent but doing so is essential to the business plan. I don't want this city to fizzle out when there's so much potential yet to be tapped.

2.5

Let's start with the obvious: there are some concessions you have to make once you commit to buying sushi from a New England-styled fried fish restaurant. Let it be noted that a sushi-specific restaurant in Edgefield/East End would go over very well. In fact, I can think of more than a couple of potential locations for such.
Antioch, not nashville. Close to Rutherford county.
Closed until the 25th.

sample_reviews.text: Simply an excellent meal.  Savory miso soup, some great chotura (medium fatty) bluefin tuna sashimi, gyoza (pork and chicken dumplings) were hot, fresh, and served with the sauce crisps still attached, and served so that it was on top and the dumplings beneath - an interesting decision that added visually.  The tempura prawns and Alaskan makimono (sushi roll) were also top notch for me.

Nom  nom  nom  nom  Just had @indo.stl brand new street food menu. Curbside style. That was some good stuff. Complex, dense flavors. Tasty ingredients. Crispy, spicy, savory, sweet. When it was supposed to be that, it hit it right on. And don't you dare skip on the fried sweet bananas!

Really great takeout brunch for us.  Top notch, fresh ingredients prepared in delicious & appealing dishes.  Cinnamon toast crunch French toast had had the crunchy shell, generous fresh berries, and a delicious maple praline.  Sooo good.  The farmer's breakfast had a great option for avocado instead of meat, which the wife immediately took.  The toast was light yet crispy.

I dunno....This was supposed to be a pretty good place, and maybe it is for most, but it wasn't for me.  I mean, it's enjoyable enough inside, and our waiter for lunch was quite pleasant and informative.  But my hot ham & cheese sandwich disappointed.  It was described as hot ham slices, smoked gouda cheese, and spicy mustard on grilled bread.

You know, I hate to say "Meh, I've experienced better", but I have.  The donuts aren't bad, and there certainly is a lot of character here.  But there are a lot of good, independent donut shops in St. Louis, and Strange just ins't up with the others in my opinion.  They do have some neat options, like the gooey butter donut.  And some interesting choices like a donut topped with Fruity Pebbles.
