sample_reviews.text: I am in love with this store. There are gifts galore, including beautiful jewelry, toys, games, and loads of candy - especially hard to find stuff that is imported from Great Britain. I bought some very cool buttons and some candy. I was also intrigued by the bottles of vintage soda pop.

I am addicted. The ice cream here is worth waiting in line for, and seeking out in the narrow alley just north of Whyte Ave. The flavours are unique and never taste quite the way I expect. My favourite are the lemon curd and blueberry, and the sea salt caramel and goat's milk, but I am having so much fun making my way through the menu.

If you want dosa, this is the place to get it from, especially if you're in the west end. I had a masala dosa (filled with potato, served with three vegetarian dips), and it was delicious. The dosa itself was chewy and slightly sour, as it should be. Dosas are made with lentil and rice flour that is slightly fermented. The dips were slightly spicy, and included coconut, tomato, and mint.

I attended a Twitter gathering at Dynasty Century Palace for dim sum. I am glad our host was Asian, as he was able to translate what all the different dishes were that were being brought to our table. As a non-pork and non-seafood eater, my choices were rather limited. The onion cakes were too soft and chewy. I had a beef roll with ground beef in a soft wrapping, in what appeared to be soy sauce.

Crystal BBQ Noodle House used to be the Jasmine Noodle House - in fact, the original signage on the purple awning over the door remains, with a Crystal BBQ sign in the window. We were not sure at first we were even in the right place.

sample_reviews.text: This Papa John's is carry out only, maybe they all are carry out only, not sure.  They always send lots of coupons in the mail. You can earn a free pizza by ordering online and there are different deals online.  They have delivery.  It is a convenient location.

Convenient location inside Circle Center Mall in downtown Indianapolis. The have a brewery inside the restaurant with a nice variety of beer.  They have salads, sandwiches, flatbread, steaks, seafood, soup and desserts.  I love the chicken bruschetta salad.

They serve breakfast and lunch items.  Even when they are busy,  their service is great.  The food is delicious, their stuffed French toast is very filling, a half order is plenty.  It is located in a strip mall near other businesses & it us a decent size.  The prices are reasonable.
Food is really good, there are lots of dinner combination choices, including vegetarian.  The have different drink specials, the margaritas are good.
The drive thru line is efficient.  I like the Mcgriddles.  They have the typical McDonald's  food and a lot lay area for children.
Do call ahead seating for busy times.  You can do this through the BJ's app.
On the weekends, get here early!

sample_reviews.text: Not only can you mix the chocolate and coconut and get an Almond Joy-like frozen treat, but their back patio is shady and relaxing, even on a 95 degree scorcher of a day.

You know what's better than hanging at Black Thorn with a pie and a pint? The smokeless air. I know, I know. It's a pub. If they didn't serve more pizzas than beer, I'd be all over the "go ahead and smoke" rules. But they serve more food than drink. Now I can go there and actually sit and eat. I'm in dive bar heaven.

Trips to Crown Candy are rare for me, as our hours don't always match up. So when a friend invited me along for a candy-buying ride, I cajoled him into lunch as well.  I usually get the Heart Stopper, but I like to mix it up. The chili tamale is delicious (if a bit on the salty side) and Paul's egg salad sandwich must have contained at least a half dozen eggs.

This place is open seemingly endless hours. My sister-in-law gets her chocolate long johns from here (and once ate six and one sitting!) I think they are just okay. I have received less-than-fresh donuts here, and that's a no-no in my donut book.

The ONLY reason we visited this establishment is because we had a gift card, and were headed down to the Blues game. Otherwise, I had pretty successfully avoided this "seafood" restaurant.
I had already read some reviews, and braced myself for service issues. Our server wasn't bad at all, but the timing of the kitchen was really off. Salads arrived, and fifteen seconds later, so did our entrees.

sample_reviews.text:
Let me preface my review by saying this is not yolk. This is not Eggs and I. This is not Le Peep. I could go on and on, just want it to be known that this is not the standard brunch spot with standard brunch fare. It is so much more than that. 

I have lived in Indy for greater than 6 years now, and have been hearing and reading about Milktooth since its conception.

One of the more underrated breweries in the city. Their beer is stellar. Solid for all types of beer lovers whether fan of hoppy or darks. Usually have close to 15 or so on tap at one time. Good space with some standard board games and Jenga to play. Cool historic building.

I really liked this place. Was nice environment in the bottleworks hotel across from garage food hall. Big space to sit indoor or out on patio. Uses roaster out of Wisconsin (rub). Had single origin Ethiopian on drip which was very good and their cold brew nice and smooth, light bodied. Their oatmeal blueberry cookies were delightful. Heated up with some whipped cream even more delightful.

This place is essentially the chipotle or qdoba of Indian food. You choose your style, weather it be bowl, naan pizza, or wrap, then choose meats, sauces, add ins, etc. Being a steamed table, the temperature and freshness of the food varies between choices. For example, I ordered one chicken samosa and one veg samosa, and both were barely warm.

First of all, I commemorate the owners of this restaurant for the money poured into their facilities with renovations. The decor is all updated and the whole feel of the place is much more nice and clean. Secondly, the food is still very much the same. I have been to this restaurant a couple of times prior to the renovations and once after, and have never been impressed by the food.

I'm not a fan of fake meat, so I skipped the burgers and sandwiches and ordered the udon noodle salad.

Happened by when there was no line. Got a mango water ice: delicious with bits of mango throughout. So good, so refreshing on a hot day. 

My husband got a gelati with vanilla ice cream and lime ice. The lime was soooo good. So much fresh lime flavor. 

Definitely in my top 3 water ice shops. 

So glad I made it before they close for winter. I'll be counting the days until spring so I can return.

Chain restaurants suck.  But when you have two tired children in tow, burgers, fries, free balloons, crayons, and dancing waitstaff are just the ticket.  It saved our afternoon.  The burgers and shakes are actually pretty good.
Fries were hot from the oil, which is good. The burger was super-thin and lost in the bun; not very flavorful, either.  My family loves their shakes!

sample_reviews.text:
Very fresh fish.

When I worked for Yelp, I was given a list of some of the top restaurants in Tampa Bay. Pacific Counter was on that list. I had never heard of it before so I texted my old boss, Brett N., asking if he had any idea about the place. He essentially told me if I interviewed for the position now and said I didn't know Pacific Counter, I wouldn't get hired.

Ginger turmeric tea blows me away. 

I'm going to be honest, I had thirty minutes to kill, I was in the area, and I needed to use a restroom. The Blind Tiger had a restroom for me to use and I could sit and do work after. 

My neighborhood Blind Tiger is in Westchase and I had the ginger turmeric tea there. It was amazing, so I ordered it here as well.

They have a traveling froyo truck that will come to your neighborhood during COVID-19!

My neighbors are doing all the work for me and arranging for food trucks to come to our neighborhood. All I have to do it put it on my calendar and show up. These are the best type of neighbors. 

The froyo truck parked in my neighbor's driveway and was there between 4 p.m. an 7 p.m. on a Wednesday.

Might be newly opened pains, but I wasn't a fan.

It's tough to write a review based on one experience, but with so many awesome restaurants in Tampa Bay if I dislike a meal I am very unlikely to ever go back. I really disliked the black bean burger - it was to the point that I ate around the patty and basically left it sitting on my plate. It was incredibly dry.

Valentine's Day Dinner Review: Beautiful restaurant, meh food.

For Valentine's Day they have a prix fixe menu of one appetizer, one soup/salad, and one entree for (depending on which entree you choose) around $50 - $70.  Weirdly enough it is the dessert that you can choose to buy a la carte (all choices were $6).

As others have mentioned, the view is spectacular.
Valet is free before 3 pm and $5 after 3 pm
Medium size at lunch is huge! I had enough for lunch and dinner.

sample_reviews.text: This new restaurant was very busy and happy we made a reservation for 5PM.  We went in and there was one person in front of us waiting and then another 10 or so arrived while they were trying to figure out the reservation and seating. We've ordered a few times from Sam's.  The last time was directly calling them rather than using a third party food delivery as they offer free delivery over $18.  I called at 6PM and it took an hour for the food to arrive but it was hot when arrived.

I've been coming to this restaurant since I was a kid and recently had a business lunch here and found the food to be the same, delicious! The décor isn't one to write home about, primarily greenish booths that can be a little tight for those with a belly.

I had never heard of this place until a colleague suggested we go.  I was a little leary as the entrance is in the alley but when we went in, it was decent decor.  I decided on the bento box which came with soup, chicken teriyaki, tempura, and sushi.  Compared to other places, it was average.  I found the chicken to be a bit bland and sauced up.

Doans is in a great location that makes it easy for business lunches.  It gets busy though, so I recommend making a reservation.  We arrived for our noon reservation and were seated in a booth.  I ordered my regular vermicilli bowl and this time with the grilled beef for $9.45.  The beef was a bit oily and wasn't that tasty, and it was laid upon a bed of noodles.

Alright. My name is Jen and I have a problem...

This particular location (Ajo/6th) is one of the better ones I've been to.

sample_reviews.text:
This place has a fond place in my heart. 

When I first moved into the neighborhood Superior Grill was always a nice Wednesday date night to get your chips and queso on. Now that I actually live a lot closer than before, I rarely if ever get a chance to just walk over and have some good ole Tex-Mex.

As I have said before, I am not much of a breakfast guy, but when it is the weekend and the lady wants to go try a new spot, you do what you are told sometimes. Thankfully, my lady is awesome and knows the best places to try. That my friends, is why I am writing about Dots Diner. 

If you listen to the radio on your drive into work in New Orleans, you have heard the commercials.

Fantastic! 

Came here before catching an outdoor concert. It was nice outside so we decided to sit outside. Our server was very knowledgeable and super nice! 

We got the Carne Asada, which was super delicious. Order it medium rare, just trust me! We also got the fajitas which were also pretty good. Everything about what we ordered here was top notch Mexican.

I come here a lot for the Saturday donut pickup for football players since it is so close to where I work. The donuts are always good.

I finally tried their breakfast. The food was pretty decent and freshly made. They were out of a lot of items but I was still able to get a pretty decent breakfast/brunch! Again, the donuts are top notch and their breakfast is decent enough to get you through.

I love restaurants that love to have a good mixture of New Orleans and Italian cuisine. Porter and Luke's is a perfect combination of both! The portion sizes are immense! If you think about ordering an item and expecting something light think again bud because this place does not believe in small portions. Trust me though, I love that about this place!

So here's a good thing, the yogurt is actually pretty darn delicious.

The downer is, two cups that were not even half full cost me 10 bucks... ouch.
WTF wing are AWESOME. Beer.is great. Come here and embrace the win.

sample_reviews.text: This is my favorite Vietnamese restaurant in NOLA right now. This is both due to the quality of food and service that this place gives its customers. They are the sweetest people and the food is so good. I have had pho on several days where I have not been 100% and it has perked me right up.

Before I called NOLA home, I was fascinated by its old school steakhouses. This is the city where Ruth's Chris started for crying out loud. I was determined to eat at these iconic places at some point. The price point was really what was throwing me off. Well with Mr. Steakman himself, my dad, in town I decided it was time to man up and test the water of one these establishments.

This year I have been reunited with my love of all things tiki. I wanted a big tiki bar birthday blowout with classic (read: not sickening sweet) drinks, 60's music, and an atmosphere where all my friends could hang out and have a blast. Well I didn't get my birthday party, but I did get Tiki Tolteca this summer and it almost makes up for everything else. I love this place so much.

I went to Santa Fe the other night because Lola's was packed and the group I was entertaining were far from patient. So, we hopped across the road to Santa Fe. I had never eaten there and was not really expecting much. Besides a decent margarita I didn't get much.

Praline Connection has been one of my biggest letdowns in New Orleans so far.  They claim to be a true "soul food" restaurant and although reviews varied, it seemed to be a place that I should check out. So, one night before hitting some music spots on Frenchmen St, we decided to stop by and try the Praline Connection. There must have been 12 servers and only two full tables in the place.
No beers on tap but good selection of Abita and Nola beers.
Your finest champagne, sir!

sample_reviews.text: Some of my coworkers and I were in town for training and the president of our company had mentioned this place to try. Never in a million years would I have found this place by myself, so I was happy we were in a group. Very small, cozy feel to it and when I saw the sign for milkshakes, I was in heaven! I ordered the chicken philly with onions and the fries with a side of cheese.

My friend and I were downtown and it was Devour so we wanted to check out a new place. We chose Burger Study and loved that the garage was right next door! It was raining and we were being wimps! We started with the fried avocado, which is heavily breaded as a heads up. Not really what we were looking for so we scraped off a lot of the breading and ate the avocado!

We got there five minutes before they opened and there was already a line for the drive through!  Dining room is closed so you have to through the drive through to order.  They have signs posted with menu items, flavors, and toppings by the entrance.  You get to the first window and place your order and then pull around back to the front and they will bring out your order to you.

Ok, so love that there was a dog to greet us upon entry! So friendly! We went up to bar and the lady was very informative. She gave everyone a sample of every beer they had on tap. I. didn't get any since I was driving. I tasted some of my husband's though and really only liked two of them. You can buy "bullets" which is 32oz bottles as carry out, but you couldn't drink there otherwise.

Very cool location, other than trying to figure out the parking situation. I never could figure out how to get into the lot. That could just be me, but there was open parking on Mass ave so I just did that. Very pleasant host and server. Server was super knowledgeable when it came to the dishes and drinks.
They do not take reservations and your whole party needs to be present to be seated.  No hard liquor license right now.

sample_reviews.text: What a quaint little gem in Broadripple Village. The atmosphere is whimsical filled with lots of pink and white flowers. You are met by the most friendliest staff who are very anxious to explain the decadence that awaits you. I had the prize winning Earl's Court chocolate cake, the very recipe that launched this place into existence. Every bite was worth every calorie.

Every year I get the opportunity to volunteer at this event to raise money for the Leukemia & Lymphoma Society . I truly thank them for supporting such an awesome cause. 

The event is a well run machine. The Brewers Guild of Indiana recruits volunteers, people like you and me to help make this event run smoothly. It features only Indiana breweries.

I've eaten at Delicia, La Mulita's sister restaurant a few times but had never ventured here. The question I'm asking myself is, why? This little unassuming place has the best shrimp tacos in the Broadripple area.  Believe me I think I have hit them all. The simplicity, the shrimp was cooked perfectly with just the right amount of seasoning.

So I go to a Pancake house and order an omelette. First let's back up. This place is all the rave over Nashville. It was hyped, so I was hyped. The line stretched a half block but moved quickly.  We were seated in about 30 mins. 

The place is nostalgic, with simple German style decor. The staff uber friendly. They had over 21 different pancakes to choose from. But, none gluten free.
Surprisingly good food for an off track betting spot. The grilled shrimp appetizers are a must. You won't be disappointed.
Always delicious and always friendly.
Sunday Mimosas

Not sure why this place has such low rating as of my writing my review? 

I came here with coworkers a random Thursday morning and was shocked at how dead it was. Maybe it's the odd location or maybe everyone had already came in and had their breakfast before work? 

I was quickly greeted when I entered. I ordered a green tea, and it was delicious.

I was a bit hesitant in trying this space out as I was fearful that it was going to be exactly like the Three Carrots City Market location. The City Market location is a bit too fried for me and just doesn't do much for me. 

I've been a vegetarian for nearly 5 years so I've had a fair share of vegan/vegetarian meals in those past five years.

The saying "All Alidi's are the same" just isn't so true anymore. 

This newly-opened Aldi took things to the next level for me. Wowza. It's spacious, airy and open, and feels like an actual market/grocery store. I'm used to getting very up close and personal with other shoppers at my usual go-to Aldi's as they're so small and petite.

Well, it's hard to even force yourself to go to a place when you walk by the kitchen door (it was opened in the Marsh parking lot) and see one of the chefs drinking from the water bowl and spitting into one of the sinks where food was nearby.

I will say that with the high amount of five star reviews from fellow Elite members for Indy Taco, I did have some sort of expectations going in. 

I love the location because it's right near my apartment but I can see how the location could also be a flaw. We went on a Friday evening at 7pm, and it was quite busy. There were two others in line and more than half of the seats were taken.

Grand new opening at new location (full restaurant) opens August 10, 2021! 

Must try the coconut brownie!
Breakfast sandwiches are premade so you cannot request any changes or substitutions!

I've only gone here once...and cannot wait to return. Myself and my fiance went on a weeknight for dinner. Orders as follows:

Me- Truffle burger (medium rare) ,regular fries
Babylove-Bacon cheeseburger (well-done), truffle fries

and we both shared a strawberry milkshake. 

Everything was fresh and to perfection. Great and prompt staff as well.

Stopped in here randomly for lunch on Friday afternoon with my fiance after ink sessions at Eddie's. We were in the mood for sushi and figured we'd give it a try since we noticed a board with specials earlier. 

Started out with fried calamari and some drinks (a soda and a liter of sake). Got the 2 & 3 roll specials as our entrees. Everything was excellent.

Helpful staff, great sales, and a vast selection. You can easily do all your shopping one place. Even if your beer snob friend needs that imported clusterfuck limited edition stoudt. They probably have atleast have other selections from the brewery if they don't carry it already.

Shit's changed, man.

1) 2 people to a hookah at $12+ tax currently. Still cheap but....
2)Staff is now very rude and unwelcoming for the most part.

Time to find a new spot.

Decided to try them on Grubhub since we were in the mood for cheap Chinese. Order came on time. 

While not bad, the food felt overwhelmed by veggies. My fiance's chow mein was literally a mound of  onions with bits of shredded pork here and there. 

When we called back to exchange it, they couldn't find our order and hung up on me when I went to search for the  receipt or at least look it up.
$2 Miller High Lifes 7 days a week now. Jen is also one of the best bartenders ever. :)

sample_reviews.text:
What's not to love about an Aimee Olexy and Stephen Starr collaboration? This new spot has all the ingredients for a match made in heaven. 

Although it only opened in the Winter of 2018, I felt like eons had passed before my first visit in the middle of May 2018. Trust me, it had been number one on my list of places to try for a while. 

So, my first visit was during lunch hours.

I don't know how long I've been saying that if the world was ending or if I was dying, the first thing I'd do is rush to Vetri. Thankfully, neither scenario happened, and I was privileged to finally dine there last night. 

With that being said, I obviously preemptively put Vetri Cucina on a pedestal surrounded with unrealistic hype.

Suraya lives up to all of the hype, as you momentarily get whisked away from Fishtown and into a Lebanese culinary delight.

I snagged a dinner table for two at an early evening time slot of a holiday week. There was also an option to sit at the counter, but I was happy with our cozy table against the wall. 

Suraya was much brighter than most Middle Eastern restaurants I've ever been too.

Why, why, why do I keep going back to Night Market?? Isn't the definition of insanity doing the same thing over and over again and expecting different results? I don't know why I keep expecting to have a good experience at a Night Market. 

In theory this sounds like a cool event. Well, apparently by the size of the crowd I'm not the only one that thinks this.

I was so excited to bring my husband back to La Collina after my lunch a few months back. Last night we were driving by on 76 and thought this would be the perfect opportunity to give it a try. Since we were trying for a last minute reservation, we called ahead and confirmed they could accommodate. 

So we should have just left upon arrival.
Coffee cup sizes aren't labeled well.
JetBlue treats customers like humans vs cattle. What a luxury!

sample_reviews.text:
Came out here for a gathering of Main Line Yelpers and had a lovely time! The outdoor patio area is festive, spacious, and comfortable. I would come back here in a heartbeat to soak up the sun and enjoy drinks with friends.

The inside was sleek, dimly lit, and also comfortable. I felt very relaxed and enjoyed my evening.

Walking into Ariana feels like being invited into your extremely cool friend's living room. Beautifully ornate rugs, padded chairs, colorful wall hangings, sweet shisha smoke hanging in the air, delicious smells wafting from the kitchen, and a hip, diverse crowd in deep states of chillness and relaxation.

As I walked down narrow Tilton Street to join the pals I was meeting at Czerw's, I noticed a door slightly ajar, revealing stacks of fragrant wood and huge, lovely slabs of hanging bacon in the smoke room.

Gross. The restaurant is dirty, and tables were allowed to sit forever with used trays and half-eaten food on them. The service was slow and it wasn't that crowded. I ordered a shrimp platter, an order of mozzarella sticks, and a root beer, which came up to $16. Steep!

My mozzarella sticks tasted awful--wayyy too salty, first of all, and they were just frozen sticks out of a box.

Awful! I was treated like a criminal here because I wanted to have my check-card run as a credit payment for my $5.65 worth of cat food.

First, the cashier very aggressively asked for my ID. OK, but why so aggressive and borderline-accusatory about it?
Handcut fries here are legit.
Great spot! Cozy seating, warm and friendly service.

i used to work only a few blocks away from here, which made it incredibly easy to stop by for lunch. those were the days... i've tried a little bit of everything on the menu by this point, even though i still rely on my two favorites - chicken with garlic sauce and dan dan noodles.

other good picks include: 
- wontons in chili oil
- cold sesame noodles
- eggplant with garlic sauce
- dry pot...

the quality of their food and service were stellar during our 3 hour long visit. if you enjoy italian food, fine dining, and/or tasting menus, you'll be over the moon enjoying an evening at vetri.

all of the dishes were phenomenal, but there were a few that stood out and would make the perfect line-up in my book: the sweet onion crepe, tagliolini with king crab and caviar, and pistachio flan...

their tagliatelli al limone with freshly grated Sicilian bottarga is mind-blowing!  if I were to know when my last meal on earth would be, I would make sure to have double portions of this pasta on my table. it has the perfect balance of brine from the bottarga and acid from the lemon - which brightens up the entire dish and keeps you coming back for more.

seriously, it is a must try!

I LOVE Cajun/Creole cuisine. I would say Beck's is probably one of the better options in the area if you're craving some food from New Orleans, but it just doesn't compare to the food down south. We had the jambalaya, shrimp po' boy and a small side of their blackened chicken alfredo.

I've been meaning to check this place out for a while - Philly's been stepping up its ramen game, yay!

However... Cheu is not a ramen shop. They serve a variety of noodle dishes - only one of which is actually ramen. The miso ramen was good, but I probably wouldn't come back to Cheu for it.
They have my favorite drink in the city - the frozen blood orange margarita!

I went on a diet and then I went to dinner at the Khyber. Everybody, let's have a big LOL. 

This is NOT the place to go if you're looking for a light meal. Bacon grease popcorn was salty and crunchy and god, heaven, really. Nachos were a major disappointment (chips, cheese, sour cream, chives. No tomatoes, guac, beans, nada).

I love this place.

Top recs: the coconut curry and the wings. The wings are insane.
Be aware of: how absurdly heavy the kimchi/matzo ball soup is. It's a LOT.
Other good stuff: the service, dumplings, great beers on draft. 

It's not cheap. There's usually a wait. They'll give away your table if you don't get there quick enough.

I finally went to In Riva on Saturday afternoon with friends, after a lovely hike in Wissahickon. It's only about a 10 minute drive from the park, and you don't have to worry about finding parking in East Falls- they have their own lot. 

We were celebrating Becca's birthday, and ordered two salads and three pizzas to share between the five of us, which was a perfect amount of food.

Ugh. My boyfriend and I had an hour to kill before the 9:30 viewing of Black Swan at the Ritz Saturday night. Zack was hungry and wanted something to eat-- Pagoda was right next door to the theatre, so we thought we could get in and out relatively quickly. We we were wrong.

We stood in front of a woman (presumably the owner) for about five minutes as she ran credit cards for other customers.

Lee How Fook was wildly disappointing last night.

After a day at the Midtown Village Fall Festival, our group of four decided to keep walking north and feed our hungry bellies with some quality Chinese food.
No longer does gf biscuit bfast sammies
Don't sleep on the wings. So good!

This was my second visit to Ritter's this week.  I prefer the W 57th location over the rest of the city.  Two words: Blueberry. Glaciers.  I can't get enough of them and this location serves them creamy and well blended.  

If you love ice cream, the custard at Ritter's will make your heart and belly happy!

I have been here a couple of times now and can say that have never had a bad thing to eat, or drink of course! The menu is super fun and being Latina, I'm always a little weird when I see things like "chorizo, tamales, empanadas" but let me tell you that Livery does it well.  

Kudos and have fun trying everything on the menu!!

I stopped by Nook on a random stroll and I was SO happy I did.  I don't follow a paleo diet by any means but I do try to stay away from dairy and grains as much as I can and let me tell you...in love.

There is something really for everyone on the menu. I had an omelette and a side of greens. My favorite thing was the chocolate pudding. Secret ingredient: avocado. It was perfect.

Revolucion is one of my favorite places in the city, mainly because of the margaritas. The food is always pretty good and the service is quick (unless they're busy!). 

I absolutely looooove the salsas.  I can just keep on eating them with those yummy chips. I wish you could mix your tacos, but even though you can't they're pretty darn good!

The pizza is always great!  I also started with the tomato bruschetta and it was delicious.  My friend and I shared a small pizza and 2 starters and had pizza left over for yummy lunch the next day. Can't wait to go back.
You have to eat the taquitos!!!!!
Yes, you do NEED the guacamole.

sample_reviews.text: Thai Orchid has all the elements of a good restaurant: sketchy and random location, ugly/ghetto décor (ok, it's actually pretty nice inside but I was trying to prove a point) and most important, tasty food. I remember asking my buddy about this place back in the summer and wanting to check it out.

Numchok Wilai is one of my favourite Thai places in the city. I've been there at least half a dozen times by now, both for lunch and dinner, and have always had an excellent meal. You know it's going to be good when half the people you invite aren't even aware of it's location. Never judge a restaurant by it's exterior. Or in my books, the shabbier looking, the better.

Don Day is easily one of my favourite Korean places in the city. I've been here a handful of times now since my original post (see here) and have yet to experience a bad meal. This is a quintessential hole in the wall, industrial restaurant that seems to be scattered all over Edmonton.

The moment you step through DD's doors, you're greeted with a heavenly aroma.

I don't even know where to start with this review but maaaaaaaaaan, every time that I go to Dream Tea, I die inside. With four locations in the city now, the Dream Tea empire is taking over Edmonton. Thank god there's a couple new BBT shops in town to give them some much needed competition (step it up Yumioca, Purple Bubble Tea and Simply Yours Cafe).

I've been to their OG location too many...

Yikes, Buster's. What is up with the $$$ prices?  Take a look at their menu - a SMALL pizza is $18! Wow. Lucky for me, this was a work lunch so I didn't have to pay. If I did, there's no way I would order from here.

One item you should avoid like the plague is the caesar salad ($3.99).

So, full disclosure: I have not been in the store yet, but I have a really nice coworker who's brought Donut Refinery donuts to our office a few times. I can say without hesitation these are the best donuts I've ever had, ever...and I've never been someone who loves donuts.

Why are these great?

1) Build your own creation. You pick the glaze + topping.

When I stay at a hotel, there's always something off about the experience that I don't love. The Loews Philadelphia Hotel is my favorite hotel stay, hands down, of recent memory!

Reasons to love the Loews:

+ Convenient location in the heart of downtown Philly.

My experience at Budweiser in STL wasn't typical - I was there for a private event - but they pulled out all the stops. This is practically a college campus for beer. There are so many buildings and it seems to go on forever!

First stop: a photo op alongside the Cheers wall. Luckily for us, a Clydesdale was there, too!

I like Chipotle, but this location isn't at the top of my list.

For starters, I got there and they were out of chicken. And veggies. Like out. Granted, it was pre-5pm so not quite dinnertime rush yet, but still. The nice lady did offer me a free drink while I was waiting for the chicken to finish cooking.

Bonus: it was the best Chipotle chicken I've ever had.

Did you know there's a Kroger tucked away off the main drag in Broad Ripple? Me neither! It's an old school Kroger that feels like it's stuck in the 1980s, but it gets the job done if you're looking for something basic. The layout is a bit odd, and it feels more like a glorified CVS than an actual grocery store. But, it's convenient if you're cruising through Broad Ripple.
Head inside to the Pro Shop to purchase drinks to take out to enjoy on the new Midtown Plaza! Beer, cider and soft drinks.
Opening July 2!

sample_reviews.text: What an awesome diner. As we were sat down our waitress was there with water and a friendly face. We placed our drink order and settled into the menu that is not huge by diner standards but has many more options than anyone needs. I settled on a lunch special, the albacore tuna on croissant, it also came with fries, soup and a dessert for 10$!!

Always a helping hand when you need one here. Any question you may have they are right there with an answer and even with all the help their prices are the best in town. I just recently joined the canal club so we get even more $$ off on some of our favorites and I can even fill my grower up here!!

35 Tv's with every game possible going on is what we were looking for and we got that plus some pretty good eats on the cheap! Our table ended up going for the 100oz draft beer which sounds like a lot but not really for 4 guys. It's just easier to have your own tap right at the table. 

We went for the nachos to start with all the meats as well as jalapeño and guacamole.

I don't see what the big deal is with this "organic quick serve" style burger chain. The burger itself was kind of bland, but the toppings made up for that. Our experience was just not overall that pleasant. The store seemed dim, weird thing to feel when walking into a quick serve joint. We waited 19 minutes just to order and then exactly 30 before the food was brought to our table.

So we had an up and down experience here. We weren't greeted by our waitress within the first 5 minutes but by then we had to use the restroom.  Upon coming back and hanging out a bit the manager noticed we had menus still and quickly apologized and got us going. This happens so no biggie, good to see the management paying attention and on the floor.
In a pinch they quickly made us 11 pizzas for our party! Delivered on time in less than an hour! Nice!
Why the 3 beverage vending machines outside the store are seemingly always out of order is beyond me.

sample_reviews.text: Whataburger my inner Texan craves thee!!  One of the cleanest and quickest Whataburgers in Tucson.  Make sure to order their specialty burgers (A1 Thick N Hearty, Monterrey Melt, etc), since they wipe the floor with In-N-Out's tiny burgers.

Even nicer than the one that closed in Marana.  Came on Friday afternoon and got their happy hour which is amazing. Was surprised to see they still have the same deals. Got wings, appetizer, fries, and two 25 oz beers for 24 bucks which is awesome. Our server got our food quick and the food was as good as I remember it. I'll definitely be back.

Finally Tucson has a Barro's!!  Been waiting forever to see one here, and alas the day is here. The restaurant itself is clean and beautiful. The food is amazing, especially the lunch specials. The wife and I got 6 slices of pizza two drinks all for 13 dollars. Even their regular specials are pretty cheap. A great addition to the Marana area.

Wow looking at the ratings is hilarious.  It definitely deserves one star.  I tried to order pizza on their website and it wouldn't work.  The wife tried numerous times, so we gave and decided to order by phone.  Huge mistake.  Let's just say this place is no bueno and leave it at that.

Okay, I thought, I'll just buy them on eBay, well at least the one trilogy they said they didn't have.  Not only did they have it, but they had at least ten copies!!!  Okay, I'll buy the other trilogy I though since I didn't buy that one on eBay.

sample_reviews.text: Bailey's Range is one of the best spots for a burger in St. Louis. The menu offers a vast variety of burgers, shakes, and local beers. I ate here many times while working downtown and despite the restaurant always having a steady lunch crowd, I was always seated quickly.

My boyfriend and I had a wonderful evening celebrating our anniversary at Carmine's. The food was FANTASTIC and the staff was very accommodating and thoughtful. The dirty martini that I had was amazing, and variety of old fashions on the drink menu were impressive. I suggest the dark cherry.

I have had some of the best sandwiches in the city from Ninth Street Deli. Everything on the menu is delicious and prepared quickly and fresh. The punch card is an awesome incentive to dine frequently, and the staff is very laid back and welcoming. The Cajun bacon is a unique and delicious choice, different from anything I've ever had in a sandwich before.

First time dining at this location and the service at Cheddar's was absolutely terrible. We saw our waitress a total of three times during our dining experience, once to take our order, once to deliver the order (almost an hour after placing the order), and once to hand us the check - with not so much as a "thank you for coming in," or "sorry about your wait" with the check delivery.

This is my new go-to restaurant in Edwardsville. The rotating menu makes for a new and unique dining experience each visit, and I have never had a bad meal here. Everyone in my family can find something they like on the menu, and their staff is attentive and personable. Social Gastropub's deals on Groupon make this place even easier to love! Definitely worth a look.

sample_reviews.text:
Restaurant des Familes is one of my favorite restaurants. I have dined here many times over the years and have never had anything less than a great meal.

The first thing you notice as you pull in to the parking lot is the restaurant building. Built in the Acadian style, the restaurant appears to be an old home from the outside.

The Steamboat Natchez is a must see for any visitor to New Orleans.

My love of the Natchez stretches back all the way to my childhood. I realize most readers don't want to hear about that so I'll start with why everyone should take a cruise on-board this ship.

I love Moe's.

I'm not sure I've ever give a fast food joint five stars, but Moe's earns that rating. Here's why:

Convenience

I don't always have an hour or more for lunch. Sometimes I need something quick yet good that I can eat on the go in my car. It's not ideal, but I'm a busy dude. Moe's offers online ordering, paying and has a convenient drive up window for online orders.

New Orleans is world renowned for having amazing food. Our chefs win awards and some have even gone on to become TV stars. Apparently the aquarium did not get that memo when they opened their food court. The food here is downright awful.

I realize that the aquarium primarily caters to children and families, but how about throwing the parents a bone and open a good food place?

This station is a great example of why Yelpers should review gas stations.

First, I will begin with the reason you will likely find yourself here. This station is the last place to stop for fuel before The Causeway Bridge.
They now have BBQ shrimp every Friday during lent!
The specials that come from the mind of Chef Rob never cease to amaze me. If they have the special brisket and debris sandwich get it. It's phenomenal.

sample_reviews.text: I love a good bowl of Ramen, and this is the place to get it!  We got here at lunchtime today, and were told it be a 30 to 45 minute wait. We were pleasantly surprised when we ended up waiting less than 30 minutes.  The set up inside is nice with 2 bigger, longer tables, and booths along the side, so there's plenty of seating.  Our server was excellent checking back in on us several times.

This food truck came to us at work this week, and I hadn't tried this one before.  It was awesome!  I had the special, which was lemongrass chicken, it was so full of flavors!  I loved the fresh herbs on top!  A good-sized portion of chicken, rice, veggies, and spice, and it was just $7.00 - a good price for a food truck dish!

We went for the 5 before 5 on a Friday.  It was hard to find parking, it was completely packed just 2 weeks after opening, we were pleasantly surprised to get seats though.  I love the décor with big plants, lamps, walls of wine, indoor and outdoor seating, a bar, and different types and  sizes of tables, you will find a comfortable spot for sure!

I went here with my husband after "Meet me at Maynards"  We forgot to show them our hand stamp, and went back after only to be told there was nothing they now do to give us the discount.  I mentioned sometimes a place will give a free churro, or something like that (they're a dollar) , but no, just got told no... not good customer service.

Had dinner here with a friend last night.  I love the city feel, and the windows, however, we were placed in the room in the back, which was ok, but loud, with several families with loud kids.  I had the chili relleno, which was cooked well, how it should be.  Usually is overcooked, but this was perfectly al dente.  It was pricey at 16.00 for a chili relleno with no meat.
Grilled peppers are the best!
Cash is preferred if possible!

sample_reviews.text: The various Tria locations are all great, and this one - perhaps the original Tria? - is perhaps my favorite, being a bit more roomy compared to the Tria West. Seating upstairs provides a somewhat quieter area. I recommend getting in a bit early, as the place does fill up. Incredible wines and beers, as well as the food (tapas size) selections.

What's not to like about a place where you can walk in, get a totally satisfying lunch in a matter of minutes from the buffet (plenty of vegan/vegetarian choices, traditional Indian dishes), all for $4.29. Mostly a takeout place, it's also handy for that quick lunch. For eating-in, you can also order from their menu, as the buffet is somewhat limited compared to the full menu.

Finally had a chance to stop in, having known B-2 and the original Benna's very well. I was impressed. Great windows onto south Broad. They only had one plain bagel left when I got there for a late lunch, so I went with the whole grain bread for the mozzarella/basil sandwich. Excellent! The coffee is great, and staff very friendly. My new fav place to grab a coffee or quick bite in the old 'hood.

Perhaps my expectations were too high for this Fresh Market - quite frankly, I was disappointed with it. I had not shopped in one before, and was expecting a more high-end experience all around. For me, the Acme will remain the chain-store source for many routine product purchases, while the Weaver's Way Coop or even Whole Foods locations offer much more in terms of specialty and bulk food...

Stopped in most recently for a quick beer and sandwich at the bar. The bartender, Eric, of the "Five Erics" fame, was excellently attentive. I was a little worried that my food order, the eggplant parm sandwich, might take too long in the kitchen, but - voilà! - it appeared so quickly, I thought it must be someone else's order. It's a great sandwich, which you can get either with fries or salad.
Cask is on happy hour list!
Monthly art exhibits!

This review has been sitting in my draft inbox for way too damn long. 

Oh Chef Nina Compton,

Thank you. Thank you for sharing your beautiful mind with us. Thank you for letting us indulge in your creations. Thank you for helping shape the city.

I first tried CL a few years back when I was in town for work and we reserved a tasting for about a group of 12.

Mawi for the win!!!

This place is like a little slice of heaven for me and reminds me so much of my hometown, El Paso. Like a specialty little shop, they knock it out of the park for tortillas, tostadas, tostada chips, salsas, and a few other items. Will and his family are the absolute sweetest.

Y'all, I have no problem driving from Uptown to Metairie for their food products.

I was always willing to withdraw cash to get my Creole Creamery fix; however, they now accept cards and I can go swipe my card like Paris Hilton at Juicy Couture circa early 2000s.

They have some of the best flavors ever. I can't get enough of them.

I've ordered breakfast from here through Uber Eats when I'm hungover and not in the mood to get out of bed or cook at that. It's held up perfectly fine and gets the job done. It's within a short distance so I get the food delivered fairly quick. Pricing is more than fair and I do like the hashbrowns here.

I'm on a boat, I'm on a boat is what kept going through my head as I was boarding this New Orleans beaut' and in awe of it's presence. Whether it's your first visit to NOLA or you're a resident of this beautifully cultured city, you've seen the Steamboat Natchez laying alongside the Mississippi Bank adjacent to the French Quarter.

The sounds of their steam organ fill up the air as you're walking...
They have a check in special so make sure to use it
If you don't have a C02 tank you can rent one here!

sample_reviews.text: This joint is pretty neat. Friends and I came here for birthday celebrations. We like the vibes, very chill and fun. Wasn't crowded when I was here, but just enough people where there was pretty sufficient business. Not a lot of games to play, but enough to have fun. You have to pay to play some games. I thought drinks were fairly priced for the location and quality.

We came here with my coworkers and friends on a Thursday night to celebrate my birthday early. We have heard great things about this place, we decided to try it. The place serves sushi, cooked sushi, and vegan options (very cool). Everyone had raw sushi varieties, miso soup, seaweed salad, and we also brought our own wine. It's byob! Their salmon and tuna sushi was VERY fresh and high quality.

Huge brewer with restaurant seating! We came here for a birthday party/happy hour with coworkers and this place did not disappoint. Adequate table and seating for our large group. The beer was great and the food was even better. We were very into the outdoor seating option as well! You can just literally go out to the outdoor tables anytime you want.

The three stars only because after two visits of great service, but the one time when I went alone for a takeout order, no one greeted me. It's awkward because two hosts and a waiter were just standing at the register not even saying anything to me while I waited in front of them and they only glanced at me. Rude!

Okay, this place is my mother's favorite. And I can definitely taste why. Their curry and coconut infused flavors in their food are REAL authentic Thai ingredients, not that nonsense from a jar. Literally the real deal and no shortcuts. Real Thai food has strong tasting aroma, that's easy to differentiate from easy Thai commercial cuisine.
Towards the end of the day, 10 to 15 minutes before closing, they have deals for two of any remaining fancy donuts for $2.50! Not including any of their fresh donuts.
They have outdoor seating and cute indoor seating. Be warned. The line can sometimes look long, but it's fast paced! They know what they're doing.

sample_reviews.text: Loveeeee this place. The seafood diavlo is to DIE for. Fresh, spicy, and tasty. Also, their selection of pizzas are just what the Dr. ordered. I go here whenever I was good food for good pricing.

Absolutely divine. This Philly Girl wishes there was a Muriel's up north. Say yes to the drinks, my old fashioned was perfect. Commit to the turtle soup and gumbo. Give a hallelujah to whatever entree ends up on your dish, and a small amen for the creme brulee and bread pudding. Every time I go to New Orleans this is a must have place to be. Not touristy just delicious.
Roti, yes! Beef patties! Yes. Call first, sometimes hours are erratic.

The customer service here is ATROCIOUS! I tried to make a reservation  for here a group of 10 (two months in advance) and you would have thought that I was the worker and they were the customers. 
The food is mediocre and procey. The view is spectacular--but that's all. Don't believe the hype.

after getting yelp's weekly email, I was looking forward to trying the pie. Unfortunately this place didnt deliver. The lighting was creepy kind of drs office meets drug store. The apple pie looked delicious (see photos) but it was DISGUSTING. Apples were crisp with no gooeyness in the filling and a strong overall chalky taste. Something went wrong.

sample_reviews.text:
Oh this place is getting 5 stars right off the bat! 

Gave them a shot tonight. I'm so happy I did. 

I went with the Mix Fajita platter. The steak was flavorful, the shrimp was huge and fresh, and the rice and beans were perfect. 

My wife loved the chicken fajitas and the guacamole. That guacamole is in the running for best I ever had and the chips are the perfect crunch.

I'm having lunch here at the new CRISP Chik'n as we speak. And here's my honest review. 

Honestly, this place should have a line out the door. You all need to try this out. 

I tried it during its soft opening week. So it will remain to be seen if they can maintain this quality when it gets busy. But I will say this. 

This chicken breast is incredibly soft and juicy. Just look at the picture.

My God, why haven't I tried Nick Filet until now?

Oh yeah. I'd been working from home for 19 months and haven't been up this way since. 

Well it was worth the wait. 

The Classnick is simple perfection. The steak is cooked medium rare which is totally right by me. It's perfectly seasoned without being overly salty. The roll is perfectly crusty. 

And old bay seasoned tater tots? Sign me up!

I've had some OK meals here in the past.  The salad bar is fine.  The last meal I had here however was disappointing.  It was the seafood trio.  Only one of the three types of fish was any good, the garlic shrimp.  The cod and salmon were bland.  It's hit or miss anymore.

Review update is in order now.  Boy, is it ever in order!

I decided to have a bunch of friends here to help me celebrate my 40th birthday recently.  Now, I will always say that this place has plenty of good things going for it.  Yes, the crab fries are awesome.  Yes, they have some good sandwiches.  Yes, they have plenty of beer.  Yes, they have lots of TV's which is great for a game.

I want to thank them. Sabrina went above and beyond to correct an error in an Uber Eats order for me tonight. 

I appreciate your efforts. You're always 5 stars in my book. Thank you.
The turkey club. Possibly best in Delco.

sample_reviews.text: The Honu is an amazing restaurant in downtown Dunedin. They serve island-style dishes but very creative. Everything was so nice and fresh. They have a front patio, indoor dining rooms and the coolest "Polynesian tiki bar" and tables in the back. They frequently have live music as well. We started with the Poke Trio with ahi poke, shrimp poke & lomi lomi salmon.

So excited to have this sushi restaurant in the Wesley Chapel/Lutz area. Located across from the Tampa outlets. The restaurant itself is new and very clean with wonderful decor, high ceilings, a sushi bar and regular bar with one television. Exceptional service. The food is fantastic and fresh. So delicious.

I can't believe I have never tried this place for breakfast or brunch! I have been missing out for all these years. Service was excellent. The restaurant was packed at noon on a Sunday afternoon but we only had to wait about 15 minutes to get a table. All servers are wearing masks and the tables are socially distanced.

Frankly some of the worst service out there.  Barely acknowledged me because I was with my children.  I might be a better tipper than the guys but i don't tip for bad service.  Need to treat everyone as an equal customer.  I'm less than impressed and will not return here.
Good place to eat when you are shopping.  Good sandwiches,  Gelato and crepes.  Limited seating so sometimes it's very busy and you can't get a table.
Check out the $8 bottomless mimosas on the weekends!
Definitely try the $8 bottomless mimosas at brunch Saturday and Sunday

I love this place! Good, fresh food with seasonal options available to keep things exciting and not expensive. Good vibes in the restaurant and the staff are very helpful and kind. Wine Wednesday's are also a plus!

I got the Philly steak. It was fast service and they were very friendly. The sandwich was so good. Very flavorful and well priced. It was so delicious. This chain should spread out and expand to other places. Good healthy drunk food also!

sample_reviews.text: I had been introduced to Bacchanal about 6 month earlier and loved it. I am happy to report that having gone last weekend it is even better than before, they now accept credit cards everywhere which is glorious!! Bacchanal has also become much more well known so you have to get there earlier if you want a table.

This is for the pop-up dinner with Georges Perrier on Friday July 24th

So for those of you that aren't aware, Marc Vetri does pop-up dinners on the 2nd floor of the restaurant when the room isn't rented out. He doesn't announce that he is doing this until the day of the pop-up and he uses social media to let everyone know.

Feeding my Federal Donuts addiction I went over to the new N 7th St location. I had been going to the 2 St location for a long time and was used to it there. I will now be at the N 7th St location since there is parking and actual tables. Now that Federal Donuts is serving their chicken sandwiches everywhere its just amazing.

A buddy and myself went to Pub and Kitchen for dinner on a Thursday night.  Our apps were extremely good, I got the smoke wings and my buddy got the carrot salad, both very good.  The problem is that our entrees took more than 40 minutes to come out.

I was expecting more form this place, we went in for a late lunch after moving into our new place in the area. The service was good, our server was very sweet and attentive but unfortunately that couldn't make up for the food. The fundito queso was good but the chips that came with it were pretty limp, made it really hard to eat it. I went with the gringo burrito with pork belly.
Awesome place, will go back for the salsa. They give you tortillas right off the machine, hot and fresh.
Amazing pizza but definitely save room for the Affogato

sample_reviews.text:
Instead of using hot sauce, this place uses chopped red chili peppers. I know this because upon ordering my asparagus dish - hold the tofu please - "Thai" spicy, and having read Yelp reviews asserting that the spice level wasn't to par - I kindly asked for a side of hot sauce. 

Presented in front of me was a plate of chopped stir-fried asparagus without oil oozing and slopping around my plate.

Sbraga...is nothing like you'd expect. Instead of elegantly peregrinating into a darkly lit steakhouse environment with velvet booths and leather menus, you classically saunter into a a rustic-meets-modern couture.

I found it! The room with the bathtub and the shower! It's on the 29th floor...and the view from here is damn fantastic - it's like looking down from Heaven on the River and the Ben Franklin bridge. It's even more magical when NJ decides to have fireworks on a non-federal holiday.

Super friendly staff accompanied by super comfy beds that you sort of melt into along with an impeccably furnished...
Everything here is burnt...or at least the pizzas are. The salads look kind of burnt too.

Trivia night on Thursdays is pretty rad...if you're into sports and 1980s pop culture...or are just out to consume the widest range of beer in the shortest amount of time in a sort of down-home restaurbar environment. (Think Cheers + Applebees.)

The real problems with Gullifty's are outside. The porch-deck-bar-thing has a grill, but the menu is limited to beefy delights.
Closed until September 4
July 20- August 18 2012 not open during the day.

sample_reviews.text:
Le Mouton gives me hope for what Channelside can become. As the other reviews have mentioned, the owner did a great job of creating an inviting space. The decor is much like his baked goods- simple, unassuming and well done. 

I should start be mentioning that I don't eat sweets so I can't speak to those.

Simply the best Seafood Market in Tampa  Bay. It's really out of the way for me and I still go at least once a month. They've got a great selection that fresh with fair pricing. They are definitely the place to go during crawfish season! The guys that work there are also great. I swear one day I'm going to grab a beer in there and hang out for a little bit.

The breakfast sandwiches from here are little gifts from heaven. My dad used to stop by here in the mornings when I was home from college. I would walk in the kitchen after a long night out and find this amazing surprise waiting for me.

They are basic. But, when basic is done well, it makes all the difference. My personal favorite is the ham, egg and cheese.

I was shocked when I came to review this place and saw it's got a 4 star rating. ARE YOU KIDDING ME?!?! It's even more shocking given that there are 2 chinese joints here and this is definitely the lesser of the two.
I work in West Chase and was craving egg drop soup one day so I thought, "Hey!

I love Tanya & Matt's. I work right around the corner and if it wouldn't make me weigh 800 lbs I would go every single day. 

They have an ever-changing selection of flavors. They have a smorgasbord of toppings that you can choose from including these tiny little juice ball things. I haven't tried them yet but they look really intriguing.
The special fries have a shake on them that might make you get up and dance
BYOB!

sample_reviews.text: This is quite an interesting dining option in the area now.  The vibe reminds one of a techno dance club or stripper joint when you first walk in so you aren't quite sure if you are in the right place but you are.  They have a really extensive menu with a nearly overwhelming and sometimes confusing array of choices including à la carte along with all you can eat lunch and dinner options.

This is a great locally owned old school grocery that has lots of fantastic classic New Orleans food!  Their prepared foods and deli are great, the bakery is amazing, the meats are high quality and they have a wonderful cheese selection.  They also have one of the best wine and liquor selections in the area and carry things you won't find at any other local stores with great prices to boot!

This is the premiere Arby's location in the entire world.  Arby's makes a decent roast beef for a disgusting fast food chain.  Most of the sandwiches and the curly fires are a cut above your typical fast food selection.  On the edge of the French Quarter, which is home to some of the best gastronomic delights in the world, one would ask "why" people eat such drudge as fast food?

Ok I went here after hearing from several girls I know about how great, fresh, and wonderful it is.  I'm sure the food is fresh, light, and delicious to some people but I have to totally disagree with anyone that calls it "healthy" as it certainly isn't.

I'm a big fan of the other two Pho Orchid locations in Metairie but this one needs serious intervention by management ASAP! I'm done with this location until at least the end of Carnival and I had been excited about it as it is near my home and I had planned on eating here often BUT this is not up to par with the other locations.
Boo no burger on Summer lunch menu.
50 CENT inflation/gentrification!

Good coffee. They serve Kahwa here and it's made just right. Interior is like a steam punk kind of decor and vibe. Pretty cool. Lots of tables and places to sit. There is also seating outside. 

I've only had their coffee and soups so far. I had a tomato bisque in a bread bowl on one visit and it was really good.

Ambience: 5/5 The restaurant is beautifully decorated and classier than other establishments we've been to. Cloth tablecloths and napkins. Nice water glasses. Full place settings at each seat. The guy taking care of us was dressed very professionally.

Cleanliness - 5/5 No complaints! Everything at our table was pristine. Place settings were all good.

One of the best birthday dinners I've had. A special and intimate night for sure.

You need a reservation right now, they are not taking walk ins. Free parking right behind their restaurant where it says customer parking only.

We made a reservation for the omakase shogun with the sake pairing. I was honestly SO full from this meal. My husband had to help me finish my food and sake lol.

NOPE. I gave this place I think 3 tries and every single time was horrible. Food is really cheap but is blehh in taste. Service is reeaalllyyy slow. Like I think I waited almost 30 minutes once just for some general chicken. 

Inside is very small with a couple of tables and a few chairs by the door for you to sit while you wait. Very dingy/dirty inside. The last time I came was the FINAL time.

Been here a few times now...Yelp's two star rating describes it best: "Meh. I've experienced better." And after our last late night venture here, we probably will not be back. Unless we are super hungry and/or drunk as this place is 24 hours. 

Food comes out fast. Like scary fast.
10% off bill for grand opening until 12/31/19
They serve sushi now.

LA Smokehouse has it right.
Great food, Friendly Service, Central Location, and need I say it, smoked meat perfection.

Alright, so moving to Harahan I did not know what the restaurant situation would be.
How it would compare with NOLA.
Well, as far as Pizza goes, Happy Italian is AWE-SOME!!
Perfect Crust, delicious peperoni, and for a 16 inch, peperoni pie, less than $15.00!!!
These guys take toe snooty uptown joints to task, and WIN!!
Indeed, there is life outside of the 70125, and it resides on Jeff Hwy.
Who'd...

This little gem, tucked out of the way has the best meats anywhere..

If you are in the area, stop on by, you will not be disappointed..

And, although my expectations were not very high..

I ordered the side of Mac and Chees, also, standard fare.

Like this place.

Considering there are already 100+ reviews, it is hard to say what has not been said before.  Excellent place.  The bowls work as either a snack, meal, dessert.  
I had a quinoa one the other morning.  I liked the heater in their seating area.  The cashew milk is good too.  I had not had quinoa before, I know right.  I am behind the times, oh well.

I saw dolphins, well my sister saw them and then she showed me.  So cool, I love sea creatures. 
     I shared a bbq chicken salad with my mother, it was good.  I liked the frispy tortilla strips they put atop.  There was bacon on the salad, not really my thing, most of y'all would love it though.  They have really tasty nachos here.
     Lots of green space and a huge sand pit for the kids, too.
I was staying with folks who prefer to have a healthy liquor cabinet sooo we stopped by here. Lots of choices to be had. They got rye and wines. It was interesting to browse the selections.

For those who remember when this place used to be a Fish & Chips joint that randomly sold coconut soup (tom kha what what), I am with you there.  Then the Thai element took over, I'm sure it's a good change, the fish was greasy (but good).  Haven't been here in a few years, but always loved that coconut soup, choice of tofu/shrimp/ or chicken.

Yums.  Seating seems pretty packed in here, but it's comfy enough.  Ordering process is pretty streamlined, same affordable quality sammies.  I had a five dollar coupon (meal deal), so hello turkey sandwich.  

Downtown/uptown locale offers good people watching.  I chatted with some students from EF, who were from Belgium.  Pretty cool.

sample_reviews.text: I was teaching my dad how to drive and decided to stop by here since I've been to the one in Carson City before. Just like the other Dutch Bros, the menu is creative and large. The Bro menu and Bro Baristas make this place feel real. Customer service and all feels legit. It doesn't feel BASIC... There was a long line in the drive through, so we just parked and I ordered from the small window.

This little mom and pop shop or as people said, "son's" shop is a nice little place for some good Peruvian food. I'm not Peruvian so I can't comment on the authenticity of the cuisine but I as a foodie, I can say that the dishes here are well executed. After scoping this place out on my own, I decided that it was good enough for my friends and family. Anticuchos were flavorful and tender.

Yum Yum Num Num Boba! My family and I always order a few gallon of this stuff especially the Tiger Milk Tea. We like to add pudding and crystal boba to our drinks to add some jazz. We don't like it sweet so we go with the 25%. YMMV. Staff is always friendly even when the owners aren't there. Can't wait for them to open back up to normal operations.

The owner of this place must have lots of money. Worst service and worst Sushi in Reno. Worst Teppanaki also. Ever since they changed owners a few years back, this place has gone down hill and the owner is too ignorant to notice. Can't wait for this place to go out of business so hopefully it will be replaced by a better restaurant or owner.

Wow... where to start? This place has gone extremely downhill. You can quote me on here. This place will not last unless sold to someone who can run a supermarket. I would say this will happen in 2017.  First off... It's a Raley's hand me down and not the good kind. They gotten rid of their best points, no more Deli/Bakery/Café.
If you can't find parking next to the spot, head over the train tracks onto 3rd street and there's usually parking there.

sample_reviews.text: OK...this was my second visit to Eau Cafe for Sunday brunch. And just as I remembered the options seemed never ending. If Willy Wonka ever decided to serve food instead of candy...it would look like this place. It takes about five minutes just review all the choices. Although at $46 a person you would expect to have just about anything you want.

Wow!...I'm I glad I discovered this jewel. A recent trip to Alton to attend a show at the Jacoby Art Center put me on the hunt for a place to dine before the show. Once again I put faith in my fellow Yelpers to make a choice and Gentelin's stood out. I was also swayed by its relatively close proximity to the art center and a direct walk (four or five blocks) between locations.

I've never written a review while I was still in the restaurant until now...I just had a great breakfast and I can't wait to come back and try everything on the menu! Being my first visit I went with the classic breakfast...eggs, bacon toast, potatoes, coffee and orange juice. It was classic on steroids!

Tony B's surprised me. The food is pretty good. I've been there two times for lunch, choosing their buffet both times. The choices include salad, two types of pasta, pizza and cheese/garlic bread. 

The buffet isn't large, so I was worried about the kitchen keeping it stocked. But after every mad rush of patrons the staff would bring out more food so you're not waiting.

Modesto's recently updated their menu. More great choices all reasonably priced.

If you've never had it, try their bread pudding!
Great selection if wines.
Great place to celebrate your wedding anniversary!

sample_reviews.text:
HOLY SHIT! BEST HOT DOGS OF MY LIFE!

 I work right off of South St and one of the other tattoo shops told us about this place told and we RUSHED over.. They have every kind of hot dog you can think of! I am from Seattle and they actually have a Seattle dog! Cream Cheese and all!

Everyone was really nice and the place was cute!

AMAZING! 
These donuts are so awesome. I like you can get fancier flavors but picky eaters can also enjoy them, they aren't too crazy.  I can not wait to try and the fried chicken, it smelled amazing and I keep hearing great stuff about it!

This is hands down the best BBQ/Southern style food in the city. 

I don't even really like pork but their pulled pork is SOOO amazing! Pretty much everything I get here taste great and never disappoints. I could eat the hush puppies, pulled pork, and mash potatoes w/ gravy everyday of my life! I also really love that they have vegan options, it makes going out with a bunch of people really easy!

The pickle chips are amazing! Most the time the waiters are pretty awesome, plus they have a great jukebox! The only bad thing I can say is that one of the  salsa tastes like hot dogs! haha

Get the pickle chips and chicken burrito!

Summer and Dillion are awesome bartenders! Tuesday Boozeday is always the best day out of the week b/c of them! The Endless Summer and the Breakfast shot are my new two favorite drinks.

They also have the best french onion soup in the city.. It has a giant piece of roast beef in the middle of it! Yum..

Also, please remember to tip them a lot for putting up with your drunk ass.

sample_reviews.text: Great beers and food, always check the specials menu and order from there because it's bound to be out of this world. I love their fish tacos and salads. It's obvious the kitchen and service staff care about what they do. I've even seen the chef come out and address tables with food allergies to make sure they got a meal they enjoyed.

Always enjoy the food, the service is fast, especially if you beat the morning crowd on Sunday's after church. We had Will today and he was awesome. He was patient with my weird order request and made sure it came out even nicer than I hoped for. He hooked us up with condiments and everything we needed before we needed it. Prompt service, waters filled on time, friendly, thank you!

I never have this great of an experience going out to eat. Our waitress was so sweet and punctual. I was so happy to have such a delicious and extensive vegan menu, so much so I ordered way too much. It was busy but intimate, and even the host was great giving us a great corner booth on a busy night. Awesome experience, always is here :)

I loved this place for the ambience and beer selection. The veggie cuban was unreal! Had a great experience, and then we left our concert tickets on the table when we left....
The busboy came to bring us our tickets all the way at the venue! As soon as we realized, we turned around and he was running up with tgem. Not only a great bar with great food, but a rad staff! Will definitely be back!

One of my family's favorite spots. Everything we have had here is amazing, especially their specials menu. Sweet service staff and very fresh food. I crave this place constantly and have recommended it to many people who have loved it as well.

sample_reviews.text: Ok second review on local taco, and that's because I finally got to try their breakfast. One word, exceptional. They offer about 9 breakfast taco options. We had 4 of the 9 and thought they were all very good. Blended with a number of ingredients, you can really taste all the favors working together.

We have been coming to this location on and off for the last few weeks and continue to come back because of the staff. This location is quick and friendly, which is why they continue to get my business. One weekend, my girlfriend and I were waiting and the hostess asked us if we wanted coffee. Such a small, but nice gesture - something that 100% stands out when it comes to customer service.

One of my favorite restaurants in Nashville. Located across the street from Ascend, The Green Pheasant has a mix of classiness with a good modern vibe. A lot of Japanese inspired dishes and entrees that will make you head spin. Service was excellent!! 

Looking forward to many more visits

I'm local to Nashville and live in the downtown area. Last Saturday I was at Jason Aldean's and was shocked to find out today that my credit card was over changed multiple times. Unfortunately, this seems to be a common theme at the broadway bars. 

The phone number on google is disconnected and there is no contact us section on the website. 

STAY AWAY!!!!!!!

Besides being extremely inexpensive, this establishment doesn't really offer its niche...Mexican cuisine. They have a wide variety of menu items, but that shows me they don't have confidence in their Mexican cuisine. 

A big highlight of this place is that they give you free chips and salsa.
Excellent Bloody Mary! Ask for it spicy if that's your thing
Excellent Bloody Mary!

Salad

This summer, Cake Life is offering up an innovative dessert and beverage pairing series! Every Thursday night through August 17th, Cake Like will be joined by a beverage expert to produce a flight of five pairings.

From the folks that brought you the acclaimed Pub & Kitchen, Trattoria Carina is a casual Italian gem found in the Fitler Square-Rittenhouse area. And it may be our new go-to spot, right on the corner of 22nd and Spruce. This "Little Darling" has a small menu, but it's big on flavor and execution, all under the execution of Chef Melissa McGrath.

Hate to do this but disclaimer: This is not a rating for the food. 

Rather, it's for their delivery service, or lack thereof. Hopefully I'll be able to try the food sometime to amend the review but in the meantime, I ordered some sushi via Seamless, but it never got to me.

Entering into the Philly outpost of The Prime Rib, the atmosphere felt slightly stuffy, as if I was coming in for a power...

sample_reviews.text: The gushing over Honey's absolutely  is warranted.  Dear friends shared this NoLibs charmer with us, and we owe them an eternal debt of gratitude!  Indeed, over several visits, we have explored a substantial portion of the breakfast menu and look forward to further exploration.
Now that we know how to order the pizza (request "well done"), I must add another star.    Terrific, thin crust pizza that my husband craves.    Worth the trip.  And they deliver, too!!

Whenever Gerald S (our all-time Yelp hero) bestows five stars on an eatery or emporium, we are guaranteed good eating.  Factory Donuts fully satisfied our expectations.  

My husband adores old school cream donuts, and even though Factory Donuts' donut architecture varies from the classic configuration, my husband proclaimed his cream donuts "exquisite."  I observe that he did slightly...

What is GREAT about this Starbucks is its location, as Gerald S noted.   Given the dearth of decent "to go" coffee purveyors in this area, Starbucks is a nice addition to this neighborhood.   As other Yelpers have noted, this is a relatively small store.  It has comfy chairs and is a nice place to meet a friend for coffee and convo.

A regular stop on our errand circuit and a godsend during the winter months.   The positives are the generally good prices, the regular appearance of high end items (such as black garlic, purple asparagus, figs and heirloom tomatoes),  the sizable  array of chilis and peppers and the lovely, fresh flowers.
Hey Yelp Elites--This place is worth discovering.  Excellent breakfast and friendly waitstaff.  Best pancakes around.  FYI:  No pork products served.
Terrific new breakfast/brunch spot.   Excellent vegetarian options; the platter is divine (and the hummus is a standout).  Highly recommend!!

sample_reviews.text:
I make it clear that Yelp Nashville does partner up with our friends at the Nashville Symphony pretty frequently throughout the year but regardless of that fact, it doesn't mean that I have to love them or their home at Schermerhorn Symphony Center.
And, quite frankly... I do love them.

Sure, I struggle with the pronunciation of Scher-mer-horn, um well, I tried.

So you may not be able to tell just from looking at me but this broad has a very Sicilian father (second generation in the U.S actually) but I'm sure that as soon as I open my mouth... you'll see where all of that comes from. 

With that being said, I was lucky enough to grow up in St. Louis and was raised with the incredible food and culture of The Hill - and Italian institution.

What a fabulous Sunday feast I enjoyed here! 

After the completion of a long (and sometimes grueling) project, I grabbed a couple of my dearest friends and we headed to Moto to celebrate. This was indeed a grand idea. 

I was instantly smitten with the ambiance and textures of the space.

When we first noticed that the building that is now the Village Pub was being constructed into a new business, my boyfriend and I were stoked. When we found out that it was going to be a pub + beer garden, we actually let out cheer! Our home happens to be less than a block away so the thought of crawling home from evenings filled with mischief and relaxation sounded swell.

It's my own dang fault for coming back. 
Sometimes the idea of cheap sushi seduces me but you get exactly what you pay for.

My friend and I wanted to do an early, casual, and frugal dinner so we decided to pop by here again. I usually come here for a cheap sushi fix but decided to venture out and try a fried tofu Yu Dofu dish ($4.95) and the Rice Ball ($1.95).
Prices for juice and food are more than fair but the customer service is subpar. I experienced issue both with carryout and delivery.
Tons of interesting vegan options here
