La Patisserie is the the quintessential French bakery. The ambiance is so bright and lively it really highlights how beautiful the pastries and cakes are. It really makes you crave a hot cup of tea with other bourgeoisie.

I was stopping in after lunch at Union Loafers with a friend and was already stuffed but couldn't pass up on the opportunity to get something for later.

The hype is real! I had K-Bop for the first time at Food Truck Friday. I rushed to them as soon as I got there to beat any line and they had their act together. They had rows and rows of their signature bright yellow cups lined up and full of fresh food. 

They were handing them out like hot cakes. Seriously, those things were flying by.

Katie's Pizza & Pasta have created a beloved restaurant in STL. When I heard about their second location, I knew it would be great

I'm rarely in Town & Country but caught myself needing a brunch/lunch spot in the area for a group of 4. It was around 1:00PM on a Sunday and we walked right in. I'm sure any earlier there would have been a wait. Lucky for us the timing worked out nicely.

I was super excited to try this place based on the reviews. I LOVE Chinese food and I think my expectations were a little high. I got the Mongolian beef, shrimp fried rice, and sesame chicken for takeout with a friend.

They were short and a little confusing on the phone as expected. They didn't give me my total and just said "bye" after I placed my order.

It was a late night with a storm brewing. I was desperate, hungry, and tired. I had to make the decision, "go to bed hungry now or find food?" Just as I was about to go home I saw the great light shining from the Jack In the Box sign. 

I haven't gone to a Jack In the Box since elementary school. I fancy myself more of a Mc-Ds fella.
You've got to check out their Friday tastings. $10 for 15 wine and 5 beer samples.
Expect a 30 minute+ wait on the weekend. But don't worry, it's worth it!

sample_reviews.text: I really like this Costco off County Line Road in Greenwood. To be honest, this is my one and only Costco I've been too. That will soon change. It seems like a totally different mind set for the employees here vs. Sam's Club. The atmosphere here is NOT hectic and more laid back. The cashier lines are short, if at all.

What's not to love about Costco? Sure, I haven't tried everything in the store but what I have, I love. Bulk items, electronics, home improvement items, lawn and garden, furniture, Christmas Trees, live florals, seasonal decor, bedding, bath towels, clothing, fresh and frozen meats, fresh fruits and veggies to liquor and household items is what we've purchased so far.

We haven't been to Texas Roadhouse for awhile so we decided to visit for lunch today. We don't often visits chain restaurants but we do enjoy Texas Roadhouse. 

When we arrived their were several folks standing outdoor and people sitting in their cars. We feared a long wait but was pleasantly surprised when the hostess said a 1/2 hour.

Stopped in after reading the reviews to give it a try. Restaurant had been opened for well over an hour. Heard and smelled someone cooking (Pans shuffling around) in back, said Hello several times but after 5 minutes gave it up. Started to worry after I got home that maybe the person was in trouble and drove back to find customers coming in and out so I relieved.

I've been trying to purchase cupcakes for weeks from this location. The hours aren't convenient unless you live close by or make a special trip. I finally made a special trip since their closed on Sundays and drove out Friday afternoon to the new location on Main Street. She's been in this shop 3 months after outgrowing old location after a year.
Lunch served Mon - Sat 11:00a - 3:00p
Brunch Sunday 10 - 2p

sample_reviews.text: Capofitto was the lunchtime destination of my coworker and I during one of the last days of work before the Christmas holidays. We work very close by and had been eyeing the place up for weeks. We walked into the restaurant and were greeted by a large gelato counter, which serves Capogiro gelato, and a bar, with the seating in the back.

Wow, this place is totally unassuming from the outside, but is absolutely worth the walk to the counter for some food. Two lassis and a chaat are about $15. This establishment has very little seating. It is best to get your food to go. It will still be good in twenty minutes, I promise!

I never had street food chaat before, so I was really excited to taste the dish.

Fact: fish tacos are delicious. Opinion: Fish tacos at Buena Onda are especially delicious.

This fast-food taco joint is a little sterile inside, but the tiny bar and beer samples can get anyone in the mood. I usually have three tacos here ($3-4 ea), and one of them is always a fried shrimp taco. On this masterpiece, two jumbo shrimp are lightly battered and fried to perfection.

Cool music to listen to? Check.
Lots of food trucks? Check.
Lots of drinks? Check.
Lots of people? Alllllll the checks.

I went to the Chinatown edition of Night Market, so it already had a lot of hype surrounding the event.

Be sure to bring your ID on a Friday or Saturday night if you roll up after dinner. Because this is a popular college destination, you can usually count on a bouncer barring access on those nights. That being said, Cactus is really popular and is usually packed to the gills with that crowd on the weekends.

Once you're inside, the decor is fairly spartan, although the walls are bright shade of...
Buy bagged coffee in store and get a free drink!
Todd was the most personable and helpful server I've ever had. He works the brunch service, so you will be in good hands if you go and he is your server!

This deer in the headlights made out very well! 

I had an acia  bowl - kale, spinach, banana, pineapple, granola, & peanut butter that was absolutely delicious! 

You can customize whatever you'd like. 

The young staff was super friendly and fast.  Noticeably clean spot too! 

I couldn't get over how quickly my selection was made! 

Filling & healthy! Boom!

Yet another great Wawa!  IDK how many WaWa reviews I've done in my Yelp life (hmm, I should check into!), but I'm a huge fan! 

How COULDN'T you be?!  The COFFEE, the hoagies and other good food (yes, you can eat healthy here too!), many beverage and CLIF bar, etc., sales, ever-free fountain water (perfect for the beach!), my bank's (PNC) ATM within, gas stations at most locations, usually...

EXTENSIVE selection of wine, vodka, beer, etc., etc, and etc! 

I found the Gallo pineapple wine I was after too. 

Helpful and fun staff, plus nice prices!

Where do I begin?! 

In addition to the (mostly) grumpy employees, you have the long check out lines with too few checkers. 

On this particular trip, I was looking specifically for Ritz crackers for a recipe. 

I found an employee near the dairy section & excused myself and asked if she knew where to find them (I told her that I looked twice)  ... SHE GOT MAD AT ME  !!!

SUB PAR!

I heard the radio ads for Jimmy John's but have never seen/been to one.  

I wasn't missing anything.

I shouldn't complain because the sandwiches were free at work, from a drug representative, but I will.

The sandwiches were just meh.  The bread was fresh but too light/airy & no big whoop?  There was hardly any meat and I think I could have eaten at least three of the 'mini's'.
best meatball Ive EVER HAD! Moist, tasty and big - wowee!
open year round now!!! (Nov 2020)

The food was acceptable but not great. I had the baked spaghetti. The serving was generous and the price was fair but I'm always bothered when I pay restaurant prices and then receive a product so basic that I could have made it at home as well or better. In the unlikely case that I return I'll try the pizza

The reason my return is unlikely is because of their payment policy.

This place offers a cool mix of gourmet tex and gourmet mex.  Some of my favorites include the mac & cheese, served generously in a cast iron pot, the chili, especially when served as chili-mac.  The tacos are tasty and burritos are both interesting for their contents and tasty as well.  One time I felt I didn't get my money's worth was for the "Tex" cobb salad.

We arrived for the first seating.  Apparently they do not let you in until they are ready to let you in.  There is no inside waiting area so you end up congregating in the alley until they are ready to seat you.

Once they do let you in you are led to your table.  The tables are all low brass trays on trivets and the seats are pillowed benches.  The walls are draped in tapestries and carpets.

This is essentially a convenience store of slightly healthier and more exotic contents.  Not cheap, not crazy expensive.  Decent made to order sandwiches. My own pet peeve but they microwave their eggs to make the egg sandwiches.  Surprisingly tasty nevertheless.

My date was in the mood for Mexican.  I've been to Dos Segundos before and although it is more prototypically what you visualize for American Mexican it was not what I was in the mood for.    I also considered El Camino Real but, once again, not quite what I was after.  I found Las Cazuelas and it was well reviewed plus it was BYO.
They exclusively use butter blend.  I hate that!!
Be prepared to stand around and wait a while for your steak because that's how they roll.  (No pun intended).  There is no clear line to order.  Even more confusion about when/how to pay.

sample_reviews.text: Fair Grinds has turned into one of my favorite coffee houses. I love the location and the coffee is great with a purpose because its fair trade. They are always giving back to the community and are involved in making New Orleans a better place for its residents so coming here is like a donation to a cause for me. They also offer local baked goods that are great.

One of the few places left in New Orleans and the Bywater in general. Frady's is an old school corner food store serving great breakfast, plate lunches and poboys. Its a mom and pop family owned and run business which is always a plus. The star here is the breakfast, 2 eggs, choice of meat, grits, potatoes and toast for $5. Now that is my kind of breakfast.

Time to update my review. Envie is now my favorite coffee shop in NOLA. The reason is not only their great coffee but its a great place to stop at night in the quarter to order dessert. Their pastries are phenomenal especially the Italian Mascarpone cake. The croissants are some of the best in town, I highly recommend the almond.

Another place in New Orleans that I booked marked but now am saddened that I cannot go to because it is cash only. Its about safety, I don't know how any business in this city can be cash only, its an invitation to criminals.

Absolutely terrible. Salsa was out the can, fish of the day was $17 catfish and the pulled pork tacos were extra salty and filled with bay leaves that almost choked me. The pork was so salty I couldn't eat it. Its too easy to find good mexican food on the cheap than to buy this overpriced crap.
Still the best roast beef
Taken home and reheated in your Breville toaster oven the next day, best in the city.

The tend to stick with IPA's and sours, but they've perfected them! My personal favorites from my first (but definitely not last!) trip were Galactic Syringe, Schmoojee Strawberry Peach, and Sun Kissed. They only fill crowlers (32oz cans) of certain beers, and they sell shirts now.

Read more at phillyfoodandbrews.com

Okay, so I've had over 20 different beers from Imprint, and I think it's time to update this. I've been here six times in the past four months they've been open, and each time is a great experience! The beers are great, and the bartenders are skilled.

Imprint is a great brewery if you like IPA's, stouts, and sours. They don't often make other styles, but the ones they have are great. The IPA's all appear to be "New England" IPA's. They are hazy and usually brewed with oats, wheat, and lactose. For the Summer, they made a cream ale, a few pilsners, and a lager. All of them were good.

The "flagship" Wawa is a joke. It's always filthy, they're always out of various items, and it's attracted a very shady crowd (thank god there's always cops here). Homeless people and "One Step Away newspaper" are always right outside ready to bother you for your change.

I've never even had the food truck, but I can tell they made absolutely no changes. The food isn't even decent for being a brick and mortar "restaurant." It tastes exactly how I imagine the food truck food to taste: cheap, fattening, with hardly any effort put in to make it, but hey, if that's your thing, then to each his own.

The inside is so tiny.
They either were out of growlers or only fill them if you bring your own?
They either got another shipment of, or still have from the first round, Two Roads Two Juicy. 16oz 4 pack runs for $16-17.

sample_reviews.text:
What can I say that other Yelpers have not? I'm not really sure so I'm just going to go with my experience.

My birthday dinner with my boyfriend last night. We made reservations 1 hour before we came in, so I was VERY happy they could squeeze us in for a last minute reservation. We both got the $45 pre-fix menu which in my opinion was one of the best spent meals I've had.

Oh my dear God, dare I say this is the best meal I've ever had in Philadelphia in my 9 years of living here? Do I dare? 

I am so happy we chose this place to celebrate our anniversary, it was perfect. Service was top-notch, professional yet relaxed (aka it didn't feel too hoity-toity.) The food was beyond compare, and well beyond my expectations.

IT'S WORTH THE HYPE and yes it's true $35 per person including corkage fee, tip for 7 course authentic Morrocan food

Okay I'll be honest, I'm not Morrocan nor have I ever been to Morrocan -- but it felt authentic to the core. The space itself is small, intimate and private -- the decor makes you feel like you're not in Philadelphia ( in a good way).

As far as Wawa goes, this is one of the more poorly service ones I've been to. 
Yes, it's a busy one because it's in the middle of Penn campus, but that does not excuse it for poor service. I frequent the one in Center City that gets SLAMMED during lunch, and they are quick, fast, efficient and friendly ( see my other review).

Came here for dinner with a group of 6 on Friday and had a below average experience. 

To start, we all ordered the roti canai which I argue is their best thing on the menu. I could eat that curry over rice as a meal and call it a day, I highly recommend that dish.

We all ordered, including a dish for a friend that hadn't arrived yet. I ordered the Prawn Mee without pork.
Slow service ! Don't come here if you have things to do. But the food is bomb
cash only. delicious beef noodle soup. both shaved and hand drawn noodles are great!

sample_reviews.text: Pleasantly surprised by this morning diner that's just a stone's throw away from my apartment, yet another hidden gem that i have never tried after all these years of having lived out here. What definitely appealed to me is that they have delivery because i am anything but a morning person who wouldnt be in anything besides my robe at 0930 in the am.

Although pizza is their game, i am crazy about their sandwiches! In my opinion if a pizza place does sandwiches, give em a try! Why?? Its all in the bread, which is basically the same dough as their pizza (and I love and adore pizza!!).What makes Tampa Pizza so special in particular is everything else in the sandwich is high in quality so i keep forgetting theyre a pizza place.

There is a saying, "Not all who wander are lost." Well, i got lost wandering around Dunedin for a work thing and stumbled on this place, as i was drawn to the decor. I had no time to really look at everything, so after i did my thing for work, i came right back, not wanting to brave the drive back hungry, and determined to check them out.

One of my favorite spots! The food they offer is made with fresh, healthy, organic ingredients. The customer service there is top notch: the employees there are very welcoming and act as if they care about their product and if you liked the food. The interior itself is inviting and gives off a cool vibe, adorned with records on the walls and checker games on the tables.

One of my favorite local spots for a cupcake! This bakery offers a wide variety of baked goods, both sweet and savory. My favorite dessert to get here is a mocha cupcake with two cake balls. The mocha cupcake is a moist chocolate cupcake, not too sweet, topped off with smooth mocha icing that is actually true to the coffee and chocolate flavor.
Try the Signature Salad with Ahi! Delish
Check out their rotating daily specials

sample_reviews.text:
In Riva makes me very happy.

After we had a Yelp event here a while back I always promised myself I would return.  Days turn into weeks, weeks into months and a restaurant that you otherwise want to go to inevitably gets missed.

Our experience at Aldine was something special.  I made the reservation about a month out and I provided the list of my wife's allergies.  Around 3 weeks out from the reservation Aldine called me to discuss the allergies.

My first experience at Southgate was probably atypical.  I got to sit and a slightly higher table, right next to the kitchen and eat all of the things.  The food and beverages were top notch and I just felt very lucky to be there.

I'll just get right to the hits.  Between the KFC and the beef tartare my palate was thrown toward different ends of the spectrum in both flavor and texture.

I wanted to love Brodo more but I just feel they are too expensive for what they are offering.

Don't get me wrong, their combinations do seem inspired (which is their tagline).  I went with the vegan fetti option and I did really like the way it tasted.  I'm saying that the price did not line up with the size of the portion.

Meh really sums up how I feel about Millers.  If you're in the area, there arent' many other options for what they are offering but on the whole, I found my experience lackluster.

The food here is the equivalent of 90s applebees.  Tons of fried items and not a ton of care given to things coming from the kitchen.
$5 cover :(
$10 card minimum. No Amex

sample_reviews.text: I'm legitimately frustrated I haven't been here before. What a gem! Nice space, really tasty bar food (a step above, IMO). The wings really are awesome - perfect sauce (get fries to dip into it!), and the fish and chips... I gotta say, I was hesitant to get them, but I went for it based on other Yelp reviews and was pleasantly surprised! Light and crispy, not greasy at all.

This place is a delight for all the senses. Super cute and trendy, with tasty plates. A little on the pricier side, but they're always jam packed; they're worth it. I splurged for the "lox and cream cheese on a bagel" which they call "pastrami salmon with whipped cream cheese and fried capers". Fancy schmancy. Comes with micro greens on a wheat bagel. And it's delightful!

Listen. You're going to spend a lot of money on cake. It's going to happen. But you won't be sorry. You'll talk into The Cake Bake Shop, look around at the beauty of the shop with the dripping crystals and petals (currently themed in harvest/halloween colors), see the employees' adorable uniforms, check out the decadent mile high cakes, and realize you don't mind. 

It's an experience.

I wanted to love this place, but I didn't. Nor did the friend I was with, who has a lot of dietary restrictions.

Appetizers: Good! I'd just get apps if I went back (but I won't go back).

Entrees: Tasteless/ small/ expensive

We went for restaurant week and I ended up spending $52 on food I didn't care for.

They have salt and pepper on the table, as if they know it's not going to be to your...

I visited from NY and was impressed with the quality of food and hospitality of the staff. 

The wine was the best part. 

The bruchetta was a bargain at $4 a plate (they give you two dressed pieces of toasted bread so it's perfect for 2 people to split a couple of plates.) A little too heavy for my liking which is why I shyed away from 5 stars. 

The hummus spread was the best plate we ordered.
Dinner pricing starts at 3 (even during the week)
PRO TIP: Make sure you chew all that Tajin candy off the straw!!

sample_reviews.text: What a treat! Enjoyed a trio of flavors. I picked Wildberry lavender, Brambleberry crisp, and Gooey butter cake. Each flavor was amazing! The taste of each one was fresh and full of so much flavor. Loved the options!

We ordered the Classic Brie, which is baked Brie cheese with garlic bread, butter, and pesto sauce. Incredible!

They also served warm fresh bread and butter. 

I ordered the Eggplant Parmigiana. It is sliced eggplant with topping of Marinara Sauce and mozzarella cheese. I ordered mine with Alfredo sauce instead of marinara. It comes with a side penne alla vodka pasta.

Picked two donuts, Old Dirty Bird & Man Bait. The Old Dirty Bird consisted of vanilla cake, chocolate icing, peanut butter, and crushed Oreos. The Man Bait consisted of vanilla cake, maple icing, and crumbled bacon. Both were amazing and paired nicely together!

Came here with a friend for a girls dinner. Very crowded, limited parking, and humid inside.

We were seated within 10 min. Our waitress was friendly and attentive. The chef was not very engaging or friendly. 

The food was mediocre. I ordered the filet and shrimp. Both were cooked nice. The noodles and vegetables were not flavorful. The rice was also not very flavorful.

Came here for brunch with a group of 6. We had a reservation. We were sat outside. Inside was not even offered to us due to a baby shower being held inside. 

The service was terrible. They took forever to take our drink order and then to bring the drinks. They also took forever to come back to take our food order. They apologized and said they were being slow due to a party inside.
Love the Malibu roll and the Hawaiian roll
The crispy canton was amazing!

Wang's Table is a new restaurant serving Chinese food in what was China East on South Virginia Street near Vassar Street.
Currently it is only take out and delivery (I saw it on Door Dash). I thought there was dine in, so I came in to eat. I placed my order and sat and waited.

The Greater Reno Grub and Gripe Group held a Chowdown Saturday at 5th Street Bakehouse to try their new Late Bakes, a small plates offering of dinner on Friday and Saturday night. It seem Bakehouse owner Dan Carter pretty much hit a home run for the group as most of the small plates were quite good and everyone seemed happy.
It opened a year ago as a breakfast and lunch place and they've started...

I can't understand how with double digit unemployment that they would have problems finding someone fluent in English to take orders from customers.

sample_reviews.text:
I REALLY enjoyed the Thai Orchid. I wasn't expecting to find Thai this good in a strip mall, but my, was I surprised. They were pretty busy when we got there, but we had reservations so we rolled right in and got a table immediately (sweet!). 

I was really taken aback by how nice the hostess and the waitress were.

OK, here's the plan:

1) come here starving, like I-haven't-eaten-since-second-breakfast starving

2) order the tasting menu

3) enjoy as a nearly endless phantasmagoria of deliciousness descends upon your table. Imagine Be Our Guest playing as the cheerful servers swoop in with each new course. 

4) sleep

-------------------------

Well, that's the gist. Seriously, get the tasting menu.

Whenever I visit the Italian Market, I find myself wondering which of the places are authentic old-world places vs. which are Disney-esque caricatures. One thing is for sure, this place is for real. From the older mother who owns the place yelling at her middle-aged sons who work there, through their knowing everyone who comes through the door, Cappuccio's is the real deal.

I really wanted to like Casselli's, but it wasn't in the cards. Maybe I just caught them on a bad night, but I didn't really enjoy much about my visit. The waiter was very unpleasant - was banging down the plates and glasses on the table when he delivered them. Maybe he was having a bad day, but I still would have expected better. 

We had mixed experiences with the food.

As I started to step down off of the bus at the corner of 19th & Chestnut, I pondered where I might be able to catch a quick bite of food before meeting a friend to see a play at Plays and Players. I don't have much time, so something quick would be awesome. I don't feel like anything too fancy, so something casual and cheap would be nice.

This place is AWESOME! Between 2 of us we tried shrimp, fish, chicken, tongue, carne asada, and pork. All were awesome. Loved the red sauce, thought the green was a bit bland but if you're not into spicy you might really like it. Folks who were working were really nice. Menu is in English and Spanish, and they do take cards or cash.

They just opened today and WOW! Aren't we lucky to now have 2 meaderies within a mile or two of each other. 
Mead is a type of fermented honey beverage. The ABV (alcohol percent) is generally 10-14% so more along the lines of wine than beer there. 

We tried Goober Vitis (peanut butter and jelly), Suzy's Cherry Pie, and the coffee one. All were PERFECT.

There is a menu online. That menu is not accurate. They no longer have any soul food items, except for the Sunday after-church brunch thing. They were out of 1/3 of the African items on the menu as well. 

If you aren't actually from this country, I wouldn't recommend eating at this restaurant as there are NO DESCRIPTIONS on the menu.

This is one of the only restaurants in Ftn Square that's open on Monday! We rode our bikes from Irvington to eat at Square Rootz...fail. So we went here. Kids will like the decor: sombreros, little tile roofs over the booths, murals. Our booth was adorned with 4 different images of Speedy  Gonzalez. 

We were seated right away and asked for "2 big waters".
Calling it "ice cream" is a stretch. "Frozen treat" is more accurate.
They use bendy straws. Yisssss!

You know the soup guy from Seinfeld? Well, imagine the same experience, but with falafels and meat instead. What you need to know: $10 per dish, cash only, one dish person, the line is long, you have to ask for veg option early!

The owner of this food truck has been doing this for years, and it shows because he absolutely loves his job.

Suraya is, beyond a reasonable doubt, the best restaurant experience in Philadelphia and soon to become the next Zahav! Everything from management to the wait staff to the food (especially arak cocktails) and ambiance was top notch and I strongly recommend coming here before the waitlist starts to grow in respect to its popularity.

Must get: literally every mezza, specifically kibbeh, samke,...

In short, Vetri Cucina is worth the hype.

It's rare to attend one of the most well-acclaimed restaurant in Philly, but when you snag that available spot on opentable, then get ready for a culinary experience that will loosen your belt and rock your socks off. Unfortunately, this review is many months post the Vetri experience, so much of my memories are piecemealed together through my photos.

After a delightful dinner and a long walk, my wife and I decided to swing by this little froyo spot to satiate our sweet tooth. As many of the froyo spots are, this place does not give you the sample cups, but the staff will have to walk over and pour it out. As a consumer, I prefer the self-serve, but didn't mind the service.

This is a major disappointment and I strongly recommend staying clear of this restaurant for any reasons, private or group dining.

Must get: nothing
Meh: service, cheesecake, chicken francaise
Missed out: none

I came here for a business meeting and despite the 'free-ness' of the meal, my wife and I promised to never return due to a myriad of reasons.

sample_reviews.text:
A Hellas of a restaurant (I couldn't resist), Hellas Restaurant represents the best of the Greek heritage in Tarpon Springs. 

The first time in Tarpon Springs you will notice two things - a lot of sponges, and block-to-block Greek restaurants. This is due to the history of Greek immigrants being hired to harvest the sponges - all of which I learned off the back of the menu at Hellas Restaurant.

Some of the best Indian food I've had, Ekta Indian Cuisine will satisfy all of your Indian food cravings.

I'm not going to pretend I'm an expert on Indian food, but I can confidently say this is some of the best Indian food on the mainline.

Great beers and a fun atmosphere, Crooked Eye Brewery will probably charm you with it's fun, independent feel (and the beer of course).

This place reminds me of NJ breweries, which show up in any cheap industrial spaces or strip-malls. Crooked Eye Brewery is located in a...uh...you know what I have no idea what it's located in.

Average and predictable as far as lunch places go, P.F. Chang's offers that chain-familiar food and a reasonable lunch special.

P.F. Chang's is always reliably good but never extraordinary. They offer a pretty reasonable lunch wok classics menu, with a few dishes around $10. I went with the classic Chang's Spicy Chicken with brown rice ($9.95).

I was here twice in the early afternoon and it was a pleasant experience. The food here is solid but a bit over-priced (no more over priced than other places in this area) and the service was adequate. Plus you get a TON of fries (in a little metal bucket no less) with your burger, so I 'd recommend splitting a dish. 

The bartenders were all attentive.
Half-price Italian Hoagies on third Wednesdays of the month!
Limited street parking in front - I'd just park in the parking lot across the street.

sample_reviews.text: It was dad's turn to pick up dinner tonight and we were all in the mood for pizza so I asked him to head to Felicia's (thanks Steve T for the rec and directions!).  He came back home (after getting lost, calling me, and finding his way thanks again to Steve T's directions on Yelp) with a small thin crust margherita (mozzarella slices, tomatoes, and a savory blend of herbs) with prosciutto and a...

I really really missed this cozy, eclectic cafe.  It's the prime place for studying or just catching up with friends on a weekend (or before a mixer/dance, if you're in HS...ahh, those good ole days).  My extra frothy cafe latte hit the spot and the baristas are still just as friendly as I remembered.  Make sure you grab a fresh-baked chocolate chip cookie with your espresso, even if it's to go.

One of my favorite bakeries in Center City.  Definitely get the hazelnut cake.  The chocolate shavings and fluffy, creamy, buttery, and light whipped icing is absolutely amazing.  It literally tastes like the best tiramisu in cake form.

I would give this sorry excuse for a Manhattan Bagel zero stars if I could.  Stopped in to grab coffee and a bagel with cream cheese and ended up waiting over 25 minutes for my food.  No excuse/apology/"miss, your order is coming up."  There were only 5 customers (with normal-sized orders, not catering) in front of me!  The place itself is tiny and not very clean.

Not great at all.  The coffee is pretty bad and my chicken noodle soup was extremely salty.  Stick to Au Bon Pain for better and fresher options.  The best part was the multigrain flat-bread, which was only semi-decent.  It is one of the few options still open at midnight at 30th Street Station, though.

sample_reviews.text: Better than I expected, good service and pretty lively and kept clean inside. I got the miso grilled salmon rice bowl with veggies and it was really tasty. Nice amount of veggies with brown rice, so a great healthy and good tasting option.

Very interesting, cool rustic hideaway up the pass. Definitely tucked away with no mobile service. It definitely has character, history and sounds like a hopping time during the weekends when they also have live music.

I got the Tri-Tip sandwich which is the go-to item and it did live up to the hype. It was one of the best tasting tri-tip beef I've ever had.
Ordered a chocolate mouse cake to bring to a dinner and it was fantastic! Really light, airy, chocolaty. Reasonably priced too. I'd totally recommend this place!

Very cool looking restaurant and had higher expectations for the food. They delivered my tacos appetizer with my main meal which was unfortunate. The corn soft tacos were weirdly slimy, thick and undercooked. I ended up just eating the filling and leaving the tortilla.

Second time here and today was a bit of a disappointment compared to my last visit. Food was ok, pretty average sushi. The rice was a bit gummy in the roll I ordered. But the service was pretty bad.

I ordered a few items, gyoza, grilled collar and some sushi. I asked for not all the food to come out at once. Didn't want food to get cold  and to have some time in between all the dishes.

sample_reviews.text: Excellent stop on my way home from Busch Gardens. The staff was friendly and went out of their way to go get my friend and I a cup of water when they don't necessarily carry cups since this is mostly a takeout joint. The pizza was excellent. They run a tight ship and we're very well staffed.

This is a prime location for this restaurant. They have a huge outdoor bar patio area and the inside is large as well. Plenty of space for large parties or private events also. The food is always good and the drinks are always solid. It is on the pricier side, but worth it considering the view you have of the bay is ridiculously beautiful!

Absolutely loved this place ! We wanted food after 10 and they had it - great menu and strong drink! Cool vibe with fun music too! Must try the veggie Cuban and maybe e even take a scooter to get there like we did!

This is a pretty cool venue, but the staff needs a bit of work. The bartenders all seemed very grumpy and barely made eye contact- We waited almost 15 minutes for someone to take our drink order and the bar was not that busy. When my two friends and I got our tabs, all of the tabs were completely incorrect.

Good spot but very dark inside. I didn't want to feel like I was in a dungeon so sat outside in the extreme heat. Brie and blueberry crepes were decent, but came out cold. Best part of my meal was actually the IPA beer. I probably wouldn't be in a huge rush to return.
There is no indoor seating, so if the weather is bad, you won't enjoy this place.

sample_reviews.text: This is a gem of a vegetarian/vegan restaurant in Upper Southampton, just off of Street Road.  There is no meat, no dairy in any of its fare, but you won't miss it.  Everything is fresh, everything is delicious, and portions are very satisfying!  The main challenge is keeping enough room for dessert!  Give it a try - you won't regret it!

I could say a lot of things, with major descriptors and blah, blah, blah.  So, to save all that, I give you one word:. RIGHTEOUS!!!  They're going through some growing pains as I write this in December 2020, but I strongly advise you to be patient.  And trust me, the food is worth the wait!!!

I could say a lot of things, with major descriptors and blah, blah, blah.  So, to save all that, I give you one word:. RIGHTEOUS!!!  They're going through some growing pains as I write this in December 2020, but I strongly advise you to be patient.  And trust me, the food is worth the wait!!!

I visited this restaurant this past week.  Good marks for cleanliness, atmosphere, and friendliness of staff.  I had the lunch buffet and found it to be "ok".  While I certainly understood, with a buffet, why they'd need to dial back the 'heat' of the dishes, but it seems that they held back on all of the spices to me.

My favorite Chinese dish is Buddha's Delight, a stir-fried mixed vegetable and tofu dish.  Most establishments have this dish or one similar to it, but they are certainly not all created equal.  Most places that make this plate come up short for lack of flavor, and vegetable dishes are particularly challenging in that regard.

sample_reviews.text: Was walking from a neighboring establishment on a drizzly Sunday that happened to be Mother's Day, and couldn't help but pop in and check it out. Man, am I glad we did! The place is brand new, open less then two weeks. We were promptly seated, and the head brewer (and his wife) both popped over to say hello and walk us through the offerings.

A fabulous meal to end a lovely day, I went with a date during a massive early summer rainstorm. The team there was on point and super friendly, and it was a delicious and fabulous dining experience. The beer list is great and the cocktails and wine are top notch. The atmosphere and ambiance are lovely, and it is a really nicely appointed physical space.

Really an impressive first time at Whetstone, a place I had heard a lot about as they are in the family with one of my other fave spots, Brahaus Schmitz. Popped in for a lovely mid-Monday lunch and couldn't have had a better time. Our waitress was fabulous - friendly, quick with a recommendation, very sweet. The drink menu was excellent, and the beer list was on point.

I like this place. Food is good, although slow. Expansive menu. Great beer selection, and the outside patio is a fun place in the summer. Not inexpensive, but not crazy. Overall a fun place for a night out.

Been here a few times and always enjoyed it. Food is good, menu is strong. Beer choices are good although could be better. The last time I was here the waitress was super nice but didnt know a ton about the beers they had. Not inexpensive, but not crazy. I have been for drinks after work with coworkers, for midweek dinner with friends and with the wife for dinner on a Saturday night.

sample_reviews.text:
I absolutely love this hometown local bar. The Local overs LOCAL brews as well as vodkas how Cool! 

The atmosphere is clean, with a pool table, dartboards, boardgames and the friendliest staff ever. Sherry is always so pleasant remembers your name and just makes it a special experience. They offer trivia/questionarre on Tuesdays too.

Excellent atmosphere, lots of parking, service with a smile and great menu options. 

There is patio and inside seating. We enjoyed the live music on a Saturday afternoon. Our server was cheerful and attentive. The beer list has many collaborations and I loved the sour options. Try the collective sour of its still on the menu. Yum! 

I ordered the smoked wings with orange marmalade marinade.

I love this little taco truck. The parking lot is rather busy as it is one of the only gas stations in the area. They have a small tent with a few tables that you can wait while your food is being prepared. Service is always friendly and with a smile and the flavors are just great. Prices are right too. 

My favorite mini item so far is the azul taco with carnitas.

I wasn't overwhelmingly impressed with this restaurant.  There was plenty of parking which is nice but I believe "the largest waterfront restaurant" is potentially working against this establishment.  There were so many different levels and dining rooms that when we sat ourselves it took entirely too long to be noticed.

This is probably the first time I have ever gone to a seafood restaurant without ordering seafood but we weren't up for a big production so we chose to split 10 wings and a burger.  The wings are something I would most definitely not order again. The firecracker sauce was almost like a mayonnaise base, not pleasing at all.
First online to go order receive $5.00 off when using their website.
Complimentary soup with purchase of $8.95

sample_reviews.text:
So all around completely impressed with this place!!!! 

First of all, it's owned by a super friendly, ambitious young couple who recently moved to the states from the Czech Republic to live out their "American Dream". It feels amazing supporting them here! I had lovely, entertaining conversations with Maros (the 'M' in A&M Factory), and his girlfriend whose name started with an 'A'!

Ever since leaving Morocco I've been dreaming about heading back here and bringing the family along; to kind of relive our time there. 

I feel this is one of the most authentic Moroccan experiences you can get in the states, and definitely in Philly! 

I don't believe it's for everyone however. I feel people either love it or hate it.

We absolutely LOVED our cozy meal here at this new American BYOB spot in the middle of The Italian Market! 

 We dined weekday for my husband's birthday and basically had the entire candlelit space to ourselves. We sat in the window overlooking 9th street. It was lovely and overall a wonderful, non fussy, local choice for the special occasion.

I've always wanted to try it her so the hubby surprised me with dinner before a concert on Saturday. Half of the restaurant was dim and candlelit and a bit crowded but kind of romantic. Where we sat was in the back room with bigger parties and a bit more space.there were some kids there which was nice to see because it makes me feel more comfortable bringing  our baby along next time!

3.5 yelps. So it seems like our experience was not the norm and that's unfortunate for us. Seeing all of the other rave reviews makes me want to head back and see if the Sunday when we dined was just a rare fluke. I feel like we were jipped out of this amazing experience everyone else had. We were so excited to treat my parents to this meal.
Located where Famous Dave's used to be, near Beer Peddler's.
In a plaza. Tons of parking.

sample_reviews.text: Went on a friday night with sis and father.  Both are much more into this type of meals then I am, but was willing to give it a try.  WOW, great service, great food and a great price.  The place was clean and pretty busy when we got there around 6pm.  The food came out hot (temp and flavor) and huge portions.  My sis couldn't eat all hers and took it home for a followup meal.

I went in with high hopes as this place has been around for a looooooooooong time here in Indy.  Plus I really like to see independent places do well, especially when they are in neighborhoods that need help.  If you went by the outside look you might keep driving right by, but the inside is very nice and warm.  The service was A+ and the costs were reasonable.

Had a chance to try this place this past weekend (Feb 13th) and wow what a great place to eat.  The food was served in a timely manner and came out how and had incredible flavor.  My dad had the special of the night and I had the Shrimp Ettouffe.  We both had a bowl of the clam soup and it was great.  The owners are there and make sure everything goes well.

Tried their flull slab or ribs and whole chicken plus some sides this past weekend.  The meats were okay, kinda overcooked for me, but the sides were wonderful.  I am willing to give it another try and go with their sandwhichs next time.

Had a chance to try this place on Sat 5/12 for dinner.  The place wasn't that crowded and our waitress was very nice and on the spot all the times.  The food was okay, didn't knock my socks off but wasn't bad.  The banana cream pie was so, so with no real bananas to be found.

sample_reviews.text: Sometimes the best places are those hidden gems that you never think twice about giving a chance. I have passed by Gou Bin more times than I can count and it always caught my attention however being located at that bottom of a multi level retirement home never made me want to give it a try.

Howards in Soulard is probably my new favorite Soulard bar, simply because its far enough away from the standard Soulard crowds and typically focuses on a slighter older and more mature crowd. Howards also plays host to live music several days of the week and they have an amazing outside patio which is perfect for spending some of those summer evenings.

Sauce on the Side has been a favorite ever since they first opened in downtown, and I am so happy to see them expanding downtown, in Clayton, and soon enough The Grove! However despite the Clayton location being open for a while I haven't been able to check out the Clayton location until here recently.

As soon as I walked I felt a very familiar vibe, similar to the original downtown location.
Location has been closed for months.

I arrived on a weeknight night with a large group of friends, reservations were made well in advance. However it seemed like they were totally unprepared for our group, let alone another large group also dining that evening. Service was spotty from the start, with only one waiter trying his best to hold down two tables of 10+ customers and the rest of the restaurant.
Break Time: 2pm - 4pm
Located inside Beast Craft BBQ

sample_reviews.text:
The best breakfast tacos I have ever had. Anywhere. In my life. And I'm getting old.

I've had these multiple times, both at this location and the Fuzzy's in Brandon, and they are spectacular.

As proven by the fact that I had them at 10pm last night, these breakfast tacos are served all day and the ones I always get (Egg, Cheese, Potato and either Bacon or Chorizo) are just $2.19 each.

The...

Finally, I found this hidden gem. And the Italian Beef was fantastic.

This version truly tastes like my many trips to Chicago over the years to watch my friend AJ lead the White Sox to the World Series, Lou often get tossed in classic fashion at Wrigley and the Bears consistently fail to overcome the horrendous head coaching of Lovie Smith.

Paul puts this wonderful sandwich together with care...

This new concept from Bloomin' Brands brings a taste of Outback to the International Plaza food court.

Only the third of its kind in the world, with others in Saudi Arabia and Hong Kong, Aussie Grill features an impressive menu of burgers, chicken, ribs and even their signature Outback sirloin served sliced for convenience.

As explained to me by Tim and Tom, terrific guys heavily involved in...

Almost every single person has seen DQ's commercials for that Five Buck Lunch.

Partly on a dare, partly because I felt bad about something from two years ago (I'll get to that in a second) and partly because I wasn't very hungry, I stopped here to see exactly what that highly touted lunch special was all about.

For five dollars, I had two choices of main items and that's it.

I've spoken positively here on Yelp about pizza places who embrace and properly execute the "Pizza By the Slice" concept.

Most recently, I wrote about Planet Pizza in downtown Orlando and how they are a fine example of how to do this.
Brooke is wonderful. Ask for her when you need a table.
That Florida Tomato Soup and Grilled 3 Cheese Sandwich combination should not be overlooked.

sample_reviews.text: Franklin mills is where I get my pretzels.  Most of the time when I come I have to wait for a fresh pretzel (which I don't mind b/c their Hot). Also that lemonade any flavor is my absolute favorite because its sweet & not tart.
Just like any other location I've ever been to good customer service. Fast service. Hot food. & u don't have to go through a song/dance for extra sauce

My first time here and I was actually impressed. The food was very good and affordable. You get a lot of food for the price (well my entree was huge idk about everyone elses) I got the country fried chicken. It was so tasty I didn't need to add anything to it. I had to take most of my meal home because I couldn't finish it after ordering an appetizer.

I hate to give such a bad review especially since I had their wings & fell in love. A few days ago my hunni & I order delivery I got chicken quesdillas which were the thinnest not enough cheese quesdillas I've ever had from a pizza joint.  My hunni got the wings non breaded w/sweet & hot sauce. When the delivery arrived 40 min later the wings were breaded w/sauce on the side.

Was pretty good ordered shrimp garlic pasta wasn't anything to holler about.. they do give u bread & butter to start u off which is a plus.. the butter should be room temp tho so its easier to spread on not warm bread.. the best part imo is the dessert which u have to order w/ur meal bc its made to order.
This location sucks. I'll stick to Philadelphia mills location
Call all their numbers and still no answer. But they complain when you try to come in and order

sample_reviews.text:
The only way I could love this place more is if they named a drink after me (unfortunately I'd have to be blonde...and famous....) I can only say "C'est Magnifique"!

  A corner spot with a cute patio, this place has so much personality and pizzaz I didn't want to leave.

The Fountain is like my eccentric aunts inner "zen" came out to play. I really dig this place. The mural painted walls, chill ambience, and trendy booths really make it cozy. I've come here for lunch and dinner in the past and the Thin Man is outstanding. I love the flavors and when you add chicken, it's the perfect midday meal.

I have been whisked away!

    This new, aqua-accented addition to Cherokee Street couldn't be more precious. There's a small couch area in the front by the open window that is perfect for relaxing or studying, and several tables that are just as cheerful with their vintage feel.When you walk in you are greeted by the owners with their enthusiastic "Welcome" mixed with the mismatched thrifty...

What does this place Not have? The atmosphere is funky: tye dye, fuzzy walls, vintage chairs galore...pretty great but even better it's a music venue and upstairs, a cool hangout spot. I'm all about it. 
The food is off the wall but delicious.

When it comes to finding the real deal, the authentic European Gelato, it can be a challenge. After living in Italy last year I can be honest, I was spoiled. Now, when the craving strikes the closest thing I have is Froyo. I was a little apprehensive considering they no longer host a website and I didn't know what to expect besides several good reviews for their gelato and atmosphere.
Get the chaider--chai and cider-- it's amazing
If you like honey and almond milk the seasonal Honey Almond Toddy is the way to go!

sample_reviews.text: FUN DRINKS! I really wanted to love GenX but it was hard. The  drink menu offers a variety of fun drinks. We tried a variety of drinks and all were good. We ordered a bunch of different food items: queso fries, freebird, steak taco, buffalo sandwich and MYO Buger. The food just didn't hit the mark. The queso fries were not hot but loaded full of goo.
What can I say 2 can eat for $22 includes appetizer or two salads and free chips. It's a bargain.

2nd visit. Tried all new items this visit and loved them all. Moroccan Chicken Piadina was delicious. Basil  Hummus was my fav. The icing on the cake is the lemon  cookies. Wow! Pro-tip: hummus is $3.99 at happy hour.

I struggled between 1-2 stars. First mistake for me was ordering buffalo cauliflower not wings. This is a dish I typically love. This was not good at all. Barely tossed in sauce and not even hot on the inside. 

The rest of the fam ordered wings and gave 3 stars. 

We struggled with service the entire meal. No drink refills, never received carrots & celery ordered, and then waited for our bill.

I'm really struggling with this review. I've eaten at numerous Dickey's all around. The location in Love Field airport is my favorite. Airport food shouldn't be better than my local joint. 

The staff tonight was super friendly. Just two guys working and there was only 1 takeout guest The whole time we were dining, which is odd at peak dining hours.
Table side guacamole is a good idea!
Lemon cookies!

sample_reviews.text:
A great trendy coffee house on my favourite street in the city. Roast is great for a couple reasons 1) they have amazing lattes, the salted caramel is definitely something to be sought after 2) the prices aren't much higher than that of Starbucks. 

The boyfriend and I adore coffee in a can't-function-without-it-in-the-morning kind of a way.

OMG YES, I adore this place. There are so many different types of tea to try and the staff are so knowledgable on all of the teas! I appreciate that you can tell them how much you want and you can sit and smell all the teas for hours. This place is a go-to for gifts (people always love them) and obviously my go-to when it comes to stocking my cupboards.

This is an adorable little 22 seat restaurant on 104th street! LOVE! The boyfriend and I made a reservation for this place (you need a reservation) and wandered in only a month after coming back from New York. To our surprise this place looked like it was in New York, which is what made me fall so madly in love with it. Edmonton doenst' have enough small restaurants that serve tapas and wine.

I thing that the food is over priced for what it is. The food is good but not amazing. This place is great for business events because it is a large private venue. 

The view is great and the staff are very welcoming but I feel that this venue should be a little more prestigious for what it is.

Good try Faculty Club.

I did not even know this place existed until my mom wanted to meet me there for lunch. Imagine that, my mother knows more about places to dine downtown than I do!  Anyways I came here for lunch and it wasn't busy when I showed up but boy did it get busy as the lunch hour ticked on. The plates here are HUGE, my mom and I most definitely should have split one.
$10 pizza on Mondays!
Classy classy classy!

sample_reviews.text: It's been a childhood favorite since I was a kid.  I've had the "other" one before it's just not as a good.  My go-to is an unsalted original (don't skimp on the butter) and of course lemonade.

Memorable spot for lunch in Carmel. It was a really great experience with exceptional food. All dishes were pretty tasty from the three items we tried: bruschetta, Caesar salad, bolognese. 

Like others have shared, their fresh pasta is amazing! I had to finish it off in its entirety.

This place is a gem for Broad Ripple!! And yes, I'm pretty sure I gave it an extra star for parking.  Kidding.  It was definitely for the bomb ass Crab Fried Rice.  It's my favorite thing on the menu.

Starting with the favorite...
Crab Fried Rice - 5 
Red Curry - 4
Pad Kee Mao - 3
Tom Kha - 3

Their spice levels range from 1-6.  And warning, it's on a Thai level.

This location needs new management.  It's been closed on two separate random occasions.  First time, we were told the "computers are down."  Second time, we walked up around 6ish PM and the doors were locked with a few employees inside.  There were no signs up that would give any indication they should be closed either.
Underwhelming and underseasoned food.   Had the fish and chips and the baked falafel sandwich.
Drive-thru is painfully slow.  You gotta see it to believe it.
Awesome service!!

sample_reviews.text:
Absolutely love this tap room. The fact that the indoor is dog friendly is a major plus for this new puppy mom. And the good beer? That's just another plus! 

Most recently went in on a Friday night, planning to meet a few friends with our pups. I was pleasantly surprised to see just how crowded it was! Barely any room to sit.

I went into this Five Guys location on a rainy, wet Thursday in desperate need of a burger fix. I am currently doing the whole30 diet which means I cannot have a multitude of things. But what I can have is a Five Guys burger patties with some toppings and make myself a little burger salad. 

Well Syvia K. made that ask not seem annoying (although I know it is).

Wow! 

Yup, review is over and I will leave you one word: wow. 

All jokes aside, I got the chance to attend the soft opening last night and was so thoroughly impressed. From the ambiance, to the servers hospitality, to (most importantly) the food - five stars all around. 

I went with a group of six and we got a table right off the host stand with large windows looking our into mass ave.

So happy to finally enjoy this gem! After hearing rave reviews week after week I took myself and my friend Colleen down to fountain square to try the finer brunch of life. 

For a Monday afternoon the place was hoping! I got lucky enough to find a spot in the lot when I got there (last one available). The patio and main dining area was full so we took a seat at the counter.

The ambiance!!!! Love love love the atmosphere. It's so cute. Also the margaritas- well they're big and they're strong. Well worth the money's. 

We started with the chips and salsa trio. Delicious! Came out quick and offered lots of variety of spices. 

I ordered fish tacos for my entree and was in love. The flavors were perfect.
Make sure to sign up for the rewards program. You get a free sugar cone just for signing up!
Hours range. Best to call or drive by. The chalkboard outside updates with hours.

sample_reviews.text:
I came here twice now and wished I had more time to stop by and eat more. First time eating here was a month ago with my friend and family.

The food is all home cooked like your momma cooks kinda food. Lol. Never knew how delicious Cambodian food can be.

Called last minute for brunch reservations. Was able to snag a table. The staff here is very efficient and pleasant.

Let's chow down about the brunch.

Blueberry waffles. Fluffy, light and perfect waffles, topped with blueberry whipped butter, thin slices of toasted almonds and a blueberry syrup. Everything about this plate was heavenly.

I have been here a few times already. There dim sum is limited but the szechuan style food is amazing. MUST order items.

Crispy fried fish. This dish is slightly spicy, crispy on the outside and tender on the inside. Fish will never be the same if you try this dish.

Mopo is a silken tofu dish. Mild in flavor but softens the spicy crispy fried fish. It pairs very well together.

First time here last night. The service we received from Brendan was great. Very nice and quick. The appetizers ordered were the Brie wontons, calamari, ceviche, short-rib tacos and cornbread. They were all good. The drinks here are very good. I had the calypso Mojito. Very good. 

The entrees that we order were ok.

Alittle disappointed with the food. Service was slow and alittle lacking. Lets talk about the food. I had the mini pork belly burger or bun. That was a rendition of a bun mi... Not so good. Bread was crispy but flavor combo was blend.

The chicken wings were ok. Served with cilantro and sesame seeds were ok.

I got the pomegranate mint tea. This tasted like the tazo tea packets.
Get the General Tso's chunk chicken and shrimp. The best I have ever tried.
Turkey bowls are back

This is about our 6th visit; just love this place.

Tough street parking on Magazine St; we come on our bikes.

Interesting "sliders".

Of the several Viet places in this 'hood; yeh very best is here.

Hadn't been to Muriel's i some 6 weeks.  Got a call from dear friends Roy & Rosie, of Corpus Christi & Houston.  They had "hopped a train" & were at the Roosevelt.   

A note to Denise Gratia got us a 1:30 late lunch @ Muriel's.

They had the broiled striped bass, which they loved.

I stuck with my three appetizers, plus dessert.

Good, Good, Very Good!

Reliable, consistent, spotlessly CLEAN.

This is out No #! place on this side of the River for Viet snacks & light meals.

Only problem here is street parking on Magazine Street.

The place on Manhattan has slightly better Banh Mi; but EVERYTHING else is BETTER here.

And, did I mention that this entire place is very, very CLEAN at all times?

Rick

DORIGNAC'S, once an area favorite grocery market, has alienated me, and many other customers.

Sorry, but I just don't go there any more.

Your choice; they made theirs.

Rick

WHAT, you have no Coors Light ?  You say you don't carry it now ?

Had a very, very good C B here tonight !

Nice new waitress, too.

Rick

I might be jumping the gun on this 5-star review, as I've only planted the veggies plants I got from Dekalb Procude about a month ago. Still, at this point, the health of these plants are probably fully in my hands.

What I like about Dekalb?  Solid selection at low prices when it comes to produce and gardening supplies.

Either my tastes have changed or Smiley's has improved.  Maybe a bit of both? Regardless, Smiley's went from an option to one of my regulars over the last several months.

Getting a beef gyro is simple but delivers a wonderfully tasty lunch or dinner.  I'm hooked on their tabouli salad.  I recently tried their falafel platter.

Came early evening on a Sunday for the two-person bbq special.  Normally, I'm torn on 'feeds two/four' type meals because there usually is a catch. However, after reading the listing, I was impressed with the selection offered and figured the worse than might be done with this special would be a trade off of volume for variety.

For $75, you get the standard banchan, two miso soups, two salads, a...

Unfortunately, Gypsy was not satisfied with my 4-star review because it noted Kristin's poor customer service.  Kristin decided to continue the downward spiral of poor customer service my attacking me via email and in the note attached to this review.

Their lunch specials lured me in.  Nothing to write home about and pizza in St. Louis will never compare to Philly-NYC anyway.  Service was good and the place was clean.  $6.75 gets you a 8" pizza with topping, soda, and small salad.  Good enough for me!
Now Open!
Lunch specials 11-3; any gyro and side 8.99

We order from here every 2 weeks at work. They have a great science to the sauce and the peppers that remind me of my Grandma's. Something about the olive oil and the peppers and the sauce fuse together. The crust is not thick nor thin, just right. 
Now come on, who can do that? 
The pizza will a little grease from meats, which is expected from a aa fresh pie.

Yelp AND traffic lead me here, one auto wreck after another so I had to go around the city to get back home. Wow! So very glad I was able to visit. Sad about the other commuters heading to work albeit we were ALL late by this time. 

Had coffee next door at Capital Coffee because 2 birds 1 stone and had time to wait for the traffic to clear up. Crumb de la Crumb does not open until 8 am.

Recieved a call from Big Boss, please bring donuts to meeting. I was already in a jam about making it to work, so called ahead. They were soo nice. I said I am in running behind, they said it will be ready in 15 minutes. In out and perfect order with 2 big Cartons of coffee, 3 dozen donuts and get this all the stuffs for the coffee all ready for me to run and pay and out the door.

This is for Deli only. 3 times I have been there the head of the Meat department is inappropriate and not very good at customer service. Final and last time, someone else helped me and then get this asked the Head Butcher guy again. arghhhhh If I wanted him, I would have just asked him. Needless to say, not my favorite and probably won't go for their meat.

It's a chain, it's pizza and wings on Super Bowl when you don't have a favorite. Yes, we had rude service, yes they messed up the pizza and the wings. Yes, they are residing on expensive Charlotte real estate. Not sure how long they will last.  Did not trust their delivery, but when we went there were 4 Pizza Hut cars there on Super Bowl Sunday. Odd. Pizza meh. I remember it being the best pizza.
Young people's first job. Remember they don't know anything so be patient and confirm order

6 to 7 minutes when
Ordering pick up

sample_reviews.text:
If Southern-style dishes made a baby with Southeast Asian cuisine and they decided to raise their love child in their quirky grandma's house (but it's that grandma that always made great comfort food), that's Marjie's Grill. 

The building is a bright lil' ray of sunshine on a underwhelming stretch of Broad Street.

Kids in a candy shop. My friend and I shopping for booze to get us through the bachelorette weekend were just this in Midtown Wine & Spirits. 

We were able to walk over from the Hutton Hotel to this land of libations.

First off, wooden carts. What? Where do you even get these?! So cute.

When it's two weeks past summer bod deadline and you're trying to moonwalk your way back to the goals you set, City Greens is a lovely local option with healthy, fresh ingredients that'll make you say, "mmmm, na-nah na-nah..."

While a good pun, of which they have many, is the way to my heart, their wraps and salads have really found a way to my belly.

This is my neighborhood grocery. Competitors in this area are more expensive when it comes down to it. I know where everything I want is, that staff is nice, there are rarely lines, and I never have to park far from the entrance. A cheap bottle of wine, a couple of cheeses, some salami, and a movie from the RedBox machine isn't a bad date night either, even if it's by myself occasionally.

Er, so the last time I went, I'm not sure if it was an off day but I wasn't offered the free soda/water with my Yaka-mein, no big deal really...but when I get home to bust into my giant thing of yaka, I am sad to say no boiled egg and no green onions.
Love their shot and a beer deal $7 Buffalo Trace shot and a Wolters Pilsner
Parking in the back!

sample_reviews.text: Love this place!!!! Food is awesome. Waiters and waitresses are very nice and attentive :) go eat here! You will not be let down.

Food was delicious as well, ribeye was cooked exactly how i like it.  Dessert was amazeballs..everything was perfect.  Do yourself a favor and try this place! !!

Wow! What a cute stinkin' place!!! I love the modern decor here. Like so much. I've wanted to come here for a while and finally got the chance to. Came here on a Wednesday afternoon and it wasn't busy at all so the service was fast. When I saw they had shishito peppers, I knew I had to get that. I've had such an obsession lately. And if you've never had them, you're missing out! They were good.

If I could give this place zero stars, I would. We sat in a dingy corner with crappy lighting, the table wasn't sturdy. Waiter LOU was no fun. Awful attitude. No utensils until asked for. No napkins. No water until asked for. Then!!!!!!!  We had some of our party leave and paid for their own tab.

Hmmm... it was okay in the beginning. But then the girl stuck her middle finger at her coworker which was very unprofessional. The food was ok. My steak had A LOT of fat on it. i love this place.
Love this place. Come here all the time. Waiters are awesome.

sample_reviews.text: They're now offering inside dining that's socially distanced. Because they own Caffe Buondi next door, they've opened that up for dinner-time Convivio dining too, which gives them more space. We also called to ask for curbside and they did that as well. The homemade pasta is always SOOOOO good, even when it's just via takeout. You can't go wrong!

Such a cute spot! We've been to the Carmel and Mass Ave locations, and saw Yelp Elite Lee S serving wine here a few months back, so it had to be great right?! 

This weekend, they had awesome live musicians playing and opened up the space completely to the outside. They had arrows pointing where to order and Edit so that the traffic flowed one way.

This colorful Argentinian restaurant is such a special spot. Owner Marcos was born and raised in Argentina and found the opportunity to share that with the community here by making delicious empanadas, sausages, sandwiches and more. They were recently named one of Indy Monthly's top 30 restaurants in the city in 2021. Congrats! That's a huge deal!

I came here because Kona Jack's was closed but found out that you can order Kona's sushi here too. 

I was very impressed by their food! The sushi was incredible, fresh, and the menu had a ton of options. I ordered the Indy Roll which had crab and avocado. It was super yummy and tasted great with the Miso Soup! 

They are also apparently known for their Clam Chowder, which was incredible as well.

Surprisingly impressed!

I was hungry and knowing that they had flatbreads, decided to ride my bike over here. 

They have wayyyyy more than flatbreads! They have an amazing menu that contains everything from yummy quesadillas to brisket to maple grilled salmon. 

I tried the nachos, maple salmon and house salad (amazing house dressing).
It's 21+ only and the patio is dog-friendly!
They have four curbside parking spots out front if you want your food brought to you!

sample_reviews.text:
Yah Mon is a very contemporary Jamaican restaurant with top notch service and good food. It's the service and atmosphere which prompted me to give 5 stars instead of 4. Very attentive and friendly service, aimed to please. 

But let's talk about the food. Two types of bread are served complimentary. Both excellent but I loved the one that was slightly sweetened with cream.

As far as Mediterranean food goes, Petra (Jordanian) is the best I've had. Both locations are consistent with their flavors though I rate this location on Busch higher as their service is markedly better than downtown. The chicken shawarma with hummus and seasoned fries is a pretty perfect meal. I wish I could tell you what spices they use to season the chicken but I have no idea.

Since I eat out every meal and write reviews on primarily food-related businesses, I decided to dedicate some time volunteering at Feeding Tampa Bay, the local chapter of Feeding America (the nation's largest "hunger-relief and food-rescue organization"). The Friday night I volunteered happened to occur on their annual 24 hour "sort-a-thon".

I've been here 5-6 times but probably won't come back...I ordered a banh mi thit nuong. I've had banh mi at many places and this was prob one of the worst. the bread was too crispy,the pork was too fatty and had a weird flavor. I should've just gone to Thuy Cafe (their lemongrass chicken banh mi is very good).

Not a good experience. First of all, the place was extremely loud. I'm not sure if it's always like this or just because I went on a Monday evening when the Bucs would be playing later and they had a $5 burger special. It was a packed house.  It was headache-inducing.
Took over the old Sweet Tomatoes
Chinese bakery coming soon, also serves boba, coffee and sandwiches ..located by MD Market - Brandon, next to First Choice BBQ

sample_reviews.text:
In the style of Brett N, why 5 stars?

Because 1967.

 ---   ---   ---

Much like the fictitious TV Cheers bar, every neighborhood has a local watering hole where everyone knows each other.  For folks from the SOG, Bayshore Heights, Palma Ceia and Virginia Park areas, this is that place.

This was the first bar I visited on my own accord, and first establishment in which I legally threw back some...

Attended a private birthday party here Saturday night that featured some appetizers and cocktails, all if which were excellent. The charcoal infused drink was good, but the color made me think of grape Kool-Aid and I just couldn't shake it, mentally. The megalixer though was very refreshing and light.

The state of the Cuban sandwich in the Tampa area makes me a little bit sad. With the exception of a few restaurants, I've always got to answer a laundry list of questions when ordering one. All the way? Pressed? Mustard? Mayo?  What is this nonsense???  Just give me a damn Cuban!  Ham, perníl, salami, Swiss cheese, pickles and mustard on Cuban bread. 7 ingredients,  folks.

Was chillin' at The Independent and decided to walk over to Cappy's to give their brand a second chance.  Years ago, I ate at the location in SoHo (currently Cheap) and it was just okay.  They advertised using Grande cheese, but any real NY-style pizza connoisseur could tell that it either was NOT Grande, or they were mixing Grande with a cheaper cheese.

What a shit show!  Pulled up on Sunday evening around 6PM to check this place out based on some word of mouth reviews, only to find that the place is too big for it's britches.  It looks like there are about 10 parking places for this restaurant, which means your only other option is to park in some of the nearby county beach areas, which will cost you money.
Everyone was masked up. Hand sanitizer all over the place. Food was good, but could use some tweaking. Will be back.
New address is 7045 Ridge Rd

sample_reviews.text: Came here for sips and got a cocktail that included gin, mint, cucumber and lemon I believe? Absolutely delicious and so refreshing on a hot day! Will be coming back for food and other cocktails in the future, but Aldine, I am quite happy so far.

Holy moly this place is delicious! And incredibly affordable!! 

My boyfriend and I started off with the cold dips appetizer and loved all of them!! I was wary of the caviar one, however, it kind of ended up tasting like lox cream cheese. The potato and garlic one was also delicious, but beware. It's very intense on the garlic. Grilled pita was served piping hot and a perfect compliment!!

YAAAAAAAAAAASSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSS!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!! This is so incredibly exciting to see close to my home as my fiance and I got through college cause of these burgers. Any one of them, you will not be upset. The bun is fantastic, the meat is well seasoned, the vegetables he puts on there (and pickles) are perfection, and I'm always a satisfied customer!

Keep 'em coming Josh!

I am giving this place three stars because I think I did it wrong. I'm the kind of person who doesn't feel full when I get a cold meal, and that's exactly what I did.

Chipotle style line up, but sushi/teriyaki fillings. I got the spicy tuna, with cabbage, carrots, cucumbers, avocado slaw, rice, and black pepper teriyaki as my sauce. It was really a giant sushi roll.

Got a southern sandwich and was felt very medium about my sandwich. The chicken was nice and crispy, but seemed slightly undercooked. Also, the combination of the pretzel crust and tomato marmalade made for a very sweet sandwich. A little too much for me. I had to put some hot sauce on it to balance it out. 

Good things were the coleslaw. They used kale as well as cabbage. Made me feel healthy.

$11 yards pale ale pitchers, pool tables, close to my house. 

Enough said.

Thanks for the reminder, Yelp! I love Fair Grinds, yet I apparently never wrote them a review. So here's the five star review they've been missing for almost 2 years now. 

First a mention on how they are one of the only coffeehouses in NOLA that's dedicated to fair trade coffees and all of their tea is organic.

Lilly's about saved my life last week! With temps dropping to the point of actual *icicles* forming, I still had to be out and about (not such a bad thing as pretty much anywhere is going to be warmer than my old drafty house) and although our intention was to get one of the amazing salads at nearby HiVolt, all I had to do was walk by Lilly's and change my mind because... well, vegan phó.

I can't leave this review at 4 stars. Nope, Mint is a straight-up five.

Five for the lychee mojito. Five for the creative cocktail menu featuring an array of tropical Thai flavors!

Five for the friendly, attentive servers.

Five for those divine avocado rolls, bursting with fragrant herbs, chewy vermicelli noodles and silken avocado (dipped into peanut sauce reminiscent of manna from...

Meh to the Bloody Marys. Rare is it that I'm not compelled to order two, and here, I was not. 

And trust me, with the way I was feeling that day... it was bloody necessary. A bland Bloody Mary is a sin in New Orleans and I hold Bloody Marys up to my city's high standards.

As others mention, the front patio is all what this place is about.

It's always a good sign when you're warmly greeted upon entering an establishment. I don't know about you, but it's rare to be utterly disappointed after that. Or, perhaps you just don't let yourself be. The importance of good reception should not be overlooked!

With that in mind, I bring you my review of Ricardo's Italian Cafe.
Fried pickles are vegan!
Killer vegan options, including mushroom boudin balls.

sample_reviews.text: Wow. All I can say after the inspiration that was Penang on Friday night. We were able to get a table for 2 within about 5 minutes of walking in, but I recommend coming early or getting a reservation. Once inside, food was fast, waitress was kind and quick to get our order, and everything was fresh and hot.

I found this place recently and I am never letting go. On a quiet side street, find your new solution to lunch sandwiches, cheesy appetizers, chicken wings, or really anything you might want. Delicious and affordable mega fries, amazing 2 foot hoagies, and the panzerotti was a delight as well. Roast pork sandwich and blt were my favorites.

Open when I needed it most. Wanted to get some rice and beans and meat in my diet and this spot gave me a good pick. The chicken and pork here are cooked in different styles, but every dish tasted well flavored, coming with a fresh mini salad (if you ask) and amazing rice. My favorite two parts, though, were sweet corn tamales and the potato ball. OMG, what?

The portions on the daily deals are crazy small... Like smaller than kids portions. I never would have ordered knowing that for a few dollars less, you get less than half of the food in a regular dish. Cracker Barrel is usually a safe bet, but avoid the deals for sure.

The lines are always extremely long and the customer service here has never been great, but at least I was able to get tasty food. This most recent visit, I tried the mac and cheese and a small hoagie, but I was extremely disappointed.
Hours not as listed currently (Feb. 2021)

Amazing vegan pizza in the hot food
section!

sample_reviews.text: We had a truly phenomenal meal and experience at Sushi Sho Rexley. We enjoyed the Samurai Omakase (the restaurant only offers omakase on the weekends- two different versions). Each bite was better than the last.

I don't know how I haven't updated my review sooner! Brick & Mortar is one of our absolute favorite restaurants in the Tampa area. The food is phenomenal. The service is very good, too! David is our favorite server. I haven't had a bad dish but our favorites are the burger, short rib fat noodles, and bouillabaisse. We haven't had a bad meal here.

I had heard people raving about Paci's for a while, so I had my eye on the opening of Mirro's. Hubs and I decided to give it a try for Pizza Friday. We weren't starving so I didn't call to order until 7:45, which was a bit of a mistake because I was then quoted a 1-hour wait (the total time ended up being about an hour and 10 minutes). But honestly, it was well worth the wait!

Walked in around 12:30pm today (Sunday). Realized they had a brunch menu after we sat down and we were disappointed because there were only 4 lunch options (the rest were breakfast). We sat for about 10 minutes before our server came over for a drink order. We each asked for water and I ordered a coffee. The server rushed off before we could order our meals. 5 minutes later, no water.

This place was OK...but only OK. The crepes were so underfilled....I honestly think I got one tiny piece of olive in my vegetarian crepe. The best one was the nutella and strawberry crepe....and it's pretty hard to screw that up. As other reviewers said, there was no A/C.
This lovely place is RESERVATION ONLY! They will not be open unless they have reservations. And they only source enough food for the reservations that are made in advance - everything is fresh!
Ice cream sandwiches are delish!
