sample_reviews.text:
What can I say? I think I found a new favorite restaurant!! 
This place has it all. From pizza and burgers to Italian and creole dishes; and it's all delicious. 
To start, we had onion rings and swamp fries. The onion rings were big and yummy. The menu boasts that the rings are triple battered. The remoulade sauce served with the rings was divine.

The service has been great for the past two times that I've visited there. 
I'm literally in and out for lunch before the lunch hour is over! You can order at lunch from their dinner menu also. The portions at lunch are still a nice size, but if you are truly hungry, order from the dinner menu. Prices are moderate. 
Their food is delicious. I love their guacamole.

Open for take out or pick up orders! No one was sitting inside eating. 
The staff wears masks and gloves. 
I love their burrito bowl. I get it with brown rice, beans, steak, pico de gallo, cheese, lettuce and guacamole. I also order a side of chips and queso. 
You can also order the bowl with chicken. They have grilled onions and mushrooms to add.

The Pork banh mi was mediocre at best. The French bread was harder than I'm used to eating from any Vietnamese restaurant. The flavors weren't there. The sandwich was bland compared to other Pork banh mis that I've tasted in the city. 
I also had the avocado and green bean rice. Basically it was white rice with black pepper.

I was not overly impressed with the Poke bowls. If I wanted someone to throw together ingredients in a bowl touching each other, I would have just went to the grocery store. It was just OK. 
I enjoyed the pork dumplings. They have a little kick to them. But they were pretty good. 
The waitress recommended the crab balls. They were not very flavorful and pretty dry.
The Hawaii Roll is sooo yummy!  It's tuna topped with snow crab and mango.
Now serving Sunday Brunch!

sample_reviews.text: I've been to several Sweet Cece's locations, but I wanted to review this one specifically because I think it reigns superior! I've noticed this one always has every topping available. I went to the Sweet Cece's in Hillsboro Village, and that location only had a fraction of the fruit toppings available.

I have found my favorite sushi place in Nashville! I've been here three times in one week. 

Despite what anyone says about Sam, I think this place is fantastic. It's very straight forward. You sit down and wait for Sam to address you so you can order (that may mean 2 minutes or 15 minutes). All the rules are pretty much posted on the wall. 

If you don't like Sam.... or better yet...

Before heading to Ninki, I read all the mixed reviews- so I was a little nervous. However, I was craving sushi and the 50% off of selected sushi rolls seemed too good to turn down, especially on this part of town where the pickins for good Asian cuisine are slim. 

When we walked inside, a friendly host greeted us. The restaurant was more spacious than I expected.

The Woodstock burger I ordered was good, but not necessarily worth the steep price. The fries were also average. I definitely liked the Jack Daniel's maple ketchup. I think I walked away paying $20 for a burger meal, including a drink and tip.

The service was also average. Although it appeared pretty slow in the restaurant, the hostess wouldn't seat me until my friend arrived.

I can see where this place could be really cool and appealing, but it just wasn't my cup of tea. 

First of all, I found ordering to be very stressful. When you walk in, there is a counter with pencils and order "forms." You check off all the items you want and hand it to a cashier. Problem is... there are no prices or descriptions listed on that form.

FIG LASSI!!!!

I almost wish that I never tried the lassi here....almost.  I am now addicted and I can't stomach that thick gross stuff served pretty much everywhere else.  I am totally in love with the lassi here.   It is light and fresh tasting, and not too sweet.  And the flavors they use are very fresh - no artificial flavoring here.  Amazing.

I was really excited to try Kermit's and their food did not disappoint.  

Regular/ red pizza: Flavorful sauce, perfect amount of cheese, amazing thin crust which still held up with the ingredients on top.  Delicious.

White pizza with mushrooms and leeks: This is so good!

This place will give you what feels like an authentic Afghani experience, although I've never been to the country.  The patrons there all seemed like friends and family of the owners.  There was a large party watching Afghani Top 40 music videos while smoking hookas.  Everyone was speaking a foreign language, the décor seemed to be imported from Afghanistan.

I would have never gone here if my friends didn't invite me to meet them out at this bar one night last weekend.   I admit to being a bit of an atmosphere snob most of the time, and this place wasn't on my radar - I actually never heard of it until they texted me the name and address that night.

This place is big.  It has a downstairs restaurant area and then a bar upstairs.

Everyone who knows me knows that I don't eat meat.  When this place opened, everyone started asking me if:  I'd been here? Did I love it?  How much did I love it?  Isn't it SO GREAT??  I really wanted to love it! But in reality, I'm not so sure.  It's more like a kind of like situation.

I visited a few times to give HCV several attempts to impress me.

We stopped in for dinner on a Wednesday night after dropping our son off at college.  We sat in their lively outdoor patio and tried several of their small plates.

Apparently the menu outside is a big different than inside.

A lively bar with lively food with a bit of a Mexican twist.

We stopped in here for a great drink made from orange juice and rum and enjoyed their famous Nashville hot fried chicken taco.  The taco had huge chunks of fried white meat drenched in a spicy cayenne pepper sauce with a dab of cooling coleslaw on top and served on a street-taco-sized flour tortilla.

A clever boutique hotel right in the middle of downtown with a great sense of design.

The more I travel the more I'm in search of unique, specialty hotels that break the cookie-cutter mold of the big chains.

We arrived here a little before our reservation on a bustling Saturday night and were seated promptly at a nice round table by the window.  It was several minutes before our waiter was able to get to us so we gave him our order right away as we were on a bit of a tight schedule.

The Cesar salad came our right away - fine, very traditional, nothing too special and we had to ask a second time to...

Wonderful if your a teen, another fried fast food restaurant in my book.  Perfect for watching the big game with a crowd but not exactly my cup of tea.

Only reason I came was that I was dropping off my son at college and I let him choose.  Even my " healthy" salad tasted gloppy.

sample_reviews.text: Before I start, I have to give thanks to a Yelper extraordinaire, Kendrah R.. Without her advice it is unclear if I ever would have heard of this place. I will not bore you with all of the delectable details, and attempt to keep this short. 1) You HAVE to order the antipasto here. Tonight it consisted of more than 15 separate components, each perfectly executed.

Finding a place like this can be a bad thing. Especially if you like even the most basic examples of popcorn. For here you will find the humble kernel elevated to its most a maize ing(tm) heights.

It was the close-up photos on the outside of the business that peaked our interest. We decided to enter but the door was locked. A kindly woman in a neighboring shop noticed us and ushered us in.

I've sampled Dave's treats at a fancy food festival held on Grounds for Sculpture in Hamilton NJ years ago. Back then he had a location in Hopewell that by the time I visited, was permanently closed. So I book marked this spot and finally got here on a Saturday night. The Yelp info was lacking as to hours of operation, and the phone number given was just a recording with not much info either.

If you are walking around GFS and are a bit peckish, this is not a bad choice. Food is very casual but fresh and prepared when you order. Soups are homemade and are usually very good, pizzas not too bad for kid's tastes. But you don't really go here for the food alone, more for the convenience of not having to leave the park, and also for the great atmosphere in the courtyard.

Breakfast only review. Cool chrome encrusted diner that is very cozy and clean inside. I loved all the booths that were available. And I was impressed with the customer service provided by the owner or manager as well as our server.

My gf opted for the pumpkin pancake special. They accommodated her request for chocolate chips to be added.
Pine land farms for corn always!
Rat's is no longer under the SStarr umbrella. It is owned now by Constellation. This is a downgrade in quality.

sample_reviews.text: In my mind, a line out the door and sometimes filling out into the parking lot can never be a bad thing. This line situation has ALWAYS been the case, and I've been coming here since I was little girl when trips home from grandmas on I-70 meant taking a detour off the interstate to Gray Brothers Cafeteria.

New location, same great wonderfulness. I don't have enough great things to say about 3 Sisters. 

Sometimes during weekend brunches, the wait times seem not tolerable, especially when you're starving like I usually am when I come here, but I can PROMISE you that this gem in Broad Ripple is without a doubt worth the wait. 

Let's talk food.

Fresco is out of this world good. Seriously, they make some of the better sandwiches I've ever had. My office orders from them frequently for lunch meetings, and the owner is always accomodating to our requests. 

I've had the caprese sandwich, the Italian beef and the stromboli and all have been great.

I was sadly underwhelmed by this place. The Butler basketball team has raved about Tomo on Twitter for years, so I figured, good enough for the Bulldogs, good enough for me. Not so much. 

Granted, we did have a wait and it was busy, but when my friend and I finally got a spot at the counter, we couldn't get anyone's attention to take our order.

Ehh... methinks not.

A group of 6 friends and I visited Asian Harbor on a weeknight for a birthday celebration and I have to admit I was pretty disappointed. Not by the food. The food was delicious and the portion sizes were huge. However, the service was a huge miss for me and unfortunately, because of that, I don't think I am likely to return. 

The restaurant was not busy.
Closed on Sunday
Half price ALL sushi rolls on Sunday!

sample_reviews.text: OMG!  I had the best fries ever!  I got the steak frites (a New York strip steak cooked to perfection & shoestring fries).  The fries had the perfect amount of saltiness with a touch of rosemary.  Hello, foodgasm!  Foodgasm, after foodgasm.  Thank you, Cafe Provencal for making my taste buds happy!

Words could not describe my excitement when I entered this store.  I have a long standing love affair with chocolate from this place.  The pates de fruits are delish!  Tiny place, but insane amounts of flavors.  Much smaller store than the one in Maplewood.  Parking is a bit of a pain, but well worth it.  On the same side as Pastaria.

I came here to have dinner with a friend who recommended this place for their steaks.  I would never think that this was a restaurant judging by the outside appearance.  It looks like a house.  The interior was very inviting & warm.  I loved it!  The New York Strip steak was soooo good & delish!  The meat was very tender.  Loved it!  This is what I miss about the Midwest, great tasting beef.

While I agree with Nancy P. about the elegance aspects of this restaurant, I had a different experience than Nancy P..  I've been there twice for lunch.  The first time I went, the sandwich that I got was ok.  Kind of greasy if I can recall, but that was my fault for not telling the waitress about my special needs.  The chips were good, but not the sandwich.  The second time wasn't sooo good.

Ambience was great.  Staff was so-so.  Breakfast here wasn't all that great.  There could have been more variety instead of the usual hash browns, eggs & bacon.  Albeit, breakfast here is tasty.  Choice of teas was lacking.
Morning Glory muffins are super delish!  The Dolci Della taste like Corn Pops but only better.  Much better.
No more happy hour for bagels.

sample_reviews.text:
COVID19 Quarantine Review (Pick Up)

Great Taste is my go to Chinese Food spot, and I was happy to see they're still operating during the Quarantine. 

For my most recent order, I called in my order on a Saturday night for pick up. When I arrived at Great Taste to pick up, I was happy to see they had a steady business. 

Upon arrival, park and head into the storefront.

After hearing about the newest ice cream shop in our area, Lickety Splitz, I couldn't wait to give it a try. My first time here (definitely not my last) was on a Wednesday night around 8 PM. Lickety Splitz is connected to Laurel Lanes Bowling. 

Lickety Splitz offers a variety of flavors - both sweet and sinful. The difference? Sinful is alcohol infused.

Johnson's Corner Farm is probably the most popular farm in the area, and anyone growing up locally has been here a handful of times. 

The Farm offers a "Farm Market" that sells their local produce and baked goods, tractor rides, seasonal fruit & veggie picking, a children's area with play equipment and a splash pad, and a small "zoo" where you can feed goats, chickens and cows.

I recently spotted a groupon deal for Best Tour's Cupcake & Macaron Tour Of Philadelphia. Sweets at a discount? Of course I bought it! 

I scheduled my tour for a Saturday in the Fall. Two days before the tour I received a text message saying the tour was canceled due to low participation.

This Acme is one of the smaller and less crowded grocery stores in the area. I have never had an issue with customer service. In fact, I can't say enough nice things about the butcher at ACME. He was kind enough to help my mom and I select the appropriate size meat for a party and even cracked the ribs and trimmed some fat for us.  

Selection and inventory here is typical to any grocery store.
Non Alcohol Options Available - Nitro Draft Coffees & Teas
Great select of waterice flavors. Plenty of parking. Cash only!

So my wife and I decided to drop in after I saw a bunch of people on Facebook raving about this place. They were saying stuff like it's the best restaurant in the CH Mall and that it was "an amazing restaurant"... People went on to day that it was one of their favorite restaurants.

The décor was nice... didn't look overly high end, but it was nice for a store in the mall.

The menu is small...

I only tried this place out because my office is within walking distance. Before I went, I checked out Yelp. I saw so many negative reviews, but I was really jonsin for some Chinese food.

I took a chance... I figured that a lunch order of General Tso's Chicken with fried rice and wonton soup for $6.00 sounded super cheap. But I could just throw it out if I didn't like it. 

Long story short...

I come here at times since it's so close to work... today, I stopped in to get some lunch. sample_reviews.text: Dunedin Downtown market is held on Fridays and Saturdays at Pioneer Park. On my recent visit on a Friday the market was bustling with people and vendors. Lots of fresh produce, plants, crafts and creations were featured by local merchants. I enjoyed seeing Dancing Cats there--check out their great concoctions! 20 Shekles is also always a delight to see.

A small bakery with some very avant-grade products. Sami's Bakery products are well known amongst the health conscious and those with dietary needs or restrictions. Sold in many local specialty markets and ONLINE, their products are suitable for people who strive to eat gluten-free and who want healthier options.

What is there not to like about Target? Except perhaps you always leave for more than you came with but is that really a bad thing? I admittedly usually find more stuff than I initially go in for but I'm not hating on it. I like the offerings at Target and am glad that I can shop here locally. 

I really am impressed by this Target!

I was happy to try this newer South Tampa shop which offers healthy options for meals or snacks. Enerjuicer features smoothies and acai bowls as well as snacks and some quick grab meals. I feel that they have a great selection of natural, healthy snacks and their smoothie selections are varied. 

The fruit used is fresh and on display which I liked seeing.

Fast, Fresh, Flavorful!!! This new quick serve restaurant fits the bill for an easy and efficient dining experience. I believe it is the creme de la creme of "fast food". 

Located in the "Clearwater Mall" off the well-traveled Gulf to Bay Blvd. the restaurant has a good amount parking adjacent to it. Newk's Eatery is a restaurant concept that has found the Tamoa Bay Area from Missisippi.
Margarine, not butter, applied on baked goods. A side of butter is available upon request

sample_reviews.text: Food is great for the price and the portion sizes! The employees are all very nice and the service is quick. You can expect to have a good filling Italian or Greek meal on a budget. I got a gyro this time and it was great. I'll definitely be back!

Came here twice, the first time was shortly after they opened and the second was a random time during the day so I was able to dodge the long lines! 

I looovvveeee their rolled ice cream. You get to choose the type of mix in you want with your ice cream as well as toppings to go on top.

This is my favorite taco spot! Their tacos are stuffed with flavorful, juicy meat and the portions are very generous! I'm also a huge fan of their tortas and tamales. The food is fresh and the prices are reasonable!

Sake House is decent for college students that want cheap sushi but I wouldn't recommend this place for those that are looking for high quality sushi. 

This restaurant has both positives and negatives. The positives: it opens late, close to the USF campus, cheap sushi with great lunch and dinner specials (ex. pick 2 rolls with soup & salad for less than $15), and big cuts of fish.

Mei's is the mom and pop shop you've been looking for. 

Located in a small plaza, this restaurant doesn't look the most appealing while you're driving on Bearss. But don't be fooled, disguised as a primarily pizza shop, Mei's has a wonderful assortment of Taiwanese food which definitely exceeded my expectations.

Mei's is relatively close to where I live and from the USF campus.
Discount for USF students! Bring your ID!

sample_reviews.text: I have been to Rockwell Beer a couple of times now. It's like if a pretty smart kid erected a building with brightly colored shipping containers and cinderblocks instead of legos and then filled it with beer. When you sit in the bar area you can see the tanks and the pallets of product. If you're my age you can't help but think of Bob and Doug running around Elsinore Brewery!

How are you going to give Andy's Custard anything less than a 5? The custard is creamy and delicious! Every location I have been to is speedy and efficient.  I usually get either a cherry chip concrete or the cherry bash. Today I got cherry chip. Sometimes it's best to keep it simple! Andy's Custard is the best!

I had nachos on a Wednesday afternoon. The tortilla chips with the concession stand nacho cheese sauce really hit the spot! My coworkers got ice cream and next time I'm going to have to try one of the milkshakes. They looked delicious! We will be back!

I hadn't been to Yolklore since summer 2016 when they opened. I went on a weekday morning and ordered The Nest Egg (Biscuit crust, 2 Buttonwood Eggs, Bacon Bits, Cheddar, Pickled Red Onion, Preserved Lemon) and a hot tea just as I had done previously. I still find the community seating situation here a little uncomfortable and intrusive.  My breakfast can best be described as a hot mess.

This was a particularly busy, dirty, and chaotic Walmart. I don't think I had ever been here before and I doubt I will be back. I needed a new wireless router. The one I wanted was $29.99 but they lock them up so you have to find someone who will actually help you. You can't count how many items over $29.99 aren't locked up. What's the point?
Don't do it. Go somewhere else!
6/6/18 Permanently Closed

sample_reviews.text: I stopped in to pick up a cake for a birthday party.  I got the 8 inch 'Seeing Spots' with a vanilla chocolate chip pound cake and chocolate butter cream frosting.  The cake was amazing- so moist and delicious with layers of chocolate chip and butter cream.    All the other pastries also looked fantastic as did the pizza. I cannot wait to stop in to try their pizza next time.

Sometimes you just wanna throw on elastic waist pants and gorge on delicious southern comfort food in a non-judgy environment.  Fat Ham is the place to do it.  The food was decadent and delicious and the staff was extremely friendly.  Kevin Sbraga was even there to help us pick out what to order...or what NOT to order!

What's better on a cold day than a sizzling bowl flavorful soft tofu soup with kimchi & beef.  This place is fantastic and extremely cheap.  They have a nice selection of different soondubus- i can't want to come back to try the seafood one.  They also give you a nice selection of banchan (small dishes before the meal), which comes with an egg for you to crack and mix into your soondubu.

Ugh, I get ass-raped by the prices here but yet I still go because I am lazy and it is only around the corner.  For a while, this was the only grocery store I was going to, until I realized that the price of tomato sauce really had not gone up to 9.99 a jar (and yes, it's regular tomato sauce that they sell at Shop-Rite for 3.99).  

On the plus side, they do have a fairly good selection.

I really wanted to love this place.  The place is the former Warsaw Cafe location and is super cute on the inside and out.  The service was a bit slow, but the owner/waiter/food runner was extremely friendly.  The restaurant was relatively full but they only had 1 waitress and the owner who functioned as a waiter/food runner.

sample_reviews.text: I confess!  I've become not only a bit of a coffee person but I love going to La Colombe for my morning cup of coffee.  My friend is a huge fan of La Colombe and so I tried the Colombian coffee and it's definitely the best cup of coffee around!  The Colombian is a light roast and now Starbucks overpriced blonde roast, Veranda pales in comparison to La Colombe's Colombian in my humble opinion!

I come here often with friends and family, I'm a fan of this Marathon and Marathon on the Square.  I love the outdoor seating here in nice weather because for whatever reason, this Marathon and the one on the square tend to get a bit noisy.

Okay, I admit it!  I've been won over by this Marathon!  I'm more apt to use their delivery or pickup services- especially because I live 2 blocks away!!!  What made me score 5 instead of 4 stars recently are two recent positive experiences- one, I ordered delivery and seriously, no joke, it was at my door within 10-15 minutes, WOW!

I come to this Starbucks often because I'm frequently in the area.  I really want to be a fan of this Starbucks, after all they do have friendly baristas but here's what annoys me:

They almost never have decaf brewing so it's impossible to get a decaf unless it's a decaf Americano or on the clover press- I've asked for a pour over and they don't have a pour over system- what?!

This is a great CVS, the pharmacists there are so knowledgeable and kind, the pharmacy staff is very good too.  There is a good variety of items and I'm always using my Extra-Care card.  It's nice to know and trust the pharmacists, I feel welcomed when I come into the pharmacy.
Cute market, good food choices, but it would be nice if they had a chip reader for CC payments or accepted Apple Pay.

Just want to give a shout out for Pietro's!
I had a delicious veggie pizza here recently!  
They're staying open currently and are practicing social distancing.  Very friendly and fast service!

sample_reviews.text:
Oh LL, where have you been?  You're just what the McMullen Booth area needs!

I let a few friends prior to a concert at Ruth Eckerd and we had a lovely time. It's located in an unassuming strip center, but don't let that deter you from stopping. 

The wine selections were solid, the menu was interesting and enough variation to appeal to the masses and the atmosphere was inviting too.

Fancier but not overpriced, Muriel's offers a wonderful taste of NOLA. Overlooking Jackson Square allows for great people watching and horse drawn carriage viewing, which kept our daughter's interest. 

We both went with the pre fix menu and were very pleased. The gumbo appetizer was the star and the shrimp and grits meal was the standout.

Exciting times for Oldsmar!  We now have our own First Watch!!!!!

Quaint, high quality, fresh and interesting mix on ingredients, First Watch is a solid choice for breakfast and lunch. I really enjoy their seasonal soups, breakfast frittatas and center cit bacon.

Probably the worst dining experience we have had in a long time-it was so bad, it was comical. 

This place is run by a bunch of kids. I didn't see a manager or owner anywhere. Initially we were sat in a dark area, away from the windows and any other patrons. We immediately asked to be moved. It was depressing.

Meh...that's how I sum this place up. The bagels were not on display, therefore not tempting me with their potential yumminess. 

I ordered a cinnamon raisin with honey/pecan cream cheese. 

I felt that my bagel was a day old-definitely not fresh and at $2.85 I was disappointed. 

Not much else to say....
pricey and sllloooowwww
Order the tacos!  Tamales too

sample_reviews.text: There's are reasons why Isaac's is world famous. It's because of their delicious burgers on toasted buns with grilled onions crisp bacon, guacamole, jalapeños, lettuce and tomato. They also have TWO types of delicious and sumptuous veggie burgers: one bean based and the other on a homemade tofu patty. Also because of their spicy chili. And their wings.

What a great market with wonderful delicacies from Eastern Europe and Russia. Bulgaria. Georgia. Cheeses. Smoked fish. Canned fish. Soups. Pierogies. Bags of frozen gooseberries, currants, and lingonberries. Honey. Desserts. Beverages such as cherry juice. Cookies. Meats. 

Fun to explore and try things. You can hit it after Mr.

They always have great deals and the customer service for me has always been excellent. I've done drive thru and walk in (pre-Corona). Soft serve cones (cups now because of Corona) for .50 at the mo. Combo deals. They gave away kids meals sans toys during the early Corona lockdown. The food is inexpensive and the quality is... Affordable. I have fun visiting and they always take care of me.

I can't get past the flies. As soon as you sit down with your food they are everywhere. Flies, flies and more flies. Thank goodness  they aren't the kind that bite.

It's a cute stand open Friday and Saturdays. Wood fired. Outdoor picnic bench seating with flies.

The beef ribs are meaty and tasty. Don't even need sauce although they are unseasoned. Flies.

The pulled pork was limp.
They allow smoking inside. Gross. And the DJ was terrible. Otherwise it's a nice little club next to Kelly's. And a fun mix of people.
I think this may be Pho Bistro now? Remodel? New place? You heard it here first. Where has Mark P. been????
New Peruvian sandwich place.

sample_reviews.text:
Brunch.  BYOB.  Baklava. Beautiful!

Why has it taken me so long to get to Kanella?  I don't know, but I've been twice now in the last month and I can't wait to go back.  I wasn't surprised when it was easy to get a reservation for an early Wednesday dinner.

This has quickly become one of my favorite spots in the city whether for lunch, light afternoon snack, dinner, late evening snacks . . .  There is  never a bad time to go to Tria.  Their ever-evolving wine list always features a wide enough selection to suit anyone in any mood, whether you want a cozy, familiar Cali Pinot Noir, or something adventurous from South Africa with powerful bell pepper...

This update (and upgrade) to my review of Khyber is long overdue!

I think the most ringing summary is that I have since moved ~1 mile further away from the Khyber, and I still go there just as often.  I have now sampled most of the menu, but the truly excellent Southern barbecue is what usually calls to me.

I have been trying to find a solid "nice" pizza place in Philly for years; after my first trip to Rocchino's, I'm still  searching.

I came with my husband for a late-ish pizza dinner on a recent blustery Saturday night around 9:30.  A few tables were occupied, and we were pleasantly greeted at the door and seated immediately.

The South Street Night Market was just another nail sealing me in my "I'm officially too old for this sort of thing" coffin.

I made the mistake of not getting there until after 8:00, by which time the crowds barely allowed one to move down the street.  I stood in a 45-minute line to get a grilled cheese sandwich.  It was expensive, not very large and not very good.

sample_reviews.text: Amazing. I haven't ventured out to Collingswood before, but this is an incredible place to go for a nice dinner. It's BYOB so be sure to bring your own to really round out the meal. First, the service was incredible. Our waiter was attentive, would literally bend over backwards for us, and an incredible host. The off-menu prawns were delicious, tender, and complemented well with the sauce.

This place is great. Reportedly on of President Biden's favorite spots - and I can definitely see why. The food is great. I highly recommend the risotto, and especially the fried calamari. The ricotta gnocchi was creamy and delicious, but it was definitely a small portion. The ambiance in intimate and it is definitely a great place for a date.

This place is great. I feel like Philly has the whole spectrum of seafood (ranging from pretty mediocre to incredible), and Tuna Bar definitely falls on the latter side. Tucked away next to the Delaware River, this place is a great place for hanging out with friends, going on a date, or just grabbing a drink. My friend and I got multiple sushi rolls, and drinks as well.

I'm not going to lie - I'm not the biggest fan of this place. The cheese curds are great - they are crunchy on the outside, and soft and gooey on the inside. But $9 dollars for a cup of 5-7 cheese curds is just a hard bargain. It really is. I'm glad I did it, but I will be a little hesitant on going back because of how steep the price is.

*HAPPY HOUR REVIEW* 

I came here for Happy Hour and it was good. They have a wide variety of food available,  but their Happy Hour menu was fairly limited and standard. My friend and I got two beers, both sweet and savory chicken wings, and a plate of their bonchon (which we got kimchi). So here's the thing.

sample_reviews.text:
Color me surprised that this is a new review for me. I know I've eaten at this restaurant previously. And at multiple iterations of other restaurants in this space over the years...

Last night I enjoyed their delicious Coolinary menu and it did not disappoint. We had early reservations because it was packed on a Tuesday! So I was able to enjoy a HH old fashioned to start. Yum!

Can I just say WOW! I was super impressed with almost everything I ate. 
It's a buffet line and you can choose between one to all the items on the menu. I had the mac-n-cheese, okra-tomatoes-corn medley, collard greens and fried cauliflower. My friend had the white beans, jambalaya, mac-n-cheese and greens, plus a sweet potato pie. The white beans were amazing and I was jealous.

I parked near this shop for the parade on Saturday. And thought, "bittersweet? I know them... pop up? market? who cares? CHOCOLATE!" So I stopped in. I was in desperate need of a tea and they had several choices, iced. The mango was perfect, strong but not bitter.

I also decided I deserved an orange cranberry scone and some chocolate bark. Both were super tasty and the scone was HUGE.

It makes me sad to discover that this classic bar is now a daiquiri shop. The great thing about stopping at this old school narrow bar was that the drinks were well made, the clientele was reasonable and you were able to use the hotel's nice bathroom.  

Now it's just a sad, sad, sad bastion of machines and neon and no access to the hotel that houses it. Blah.

I stopped in for what I thought would be a quick visit on Friday night. Wow. I should have stuck to my regular store. This was only my 2nd visit to this location.

The aisles are tight. Be prepared to say excuse me to other shoppers every few minutes. The aisles that aren't tight? Piled high with stock in the middle of the space so that you still have to run an obstacle course.
Ticket system during parades
Order at the counter!

sample_reviews.text: Skip the cronut! Had it and it's fine, but the real strength of Swiss Haus is that it is a good bakery in Center City. It seems the trend around CC has been specialization:  cupcake shops galore, French/Italian pastries, etc.. all  having their place, but sometimes you just want to have a real bakery as a bakery is intended:  cakes, cookies, pies..

Upgrading this to 5 stars. I've now visited this place at least a half a dozen times and the food and service is just consistently great.  This evening I had a salmon Florentine for dinner and it was fantastic. The draft beer selections have become more varied and interesting and the place is always clean and the atmosphere really enjoyable (get a seat near the fireplace this time of year).

Fabulous dinner at Aldine last night.

The food was incredible. We shared several items. First plates: crab crudo and corn custard w/ maitake tempura mushrooms.Second plate: house-made sausage with peach aioli. Third plate: Skate served with a delicious sweet & sour red cabbage. Dessert: Chocolate cake with pistachio.It had just the right amount of sweetness.

Brodo, I really want to like you. You are located in my office building which would make you super convenient for a quick lunch and also your menu items sound delicious.  Part of me also wants to support a local business.

The first time I went about a year ago I had the Vegan Fetti salad and found the flavors to be nonexistent.

Went to St. Declan's Well this evening and really enjoyed it. I've been to a number of UCity bars and I found this one to be by far the best for food and atmosphere.  I should note that I'm in my 40s, so New Deck and others like it don't appeal to me. For college students the place may be too staid, but I find it to be a nice addition to the UCity neighborhood. St.

sample_reviews.text:
This is a new restaurant from the same folks who brought us Sopapillas and it is located next door to the Franklin location. Being a fan of Sopapillas and a lover of a good burger I was pretty excited. Pleased to say I was not disappointed.
Met some friends last night and even on a Tuesday eve it was pretty full though we were seated with not much wait.

As promised, I returned to Franklin Burger and had the Blueberry, Blue Cheese and Bacon Burger. It was wonderful and I don't historically like Blue Cheese! Sweet Potato Fries were perfect though I had to take them home as I might have over indulged in the Fried Pickles.  Great Place for a great burger!

Ah, Puckett's! With good reason, Puckett's has been a staple and favorite of downtown Franklin for years! Loved by locals and visitors alike, it offers a great variety of Southern type fare.
We came mid day on Tuesday, seated right away. Our server was very friendly and helpful, as you would expect.
I ordered Mojo Burger and it was fabulous. The bbq on top was cooked so well and so flavorful!

Hadn't been to Judge Bean's in a while. Probably due to location, it's bit off the main road so you are unlikely to just drive by. It's basically a BBQ type place, lots of rough wood decor, not too brightly lit, smells of smoky meats. 
They have a good selection of beers including beers on draft. I went with Black Abbey's the Rose, which was on the rotating selection list.

Barbara's is just good ole cooking. The poppy seed chicken is a must have and the rolls are good, but not light and fluffy, so pace yourself! 
If you get lucky you will be there when the cobbler comes out of the oven. If that happens, order it, doesn't matter what kind of cobbler, you won't be disappointed!
Happy hour 4-6 M-F when burgers are half price

Loved the sauce, very light. The pizza itself was that great crust, little burned on the edges with bubbles. The Wood Fired salad was excellent. Fresh snap peas, ramps, small bits of asparagus with shaved cheese and prosciutto.
Nice presentation on the drinks.

Do a lot of take out from this place. Food is always fresh and the sauces are very good.

We were supposed to eat at The Back Porch cafe for dinner, but they were closed for refrigeration problems. Owner said they had been down for 3 days so I'm not sure why they never picked up a phone and called us, but it worked out. We went down to Carisma and had a wonderful dinner.

Very disappointed in what we bought. The rum cake, that people rave about, was dry , had no rum taste and was covered with cashews.
Who covers a rum cake with cashews? Red velvet cupcake was good.
Cannoli was good, but far from the best in this town. Devils food chocolate mousse was dry and tasteless.
Everything looked great, but thats where it ended.

$10.75 for a glass of bad Pinot Grigio???
Seriously, Cavit Pinot Grigio at a top steakhouse is bad enough, but to charge $10.75 a glass for a bottle that costs $12 in a wine store is an insult. It's no wonder Capital Grill is 2 deep at the bar, and Mortons is empty. The manager needs to address not only the quality of wines by the glass, but also the prices they charge.

sample_reviews.text: I absolutely love the food here. My boyfriend, me and my mom ordered McAlister's the other night. My mom ordered the JustASpud, but added shredded cheese and steamed broccoli. The potato was huge, and I don't mean like just a little bigger than normal. It was enough to feed 2 people. It was as big as the to go container that you would normally get a sandwich and fries in.

This place was super cool. They literally made the ice cream right there while we watched. My man and I shared the So-Cal option. It was vanilla ice cream with fresh bananas, almonds, and coconut. Then you can add all the toppings you want. We got strawberries, caramel and blueberries. It tasted much colder than regular ice cream as well.

This place is still as good as I remember. My son is on town for a little bit and he wanted gator tail. So the four of us decided to come to Ballyhoo. The only thing is there wasn't much meat for the gator tails, more breading than anything. However, the dinners we all got were still really good. We will be back again.

Plans were changed a bit this morning, so Ty and I go to village inn for breakfast to go. Well the remodel is nice, but I think they forgot to leave some money for the food. I got a scrambler which I always love.  Not this time. Mine came with tortilla wraps which tasted like they have been there for three weeks. What used to be hash browns is now a ball of sticky potatoes.

This location is the slowest. I have been sitting in the drive thru for 8 mins. They only take one order at a time and will not take another drive thru order until the one ahead of you is completely done and gone. I've only been sitting here waiting is the other location was out of the caramel for my coffee.
The banana pudding is amazing. Only seasonal though so tomorrow is the last day for that.
The purple drank is the best!!

sample_reviews.text:
My wife and I stopped here while running errands in the neighborhood at about 4:30pm on a Thursday.  We were immediately greeted by the owner who was helpful explaining the options available and offering us samples of the different flavors. 

Once we made our choices he went to work on creating our treats.

Since we have lived in the area, my wife and I have tried many of the buffets and restaurants in the casinos, however, we had never been to the Buffet at the Eldorado. We noticed on a billboard this week that Silver Legacy, Eldorado and Circus Circus are now offering a 20% discount on restaurants to locals. We decided to check out The Buffet at the Eldorado.

For our weekly lunch date, my wife and I decided to check this place out on a Friday just before noon.

As we approached the building it was obvious it was very cool!  There is ample space to be socially distant but will one day be able to accommodate larger groups of people who can enjoy food, drink and live music.

This happens very seldom that we give a poor review. But this made no sense. we were paying customers and they have a policy to keep space open for something that they cannot count on. In the end, we spent our money somewhere else. while they had and empty table. 

We came here about 5pm after the Italian festival on Saturday afternoon.

We came here a little before noon on a Sunday after walking through Reno Art Fest. It was not very busy and we were seated right away.

We were quickly brought waters and our orders were taken shortly after.
Turbo does a great job! He is friendly and a hard worker.
We predict this place is going to give Chick-fil-A major competition

sample_reviews.text: I just love this place so much! I get my hair done at a salon nearby so I've made it a tradition to stop in before every appointment. I've only came for breakfast but would love to get lunch soon. I usually get the Lemon Juliet smoothie which has Lemon, blueberry, ginger, mango, pineapple and orange juice. SO fresh and delicious.

Best Mexican food in Pinellas Park! Tucked away and part of a hotel it may be easy to miss but just let GPS do the work and get ready to enjoy some really tasty, authentic Mexican cuisine. I got the Chicken tacos which were only like $2 each and so good! Perfectly marinated, juicy shredded Chicken with all the fixins.

If you live in Clearwater and you've never been to Florida Subs and Gyros you're missing out! Really good authentic Mexican food as well as tons of other menu items like burgers, gyros and sandwiches. The tacos are what I go for as they are cheap, fresh and scrumptious. The chicken and el pastor are my favorite.

Came here for an impromptu lunch with some friends on the way to the beach. Love that they have ample parking behind the restaurant so you don't have to worry about the scant parallel parking out front. The inside is beautiful, especially the indoors garden style courtyard. My single guy friends said it would be a nice place to take a girl on a first date.

I loooooooved this place! Total foodie place in cute downtown Dunedin. I'd never been to a Hawaiian restaurant before so many of the items on the menu were new to me like the Spam Masubi which is basically grilled spam on a block of rice wrapped in seaweed wrap. At first I was skeptical because I'm not big on spam but I'm glad I tried it cuz it was so good! I wanted to get another one!
Drunken Shrimp is a must. I'm addicted to the stuff!
Great authentic Mexican style tacos - try the El Pastor tacos!

sample_reviews.text: I love this Morning Call. I was so happy when I heard they were keeping this location open. My routine as of late is to come here for a nightcap after a fun night out on the town. Nothing else quite hits the spot like hot beignets and a cafe au lait. I love these beignets in particular because they are so fluffy and have those lovely air pockets when you bite into them.

I have had the pleasure of dining at Ralph's numerous times and the food and service is always extraordinary. The turtle soup is a must have, the broth is dark and earthy with an intense flavor profile. I typically order seafood here and have always been happy with the outcome. They can do amazing things with fish. 

I have also had the pleasure of attending a few private events.

If you go to Norma's for one thing, let it be the Cuban sandwich! Yes, its a bakery and all their sweet things are fabulous too! But, I can honestly say its the best cuban sandwich that I've ever had in New Orleans including the one at Jazz Fest that people love. The pork is legit fall apart good and so full of flavor. The rest of the ingredients are top quality and they get the ratio just right.

I  came here for lunch one afternoon looking forward to trying it and left hungry because I lost my appetite mid meal and here is why: 

I unrolled my silverware and there was cheese encrusted all over the fork to where the prongs were not even visible. Ok, strike one but not the worst I've seen so lets just grab another fork and move on.

I like the products at World Market, but unfortunately the management and the lack of professionalism from them is why I have stopped shopping with them. They remind why I hate national chains, because they don't care about customer service. At the end of the day, you are one of a million customers so who cares if you leave unhappy.
They got a new chef and he new menu sucks. The end.
Magic Bakery now open! Unique and delicious treats!

sample_reviews.text: I just tried the Tempura Shrimp and Gyoza appetizers during lunch. Despite the Christmas rush at nearby Trader Joe's there were few diners. 5 medium sized Tempura Shrimp and 5 Gyoza, each dish for three bucks. Can't beat the price. Shrimp could have been a little crisper, for my taste but tasted fresh. Gyoza were not as tasty as the best I've had but well worth the price.
Terrific Lamb Burger. Pleasant atmosphere, good service, appropriately priced.

Terrific little place that finally opened. Great fresh Mexican and Salvadoran food at very reasonable prices. However, it is not fast food. The food is prepared to order and, while the place is not large and only has seating for about twenty people, it can take a little while to get your order. We ordered the shrimp tacos which had grilled shrimp and it took about ten minutes to get the order.

Is Raley's going OUT of business? It certainly looks like it. Empty shelves, very few items in the fresh doughnut glass case and no bakery bread. Still no place to recycle plastic bags (how hard can THAT be?) Hard to believe that I'd miss Scolari's. Can we get Safeway to take over the store?

Still no plastic bag recycle station. At least the shelves are getting filled BUT I bought some mini-croissants from the Bakery case and they are tough and stale. Box says Sell By: 11/7 and today is 11/6 but who knows when they were put out. Now I know to check sell by date on their Bakery goods and look for the ones that are the furthest back.

sample_reviews.text:
This is a place that truly serves a neighborhood well: fresh locally grown fruits, vegetables, meats and seafood offered at reasonable prices. Add to this generous discounts for educators, those residing in the Hollygrove area, SNAP recipients and...those using the Yelp app and it's easy to see why it's so popular.

Their passionate staff will answer any questions you have about the weekly items...

On a dreary, dank, pollen-ridden spring day in New Orleans, a new coffee Mecca was born. Polished and poised, eager staff made a delicious pour over from a boutique roaster.

There's pastries and lunch items to come, but I was really there for the magic elixir that stirs my heart and hastens my thoughts. I wasn't disappointed. Can't wait
to try more!

Pepper Pot. This sticks in my mind because you went to the grocery store on vacation and saw a can soup sitting on the shelf with this name and thought, "What's that? Who would eat that?" Well, stop what you're doin', 'cause I'm about to ruin the song and the soup that you're used to.

Unless you cannot eat seafood, make sure you get this! This amazing velouté will transform your life.

Pros:

Fast servce
Wide vareity
Decent tableware

Cons:
Bland, Americanized renditions of Vietnamese food
Bland, bland, bland

I miss really good pho. And spring rolls. I'm guessing the clientele that frequents this place are people who don't like spicy, or spices that would be used in any decent Vietnamese restaurant. Or, they live/work nearby.

I think I was here on an off day. The famous bread pudding had curdled, and our bread and a couple of entrees were cold. And, our newbie service staff were afraid to walk to the other side of our table and handed our plates to us. 

Don't worry - I believe in the concept of the place, and will give another chance to upgrade the stars.
hard pass on gluten-free items.
#EssenceFest

sample_reviews.text: A big shout-out to The Bellwether & Polite Society on #ThankyouThursday. I enjoyed that you combined both of your menus and provided #touchless #curbside service at The Bellwether. You made it so easy. Who would have thought a restaurant on the third floor, could do it so smoothly. Those cameras did a great job.

I'm giving this Schnucks 5 Stars, simply because they had what i needed and wanted. One of my main Christmas gifts was a bottle of Rye from the local distillery, Switchgrass. Living in Illinois now, Switchgrass is not available. It is not distributed there yet. Hope they come soon. 

Any how, the liquor department at this store was impressive.

I am more of a doughnut guy, but how you spell it doesn't really matter. Because, these are some of the best tasting cake donuts I have had in the St. Louis metropolitan area. They are moist and the icing is not overly sweet.

Oh, sad day in the land of Yelp. I have a new restaurant listing on my No-No list. 
Our lunch today at Tin Can Tavern was a total disappointment in all categories. 

We started out with chicken wings, that were rubbery and tough. It also grossed me out that I could see the yucky bumps on the chicken skin. I'm not sure, but I think they were just tossed in Frank's Hot Sauce and that was it.

Three Dog Night had a popular song that hit number 1 on the charts in the 70's. It was appropriately called "One". In the lyrics, it stated, "One is the loneliest number that you'll ever do". That may be true in love, but when it comes to dining sometimes you just want to dine alone.  If you do, avoid HWK.
I'm sad, no more Happy Hour - That's a bummer.
Hooks Under The Bar

sample_reviews.text:
I have come to spice finch two times and had excellent service + food both times.
My second time around was for my birthday and we had the honor to sit at the round chefs table. There was 10 of us total so we fit the space perfectly. 
Our servers were great about checking on us and giving us proper recommendations for our group.

Easily one of my current favorite restaurants in Philadelphia, Suraya has been my primary reason for venturing north of Spring Garden. I have been here about 5 times for dinner, ranging from eating in an intimate setting to a large group of 10 for a chef tasting.
Celebrating their Lebanese/middle eastern heritage, the owners have done a tremendous job with the details.

I was excited to try this place after a few friend's recommendations. I got their boiled chicken combo which includes innards. Not going to lie, I skipped the innards but I thoroughly enjoyed the boiled chicken, ginger rice, radish soup & all the yummy sauces plus garlic. It's very affordable and the service is great!

Came here for the lemon bars, the beautiful cakes and stunning paper displays. The service is always so friendly and they also serve coffee. The space is bright and inviting so I enjoy sitting here with a friend or bring my work for a change of scenery.
The cake though --- it's dry for my taste.

After a long day of work all I could think about is where I could take care of my noodle craving. As soon as Empress Garden came into mind I put my mind to ordering the Taiwanese beef noodle soup. The beef broth with the pickled veggies is so delicious with soft noodles. I also love the zajiangmian (Peiking noodles with meat sauce) which comes with crunchy string beans and tofu and ground meat.
I hear the Panna cotta is very good
Rewards card : spend $200, get  $10 credit back

sample_reviews.text:
Three Muses was a recommendation from the Yelp Community, so thanks for continuing to lead me on the right path Yelp!! 
I was in NOLA for a Bachelorette Party (I know, but we were not obnoxious I swear!) and I booked an early reservation for us at Three Muses. I love the live music and the vibe here. Sometimes music at a restaurant can be obnoxious and obtrusive, but this band was perfect.

Hemingway's is just the perfect date night spot for a romantic evening or an intimate meal with friends. Trust me on this one! 
After a hectic week I came here for date night on Friday and immediately was impressed with the romantic yet speakeasy vibe.

Everything about The Green Pheasant impressed me. The brass hardware paired with bright teal chairs and elevated ceilings gave an upscale, opulent ambiance that I love and is the perfect setting for a fantastic Friday night meal. We met some friends here for the first time and decided to order a lot of things and share.

NOTE: William Collier's is only open on the days that Marathon Music Works has a show, not that I will be returning. 

I never liked William Collier's but found myself here before a show and decided to give it another chance. Unfortunately, it was worse than my other experiences.

Sorry Sylvan Park. I love all of your establishments except this one. The tacos are WAY overpriced for what you get; I've tried everything from lobster tacos (at roughly $4.50 PER taco) to the veggie and chicken ($3ish). Not impressed with any of them. Plus the salsa costs extra (I hate when they do that!).
They open on Monday, July 29!
1/2 off all bottles of wine on Wednesday!

sample_reviews.text: The oysters here are superb.   The bacon garlic, Texas bbq and buffalo are all on point.  I ordered at the bar and it was cool to watch them prepared right in front of you.   I also highly recommend the taste of the bayou sampler, the chicken and andouille gumbo, crawfish etouffee, and red beans and rice.  Red beans are some of the best I've had in New Orleans.

The place is a tad pricey, and you're going to hear alot of hipster trance music.  But the food is on point! I had the beast breakfast sandwich and it was perfect.  My girls chicken pesto wrap was also very good. My side was black beans and they stole the show.  Probably the best I've ever had.  I'm gonna be back here fosho.  Maybe with some headphones lol (yea I'm old)

Definitely the place to be for lunch.  The stuffed Trout with crabmeat is one of the best meals you will have here in New Orleans.   And its 10.99.  Absolutely delicious,  and they have the best mac n cheese in the city.  Can't wait to come back and have the rest of the menu since I now live in the Upper 9th.  For those that are visiting you will not be disappointed!

Small portions, overpriced but very good.   I had the napoleon Benedict and it was tasty.  But small, im still hungry, amd 15 bucks,  if I pay that much for some eggs I wanna leave stuffed.  Didnt even get toast.  Its a cool place for sure, with alot of history, but not a place for locals
Chicken and grits where pretty decent but did not blow me away considering the price and the wait.
Best fried chicken sandwich in the city, hands down.  Damn that was good!
Late night debauchery, this place is bar one!

IT IS WITH SUCH SADNESS THAT FEMINGS HAS CLOSED IN INDY AS OF MAY 1.

I have been avoiding Taco Bell since I had issues at the location at 71st and Michigan. But today I gave into the craving and ordered thru the app today for this location. I pulled into the drive thru ready for them to not have my online order but they did!!  Young lady at the window was friendly. Food was correct. Craving solved. And Frys are back!!

I have not reviewed this location in awhile. Management has changed numerous times as well as staff, but the the food and service remained great. The young man working the drive thru the last couple times I've been there is unfriendly to rude and seems to have a major attitude.

I feel like something is going on at Yolk. New management?, new owner?, staff turn over? Food was mediocre, service was slow. They no longer have butter, jelly, creamer as part of the table set up. Bar area is so loud. Be prepared to not hear the person directly in front of you or be prepared to yell.
Very loud. If you want to actually carry on a conversation good luck.

sample_reviews.text:
OK, so if you're in the Tampa Bay area and you haven't been here, you're doing it wrong.

This is THE place to come if you want fresh pasta, hot sandwiches, deli meats, Italian sweets, fine cheeses, and well, anything Italian you can possibly think of.

Minimal, Instagram-worthy coffee shop which fits a special spot in St. Pete.

I've been to some coffee shops in my day, and this reminds me of a more upscale coffee spot reminiscent to Downtown Credo in Orlando. Not exactly the same design, but the same hip elements. 

The coffee here is pretty much no-frills.

I have been to the Rollin' Oats in Tampa and always really liked the selection but never made my way over to the St. Pete location.

I love this location much more than the Tampa one. It's cleaner, a little bit smaller, and the people are much friendlier. The selection is basically the same, but the layout here seems a little bit more organized than the Tampa location.

I know the menu just changed to Mexican today so I am way ahead of the game here and perhaps that's unfair, but OE Market SHOULD NOT serve Mexican food.

There was a ton of hype leading up to the Market Goes Mexican release and yes, I was excited. But when you really think about it, the Mexican vibe does not go with Oxford Exchange.

I am sort of torn on this review since the food I had was actually quite good but the service was atrocious at Brick House.

A group of us came in today for brunch and I was really excited since I had never been to Brick House before. I drive by this place all the time and it is constantly busy.
The art on the walls rotates every few weeks!
Fried pickles will give you life. Get the chipotle aioli to dip them in

sample_reviews.text: This place is so cute! I stopped in for their soft opening and again a week later. First, they offer decaf espresso. THANK YOU! Second, they have vanilla oat milk, I never want to drink a nut milk again. Third, they have soft serve. What more do you need? Oh a really great bookstore connected to the coffee shop? Yes, they have that too. Seating?

This is in my top two favorite bars, the other one, I'm not sure it changes but I would probably have to go with 3 Crow because of their Bushwhacker.  Off topic.  The Melrose expanded and I couldn't have been happier.  They have Pop-a-Shot, pool, Big Buck Hunter, probably the best jukebox in the Nash and decent pub food.  The expansion, a patio out back with ivy growing down the wall.

Loved it! This place isn't just a restaurant, it's an experience. They have Toto toilets! They seriously thought this through in every way imaginable. 

We stopped in for lunch and I got yakisoba; wok fired noodles, pork, vegetables with a sunny egg and my husband ordered a sando set, a la bento box-esque with pork katsu. His meal came with miso soup, side of the day, rice, and pickled veggies.

Nashville is over-saturated. There are too many restaurants at too high of a price point offering mediocre food and service. I almost want to stop trying new establishments and let Darwinism settle the Nashville restaurant scene. I'll be over here eating at my old staples. 

I stopped in for lunch and it was empty. I ordered the cacio e pepe and it was inedible.

Oh, SATCO.  You're not the best taco shop but you do have your moments.

I come to SATCO for:

1.  An inexpensive lunch.
2.  Queso
3.  Outside seating with music loud enough to drown out thoughts of my To Do List.
4.  Great for groups.
5.  Cheap beer.

What I don't come to SATCO for:

1.  Authentic Mexican food.
2.  A quiet lunch.
3.  A Vandy student-free lunch.
4.
Great open space. Cocktail list is affordable and so far very tasty!
New menu format, a list of $6 cocktails all the time and snacks between $6 and $8. We tried the hot chicken, pimento grilled cheese, and fries with bacon, chorizo and gravy.

sample_reviews.text: If you are a Skyline Chili fan, then this is a good location to go to!  Skyline Chili is definitely an acquired taste, so if you've never had it, be prepared that it is probably unlike any chili you've ever had.  First, it is more of a meat sauce than a thick, meaty chili.  Plus, it has a strong flavor of allspice and cinnamon, which is pretty unusual.

One of my top breakfast spots in all of New Orleans.  I have managed to make it here for my last three trips to town, and though I have the same thing each visit, I LOVE it, and can't order anything else.

What is this item I must eat every time? Bagels and lox! I first heard of this place when Guy from Triple D featured this restaurant and made his own bagel.

I have to say I really enjoyed every moment of my experience here. Parking was easy because I was there early, and they do have a private parking lot.

They have tables to sit at and a bar both outside and in, and the bartender Elise at the inside bar was friendly and efficient.  As they offer a happy hour until 6 pm, I started with one of the happy hour cocktails.

$600+ (with tax) per night rooms?  hahahahahahahahahahaha ::wipes tears of mirth from my eyes::  Oh Hard Rock Hotel, you have a seriously over-inflated opinion of yourself!  We booked six rooms for our holiday party with no discounts, and I seriously feel we are owed half that money refunded.

The hotel is currently (12/2018) under 7 days a week/24 hours a day construction.

Villagio Cinemas has only been open a relatively short time, and they have the potential to be great, but they need to focus on 2 important details: the theater ambiance and the food.

Since I love the other movie + dining theater in town, I'll try to compare apples to apples:

When you walk in here, they look at your ticket, tell you which theater it's in, and then you go seat yourself, picking...
Make sure you sign the book they bring your receipt in!
DO NOT use the self checkout-it jams constantly & you end up waiting for a worker to come clear it.

sample_reviews.text: Wednesday is 50 cent wing night, increments of 10. Pick your sauce. Decent deal. Wings were large and meaty. Not the tiny ones you get at the well Known wing joint, starts with a B and ends with a W and has another W in between. One of our orders of them was pretty dry. I don't know if the sauce ran off before it went into the basket or what but we asked for more and she got it to us quickly.

We live nearby and I was just reading all of the really negative reviews of this KFC. We have been here numerous times and we have never had a bad experience. Not once. We have ordered to go (never ate in) inside and through the drive thru. Each time they got our orders correct and it was fairly quick to arrive in my hands. Staff have always been friendly and accommodating.

Stopped in as we were looking for a new to us place. Plenty of free parking. Greeted immediately and sat at the bar. Tis to watch which had game day on. 2 other tables were there and another couple of guys at the bar. Ordered a couple of drinks and the pork skins. Bartender/Waiter was nice. The pork skins were prepared fresh, lightly seasoned and a nice spicy  mayo for dipping.

We ordered the jerk chicken and soup at lunch. It was all good. When we went to pay the waitress/bartender said "because they keep changing things and never tell me anything your soup will be $5 instead of $2." So we paid it because we hate to be 'those people' but in hind sight I should have asked for the manager.

I will update this if BJ's actually responds to my complaint. I was there yesterday. Purchased a rotisserie. Scanned my card and they said it was expired. This store didn't open until April 2019. My card should not expire until April 2020. They said it expired in November. But the rules state if it expires in November that you get until the end of the month to renew.
It is your average Sams Club. Same set up. Clean and spacious. Friendly greeter.
Stop in for a drink or some decent bar food. Staff are all friendly and appreciate your business. Fish tacos rock!

sample_reviews.text:
I'm so excited about having Spanish tapas in Nashville!

My husband and I drive to Louisville when we crave Spanish tapas as that has been the closest place to Nashville to get it. It was my birthday and I normally go to a familiar restaurant on a special occasion. When I saw this new restaurant I knew this was a must!

Meagan was our server. She was great!

The one thing I really wanted for breakfast while in New Orleans was Grillades. I have made them at home from a recipe but never had them in a restaurant. When Yelp searching this place popped up so it was a must for breakfast. 

The restaurant has a diner style atmosphere. We were seated by the window so we got to watch traffic pass by on Canal Street.

We try to eat somewhere special on our last night of a trip. This was our first visit to New Orleans. We started by looking for a Landry's restaurant since we are club members. When this place popped up we made reservations. 

We were seated at a table in the bar area. We requested a low top instead of a high top table on the reservation. Don't make this mistake.

Disappointing Burger!

Several of our friends have been raving about how great this place is so we decided to stop in for lunch while running errands.  It seemed dark inside for it to be so early in the day.  The chairs aren't very comfortable and you don't want to move around a lot due to the close proximity of the other diners.

Romantic Atmosphere!

The coolest thing about this establishment is the atmosphere.  The brick walls with the Italian pictures and the candlelit tables provide a romantic scene for a great date night.  The service was excellent.  The servers stand by the door and are at your table the second you need anything.

I only wish the food would have been better especially for the price.
They have peanut butter sauce!
Restaurant week is so exciting!

Damn I say.. just damn!  If I were to ask you what is the definition of "Succulent" , you'd probably come up with... tender, juicy, and tasty. I'd say, or should I say my partner in crime Jim H. and I would say you are correct my yelp family. An OUTSTANDING brewery located out here in West Chester! 

  Levante Brewing, all you guys do is hit homeruns with every craft you produce, with every concoction that one can think of has me wanting to frequent you more often.

I'll make this short and sweet people, and normally I don't write short reviews. This is an awesome brewery that you need to visit at least once.

Place hasn't changed a bit.. A service garage flipped over and used as a beer distributor with the left over oil stains saturated into the concrete. Grotto's Pizza is mainly popular in Delaware, being I haven't seen one north of Delaware. This wasn't in my original plans for means of consumption, but I have had them before. Well I guess times have changed along with the quality I remember of past. I have been to several of their locations little further south of Delaware, closer to O.C. Maryland.
Ardmore Q no mas ! Sophie BBQ will be taking over.
Yea it's not like L.A.

sample_reviews.text:
What's not to like about Crown Candy Kitchen???  It's charming, it's in St. Louie and it's family run. We went on Valentines Day which proved to be a wait to get in. You seat yourself once you see the table has been cleaned. They only have booths for four or for two. So if you have a large group you may have to split up. 
Very charming interior and great service.

Had a fantastic experience here at Red Guitar last weekend. I've only been here once but wow it's very good. It is a rustic place, no frills. Just great pizza. No alcohol but I ran to the cool dive bar next door and had a cold one. I got the Rosa pizza. This pizza is so special!!  Unique flavors, pistachios on a pizza is amazing.

The fish and chips we got tonight was fabulous. It's a very large piece, not oily and cooked perfectly. The slaw was also delish. It was a vinegar based red cabbage. Really good. We also ordered a Mac and cheese with chicken strips. That was good too. Included bread and a small container with house made crackers and cheese. The best fish and chips I've had. Just added a squeeze of lemon!!

Great service and a really cozy place located in Ladue.  I got the fish special and the fish was overcooked.  The calamari was also overcooked, too chewy for my taste.  It was crowded so they must be doing something right.  I should have sent it back but just did not want to wait for another to be made.  A nice place, may try again someday.

I love Schlafly so it pains me to give only 3 stars. The food is usually great but Friday night something was off. Pretzel Crusted chicken was really dry, burger over cooked, pork chop nothing to write home about. Of course the beer is always good. We sat outside in the covered patio. Beware, it is really noisy because there is nothing to absorb the sound. Lots of echoes and it is hard to hear.
Give a decent tip. This is hard work.
Be prepared to wait in line. Don't come if you're in a hurry or give yourself plenty of time

sample_reviews.text: Came to try this place out based on some neighborhood recommendations and boy was a I not disappointed.  The restaurant is family owned and was so helpful in trying to decide on what we should get.  The pizza was amazing and very authentic to the NY style it advertises.

Stopped by at the start of Thanksgiving weekend to get my nieces an assortment of mini-donuts for the long weekend.  I had been meaning to try this place out since it opened as I frequented the previous tenant's mini-donut shop often.  The staff was super friendly and helped me pick out the perfect assortment out of their plentiful selection.

My order was made fresh and done very quickly.

I'm quite the fan of this place.  They have an extensive menu and the items I've always had there are great.  The restaurant itself is very large and they offer ordering at the counter and a seat yourself option if you are dining in. 

Their burritos are massive and a good deal for the price.  Their queso is one of the best, in my opinion, in the entire Tampa area.

I had high expectations when I went to this place recently after some recommendations from friends.  We visited on a night when there weren't very crowded so it was nice to be able to have such an attentive waitress and service.  I was a bit disappointed when I didn't find as much on the menu as I would have liked to have seen that would appeal to me.

If you're looking for a quick bite for lunch or dinner and are in the mood for a pizza or sandwich, this is the place for you.   They have a vast variety of pizzas of assorted sizes and can also serve by the slice if that is your fancy.   I was a tad disappointed with their salad and/or low calorie menu selections as I have been calorie counting since the start of this year.
Beat the crowds and get in early for grand opening specials!
Don't bother using DineTime to get in line before arriving. They wont honor it despite the app allowing you to check in and showing you how many people are ahead of you.

sample_reviews.text: Tower Grove Farmers Market and Bazaar has to be seen to be believed.  You know those fabled community areas where everyone gets along and there is much fun to be had?  This is that sort of place.  You can come here for community outreach groceries, free yoga and a whole lot more.  The people here are very friendly, and some are even eccentric.

Anyone who knows my husband and I knows that chocolate is a sure fire bargaining chip and is the supreme reward for any job well done.  This store is heaven, and sells imported chocolates, wines and more luxurious items that should be staples of everyday life.  

We love coming here and getting chocolate bars and boxes of truffles to enjoy.

Momo's is an amazing restaurant that is top notch in both decor and in food.  Doing both right is a rare feat, and really there is nothing lacking in this establishment.  The staff is very friendly and is outgoing, and they really make you feel at home here.  The different styles of seating is fun, as is picking from the menu due to all of the choices.  

The mezes [tapas] here are fantastic!

C J Muggs is a cool enough place, but it kind of has an identity crisis - which isn't a bad thing.  Visit during the day and you will find it somewhere between a casual to elegant restaurant.  Your chair will be pulled out for you and you will be handed a menu.  Visit during the night, especially on a weekend, and you will find a rowdy crowd and live music.

Great as a total package, slightly better than average food is how I would rate Kitchen K.  Look people, it's very rare that a place is going to do everything right.  Kitchen K does better than most.  The menu is interesting and sometimes changes, and there are some weird things served here that you won't find everywhere else.  How often do you find bouillabaisse and pork chops on the same menu?

sample_reviews.text: This place has the worst location. But it's a must-see. Just go. You will think you landed in the middle of 9th Street in Philly.

A best-kept secret that I'm sure will get mobbed eventually.

This is the best breakfast in PInellas County and quite possibly the entire bay area.

From-scratch buttermilk biscuits. Great quality bacon. Well executed omelets. Innovative specials. This kitchen knows what it is doing.

Go here and try to get there early, before they open, because there may be a line.

Excellent food, they are located in the old Kombo Kitchen truck in the same place on Dale Mabry. I had the pad thai, fried rice and chicken panang, all excellent (see photo).

Really nice people also.

This is a perfect example of "looks can be very deceiving." This store had a remodel within the last year and it's fucking fantastic. Wide gleaming aisles, outdoor dining, they are really trying to go the Whole Foods/Fresh Market route. The place is a "natural" food & toiletry palace.

But they fail.

THIS PLACE REALLY FUCKING PISSES ME OFF. They should change their name to Dairy PLOY.

I live literally 5 minutes from this place and I love ice cream. However the last time I was here the ice cream was old: It had ice crystals and tasted/smelled like the freezer. So I gave it another shot last week. They still serve old ice cream.
I come here at least once a week. It's the only place in Tampa that has consistent quality food and preparation.
The best time to come here is early Sunday morning when all the Jesus freaks are at church. You can worship at the altar of TJs all by yourself.

sample_reviews.text:
MMMMMMM Pandora's.

I love this place. Juicy tasty snowballs in my neighborhood with a friendly staff!

Their ice isn't as super soft as hansens or plums but it's pretty fine. None of that course ice chip crap here. I'm particularly fond of their off menu flavors like Strawberry Iced Tea or Strawberry Lemonade. Not too sweet but very flavorful.

I love Hollygrove market and farm! The people here really care about the neighborhood and the food they supply. 

I love the box. For $25 you get a good amount of really fresh organic produce. I have a bunch of weird allergies (Jalapenos I love you from afar) and they allow substitutions (more satsumas? OMG YES!) if you ask. 

I like to peruse their clearance table.

I've been a fan of Nino's for a very long time. I don't make it there very often since I moved away from p-town.

Story time:
Back in the day when they first opened  ('96-ish) my high school friends and I would go here. Often we didn't have the cash so we'd split plates. Nino (the owner) would see us doing this and would give us extra food because "growing kids have got to eat!".

We did family day which was nice *after* we got on the boat. 

What really sucked is they had to go through everyone's bags before getting on the boat so they had a super long line and only one person checking bags. While the place they have you wait in line is shaded and has some (working) fans it's still hot as hell in there  with everyone packed in like sardines.

I want to love this pizza hut I really really do. See I'm a sucker for their BBQ beef pizza (which you should totally try). I order from these guys often because of that pizza and frankly their delivery sucks! 

First of all I order online because most of the time their CSRs are clueless and very abrupt.
This grocery has kid sized carts!
Omg this Walmart has the real sugar cherry Pepsi!!!

Key chains, pens, back packs, kids clothing, hats, T-shirts, polo shirts, jackets, jerseys, stickers, clocks, pictures, autographs, and...

In a previous review, it states pretty much nothing has...

My heart may reside in St. Louis, but my stomach yearns for Chicago. I'm fully convinced that Black Thorn makes the best deep dish pizza in the 'Lou. 

This is one of those places that more people should know about.  Sure, everyone's been to Pi, but you haven't had deep dish 'til you've tried Black Thorn's version of it. The sauce? Spicy. The toppings? They load 'em up. Cheese? Plentiful.

Another St. Louis institution I have somehow missed all these years - don't wait as long as I did, 'cuz this place is a true treat! They have a huge menu of ice cream themed cocktails, as well as traditional cocktails, including a wonderful Old Fashioned.

The perfect spot to get dinner before seeing a concert at The Pageant. Every time I've been here the staff has been friendly and the pies delicious. Is it Chicago style? Not quite, but it's unique and also delicious. Get the "Bucktown"!

I've been to a bunch of BBQ places around STL and St. Charles, but have somehow missed Hendricks until now. First impressions? Interior decor is nice. There's a patio, which is of course where I opted to sit. They have moonshine...and they even serve whiskey flights. Gotta hand it to them for having a nice focus on the sophisticated drinker.

As for the food:

The menu selection is pretty wide.

After watching the St. Louis episode of "Man Vs Food" a few too many times, I at long last made my journey over to Crown Candy on a Saturday afternoon to see what the fuss was all about. Not surprisingly, there was a huge line when I got there, so I knew it was going to be pretty amazing.

Bandmates and I decided we didn't want to wait, so we actually just walked in, and discovered there was an...
