sample_reviews.text: I am surprised I have not reviewed Muriel's sooner- I have been here several times. Each visit has been as close to "impeccable" as you can get. The restaurant is beautiful, with so much interesting art and decor. They will give you a tour if you ask- the upstairs has some super cool rooms and stories as well. The service is top notch.

Delicious. Hot and fresh. The wings are perfectly seasoned. The rice doesn't look seasoned, but it is delicious and so flavorful. Ask for extra egg in the rice, you won't regret it. They don't answer their phone to take orders, but it doesn't take more that 10-15 minutes to go in and order. So good!!!
Great atmosphere, nice selection of beer, and friendly bartenders. The sour beer was especially delicious. I will absolutely be back!
The cashier here couldn't have been less friendly if she tried. She was curt and rude. I have stopped here before because it is so convenient but never again.

Same cafe has never been my favorite sushi place, but it is usually decent. However, I had a pretty bad meal here recently, which was even more of a disappointment. Our table ordered several types of rolls, and the chicken Katsu. The sushi and rolls were very dry and just kind of old tasting- it was like eating grocery store sushi :(
I was super disappointed!
Chicken fries- delicious!
Get the black pepper chicken! They will do it as a combo plate if you ask, and it is soooo good!!!

This is a very consistent, high-quality restaurant with truly authentic food! I have eaten here many times, and each dish has been hot, full of flavor, and beautiful portions! 
On my last visit, we did dim sum, which is available all day Saturday and Sunday!  You order from the menu and every dish that we had was truly scrumptious!!!

Another exquisite meal from Trattoria Marcella with a spaghettini in a saffron red sauce.  The aromas wafted into my senses before I even tasted this most delectable dish!  
This restaurant has remained one of my top choices for their exceptional quality of cuisine, their attention to detail, the excellent staff, and ambience..since 1995.
Considering how many restaurants we have in St.

This is a most wonderful space, both in its physical appearance and ambience, as well as the fact that so much of the menu is home made with great loving care and attention to every detail... I have known the owner for many years and this was her first venture into the restaurant business and she has done it with the same style and flair that she displays in her life!

A wonderful neighborhood restaurant with pastas salads steaks and more!  A very accommodating staff and the owner cares a great deal about your comforts and satisfaction!!  The pasta elizabeta with lots of seafood is yummy and the portions are large! They are very accommodating with any modifications or requests and the atmosphere is warm and inviting...

I wandered in here spur of the moment Around lunchtime and I was delighted with the setting and atmosphere! There was a balcony in the rear set apart from the rest of the restaurant, and comfortable seating with generous space between tables.  There is a large variety of options and I had the chicken salad platter and it was delicious with large chunks of chicken and a lovely fresh salad.
Great choice for a beautiful and memorable evening or lunch in a lovely area. Attention to every detail!
A beautiful, hip atmosphere, the staff's attention to every detail, the luscious cocktails, the creative and exceptionally fresh cuisine options from starters to dessert, make this a top choice...

If you live in Indy, I doubt you're using Yelp to weigh going to Yats because you already know what to expect which is affordable tasty food served up on a disposable (though never biodegradable) plate. 

Beads
Tasty bread - get the double order

I've never had Portillo's before. I went during an ice storm so I had the whole place to myself. The Italian beef, dipped, was incredible. That thing was a sponge and I had to take great care while mowing it down. Easy, son! Don't get cocky!  EVERYTHING on the menu looked good. 

The presentation of the food is a little bit on the sloppy side but it's not a huge deal.

I know that I've now reached the summit of middle American dadism because I'm writing a rave review for Costco and I'm okay with that. Costco is a financially sound company that treats their employees well, has a great return policy and offers a much wider variety of goods and services than you would probably expect.

Beer prices are great and atmosphere is pretty much what you'd expect. Can't speak to the food but it appears reasonably priced. If you're here to place a bet you'll be in line a while until they get the kiosks set up.

It was a pretty so so experience at the Hot Box Pizza in Frownsburg. "Hey, we never get Hot Box" I said. Placing an order online was very easy and it was hot and ready when I arrived but the pizza, itself, failed to deliver.  A large Aloha pizza comes to about $19 after tax. The pizza did not have enough sauce and the ricotta cheese was only technically there.
Never had the chance to eat here but the Gnaw Bone and SuperFly are pretty tasty at $9 for a growler fill.
You can't get Panoony's anywhere else.

Learned about this all organic establishment from my daughter who is a big fan and now I am too!
Ordered the Yummus Bowl with a packet of organic Green Goddess dressing and loved it! My daughter opted for the Nutty Bowl and she really enjoyed it!
Definitely planning to return to try other menu items such as the juices and toasts.

What a GREAT return visit after the pandemic! We enjoyed a lovely Sunday brunch experience -- great food, drinks & service at the bar! Hubby and I both commented that Delicia will easily become our new go-to-brunch spot! :)

We enjoyed an early dinner on a Sunday with my folks at Stone Creek. We were seated in a booth in the back corner of the restaurant which was ideal since my folks have experienced hearing loss and often find it difficult to enjoy a meal with conversation at a restaurant if we're not seated away from the masses.
Our server, Brie, took great care of us during our visit.

HUGE DISAPPOINTMENT! WILL NOT RETURN!
On recommendation from a FB friend, we decided to drive to Broad Ripple for an early Sunday dinner at this establishment. Posted hours for this restaurant indicates they are open until 8:00 PM on Sundays. We arrived before 5:30 PM only to find a handwritten sign posted on the exterior of the door which read, "SORRY ... SALES MUCH BETTER THAN EXPECTED.

Will not return. Have given this location numerous second chances for improved customer service, but have observed no positive changes. Their drive thru service is terrible, both in terms of employee attitude and communication skills as well as their seriously lacking audio technology. This location is a complete disappointment and in need of a serious management overhaul.

sample_reviews.text: I really don't know where to start. You will be greeted in a warm Hawaiian way! Suffice to say, it can be very tasty and fun to eat healthy! Breakfast choices include Oatmeal Pancakes, (try the Organic Macadamia Nut Sauce as a change from Maple Syrup), the Loco Moco (a bowl with your choice of Basmati brown rice or Jasmine white rice with a lean free range buffalo patty, an organic egg over easy,...

Wasn't aware this place existed off Race Track! I was called to a Race Mgmt Meeting here and blown away when I got here.
I had been to the Ybor location but wasn't expecting what I saw! When I hit the Yelp page, I had to run around looking at all the facets of this jewel!

Needed overdue breakfast after my run but wanted to get to the Outlets to score new running singlets. Figured I would have to settle for a corp joint but saw this place and opted for local.

Amy served me and briefed me on Brunchies. I looked around, found the atmosphere clean and airy.

I went here one Saturday afternoon. I walked in and it was like a ghost town. The people there looked like they couldn't care less about us being there. I order the rice paper spring rolls as an appetizer. There wasn't much they could do to screw them up. Then I followed with a Spicy Tuna Roll and a Spider Roll.I figured that we would take a step up if these rolls were decent.

My granddaughter loves to come here and eat their Chicken Noodle soup...for breakfast! 
My preference is a Breakfast power sammie...340 cals. Hazelnut coffee ok but Einstein Bros rocks it better. The fact that they do post calories on their items is helpful and scare me at the same time. As a Triathlete, I can have a healthy meal under the 450 calorie amount for a cookie! It tells me a lot.
The Pancakes here are delicious and cut clean without falling apart. One of the best places for Pancakes!
Best Huevos Rancheros in Pinellas County. If it's on the menu,  ORDER IT!

sample_reviews.text: Had an incredible experience at WoB this weekend celebrating my birthday with friends. Beer list is out of this world, definitely the most expansive in the region. Staff were all really kind, funny, and focused on providing strong customer service and answering all of our questions. Group had probably 12 different beers, including some real unique ones. Also had some tater tots that were great.

Adding a new experience that bumped up my experience to a 5 star one. Had lunch here the other day and experienced terrific service, good food, and a great craft selection on draft. Prices are super manageable considering Whole Foods reputation. $10 for a delicious fried chicken sandwich with some awesome potatoes.

I love Federal Donuts. So much so that I will walk about 2 miles out of my way in the cold and rain to gorge myself on donuts and fried chickens on a hungover morning in Old City. I have had 8-10 of their types of donuts and I still have yet to find one better than the classic cinnamon sugar.

Stopped in this Cost location for a quick breakfast this morning and I was really disappointed. Something that is always super important to me, ESPECIALLY at chain restaurants, is service. As a former employee of a large chain restaurant, I know that management is everything. 

This morning I was disappointed in the service I received from all members of staff.

Hill us been a constant spot for my family since we moved to the area years ago. They are quick, nice service, and fine Americanized Chinese food. All of the duck dishes are delicious and we consistently order the sesame chicken which has good flavor. Recently, we have ordered the Szechuan noodles off the appetizer menu and have really loved them.
Cash only and you WILL wait, even if you get here before it opens at noon be ready to wait at least 15 minutes.
Sometimes people wait by the door to get to go food and it looks like a line to order but really isn't.

We had the delicious rosa drink and the extra fragrance made it different from  Caribbean version or Asian version of hibiscus drink.  The grill pork was delicious.

This Puerto Rican restaurant located in the same Plaza as kaleisia tea Lounge and Panda Hut, right next door to  ABC Seafood Restaurant. The interior  was nicely decorated with a cozy feeling at the sitting area. We had the relleno de papa . 3 fried  potato balls with some ham inside, tasted  crunchy and delicious. I tried the guava flavor of lotus brand can juice. It was pretty good.

This place impressed me by the wide variety of fresh bread, sandwich, cookie, cake. I bought five different bread and they were all so delicious- fresh, moist, good texture. Each bread was like $1.25  or $1.50. I also got an iced coffee- fresh brew expresso blend into cold milk and sugar ( I told her I liked my coffee sweet) so delicious and strong coffee kick.
Love it. From food, grocery, clothes, everything I need, I can find them here. good price and good selection, plus friendly staff.

We got there 30 minutes before close on a Saturday night and there was a customer in front of  us arguing with the food truck owner about a Groupon deal. It took them 10 minutes to resolve the problem so we kept waiting. We ordered 8 different taco . The tacos were just okay for me.  The Ceviche was pretty good. The pricing, service, ambience were 3 stars.

Proper post-quarantine Charcuterie board and cocktails, STAT! ..was my mood for past 2 weeks since we were allowed to dine out again. We called in for a reservation to make sure we didn't strike out for the dining room being at full capacity and used open table for Saturday night at 8 pm reservation. We came when it wasn't as busy so therefore got great service. Monday MLK day at 330p was our choice. Love that a place like this has landed in the burbs of Wesley Chapel. sample_reviews.text: This is one of my favorite breakfast places, and definitely my favorite place to get banana pancakes. Those things are massive, and they come with a delicious buttery syrup/ sauce that tastes so much better than regular syrup. This place also has the best fresh squeezed Valencia orange juice.

Delicious!!!!!!!!!!!!!! My bf and I just returned from brunch at Centennial, and we are both fully satisified with our meals!

First off, let me say... we were on our way to the White Dog Cafe. On a whim, my bf suggested we try the Centennial.

I love this place! Went today for takeout and got the "Boy Am I Hungry." (Yep... that's what it's called! LOL)  It consisted of eggs any style, french toast or waffle,bacon or sausage, (and yes, they have turkey!), and homefries or grits. Once again, my food was delicious!!!

Add to that how friendly the staff is, and Centennial is among my favorite brunch spots in Philly!

The best thing about this place is its location. It's the best place on South Street to sip on a cool drink, catch a patio breeze and people watch. Don't go there and expect to eat anything edible..... lol

I ordered a pizza, and those morons used AMERICAN CHEESE on it instead of mozzarella. Who does that? When I complained about my food, my friend and I were ASKED to leave!

Okay, Two DAYS later and I'm still feeling the effects of that cheesesteak. Why?? Did they cook my chicken cheesesteak on a dirty grill that had remnants of pork juice on it, maybe??  I am in bed BALLED UP right now.  

 Larry's..... I hate you!!!
This Starbucks charged me 83 cents more than the ones down the street for a banana bread and tall hot chocolate.  Really?? Like Starbucks isn't expensive enough.

Guy at the counter

sample_reviews.text: Lunched here today. It was nice. Sweet look to the place. Excellent roast beef sandwich. Good service. Intriguing menu. I'll be back.

Hands-down my favorite Chinese place in St. Louis (doesn't hurt that it's three minutes from my house. But why travel when the Wei Hong is nearby). 
I've never been disappointed here. The meat is well-cooked. The vegetables, fresh. The bakery items, delish. And the lo mein? Excellent. Plus it's cheap and fast and easy, and most of the patrons typically Chinese (always a good sign).

The Good Pie is better than good. What a great place.
I stopped in here the other day to pick up a couple of pizzas after the line at Pappy's was totally out of control. We stayed for a beer while we waited, and it seems like a great little pizza place.
Nice atmosphere - small joint, brick walls, wood-fired oven, friendly staff.

Yeah. I'm a hater.
Well, "hater" may be a bit strong, as I've got no real quarrel with Ted Drewes. But I just don't really get what all the fuss is about. As the little Yelp ratings guide suggests, I've experienced better.
No, I'm not from St. Louis, so I guess I don't have the hard-wired Pavlovian attraction to this place.

Went here the other night for the first time since it re-opened. This seems more like the place I'd heard a lot of good things about. Vastly improved from a visit last summer.
Pleasant, friendly staff, good drinks menu, full but not too-crowded on a Friday night. We only stayed for one round and we ran into friends, so I didn't get to check it out fully, but I'd go back.

sample_reviews.text:
While Cambodia is pretty high on my list of to-go places, I haven't yet made it there. Thankfully, this restaurant exists to give me a taste of Cambodian food in the meantime. This place is tons better than New Phnom Penh, my only previous experience with Cambodian. 

This place is awesome. The skewered meatballs are so juicy and a steal at $1.50 per skewer of 4.

The name of this place sounds a lot like "shoe kitchen" in some combination of romance languages, which would not seem super appetizing. However, the food they serve up here is quite the opposite. 

We tried the short rib tacos and the Cap'n Crunch tilapia burrito. AMAZING. Both were filled with delicious, fresh, and healthy-tasting ingredients.

Wow, who knew Italian food in a divey Irish pub could be so good? The lobster and shrimp ravioli was fantastic, and a steal at $15. Cash only, but totally worth the ATM fee. I'll definitely have to come back next time I'm in Philly.

"Meh. I've experienced better." Pretty much sums it up. 

We had a reservation for 8:30, but were not seated until about 40 minutes later. I understand that sometimes patrons refuse to leave, which is out of the restaurant's control, but that's something they should account for when booking reservations.

I came here really excited to try one of the only Taiwanese restaurants in the Philly area. I'm Taiwanese and grew up eating my mom's Taiwanese cooking, and this place was kind of disappointing. I guess it's semi-authentic, but that doesn't mean it's GOOD. I don't really get these glowing reviews. 

The beef noodle soup was fine but average. Nowhere near the best I've had.

sample_reviews.text:
Delicious! I finally made it to Cheu after months of hearing about its greatness from my friends. 

Went for lunch and even at 1:00 on a weekday, the place was saturated with hungry lunch-goers. If you're not too concerned about where you sit, belly-up to the bar and make friends with the chef. Watch as he effortlessly prepares each dish fresh.

Not only will you learn a great many things from taking a tour of this beautiful space, but the drinks are super tasty. 

For $10 you can get yourself educated and tour the distillery, which used to house horses and has some original 1906 architecture. Afterwards, you'll head up to the third floor tasting room which is rustic and gorgeous.

Beer here! The fermentaria is the place where the brewing happens, but it's also a bar/restaurant. There are a decent amount of beers on tap and you can get various sized pours, so try them all! 

The tacos are probably the stand-out food item here. The bartender told us that the beer list here differs from the one at the café, so it's in your best interest to try both places.

I don't really have great things to say about Ants Pants, but I also don't have many bad things to say either.  It's just...forgettable.  

First, it's cash only, as are most places in this area.  The wait for brunch on the weekends is insane and in my opinion, not worth it.

Healthy fast food, now that's what I'm talkin' about.  However, it does come at a price.  I had a salad, which ran me just about ten bucks after tax.  Basically, I paid ten bucks for arugula, avocado, black beans/corn and some spicy cilantro dressing.  No protein?!
The gluten free coffee cake is delish!

sample_reviews.text: Wow! Very impressive confections and cakes. Highly recommended. The cool is made with care and artistry and the result of delicious. Apple bourbon cake was complex and not cloying, but boasted a lot of sweet flavor. Fruit tart was a sweet crust with a rich filling complimenting the tart berries. Red velvet cream-cheese-frosting topped cupcake was great! I love it. Very much recommended.
Cool place, cool owners, and good location. A great choice for campus dwellers who want something other than chain-store coffee. Recommended. My go-to spot for meetings on campus.

Honestly, I can't think of a better idea for parents with young children than a playground with beer. Kids are occupied, you're relaxed, and when it comes time for food, it's easy for kids to eat. I guess you get what your pay for, because it was pricier than I thought, but still worth it. 

The playground could be a smidge safer. Put railings on the ramp instead of rope.

A classic neighborhood establishment, but it doesn't stand out among other gastropubs in town. It is popular enough to attract brewers and host cool events, but it's not as serious as Bridgid's, in example. The beer program is above average though.

Is everything worse in the suburbs? This Acme had everything I needed--but I was just picking up breakfast items. But check out took WAY too long and was tedious, too. Employees seem to know what they are doing, there just aren't enough of them, and they work slowly.
Super charming and charismatic baristas!
Great alternative to the big chain stores. Open and welcoming. Friendly staff.

sample_reviews.text: This is the only place I have found in the tri-state area that sells bottles of Choya Umeshu with real ume fruits in the bottom.  If you're unfamiliar with umeshu, it's alcohol made from a Japanese apricot.  It's sweet and a little tart.  I got hooked on it in Japan, and I am so happy I can have it again.  Also, this places carries real sake!

If you like beer at all and are around the Philadelphia area, you really should do yourself a favor and take a trip to this brewery.  There are many interesting beers that I would actively seek out, and they come in a variety of sizes. I normally don't get excited about beer as wine or cocktails are my drinks of choice.  However, I felt differently after tasting the beers here.

Maribel has changed their name, but the delicious flavor and friendly service have stayed the same. I find myself returning here often for their Mexico City style quesadillas. If you have never had this, or even if you have, it's a must order!

I originally would have given Red Robin 3 stars, but my last experience three weeks ago was so terrible that I just can't do that anymore.  My server was one of the worst I've ever had.  I just can not bring myself to go back for fear of getting the same guy and having this experience all over again.

Now that there is a Whole Foods in Glenn Mills, I don't find myself at the Fresh Market as often.  Also the last couple times I've been, it's been a let down in the quality of the food.  However, I was craving their chocolate cake this week and wanted to celebrate my last class of graduate school being over, so I decided to go and pick some of that up along with the provolone stuffed olives I...
Sunday there is a tasting menu for $38 a person. You can try a lot of different things!
Try the seasonal sides! Right now they have plantains fried in garlic oil and roasted fennel. So good!

sample_reviews.text: Nashville has a few breweries, and it can be hard for newer breweries to stand out. I went to Various Artists with a friend yesterday, and our experience was pretty amazing. I mean it's not every day that you go to a brewery intending to stay 2 hours but stay ALL DAY. That's what happened at Various Artists. We got there pretty soon after they opened at noon and got great seats at the bar.

COVID UPDATE: We at brunch at Butcher & Bee for my husband's birthday last weekend (it's his favorite restaurant in Nashville), and it was a great dining experience. We made a reservation (on Resy) for 10:00 a.m. They offered inside or outside dining, but we chose to eat outside - and I'm glad we did! What a beautiful patio! The tables were well spaced and the servers wore masks.

I visited Nectar for lunch with a friend recently, and I really loved it. I wish I hadn't waited so long (the actual day before I was moving away) to try it! So, what's great about Nectar? It reminds me a lot of bars and restaurants in my home state (Texas) - a large outside area has covered picnic tables, games, a stage, and area to roam around! It's really beautiful property.

We went to Germantown Pub for trivia night. So - I'm a bit of a trivia snob. And the trivia at Germantown Pub seems like it might be just getting off its feet, because it was a bit disorganized and a bit slow (but still fun - it's trivia after all)! 

Now to the Pub! Food was pretty typical bar food options. Beer options are okay, but it doesn't have the great beer options!

So, yeah, Old Glory is STUNNING. Higher ceilings than I've ever seen in any bar or restaurant! Definitely a highly Instagram-able place! BUT...I tend to shy away from cocktails if I don't know what half the ingredients are. And I tend to shy away from places that have 10+ minute waits for drinks. And if those cocktails are overpriced.
In December 2020 with COVID cases soaring, they are still allowing people to eat at their three tables inside. Its impossible to distance and staff is only so so about mask wearing.
It's closed!

sample_reviews.text: A great spot of happy hour, especially if your friend gets there early to save you a seat.  The staff was super accommodating of my vegetarianism and made us some specials. The moral of all the food I tried was definitely order something if it has their house chimichurri on it! They have a nice selection of beers, I went with a seasonal local craft.

Thanks to Erin M I am addicted to their burritos! At $5 it's hard to find a better deal around (ahem Chipotle, ahem) and their burritos are flavorful and filling. I can make one last for 2 meals if I'm not starving. I usually order the tofu verde since I enjoy the level of spice in the sauce.

I finally got back to Cheu after tasting their food at the pop-ups two years ago. I was surprised to find it was even better than I remember! I started my meal with a Sixpoint Bengali Tiger IPA which I have found pairs very well with spicy food. We got the brussels sprouts to start which were great with the pepitas and pickled squash.

I was unfortunately disappointed on my last visit to Las Cazuelas.  With all of our friends heading to Mexico this week, I had a craving for some margaritas and spicy food.  I finally decided to order something other than my usual and I am still regretting it 5 days later  Unlike previous visits when I had always had the vegetarian enchiladas, I decided to try the Chile Rellano.

Stopped here for a quick lunch during the week while in area for work. Service was prompt and the food was tasty. I will say it was slightly more expensive then the Vietnamese I am used to getting in the city and I was disappointed to not find the usual condiments on the table to spice up my meal. That being said, the menu was clear and I ate the whole plate.

Josef's has been a staple in our community for forever and being in the wedding business I've gotten the chance to taste their cakes at events, but I had never been into the bakery itself before the other day. 

And let me tell you, it's like pastry heaven in there.. it smells so good and there is just so many goodies to choose from.

I love trying new things and going places with unique menus but sometimes coffee places can be a little intimidating for someone who doesn't drink a lot of coffee. Old world makes it really easy to figure out what you want whether you're a coffee aficionado or a novice drinker.

This remodel of an old favorite on California popped up on my Yelp when I was searching for a place that had good gluten free options. 

We came in on a Tuesday night to check it out. The bar and finding space is very nice and was kept super clean and we were greeted right away by the staff. Our waitress was super attentive and very friendly.

Can you expect much from a Taco Bell at 1 in the morning? Probably not.. but I did expect a little more than I received... 

The service was super fast and friendly, but the food was really awful. (I know, what did I expect?) the cheesy potato griller was soggy, the Doritos locos tacos could have had a better ratio of meat to toppings, and the nacho fries were cold and limp.

The reviews here weren't great, but my friends had been in a couple times and they said they enjoyed it, so we decided to have dinner here. The new building they moved into is a really weird space. The ceilings, lighting, and decor are all very industrial and kind of made it hard to relax.

sample_reviews.text: Took an out of town guest in to get some fish tacos.  Got the battered fish taco platters with rice and beans, a cheese quesadilla, and large margaritas.  According to our guest the battered fish tacos were awesome, and the sauce on them was phenomenal and they would've liked more sauce.  The cheese quesadilla was good.  The margaritas are premixed and could've used some extra tequila.

This past Saturday we had a reservation for dinner at BELLISSIMO RISTORANTE ITALIANO in Casino Del Sol.  It was fabulous!  The three cheese lasagna is out of this world yummy!  The others in my party had the chicken marsala and the chicken alfredo.  Everyone at the table was very pleased with the food.

Yummy in my tummy! Nice greeting from the employees, when you walk in the door.  Then you step in front of the most marvelous display of gelato you've ever seen.  Good selection of flavors to choose from...from vanilla bases, chocolate richness, fruity selections....you name it.  If you get a medium or large cup, you can actually pick from 2 to 3 flavors.
Out running errands and we spotted this place and stopped in for lunch.  I've had way better pizza.  Service was good, but the food is just not enough to make me come back again.

Very good customer service, with the young gentleman working the counter.  However...we wish the food was better.  The husband said the tuna sub was not good and was packed with too many onions almost acting as a filler.  I had the veggie delight...and let's say it wasn't much of a delight.  Ok...we will stick with Subway...more freshness for your buck.
Christmas Eve dinner tradition.  Nice night out, friendly staff and fantastic food.

Hello!!!! Winning!!!! Only a month old!!!!  

If you build it, THEY WILL COME.

The spot of the former ICE HOUSE on Gentilly across from the Baptist Seminary; between 'God is good car wash' &  Church's Chicken is this god-send of a coffee shop.

Yassssssssssssssssssssssssssssss!!!!!! 

So glad they're OPENED!!!! I've been waiting. And I will say I AM NOT DISAPPOINTED!!!!

The fun starts when you get in the parking lot... The ANTICIPATION MOUNTS!!!!!

Loads of people. Full bar. Drinks galore. Food on deck.

The wait on opening day for a table was 2 hours; so we saddled up to the bar. The drink concoctions are AMAZING!!!!! SnowCones...

Ohhhhh my gosh!!!! Passing here several times before, I never knew this little Mexican Food-Mecca lived here. I mean... Sheeeesh!!! I mean call me Senõrita Teneha because I've fallen in LOVEEEEE (lub-dub-lub-dub)... From the beautifully colored exterior, the the open "get at me and chill patio" to the drink specials & the here little fishy here little fishy fish tacos...

NASTY NASTY NASTY!!!!!! Steer clear.  I've been told they've had trouble with the Board of Health on several separate occasions.  I live very near here and I won't even get a cup of coffee from here (I drive to the one on Elysian Flds & I-610).  

The last time I visited this restaurant was in 2006 and the staff were rude, incompetent, impatient & unprofessional.  I don't get anything from here.

Here to use the MINUTE CLINIC... great idea, easy to access but PRICING IS HORRIBLE. HIGHWAY ROBERY. How can they get away with charging $35 to place a TB Skin test and then $30 to read it???? Hello CVS stop being dubious with your pricing.

sample_reviews.text:
Cool little Hawaiian-Taiwanese fusion food truck.

This truck has been on my radar for months, but only recently did they have an event in Tampa. Usually, they are in Plant City or Lakeland. 

The food is an interesting fusion of Hawaiian food and Taiwanese food. I ordered the HEFTY, which is Taiwanese beef with rice on a scallion pancake.

This review is for their drive thru.

The Brisket Shoppe serves up brisket and sausage for about half the week (they are closed for the first half of the week). They have outdoor seating, but I went through the drive thru. They also have curbside service if you call ahead.

I tried a hot link, a jalapeno cheddar sausage and the coleslaw.

This review is for delivery.

My husband had a late night craving for wings. I checked Uber Eats to find out who was still opening and delivering, and we decided to try District Tavern.

The food arrived about 40 minutes after we placed the order. The restaurant is about 10 minutes away from us, and the food was piping hot when we opened it up. We got 12 lemon pepper chicken wings.

This place might serve good Thai food by 1979 standards (although I'd disagree with that too), but it's not authentic.  Having been to numerous excellent Thai restaurants in the US as well as in Thailand, the food I was served at Thailand Restaurant was not good.  The best thing about the place was the decor, which was 1979 Thai appropriate.  But the food -- no thank you.

We tried this place recently when I was wanting pizza that delivered that wasn't Dominos.  

I wanted to like it a lot more than I did.  There was something that was off about it, either the huge, slightly sweet pepperoni slices or the rectangular shaped pie or the weird cheese.

They did deliver more quickly than promised, which is a plus.
Cass Street is currently under construction. Park north of Cass to avoid crossing an active construction zone.
Currently open 10 - 3, must wear mask.

If this weren't a diet based restaurant, I would've rated them a 3. The food was good, but obviously not as good as other restaurants that cook with naughty ingredients. 

I enjoyed the pizza but wasn't crazy about the massive cherry tomatoes. I wish they were most blistered from the fire oven. Maybe that's a little nit-picky? Regardless, I picked about half off the pizza.

I typically enjoy my experiences at Chatham. We had a great, attentive bartender who kept the beers flowin'. We ordered the wings with the House sauce, which was not spicy and pretty sweet. I would order again but probably a more traditional buffalo sauce. 

We ordered a large cheeseburger pizza, which could easily feed 4 adults. The toppings were tasty.

REPEAT CUSTOMERS! We LOVED this cafe so much that we ate here twice plus ordered a box of pastries for the next morning! My husband and I both tried as much as the menu as possible: the croissant egg sandwiches (perfection), House coffee and latte, the bacon onion quiche (he LOVED it), lemon pastry, cherry cheese pastry, chocolate hazelnut, plus a to go order of pastries (the apple turnover,...

The food here is a total guilty pleasure. We love the fresco melt and milkshakes so much. 

Stopped in here for a late night cheat. We were the second car in the drive-thru and had to wait 25 minutes until we decided to drive away. What the heck, SNS??? 

I guess my waistline is happy I didn't eat those 3,000 calories, but DARN IT I was looking forward to it.

I wish I could rate this 4.5. Our first experience at this restaurant on a Wednesday night. Only one waitress was working so service was a teeny bit slow. Despite being slow, she gave us great guidance on ordering the honey wine and which spice level to choose for food. 

We loved the food! I will definitely return to eat more of the chicken dish and the yellow lentils.
Pizza crust is Paleo friendly or "regular"!
expect to wait in line for 10+ minutes and possibly longer after order has been placed.

sample_reviews.text: My fiancee' and I came for lunch one day because, well, I had a $25 gift card.  So why not?  Perusing the menu, we both got super excited about the "duo" lunches.  You pick an entree and neverending soup or salad, and it's between $7 and $9 depending on your entree, and it includes the neverending breadsticks.  And of course we opted to get neverending soup AND salad for the extra $3.

My new neighborhood seafood and poboy shop!!!  I've been here a couple times and have tried a few different things on the menu.  I've been pretty impressed so far!  Here's what I've had:
Turkey poboy - pretty good.  Nothing special, but it's hard to make a turkey poboy more than what it is.
Breaded veal poboy - excellent.  Rivals my favorite veal poboy (from Tag's in Chalmette).

I met a friend for lunch here recently and, having really like the uptown location, was excited to try this one out (I work in the CBD, so convenience...).  Anyway, we had spring rolls to start and I had the shrimp vermicelli as my entree.  The spring rolls seemed to be made freshly and with fresh and tasty ingredients.  And the peanut sauce that came with them was just awesome.

I was excited to come to Piccadilly because I hadn't been since I was a good.  Well, the nostalgia wore off very quickly.  I decided to try to be somewhat healthy and got the lemon-pepper baked chicken, fried okra (I said somewhat), carrot souffle, cornbread sticks, and pie.  
Well, the chicken was weird.  I don't know, but I didn't finish it.  The souffle and okra were good, as usual.

We'd not heard good things, mainly about service, but we decided to check it out anyway.  Service was ok enough, but the food was mediocre at best.  I had the lentil soup and wood-grilled chicken.  The soup was decent enough, but the chicken was kinda meh.  It was seasoned and cooked ok, but nothing special.  It was... seasoned chicken.  My side veggies were, well, boiled veggies.  No seasoning.
I want to do the Doberge. It's so good
Cookie dough!!

We decided to make reservations at The Bellwether for our six year met-iversary date night. Anxiously anticipated! We arrived ten minutes before our reservation time and were sat immediately on a busy friday night. The ambiance inside is industrial, romantic, eclectic, the bar is nearly wall-to-wall and there are some curtained booths along the side.

So fresh is so very good!  And yes, it is made fresh.  You choose a wrap, bowl or salad and can customize to your liking by adding proteins like salmon, chicken, steak, shrimp.  You can add zoodles, very tasty and I recommend you add these.  Zoodles are zucchini cut into thin spaghetti like shapes and they come deliciously marinated.  They also have tofu as an add on.  

Very friendly staff.

Conveniently located across from Busch Gardens, this fast food Mediterranean restaurant serves some yummy falafel Sammie's!  Made fresh so you can customize. Comes with hummus if you like and Mediterranean style pickles. 

Also got a Greek salad that I saved for later consumption. Kareem is a jokester and kept me laughing while taking order.

Within walking distance of our hotel, hubby saw the happy hour .50 oysters and was over joyed. 

He ordered up 2 dozen raw oysters and loved them. 

I had the lobster shrimp tacos and it came with 3 bite sized tacos which were very good. Washed down with a tall Dark and Stormy. 

The Chef came over and gave hubby 2 extra large oysters as he said they were so large he wanted to give them away.

If you are a die hard gambler then the hard rock will fit the bill. I'm more into tangible spending but have dropped a few $$ in the slots. When I come here it's usually with out of town guests. 

Not as nice as Foxwoods in Connecticut and doesn't compare to Vegas but will serve your desire to live the dream. No free drinks and no ventilation.

Teetering between 3 and 3 1/2 stars. 1/2 star extra for the location and surprising lack of sit down restaurants within walking distance to Clearwater beach near the pier. Lots of fried options and a few broiled platters on the menu. 

Cute decor and live music on the downstairs. We sat upstairs and couldn't hear the music.

We ordered the spinach crab dip for starter.

What a great way to end a Sunday Funday at the zoo with a stop at Ted Drewes on the way home. Today we found a good parking spot near the building. This place is so popular sometimes you need to park across the street and prance through the traffic. The selection and sizes are perfect.

The "teacher" taught us about the proper way to pour a beer, how to serve a beer, what to look for in the swish of the veil of the beer and finally we got to sample. sample_reviews.text: Looking for bottles of craft beer? The Hop Shop has AMAZING selection of craft beers from around the country. Not sure where to start? Their staff are very knowledgeable about beer and can help you find just the one for you. They also have local craft beers on tap that you can taste, drink a half or a full pint. You are also able to purchase the bottle and drink in the store as well.

A lot of good beer here plus an excellent outdoor space. They have several picnic tables across the lawn, all socially distanced, and many with umbrellas. They also have a dedicated patio space  with good social distance.

You can order beer from their website for pick up or buy it by the can/bottle there. They have 2 pick up areas to help things go quickly.

I don't get out to O'Fallon, IL very often, being a south city resident. However, I will gladly make the trek to Gia's every time because the pizza is legit tasty as heck! Of all the places on our pizza passport this summer, this is our favorite.

We ordered the chicken alfredo pizza. You get toppings end to end, nice crisp thin crust, tasty squares of pizza.

Definitely over priced. I ordered a mushroom and cheese bruschetta plate and was wholly underwhelmed. It come on a sizzling plate and the cheese looked melted but was actually quite cold, as if it was an afterthought grabbed from the refrigerator before being brought to the table. 

Also, while I was there, it was after the lunch rush so there only a handful of tables with people.

Standard Italian American food. I ordered the penne primavera which was ok. There were no outstanding flavors but nothing bad about it. The cheesy garlic bread was not as toasty as I like but at least they use proved cheese on it. Service was good, but again, nothing remarkable.
Whole menu is dairy free for those lactose intolerant (like me) and vegans out there!
The hand brew coffees are the best! Ask the barista for a recommendation and why. Very knowledgeable and can help you discover why you like the coffee you do

sample_reviews.text:
I'm a big fan of Tria but I normally go to the one on Rittenhouse.  The upside of the Tria on Spruce is that they have the $5 happy hour specials and it is more spacious than the other location.  

I was sad to see they took off the Fresh Mozz with Prosciutto Panino.  We got the artichoke one instead..it was okay.  Not a new favorite of mine.  The happy hour cheese was Daru; too mild for me.

After talking about coming to Sbraga for so long, I finally made reservations to come here for my birthday with the bf.  The seating is a bit of a squeeze.  There's hardly room between the tables.  I felt like I was mooning the next table when I got up to go to the restroom.  

Anyway, they started us off with some popovers.  Okay, fine..they are wayyyy better than the ones I make.

Coworker and I decided to order some tenders for lunch.  They offer delivery and we went with that.  It was a bit strange ordering since it's by weight and we wanted two separate orders but it was going to the same place.  I wanted three pieces but I got four (one was a smaller size) and that was around $4.50.  I got the Pommery Honey mustard.

I know I promised my stomach that I would avoid chain restaurants if I could help it, especially around my neighborhood. I'm not saying they're bad, but just too commercialized for my taste. Red lobster is usually always packed with cars, as well as the Olive Garden next to it so maybe it wasn't too bad.. It was rainy that morning after our adventure at the driving range.

My gf had been here before and enjoyed it so a couple of our friends got together and had dinner here.  It's located in a strip mall plaza and seems out of place. 

We ordered a bunch of appetizers and a variety of sushi rolls.  The tuna pizza was pretty good but was covered in a lot of sauce.  I liked the kani salad and it had a good spicy kick to it.  The fried calamari was not worth getting.
Let's check out these $1 oysters :).
Got my free sandwich :)

sample_reviews.text:
Even though I live out of town, I have been stalking DD's Instagram page drooling from afar (thanks to my sister planting her visits in my head).  On our recent visit home, this was one of the "must eat" at spots on our list. I was drawn in by the doughnuts as they change daily. 

There's an industrial sort of vibe about this place.

What an adorable little place! My sister took me here during a recent trip home for the weekend. We split an order of the omelet with bacon, avocado and cheese and opted for egg whites to be used. This came with a side of their grits were were AMAZING!  Also split an order of the Belgian waffle with Nutella and mixed berries. YUMMY! I am a big fan of flavored iced teas.

I wish I could transport this restaurant to Orlando. Because I KNOW I would be getting my Belgian malt waffle fix every weekend! 

I did a random search for breakfast spots in the area and the reviews for this place roped me in! So glad we went here!  It's set back in a small plaza and parking was really not an issue since the neighboring businesses were closed.

Came here during a recent trip home from FL.  Parking is off street or you can take your chance in the private lot across the street.  The shop was busy with tourists and students but that is to be expected since it was featured on Diners, Drive Ins and Dives.  

There is no "line begins here" sign. You just push in wherever there is space, get someone's attention and place your order.

Came here recently for lunch after being away from the area for many many years. Had been here in the past and looks like they have added to the menu and improved the ordering process to make it more efficient. 

We ordered a seafood platter to split. It came with fried catfish, shrimp, oysters , hushpuppies, crab cake and fries. The serving size is massive.

NOT vegetarian/vegan friendly. All
Broth is beef. The modified summer rolls were pathetic- shredded lettuce , tiny sliver of cucumber and vermicelli noodles.
They don't honor free rewards off the Starbucks app

sample_reviews.text:
Simply amazing! 

I'm a part time vegan--usual carnivore. But, when I do treat myself to vegan food, I want it to be really satisfying. Sweet Soulfood did just that! 

I thought Seed was my favorite vegan place in town, but this place has me reconsidering my list. The fried cauliflower--amazing!

Okay, WHOOOOO came up with the fried corn cause that stuff is BOMBBBBBBB!!!!! I get mine with the Parmesan seasoning and omg! I see there are plenty of negative reviews on here. I hope I never have a weird experience because I love this place!

Short review!

Very nice venue! I attended a comedy show there this past week and everything turned out fine. The bar is pretty open and the "den" is very spacious. The staff were friendly and welcoming. The seats were spaced out per COVID precautions. 

Only drawback was my Long Island iced tea. We didn't taste a lick of alcohol. Lol. 

Overall, nice place! Seems great for hosting events.

The ultimate nachos were the best choice (although they lacked enough cheese). I absolutely love McAlister's chili, so they never disappoint. On to the sandwiches...I had the Spicy Southwest on croissant while my guest had a basic Caesar wrap, which he substituted for ranch. The sandwiches were not that memorable or worthy of praise. His was partly empty while mine tasted rather dry.

My first time trying Andrea's left me in a bit of displeasure. I had the crabmeat ravioli, crab meat and eggplant crab cake, and turtle soup. The ravioli's sauce was very bland. Maybe I'm used to some "pizazz" in my food since we live in Cajun country; nonetheless, I was highly dissatisfied. The crab meat inside was dry and didn't appear as fresh as the crabmeat that coated the top of the dish.

sample_reviews.text: My husband and I heard from someone to try out Picas now that we moved to the area - their pizza is all square pies which I did not know. We've gotten take out twice and really do like their pizzas and mozzarella sticks. I will say though, their prices seem a tiny bit high.
Came back here after I received a giftcard and had a great experience. The service and wine and food were all great, loveeddddd the mushroom panini!
I ordered takeout from here at the beginning of the quarantine - it was just as delicious and fresh as it is in the restaurant. I highly recommend!!

I came back one Sunday late afternoon (3:00?) for lunch and the service was insanely slow. Not sure if that was because of shift changes? I sat for about 18 minutes before someone came over to even take our drink order. I was thissssss close to just getting up and walking out when the waitress finally came -- we ordered our drinks and meals and check all at once because it took so long.

I have come to Jones with 3 girlfriends for brunch before and had a perfect experience. Service was awesome, food was delicious, and drinks were great and bottomless.

So... I decided to host my birthday brunch of 12 girls here a few weeks ago.

Ok I have start by saying all the photos of the food is because we had a party of 15

We all enjoyed our food it was soooooo delicious the type of delicious that makes you shake your legs under the table.

I had the stuffed lobster salmon crusted potato with asparagus omg omg it was sooooo delicious.

Also on sundays they have happy hour all day the drinks were priced so cheap I wondered then...

Update review:

Came in when they opened at 11:30 first customers in because my husband really wanted to try the soft shell crab. He was happy when he was able to order it this time.

Much better experience the food was made perfectly the portions was great, my scallops was delicious and not over cooked. 

I had to change my star rating because over all everything was great.

Will be back again.

Went here because I was in the area and a friend always raves about this place.

I ordered potato greens with white rice for my husband he loved it and said it was the best potato greens he's has ever had.

The worst walmart I have ever been to, on my way home and rushing I stopped to get cheese and the cheese had mold on it (this is no joke) i was so grossed out I abandoned my cart and left and kept it moving never will i buy anything that has to go in my mouth from this place

Went here for dinner before a movie I was OK with what I ordered which was an appetizer zucchini sicks and fried clams, my husband ordered a veggie Burger and loved it but over all it's an OK place lots over all I would come back and give it another try my husband loves the endless fries he loves French fries.
New pizza restaurant in the area
This is something Philadelphia needed they have a list of performers lined up
